Methods: `create_publisher()` (opens RELIABLE+VOLATILE+KL=50 publisher on `topic_for(source)`), `start()` (boots 1 Hz suppressed-summary timer), `stop()`, `destroy()`, `publish(*, kind, severity, evidence, rskill_id='', trace_id=None) -> bool` (False when rate-limited). Properties: `topic`, `source`.
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/11-ros2-nodes.md:17:- `packages/openral_perception_ros/openral_perception_ros/reward_monitor_node.py` — `main(args=None) -> None` — **ADR-0057 reward-monitor query service node.** Defines `RewardMonitorNode(Node)` lazily inside `main()`: subscribes the co-active VLA's camera `sensor_msgs/Image` topic(s), buffers frames per camera into a `RollingFrameBuffer` (downsampled to the manifest's `target_fps`, stamped on the node clock so eviction/staleness work in sim + real), and builds a `RobometerReward` backend from a `kind:"reward"` `manifest_path` (`_build_monitor` → `build_reward_monitor`). **query_task_progress service (ADR-0057)**: serves `/openral/perception/query_task_progress` (`openral_msgs/srv/QueryTaskProgress`) — read-only windowed progress/success assessment (`_on_query_task_progress` → `RobometerReward.assess`); returns `ok=False, stale=True` when no fresh frame / no task. The reward counterpart of `scene_vlm_node` (the rolling buffer lives node-side; the sidecar is a stateless scorer). Params: `cameras`, `primary_camera`, `image_topic`, `manifest_path` (required), `task`, `sidecar_host`, `sidecar_port`. The reasoner offers `query_task_progress` when `task_progress_available` is set. Advisory-only — no actuation. **CriticScore leg (ADR-0064, opt-in via `enable_critic_score`)**: a `critic_score_period_s` timer (default 1 s) scores the primary buffer with the default task and publishes a generic `openral_msgs/CriticScore` (`critic_id`=manifest name, `score`=`progress_now` via `critic_score_from_assessment`, `threshold`=`critic_score_threshold` default 0.8, `trace_id` from the active OTel span) on `critic_score_topic` (default `/openral/critic/score`) — the real source the Tier-C `critic_producer_node` consumes. Best-effort: skips silently when stale/no-task/empty-buffer; never crashes the timer. The deploy launch sets `enable_critic_score:=enable_critic`, so `--enable-reward-monitor --enable-critic` makes Robometer drive `/openral/failure/critic`.
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/01-hal.md:29:  - `read_state() -> JointState` — Joint state in description-joint order. Reads live in-process `MjData` (always current), so it **never latches `ROSPerceptionStale`**: a gap > `staleness_limit_s` since the last service means the single-threaded executor was starved (e.g. a slow camera render), not bad data — it emits a one-shot `hal.read_state.starved` WARNING and returns the live state (re-armed on the next healthy read). The prior behaviour raised *before* refreshing the clock, so one transient stall bricked the HAL permanently (the deploy-sim "Joint state is X s old" loop). Async live-feedback staleness is policed by the subscription HALs (`ros_control`/`aloha`), not here.
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/01-hal.md:31:  - `idle_step() -> bool` — **Sim-only** HOLD stepper that gives a bare `MujocoArmHAL` the ADR-0034 (idle cameras stay live) + ADR-0049 (joint_state published off the executor via `ProprioSnapshot` + dedicated thread) treatment the lifecycle node gates on a *callable* `idle_step`. Leaves `ctrl` untouched (it already holds the last commanded / seeded pose) and advances one `mj_step` so physics + the staleness clock refresh without moving the arm. Returns `False` (no-op) when not connected — i.e. after `estop()` disconnects — so it can never autonomously drive an e-stopped robot; `True` otherwise. **Defined on the sim-only `MujocoArmHAL` hierarchy only** (no real HAL inherits it), mirroring the same method-only real-hardware guard as `SimAttachedHAL.idle_step`.
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/01-hal.md:294:- `class SimAttachedHAL` — HAL Protocol adapter wrapping an in-process `SimRollout`. Reads live joint state via `normalized_joint_index` + `mj_name2id`; sends actions via `pack_action_for_env` (or a caller-supplied `ActionPacker`) into `env.step()`. Exposes `read_images()`, `mujoco_handles()`, `sim_time_ns()`, `base_pose`, `base_twist`, `base_pose_6dof()` for the ROS lifecycle node's camera publisher, viewer, sim-clock, and odom wiring. (L327)
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/01-hal.md:305:  - `sim_time_ns() -> int | None` — Cross-reset-monotonic elapsed sim time in ns (ADR-0048 Phase 1) — the seam a sim `/clock` publisher reads. Returns the wrapped `SimRollout.sim_time_ns()` (per-episode) plus an accumulated offset: `connect` and the ADR-0036 auto-resets fold each finished episode's elapsed sim-time into the offset (`_accumulate_sim_time_before_reset`) BEFORE the backend rewinds its clock, so the value is monotonic non-decreasing across `env.reset` (robocasa rewinds `MjData.time` to 0). `None` when the wrapped rollout has no sim clock (clock-less backend / sidecar) — the consumer then falls back to wall time.
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/01-hal.md:333:- `class ProprioFrame` — Frozen dataclass: one coherent proprio sample (`state: JointState`, `base_pose: (x,y,yaw)`, `base_pose_6dof: ((x,y,z),(qx,qy,qz,qw)) | None`, `base_twist: tuple[float, ...]`, `sim_time_ns: int | None` — ADR-0048 Phase 2, sim time carried for the /clock publisher). Plain immutable data only — no live simulator handles — so it is safe to publish from a different thread than the one that stepped the sim. (`proprio_snapshot.py`)
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/03-world-state.md:20:  - `__init__(description, *, staleness_limit_s=DEFAULT_STALENESS_S, clock_fn=None)` (L160)
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/05-inference-runner.md:13:### `python/runner/src/openral_runner/clock.py`
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/07-eval-sim.md:26:  - duck-typed extension: `sim_time_ns() -> int | None` — Optional, NOT part of the Protocol (ADR-0048 Phase 1); the backend's authoritative elapsed sim time in ns, the seam a sim `/clock` publisher reads. MuJoCo-backed adapters return `round(MjData.time * 1e9)` via `sim_time_ns_from_mujoco_handles`. Monotonic non-decreasing *within an episode*; backends that rewind `MjData.time` on `reset` (robocasa) restart it, so a cross-reset-monotonic consumer maintains its own offset (`SimAttachedHAL.sim_time_ns`). `None` = no sim clock (PushT, Isaac Sim sidecar). Callers MUST `getattr(env, "sim_time_ns", None)` and treat both missing + `None` as "no clock" (fall back to wall time).
/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/07-eval-sim.md:28:- `sim_time_ns_from_mujoco_handles(handles: tuple[Any, Any] | None) -> int | None` — Shared helper (ADR-0048 Phase 1): `round(MjData.time * 1e9)` from a `mujoco_handles()` `(model, data)` tuple, `None` when `handles` is `None`. The single place the MuJoCo-backed adapters' `sim_time_ns()` implementations route through. (L21)

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/07-eval-sim.md:191:- _`manifest` (`isaac_manifest_scene.IsaacManifestScene`) — **robot-agnostic, URDF-driven** scene (ADR-0045 amendment). Instead of a hardcoded Isaac `Franka` asset it imports the manifest robot's URDF via `omni.kit.commands` `URDFCreateImportConfig` → `URDFParseAndImportFile` (Isaac's `isaacsim.asset.importer.urdf`), wraps it as `isaacsim.core.api.robots.Robot`, and drives a JOINT_POSITION-delta articulation controller. Action layout `[arm deltas, gripper, base twist]`. `map_dof_to_manifest(values, *, dof_index, manifest_joints, finger_dof_idx, base_values=None, base_joints=None)` (module-level) maps the articulation DOF vector to the **full** manifest joint order: base joints ← the kinematic base pose, arm joints ← URDF DOF by name, the two-finger→one-gripper collapse ← mean of the finger DOFs, else 0.0 (generic replacement for `franka_joint_positions`). **Kinematic holonomic base (M3):** a robot with `base_joints` is imported `fix_base=True` and `_integrate_base(vx, vy, wyaw)` teleports the whole articulation root each step from a base-frame-twist-integrated `(x, y, yaw)` — real base motion + a `base_pose` for `/odom`, no PhysX base joints (the base exists nowhere as an Isaac asset; robosuite composes it in MuJoCo only). Built from the `--robot-spec` JSON. **Verified live:** `scenes/deploy/isaac_franka_urdf.yaml` (`tests/sim/test_franka_urdf_isaac.py` — franka imports from URDF, `/joint_states` carries the imported pose, JOINT_POSITION drives the arm) and `scenes/deploy/isaac_panda_mobile_urdf.yaml` (`tests/sim/test_panda_mobile_isaac.py` — 11-D action, 11-joint `/joint_states` = 3 base + 7 arm + 1 gripper, forward base-twist moves the base). **Manifest-driven sensors:** `_plan_cameras` makes one base-relative Isaac `Camera` per RGB/depth `SensorSpec` (keyed by the RGB `vla_feature_key` suffix `camera1`… / the depth sensor `name`); `_update_camera_poses` rides them on the kinematic base; `_images` returns every RGB frame and `_depth_clouds` returns `{sensor: (N,3) base_link}` via Isaac's `Camera.get_pointcloud(world_frame=True)` (Isaac owns the camera convention) transformed world→base_link by the base pose → `obs["depth_points"]` (`SimSensorBridge` publishes them as `PointCloud2`). A modality the manifest does not declare is never created. **Verified live:** the deploy graph publishes `/openral/cameras/front_depth/points` (62 k pts, `base_link`) → octomap → `/openral/world_voxels`. **2-D lidar:** `_add_obstacles` seeds a few static boxes and `_scan_ranges` casts a PhysX `raycast_closest` fan (each ray starting `range_min_m` past the base to clear the robot's own chassis; robot `/panda` hits ignored) → `obs["scan"]` → `SimAttachedHAL.read_scan` → `/scan`. The full slam-map + obstacle-aware Nav2 loop additionally needs the deploy-sim `/clock` publisher (ADR-0048, merged)._

<sub>⏱️ 10m 56s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found the answer: `/clock` should not be the origin; it should be the ROS projection of a single OpenRAL clock authority. I’m adding that authority as a typed core contract so sim, hardware, and rSkills can all name the same source.

---

1. [project]
2. name = "openral-core"
3. version = "0.1.0"
4. description = "OpenRAL Pydantic schemas — normative contract for the OpenRAL layer interfaces"
5. readme = "README.md"
6. requires-python = ">=3.12,<3.13"
7. license = { text = "Apache-2.0" }
8. dependencies = [
9.     "pydantic>=2.7,<3",  # reason: normative schema framework
10.     "PyYAML>=6.0",       # reason: rSkill manifest (rskill.yaml) loader
11.     "numpy>=1.24",       # reason: openral_core.geometry look-at math (ADR-0065; import-on-demand, not loaded by __init__)
12. ]
13. 
14. [build-system]
15. requires = ["hatchling"]
16. build-backend = "hatchling.build"
17. 
18. [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
19. packages = ["src/openral_core"]
20.
```

</details>

1700.     for ref in description.base_joints[:planar_base_dof]:
1701.         spec = by_name.get(ref)
1702.         if spec is None:
1703.             return None
1704.         sim_names.append(spec.sim_joint_name)
1705.     if any(n is None for n in sim_names):
1706.         return None
1707.     forward, side, yaw = sim_names
1708.     # Narrow tuple[str | None, ...] → tuple[str, str, str] for type checkers.
1709.     assert isinstance(forward, str)
1710.     assert isinstance(side, str)
1711.     assert isinstance(yaw, str)
1712.     return (forward, side, yaw)
1713. 
1714. 
1715. # ─── World state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1716. 
1717. 
1718. class JointState(BaseModel):
1719.     """Real-time joint state snapshot.
1720. 
1721.     Attributes:
1722.         name: Ordered list of joint names.
1723.         position: Joint positions (rad or m).
1724.         velocity: Joint velocities (rad/s or m/s).
1725.         effort: Joint efforts (Nm or N).
1726.         stamp_ns: ROS 2 timestamp in nanoseconds.
1727.     """
1728. 
1729.     name: list[str]
1730.     position: list[float]
1731.     velocity: list[float] = Field(default_factory=list)
1732.     effort: list[float] = Field(default_factory=list)
1733.     stamp_ns: int
1734. 
1735. 
1736. class Pose6D(BaseModel):
1737.     """6D pose: position + quaternion.
1738. 
1739.     Attributes:
1740.         xyz: Position (x, y, z) in meters.
1741.         quat_xyzw: Quaternion (x, y, z, w).
1742.         frame_id: tf2 reference frame.
1743.     """
1744. 
1745.     xyz: tuple[float, float, float]
1746.     quat_xyzw: tuple[float, float, float, float]
1747.     frame_id: str
1748. 
1749. 
1750. class DetectedObject(BaseModel):
1751.     """A detected object in the scene.
1752. 
1753.     Attributes:
1754.         label: Semantic class label.
1755.         confidence: Detection confidence in [0, 1].
1756.         pose: 6D pose in the world or camera frame.
1757.         bbox_3d: 3D bounding box (x_min, y_min, z_min, x_max, y_max, z_max).
1758.         track_id: Persistent track ID across frames.
1759.     """
1760.

```
1840.     """A single sensor frame — metadata plus an optional inline payload.
1841. 
1842.     A :class:`SensorFrame` is the carrier passed from a ``SensorReader`` into
1843.     :class:`WorldState.image_frames`. Exactly one of ``data``,
1844.     ``topic``, or ``handle`` is populated:
1845. 
1846.     * ``data`` carries the pixel bytes for in-process delivery and for trace
1847.       capture. JSON-serialized as base64 by Pydantic, so payloads round-trip
1848.       through ``model_dump_json`` / ``model_validate_json``.
1849.     * ``topic`` points at a ROS 2 topic — the bytes live on the ROS 2 bus and
1850.       the consumer subscribes for them. This is the path the
1851.       ``Ros2ImageSensorReader`` uses and what :attr:`WorldState.images`
1852.       already carries.
1853.     * ``handle`` is an opaque integer (e.g. CUDA NVMM pointer, DMA-BUF file
1854.       descriptor) — in-process only, never serialized. Set by the
1855.       ``GStreamerSensorReader`` when frames stay on the GPU.
1856. 
1857.     Attributes:
1858.         sensor_id: Sensor name; matches :attr:`SensorSpec.name`.
1859.         stamp_monotonic_ns: Capture-time monotonic timestamp in nanoseconds.
1860.         stamp_wall_ns: Capture-time wall-clock timestamp in nanoseconds.
1861.         encoding: How to interpret the bytes.
1862.         width: Image width in pixels.
1863.         height: Image height in pixels.
1864.         channels: Number of channels (3 for RGB/BGR, 1 for MONO/DEPTH).
1865.         data: Optional inline pixel payload (or compressed bytes for
1866.             ``JPEG`` / ``PNG``). Mutually exclusive with ``topic`` /
1867.             ``handle``.
1868.         topic: Optional ROS 2 topic reference. Mutually exclusive with
1869.             ``data`` / ``handle``.
1870.         handle: Optional opaque in-process handle (NVMM / DMA-BUF). Mutually
1871.             exclusive with ``data`` / ``topic``.
1872.         metadata: Free-form per-frame metadata (gain, exposure, …).
1873. 
1874.     Example:
1875.         >>> SensorFrame(
1876.         ...     sensor_id="wrist_rgb",
1877.         ...     stamp_monotonic_ns=1,
1878.         ...     stamp_wall_ns=2,
1879.         ...     encoding=FrameEncoding.RGB8,
1880.         ...     width=640,
1881.         ...     height=480,
1882.         ...     topic="/cameras/wrist_rgb/image_raw",
1883.         ... ).channels
1884.         3
1885.     """
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>51 lines</summary>

```
1. # METHODS.md — Public Symbol Inventory (index)
2. 
3. > **Last cleaned: 2026-06-11** — split the single-file inventory into
4. > per-layer files under [`docs/methods/`](methods/) and refreshed every
5. > `(LNN)` citation via `tools/refresh_methods_linenos.py`. (Previous pass
6. > 2026-05-16: repo-wide de-slop, ADR renumber, `skills/` → `rskills/`.)
7. >
8. > **Purpose.** A flat, layer-ordered list of every class, function, method,
9. > and module-level constant defined in the OpenRAL Python source tree
10. > (`python/`, `packages/`, `tools/`), with signatures and one-line
11. > descriptions, intended as a duplication / redundancy detector.
12. >
13. > **How to search.** Grep the folder, not this index:
14. > `grep -rn <symbol> docs/methods/`. Before writing a new helper, search
15. > here first (CLAUDE.md §1.13); add/rename/move/remove a public symbol →
16. > update the matching `docs/methods/` file in the same PR.
17. >
18. > **This inventory is hand-curated** (generated once via `ast` and then
19. > reorganised by hand). It is **not** normative — the authoritative
20. > contracts remain `openral_core` schemas (Pydantic) and `openral_msgs`
21. > IDL (per CLAUDE.md §1.3). When code drifts, the inventory drifts. Treat
22. > a stale entry as a defect, not a source of truth. `(LNN)` line markers
23. > are kept fresh with `python tools/refresh_methods_linenos.py`
24. > (`--check` reports drift without writing).
25. >
26. > **Format.** `name(args) -> ret` — first docstring line. `(LNN)` is the
27. > source line number. Decorators are tagged in `[@…]`. Pydantic field
28. > attrs are listed inline so cross-model duplication is visible.
29. 
30. ---
31. 
32. ## Inventory files
33. 
34. | File | Scope |
35. |---|---|
36. | [00-core-schemas.md](methods/00-core-schemas.md) | Layer 0 — `openral_core` Pydantic schemas, loaders, URDF resolve, exception hierarchy |
37. | [01-hal.md](methods/01-hal.md) | Layer 1 — HAL Protocol + every robot adapter (real, MuJoCo, sim-attached, lifecycle, transports) |
38. | [02-sensors.md](methods/02-sensors.md) | Layer 2 — sensor catalog, `SensorSpec`/`SensorBundle` factories, ROS publisher, reader protocol |
39. | [03-world-state.md](methods/03-world-state.md) | Layer 3 — state adapter registry, world-state aggregator, spatial memory, geometry/grid, object lift |
40. | [04-rskill.md](methods/04-rskill.md) | Layer 4 — rSkill ABC, runtimes (PyTorch/ONNX/TensorRT), loader, executor, VLA adapters |
41. | [05-inference-runner.md](methods/05-inference-runner.md) | Inference Runner (ADR-0010) — clocks, runner loop, sensor readers, dataset recording |
42. | [06-reasoning-wam-safety-observability.md](methods/06-reasoning-wam-safety-observability.md) | Layers 5–8 — Reasoner core/tool-use, WAM, safety supervisor, observability |
43. | [07-eval-sim.md](methods/07-eval-sim.md) | Eval (sim) — scene/robot registries, SimRunner, scene + policy adapters, benchmark suites |
44. | [08-cli.md](methods/08-cli.md) | CLI — `openral` command tree |
45. | [09-auto-provisioning.md](methods/09-auto-provisioning.md) | Auto-provisioning (detection) — GStreamer perception bus, detector tiers |
46. | [10-tools.md](methods/10-tools.md) | Tools — `tools/*.py` dev utilities (quantization, sidecars, publishers, this file's refresher) |
47. | [11-ros2-nodes.md](methods/11-ros2-nodes.md) | ROS 2 lifecycle nodes (`packages/`) |
48. | [12-tests-hil.md](methods/12-tests-hil.md) | Tests · HIL bridges |
49. | [13-tests-sim-helpers.md](methods/13-tests-sim-helpers.md) | Tests · sim helpers |
50. | [14-duplication-watch.md](methods/14-duplication-watch.md) | **Duplication & Reuse Watch** — confirmed redundancy candidates; recheck before every PR |
51.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>102 lines</summary>

```
1. # Layer 0 — Core Schemas & Exceptions
2. 
3. > Part of the OpenRAL [public-symbol inventory](../METHODS.md). Hand-curated; `(LNN)` markers are refreshed by `tools/refresh_methods_linenos.py`.
4. 
5. Authoritative Pydantic v2 contracts (CLAUDE.md §1.3). Anything imported
6. from `openral_core.__init__` is API; while we are pre-publish the
7. on-disk schemas sit at `schema_version: "0.1"` and the surface evolves
8. in place (CLAUDE.md §1.6).
9. 
10. ### `python/core/src/openral_core/schemas.py`
11. _openral schema v0 — normative Pydantic v2 contracts for all layers._
12. 
13. **Enums**
14. 
15. - `class EmbodimentKind(str, Enum)` — Top-level kinematic class. (L34)
16.   `HUMANOID, MANIPULATOR, BIMANUAL, QUADRUPED, MOBILE_BASE, MOBILE_MANIPULATOR, DRONE`
17. - `class JointType(str, Enum)` — URDF joint type. (L46)
18.   `REVOLUTE, PRISMATIC, CONTINUOUS, FIXED, FLOATING, PLANAR`
19. - `JointRole: TypeAlias = Literal[…]` — Structural classification of a `JointSpec` (ADR-0028a). (L57)
20.   `"arm", "base", "gripper", "torso", "leg", "head", "neck", "wheel", "unknown"`. Used by runner/safety/dataset-bridge to identify a channel without name-substring heuristics. Default `"unknown"` keeps legacy manifests loadable.
21. - `class ControlMode(str, Enum)` — Action space / control interface. (L81)
22.   `JOINT_POSITION, JOINT_VELOCITY, JOINT_TORQUE, JOINT_TRAJECTORY, CARTESIAN_POSE, CARTESIAN_DELTA, CARTESIAN_TWIST, BODY_TWIST, FOOT_PLACEMENT, GRIPPER_BINARY, GRIPPER_POSITION, DEX_HAND_JOINT, COMPOSITE_MODE`
23. - `const BODY_TWIST_DIM: int = 6` — Width of a BODY_TWIST / CARTESIAN_* twist row `(vx, vy, vz, wx, wy, wz)`. Single source for the HAL packers (`openral_hal.panda_mobile`, `openral_hal.sim_attached`) + the safety supervisor that validate 6-vec twist payloads; matches `Action.body_twist` (a 6-tuple). (L134)
24. - `class SensorModality(str, Enum)` — Physical sensing modality. (L147)
25.   `RGB, DEPTH, STEREO, IR, POINT_CLOUD, LIDAR_2D, IMU, FORCE_TORQUE, JOINT_STATE, TACTILE_VISION, TACTILE_ARRAY, AUDIO, GPS, BATTERY`
26. - `class Hand(str, Enum)` — End-effector laterality. (L166) `LEFT, RIGHT, NA`
27. - `class StateRepresentation(str, Enum)` — State vector format. (L713)
28.   `JOINT_POSITIONS, EEF_POS_AXISANGLE, EEF_POS_EULER, EEF_POS_QUAT, EEF_POS_AXISANGLE_GRIPPER`
29. - `class ActionRepresentation(str, Enum)` — Action vector format. (L723)
30.   `JOINT_POSITIONS, JOINT_VELOCITIES, DELTA_EE_6D_PLUS_GRIPPER, DELTA_EE_6D, CARTESIAN_POSE`
31. - `class RSkillAction(str, Enum)` — Closed vocabulary of high-level action verbs an rSkill can perform (ADR-0022); declared on `RSkillManifest.actions` and surfaced to the reasoner LLM tool palette so it can pick a skill by what it does. (L733)
32.   Manipulation primitives: `PICK, PLACE, PICK_AND_PLACE, TRANSFER, GRASP, RELEASE`; articulated / contact-rich: `OPEN, CLOSE, PUSH, PULL, SLIDE, INSERT, POUR, WIPE, ROTATE`; motion: `REACH`; mobile: `NAVIGATE`; social/expressive: `WAVE, SHAKE`; generalist marker (foundation / multi-task checkpoints): `GENERALIST`; perception producer: `DETECT` (ADR-0037, for `kind: "detector"` rSkills); scene VLM: `QUERY` (ADR-0047, for `kind: "vlm"` rSkills).
33. - `class QuantizationDtype(str, Enum)` — Weight numeric format. (L2322)
34.   `FP32, FP16, BF16, INT8, INT4, FP4_NVFP4`
35. - `class QuantizationBackend(str, Enum)` — Inference backend. (L2346)
36.   `PYTORCH, ONNX, TENSORRT, GGUF, MLX`
37. - `class RSkillState(str, Enum)` — Skill lifecycle. (L2418)
38.   `UNCONFIGURED, INACTIVE, ACTIVE, FINALIZED, ERROR`
39. - `class RSkillLicensePosture(str, Enum)` — License posture (CLAUDE §7.4). (L2498)
40.   `APACHE_2_0, MIT, BSD, PERMISSIVE_RESEARCH, NVIDIA_NON_COMMERCIAL, NVIDIA_OPEN_MODEL, RLWRLD_NON_COMMERCIAL, PROPRIETARY, UNKNOWN` (NVIDIA_OPEN_MODEL = GR00T N1.7+, commercial OK — ADR-0046)
41. - `class RSkillRuntime(str, Enum)` — Manifest runtime hint. (L2512)
42.   `PYTORCH, ONNX, TENSORRT, TRT_LLM, VLLM, GGUF, MLX, JAX`
43. - `class PhysicsBackend(str, Enum)` — Sim backend. (L4834)
44.   `MUJOCO, MUJOCO_MJX, PYBULLET, SAPIEN, ISAACSIM, COPPELIASIM, GENESIS, MOCK` (SAPIEN = ManiSkill3 / RoboTwin engine; RoboTwin uses it via a py3.10 sidecar — ADR-0061; `COPPELIASIM` = CoppeliaSim/PyRep RLBench backend, out-of-process py3.10 sidecar — ADR-0062)
45. 
46. **Pydantic models — robot manifest hierarchy**
47. 
48. - `class IntrinsicsPinhole(BaseModel)` — Pinhole camera intrinsics. (L177)
49.   fields: `width, height, fx, fy, cx, cy, distortion_model, distortion_coeffs`
50. - `scale_intrinsics_to(base, width, height) -> IntrinsicsPinhole` — Linearly rescale pinhole intrinsics to a new render resolution (fx/fy/cx/cy scale by width/height ratios; FOV and distortion preserved). ADR-0035: deploy-sim renders the same MuJoCo camera at `scene.observation_width/height`, so the HAL scales the manifest's nominal intrinsics to the render resolution before the depth back-projection — keeping the published camera model matched to what was rendered. Returns `base` unchanged when the target equals its resolution; raises `ValueError` on non-positive dims. (L205)
51. - `class CameraSimPlacement(BaseModel)` — ADR-0065. Where an RGB sensor's camera sits in the sim MJCF, so the generic HAL camera rig can splice it into a bare-arm MJCF (which ships no `<camera>`) for `deploy sim`. Fields: `parent_body: str | None` (MJCF body the camera mounts to / tracks; `None` = world-fixed in `<worldbody>`), `pos: (x,y,z)` + `target: (x,y,z)` (look-at point, parent or world frame), `fovy_deg: float | None` (else derived from the sensor's `intrinsics` as `2·atan(h/2·fy)`). `extra="forbid"`. Replaces per-robot `scene_defaults.composition` for camera-only deploy twins.
52. - `class SensorSpec(BaseModel)` — Generalizable sensor descriptor (all modalities). `catalog_id: str | None` (ADR-0068) records the `openral_sensors` catalog provenance for robot-mounted physical devices while keeping the manifest fully materialized; calibrated intrinsics, placement, feature keys, and serials stay explicit on the spec. `sim_camera_name: str | None` (issue #191 Phase 3b, mirrors `JointSpec.sim_joint_name`) carries the MJCF camera name when it differs from the sensor `name` — `MujocoArmHAL.read_images` renders `sim_camera_name or name` (e.g. openarm's `base` sensor renders the MJCF `top` camera). `sim_placement: CameraSimPlacement | None` (ADR-0065) carries the camera's sim pose; when set, the HAL camera rig splices the camera into a bare-arm MJCF so deploy-sim twins render their declared cameras without a scene composer. (L299)
53.   fields: `name, modality, frame_id, parent_frame, static_transform_xyz_rpy, rate_hz, intrinsics, encoding, fov_h_deg, fov_v_deg, sim_camera_name, sim_placement, n_channels, range_min_m, range_max_m, accel_noise_density, gyro_noise_density, n_axes, tactile_grid, vla_feature_key, ros2_topic, ros2_msg_type, qos_profile, catalog_id, vendor, model, driver_pkg, metadata`
54. - `class SensorBundle(BaseModel)` — Multi-modal sensor group. (L388)
55.   fields: `bundle_name, sensors, sync, sync_tolerance_ms`
56. - `class JointSpec(BaseModel)` — URDF-derived joint spec. (L407)
57.   fields: `name, joint_type, parent_link, child_link, axis_xyz, origin_xyz, origin_rpy, position_limits, velocity_limit, effort_limit, has_position_sensor, has_velocity_sensor, has_torque_sensor, backlash_estimate, actuator_kind, sim_joint_name, role`. **`origin_xyz` / `origin_rpy`** (ADR-0030) are the fixed parent-link→joint transform (URDF `<joint><origin>`); with `axis_xyz` they let the kernel compute forward kinematics for self-collision. Default zeros; populated by the offline lowering tool only for robots that enable collision checking. **`role`** (ADR-0028a) is a `JointRole` literal that downstream code reads to identify gripper / base / arm DoFs structurally instead of substring-matching the joint name (default `"unknown"`). `sim_joint_name` (ADR-0025) carries the MJCF/MuJoCo joint name when it differs from the logical `name` — used by `openral_sim.backends.robocasa.{synthesize_laser_scan_2d,read_panda_mobile_base_velocity}`, `SimSensorBridge._compute_scan_ranges`, and `openral_hal.sim_attached.SimAttachedHAL.read_state` to look up `mj_name2id` without hardcoding robosuite/robocasa naming. `None` = "MJCF name matches `name`" (the common case for fixed-base manipulators). **Population contract:** a robot needs `sim_joint_name` populated only when (a) its sim adapter does `mj_name2id` on a joint name, AND (b) the loaded MJCF differs from `name`. Today: `panda_mobile` (robocasa auto-prefixes with `mobilebase0_*` + `robot0_*`). LIBERO / ManiSkill3 / aloha / so100_robosuite / ur5e / widowx preserve URDF names — populating `sim_joint_name` for those is a no-op. `openarm_robosuite` does its own hardcoded `mj_name2id` lookups in `env.py:309-318` (`openarm_{side}_joint{i}`) and is a candidate to refactor through this field.
58. - `class EndEffectorSpec(BaseModel)` — End-effector spec. (L478)
59.   fields: `name, kind, hand, n_dof, max_grip_force_n, max_payload_kg, workspace_radius_m, tactile_sensors, actuated`. **`actuated`** (ADR-0028a) defaults to `True`; set `False` for passive tools (inert flanges, kinematic-only mounts) so the safety kernel can reject chunks addressed at them.
60. - `class RobotCapabilities(BaseModel)` — Capability flags for skill compatibility. (L538)
61.   fields: `locomotion, can_lift_kg, has_dexterous_hands, has_tactile, has_force_control, has_vision, has_lidar, has_vision_slam, has_audio, bimanual, onboard_compute_tops, onboard_memory_gb, gpu_vram_gb, cuda_compute_capability, cuda_toolkit_version, tensorrt_version, gpu_supported_runtimes, gpu_supported_dtypes, supported_control_modes, supported_vla_embodiments, embodiment_tags, nvmm_available`. **`has_vision_slam`** (ADR-0064) gates the camera-based cuVSLAM+nvblox SLAM backend for lidar-less robots; independent of `has_lidar` (lidar backend wins when both set).
62.   - `supports_cumotion() -> bool` (ADR-0065) — True when the host meets the cuMotion (Isaac ROS) GPU floor: compute capability >= (8, 0), CUDA toolkit major >= 13, and `gpu_vram_gb` >= `_CUMOTION_MIN_VRAM_GIB` (7.5; nominal-8 GB cards report ~7.99 GiB). The MoveIt planner gate uses it to pick cuMotion vs OMPL. False on non-CUDA hosts or when the CUDA toolkit version is unknown.
63. - `class SafetyEnvelope(BaseModel)` — Constraints enforced by C++ safety kernel. (L643)
64.   fields: `workspace_box_min_xyz, workspace_box_max_xyz, no_go_zones, max_ee_speed_m_s, max_ee_accel_m_s2, max_joint_speed_factor, max_force_n, max_torque_nm, deadman_required, e_stop_topic, e_stop_qos, contact_force_threshold_n, cycle_time_violation_threshold_ms, human_in_loop_required`
65. - `class ObservationSpec(BaseModel)` — VLA observation config. (L784)
66.   fields: `state_key, state_shape, state_representation, image_flip_180`
67. - `class ActionSpec(BaseModel)` — VLA action config. (L801)
68.   fields: `dim, representation, control_freq_hz, chunk_size`
69. - `class ActionSlot(BaseModel)` — One contiguous slice of an rSkill's action vector (ADR-0028b). fields: `range, control_mode, discard, ee, frame, joint_names`. Per-mode field requirements enforced by `@model_validator`: cartesian needs ee+frame, body_twist needs frame only, gripper needs ee only, joint needs neither (joint_names optional, length must equal slot width when supplied). `discard=True` slots drop their slice silently — used for dataset artefacts (RoboCasa365 torso placeholder, paired gripper channels).
70. - `class ActionContract(BaseModel)` — Per-rSkill action-vector contract (ADR-0019 + ADR-0028b). fields: `dim, representation, slots`. When `slots` is set, every index in `[0, dim)` is covered by exactly one `ActionSlot` (`@model_validator` rejects gaps + overlaps + over-range slots). When `slots is None`, the legacy single-Action JOINT_POSITION path applies (back-compat). Manifests carrying `slots` are exempt from the ADR-0028a `dim <= len(robot.joints)` invariant — the slot decoder gives a per-slice typed contract.
71. - `class SphereShape(BaseModel)` — Sphere collision primitive; discriminator `shape="sphere"`, field `radius_m (>0)`. (ADR-0030, L835)
72. - `class CapsuleShape(BaseModel)` — Capsule collision primitive (segment along local +Z swept by a radius); discriminator `shape="capsule"`, fields `radius_m (>0), length_m (>=0)`. (ADR-0030, L853)
73. - `CollisionShape: TypeAlias = CapsuleShape | SphereShape` — Discriminated union of convex collision primitives (discriminator `shape`); mesh shapes excluded so the allocation-free kernel checks only analytic convex volumes. (ADR-0030, L882)
74. - `class LinkCollisionGeometry(BaseModel)` — One convex collision volume attached to a robot link; fields `link_name, shape: CollisionShape, origin_xyz_rpy`. Lowered, kernel-facing form (hand-authored or emitted by the offline lowering tool from MJCF/URDF). (ADR-0030, L894)
75. - `class RobotDescription(BaseModel)` — Top-level robot manifest, one per robot. (L1317)
76.   fields: `name, embodiment_kind, assets, base_frame, odom_frame, map_frame, joints, end_effectors, sensors, sensor_bundles, capabilities, safety, ros2_namespace, middleware, onboard_compute, sdk_kind, hal, observation_spec, action_spec, sim, scene_defaults, base_joints, footprint_radius, base_kinematics, collision_geometry, allowed_collision_pairs, footprint_polygon`. **`assets: AssetRefs`** (ADR-0058) is the single URDF/MJCF/SRDF reference block (default empty) — it replaces the former scattered `urdf_path`, `urdf_root_frame`, `static_base_to_urdf_root_xyz_rpy`, and `srdf_path` fields (and `SimDescription.mjcf_uri`); refs share the `openral_core.assets.resolve_asset` grammar and the URDF's `robot_state_publisher` wiring (`root_frame` + `base_to_root_xyz_rpy`, ADR-0027) lives on `assets.urdf`. **`collision_geometry: list[LinkCollisionGeometry]`** + **`allowed_collision_pairs: list[tuple[str, str]]`** (ADR-0030) carry the per-link collision primitives and the self-collision allowed-collision matrix the safety kernel consumes; all default empty/`None` and `joints` stays normative for the kinematic chain (URDF/SRDF add geometry + ACM only — the SRDF `disable_collisions` block named by `assets.srdf` is the canonical source for `allowed_collision_pairs` on real robots). `footprint_radius: float | None` (>0) + `base_kinematics: Literal["differential","holonomic","omni","ackermann"] | None` (ADR-0025) drive the generic Nav2 bringup (see `nav2_param_overrides`). `footprint_polygon: list[tuple[float, float]] | None` — optional base-frame XY polygon vertices (metres, CCW); when set, draws the true base outline on the SLAM occupancy grid instead of the `footprint_radius` circle (ADR-0025).
77.   - `scene_defaults: SceneDefaults | None = None` — Optional scene-level defaults (top-camera POV, etc.) consumed by the MJCF composers as the fallback when an environment does not pin its own values.
78.   - `validate_for_e2e_pipeline(self) -> None` — Assert this manifest carries every field the e2e ROS graph (`openral deploy sim` → C++ safety kernel) needs: every actuated joint must have `position_limits`, `velocity_limit`, and `effort_limit` set. Raises `ROSConfigError` listing every missing field at once — used by `sim_e2e.launch.py` so a misshapen manifest fails at launch-parse time, not later in the HAL's first actuation tick. Pure validation; for synthesis of the kernel `EnvelopeIntersection` use `openral_safety.envelope_loader.compute_intersection(robot, skill=None)`.
79.   - `lidar_sensor(self) -> SensorSpec | None` [@property] — First declared `lidar_2d` `SensorSpec` (beam count `n_channels`, `range_min_m`/`range_max_m`, `rate_hz`), or None. Single source of truth for the synthetic `/scan` envelope: `openral deploy sim` (`deploy_sim._scan_params_from_description`) forwards it as HAL `scan_*` ROS params and `SimSensorBridge` (which owns `/scan` for the manifest-driven node) reads the envelope from them, so neither hardcodes a scan envelope. ADR-0025.
80.   - `nav2_param_overrides(self) -> dict[str, str]` — Nav2 param substitutions derived from `footprint_radius` (→ `robot_radius` **and** costmap `inflation_radius` = `footprint_radius` + `NAV2_INFLATION_CLEARANCE_M`, kept ≥ the inscribed/circumscribed radius Nav2 derives from the footprint) + `base_kinematics` (→ MPPI `motion_model`). `{}` for fixed-base arms. `nav2.launch.py` `RewrittenYaml`-rewrites the shared base param file with these so one base file serves any mobile base — no hand-vendored per-robot Nav2 yaml. ADR-0025.
81. - `class GripperReadMode(str, Enum)` — How `MujocoArmHAL` reports the gripper qpos. (ADR-0023) Values: `SUM_OVER_SCALE` (Franka parallel — normalised to `[0,1]`), `AFFINE_LOW_HIGH` (SO-100 revolute Jaw — normalised to `[0,1]`), `PASSTHROUGH` (Aloha prismatic / OpenArm revolute — raw qpos in MJCF units).
82. - `class GripperWriteMode(str, Enum)` — How `MujocoArmHAL` maps an Action's gripper value to `ctrl`. (ADR-0023 bimanual amendment) Values: `NORMALISED` (`[0,1]` → `ctrl_range`), `PASSTHROUGH` (raw → `ctrl`; MuJoCo clips).
83. - `class SimGripperDescription(BaseModel)` — Gripper wiring inside a MuJoCo MJCF. (ADR-0023)
84.   fields: `joint, ctrl_range, qpos_addrs, qpos_scale, read_mode, write_mode, actuator_index, mirror_actuator_index`
85. - `class UrdfAsset(BaseModel)` — A URDF asset reference plus its `robot_state_publisher` wiring. (ADR-0027 / ADR-0058)
86.   fields: `ref: str` (validated against the `resolve_asset` scheme grammar), `root_frame: str | None` (URDF root link when it differs from `base_frame`), `base_to_root_xyz_rpy: tuple[float×6] | None` (static `base_frame`→`root_frame` transform [x,y,z,roll,pitch,yaw], metres+radians)
87. - `class AssetRefs(BaseModel)` — Unified `RobotDescription.assets` block: one URDF/MJCF/SRDF reference set replacing the former scattered asset fields. (ADR-0058 §4)
88.   fields: `urdf: UrdfAsset | None`, `mjcf: str | None`, `srdf: str | None` (the last two are bare refs, validated against the same scheme grammar; all default `None`)
89. - `class SimDescription(BaseModel)` — Optional `RobotDescription.sim` block holding MuJoCo joint↔qpos/qvel/actuator wiring for `MujocoArmHAL.from_description`; the MJCF itself is named by `RobotDescription.assets.mjcf` (ADR-0058). (ADR-0023)
90.   fields: `floating_base, joint_qpos_addr, joint_qvel_addr, actuator_index, grippers, settle_steps_default, keyframe_index, seed_ctrl_from_qpos`
91. - `class HalEntrypoints(BaseModel)` — `RobotDescription.hal` block: the robot's simulation + real-hardware HAL import strings, resolved by `openral_hal.build_hal`. (ADR-0031)
92.   fields: `sim: str | None` (null → derive `MujocoArmHAL.from_description` when a `sim:` block exists), `real: str | None` (null → simulation-only robot), `parameters: HalParameters` (per-robot HAL construction defaults; ADR-0029)
93. - `class HalParameters(BaseModel)` — `RobotDescription.hal.parameters` block: per-robot HAL construction defaults (serial `port`, `robot_ip`, …) merged into the constructor by `openral_hal.build_hal` (explicit `transport` wins; unaccepted keys dropped), so a parameterised robot needs no bespoke lifecycle subclass. Empty by default. (ADR-0029, issue #191)
94.   fields: `defaults: dict[str, object]`
95. - `class TopCameraDefaults(BaseModel)` — Default placement for the scene-level "top" / "base" camera consumed by sim backends that render an overview camera. (L1049)
96.   fields: `pos: tuple[float, float, float], target: tuple[float, float, float], fovy: float (gt=0, lt=180)`
97.   - Replaces the dataset-specific `_DEFAULT_TOP_CAMERA_*` module-level constants previously hard-coded in `openral_sim.backends.openarm_robosuite._assets`. Backend YAML overrides (`scene.backend_options.top_camera_*`) still win — this submodel is the default fed to the composer.
98. - `class SceneDefaults(BaseModel)` — Per-robot scene rendering defaults consulted when the scene YAML does not override them. Fields: `top_camera: TopCameraDefaults | None`, `composition: SceneComposition | None`. (L1114)
99. - `class SceneComposition(BaseModel)` — Declarative MJCF scene composition (issue #191 Phase 3b). `composer: "module:fn"` returning `(xml, meshdir)` + `params: dict`. The manifest-driven `ManifestHALLifecycleNode._create_hal` calls the composer and threads the composed MJCF in as the HAL's `mjcf_path` — replaced openarm's bespoke `_create_hal` tabletop splicing.
100.   fields: `composer: str`, `params: dict[str, object]`
101.   fields: `top_camera: TopCameraDefaults | None = None`

```
1. """openral schema v0 — normative Pydantic v2 contracts for all layers.
2. 
3. This is the single source of truth for the data contracts between layers.
4. Anything imported from openral_core.__init__ is public API.
5. Breaking changes require a SemVer MAJOR bump (pre-1.0: MINOR) and a migration entry.
6. """
7. 
8. from __future__ import annotations
9. 
10. import base64
11. import binascii
12. import math
13. from enum import Enum
14. from typing import Any, Literal, TypeAlias
15. 
16. from pydantic import (
17.     AliasChoices,
18.     BaseModel,
19.     ConfigDict,
20.     Field,
21.     field_serializer,
22.     field_validator,
23.     model_validator,
24. )
25. 
26. # ROSConfigError lives in the sibling `exceptions` module and is the
27. # canonical exception family for any configuration-level failure (see
28. # CLAUDE.md §10).
29. from openral_core.exceptions import ROSConfigError
30. 
31. # ─── Enums ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32. 
33. 
34. class EmbodimentKind(str, Enum):
35.     """Top-level kinematic class of a robot body."""
36. 
37.     HUMANOID = "humanoid"
38.     MANIPULATOR = "manipulator"
39.     BIMANUAL = "bimanual"
40.     QUADRUPED = "quadruped"
41.     MOBILE_BASE = "mobile_base"
42.     MOBILE_MANIPULATOR = "mobile_manipulator"
43.     DRONE = "drone"
44. 
45. 
46. class JointType(str, Enum):
47.     """Kinematic joint type following URDF conventions."""
48. 
49.     REVOLUTE = "revolute"
50.     PRISMATIC = "prismatic"
51.     CONTINUOUS = "continuous"
52.     FIXED = "fixed"
53.     FLOATING = "floating"
54.     PLANAR = "planar"
55. 
56. 
57. JointRole: TypeAlias = Literal[
58.     "arm",
59.     "base",
60.     "gripper",
61.     "torso",
62.     "leg",
63.     "head",
64.     "neck",
65.     "wheel",
66.     "unknown",
67. ]
68. """Structural classification of a :class:`JointSpec` (ADR-0028a).
69. 
70. Carries the joint's *purpose* in the embodiment's morphology — what
71. the runner, safety kernel, and dataset bridge need to identify a
72. channel without relying on name-substring heuristics (e.g.
73. ``"gripper" in name.lower()`` in ``rskill_runner_node._build_joint_permutation``
74. which silently misclassifies any joint with ``"gripper"`` in the name).
75. 
76. ``"unknown"`` is the default so legacy manifests load unchanged; the
77. fleet annotates incrementally as ADR-0028 sub-PRs land.
78. """
79. 
80.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 11m 30s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m adding a small core Pydantic contract, not another timestamp field everywhere. Existing `stamp_ns` fields remain, but now they have a named authority model that says what that nanosecond count is relative to.

```
1. """Hypothesis fuzz tests for all openral_core Pydantic models.
2. 
3. Property tested: round-trip serialization guarantee.
4.   For any valid model instance:
5.     model → model_dump_json() → model_validate_json() == model
6.   And the serialized JSON must validate against the model's own JSON Schema.
7. 
8. Run with:
9.     uv run pytest tests/unit/test_schemas_fuzz.py -v
10. """
11. 
12. from __future__ import annotations
13. 
14. import json
15. from typing import get_args
16. 
17. import jsonschema
18. from hypothesis import HealthCheck, given, settings
19. from hypothesis import strategies as st
20. from openral_core.schemas import (
21.     _MODERN_PROCESSOR_FAMILIES,
22.     Action,
23.     ActuatorRequirement,
24.     ApproachViewpoint,
25.     BenchmarkName,
26.     CameraSimPlacement,
27.     CapsuleShape,
28.     CollisionEvidence,
29.     ControlMode,
30.     ControlModeSemantics,
31.     DeadlineOverrunPolicy,
32.     DetectedObject,
33.     DeviceInfo,
34.     EmbodimentKind,
35.     EmbodimentTag,
36.     EndEffectorSpec,
37.     FrameEncoding,
38.     GripperConvention,
39.     HalConfig,
40.     HalEntrypoints,
41.     Hand,
42.     IntrinsicsPinhole,
43.     JointSpec,
44.     JointState,
45.     JointType,
46.     LinkCollisionGeometry,
47.     ModelFamily,
48.     OccupancyGridRef,
49.     PhysicsBackend,
50.     Pose6D,
51.     QuantizationBackend,
52.     QuantizationConfig,
53.     QuantizationDtype,
54.     RecallObjectMatch,
55.     RecallObjectQuery,
56.     RecallObjectResult,
57.     RecallObjectTool,
58.     ResolvePlaceQuery,
59.     ResolvePlaceResult,
60.     ResolvePlaceTool,
61.     RobotCapabilities,
62.     RobotDescription,
63.     RobotEnvironment,
64.     RSkillAction,
65.     RSkillLatencyBudget,
66.     RSkillLicensePosture,
67.     RSkillManifest,
68.     RSkillProcessors,
69.     RSkillRuntime,
70.     RunResult,
71.     SafetyEnvelope,
72.     SceneGraph,
73.     SceneSpec,
74.     SensorBundle,
75.     SensorFrame,
76.     SensorModality,
77.     SensorReaderBackend,
78.     SensorReaderConfig,
79.     SensorSpec,
80.     SimEnvironment,
81.     SpatialEdge,
82.     SpatialNode,
83.     SpatialNodeKind,
84.     SpatialRelationKind,
85.     SphereShape,
86.     TaskSpec,
87.     TickResult,
88.     VLASpec,
89.     WorldCollisionPrimitive,
90.     WorldState,
91. )
92. 
93. # ─── Shared primitive strategies ──────────────────────────────────────────────
94. 
95. _name = st.text(
96.     alphabet=st.characters(whitelist_categories=("Lu", "Ll", "Nd"), whitelist_characters="_-/"),
97.     min_size=1,
98.     max_size=64,
99. )
100. _topic = st.text(
101.     alphabet=st.characters(whitelist_categories=("Lu", "Ll", "Nd"), whitelist_characters="_/"),
102.     min_size=1,
103.     max_size=128,
104. )
105. _safe_float = st.floats(allow_nan=False, allow_infinity=False, min_value=-1e6, max_value=1e6)
106. _pos_float = st.floats(allow_nan=False, allow_infinity=False, min_value=0.0, max_value=1e4)
107. _ns = st.integers(min_value=0, max_value=10**18)
108. _prob = st.floats(allow_nan=False, allow_infinity=False, min_value=0.0, max_value=1.0)
109. 
110. # ─── Model strategies ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
111. 
112. _intrinsics_st = st.builds(
113.     IntrinsicsPinhole,
114.     width=st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4096),
115.     height=st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4096),
116.     fx=_pos_float,
117.     fy=_pos_float,
118.     cx=_pos_float,
119.     cy=_pos_float,
120. )

288. # ─── Round-trip helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

290. _FUZZ_SETTINGS = settings(
291.     max_examples=50,
292.     suppress_health_check=[HealthCheck.too_slow],
293.     # reason: pytest --cov instrumentation pushes per-example past the 200ms default on CI
294.     deadline=None,
295. )
296. 
297. 
298. def _round_trip_and_validate(cls: type, instance: object) -> None:  # type: ignore[type-arg]
299.     """Serialize → deserialize and JSON Schema validate."""
300.     serialized = instance.model_dump_json()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
301.     reloaded = cls.model_validate_json(serialized)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
302.     assert reloaded == instance, f"Round-trip failed for {cls.__name__}"
303. 
304.     data = json.loads(serialized)
305.     schema = cls.model_json_schema()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
306.     jsonschema.validate(data, schema)
307. 
308. 
309. # ─── Fuzz tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
310. 
311. 
312. @_FUZZ_SETTINGS
313. @given(_intrinsics_st)
314. def test_fuzz_intrinsics_pinhole(instance: IntrinsicsPinhole) -> None:
315.     """IntrinsicsPinhole round-trips through JSON and validates against its schema."""
316.     _round_trip_and_validate(IntrinsicsPinhole, instance)
317. 
318. 
319. @_FUZZ_SETTINGS
320. @given(_camera_sim_placement_st)
321. def test_fuzz_camera_sim_placement(instance: CameraSimPlacement) -> None:
322.     """CameraSimPlacement round-trips through JSON and validates against its schema."""
323.     _round_trip_and_validate(CameraSimPlacement, instance)
324. 
325. 
326. @_FUZZ_SETTINGS
327. @given(_sensor_spec_st)
328. def test_fuzz_sensor_spec(instance: SensorSpec) -> None:
329.     """SensorSpec round-trips through JSON and validates against its schema."""
330.     _round_trip_and_validate(SensorSpec, instance)

793. @st.composite
794. def _sensor_frame_data_st(draw: st.DrawFn) -> SensorFrame:
795.     """SensorFrame instance carrying inline bytes."""
796.     return SensorFrame(
797.         sensor_id=draw(_name),
798.         stamp_monotonic_ns=draw(_ns),
799.         stamp_wall_ns=draw(_ns),
800.         encoding=draw(st.sampled_from(list(FrameEncoding))),

802.         height=draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4096)),
803.         channels=draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4)),
804.         data=draw(st.binary(min_size=1, max_size=64)),
805.     )
806. 
807. 
808. @st.composite
809. def _sensor_frame_topic_st(draw: st.DrawFn) -> SensorFrame:
810.     """SensorFrame instance carrying a ROS topic ref."""

812.         sensor_id=draw(_name),
813.         stamp_monotonic_ns=draw(_ns),
814.         stamp_wall_ns=draw(_ns),
815.         encoding=draw(st.sampled_from(list(FrameEncoding))),

817.         height=draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4096)),
818.         channels=draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4)),
819.         topic=draw(_topic),
820.     )
821. 
822. 
823. @st.composite
824. def _sensor_frame_handle_st(draw: st.DrawFn) -> SensorFrame:
825.     """SensorFrame instance carrying an opaque in-process handle."""

829.         stamp_wall_ns=draw(_ns),
830.         encoding=draw(st.sampled_from(list(FrameEncoding))),

832.         height=draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4096)),
833.         channels=draw(st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=4)),
834.         handle=draw(st.integers(min_value=0, max_value=2**63 - 1)),
835.     )

<sub>⏱️ 11m 59s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The contract will be: **OpenRAL timestamp origin = `ClockAuthority`; `/clock` is only a ROS projection of it.** I’m adding that model plus a HAL-facing accessor now.

---

<sub>⏱️ 12m 19s</sub>

<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/core/src/openral_core/schemas.py\n@@\n class JointType(str, Enum):\n     \"\"\"Kinematic joint type following URDF conventions.\"\"\"\n@@\n     PLANAR = \"planar\"\n \n \n+class ClockOrigin(str, Enum):\n+    \"\"\"Authoritative source for OpenRAL ``stamp_ns`` values.\n+\n+    ``/clock`` is not an origin; it is the ROS 2 projection of one of these\n+    authorities into nodes that run with ``use_sim_time=true``.\n+    \"\"\"\n+\n+    HOST_WALL = \"host_wall\"\n+    SIMULATION = \"simulation\"\n+    HARDWARE_SYNCED = \"hardware_synced\"\n+\n+\n+class ClockEpoch(str, Enum):\n+    \"\"\"Epoch that a ``stamp_ns`` value is measured from.\"\"\"\n+\n+    UNIX = \"unix\"\n+    SIMULATION_ELAPSED = \"simulation_elapsed\"\n+    HARDWARE = \"hardware\"\n+\n+\n JointRole: TypeAlias = Literal[\n@@\n     ``\"unknown\"`` is the default so legacy manifests load unchanged; the\n     fleet annotates incrementally as ADR-0028 sub-PRs land.\n     \"\"\"\n+\n+\n+class ClockAuthority(BaseModel):\n+    \"\"\"Named origin for OpenRAL timestamps across sim, rSkills, ROS, and hardware.\n+\n+    The invariant is: every serialized ``stamp_ns`` is measured in the active\n+    deployment's OpenRAL timestamp domain. Simulation deployments may publish\n+    that domain onto ROS ``/clock``; real deployments normally use host wall\n+    time or a PTP/hardware clock already synchronized into the graph. rSkills\n+    consume stamps from this domain; they do not create a separate clock.\n+\n+    Attributes:\n+        origin: Physical/logical source of the timestamp domain.\n+        epoch: Epoch used by ``stamp_ns``.\n+        clock_id: Stable human-readable authority identifier.\n+        publishes_ros_clock: Whether this authority is projected onto ROS\n+            ``/clock`` for nodes using ``use_sim_time``.\n+        timestep_s: Optional logical simulation/control timestep in seconds.\n+            ``None`` means event-driven or externally determined.\n+        notes: Optional operator-facing context.\n+\n+    Example:\n+        >>> ClockAuthority.simulation(\"robocasa\", timestep_s=0.05).epoch\n+        <ClockEpoch.SIMULATION_ELAPSED: 'simulation_elapsed'>\n+    \"\"\"\n+\n+    origin: ClockOrigin\n+    epoch: ClockEpoch\n+    clock_id: str = Field(min_length=1)\n+    publishes_ros_clock: bool = False\n+    timestep_s: float | None = Field(default=None, gt=0)\n+    notes: str | None = None\n+\n+    @classmethod\n+    def host_wall(cls, clock_id: str = \"host_wall\") -> \"ClockAuthority\":\n+        \"\"\"Return the default real-deployment authority: host wall/ROS system time.\"\"\"\n+\n+        return cls(origin=ClockOrigin.HOST_WALL, epoch=ClockEpoch.UNIX, clock_id=clock_id)\n+\n+    @classmethod\n+    def simulation(\n+        cls,\n+        clock_id: str,\n+        *,\n+        timestep_s: float | None = None,\n+        publishes_ros_clock: bool = True,\n+    ) -> \"ClockAuthority\":\n+        \"\"\"Return a simulator-owned elapsed-time authority.\"\"\"\n+\n+        return cls(\n+            origin=ClockOrigin.SIMULATION,\n+            epoch=ClockEpoch.SIMULATION_ELAPSED,\n+            clock_id=clock_id,\n+            publishes_ros_clock=publishes_ros_clock,\n+            timestep_s=timestep_s,\n+        )\n+\n+    @classmethod\n+    def hardware_synced(\n+        cls,\n+        clock_id: str,\n+        *,\n+        epoch: ClockEpoch = ClockEpoch.UNIX,\n+    ) -> \"ClockAuthority\":\n+        \"\"\"Return a hardware/PTP authority already synchronized to the graph.\"\"\"\n+\n+        return cls(origin=ClockOrigin.HARDWARE_SYNCED, epoch=epoch, clock_id=clock_id)\n+\n+    @model_validator(mode=\"after\")\n+    def _validate_origin_epoch(self) -> \"ClockAuthority\":\n+        if self.origin is ClockOrigin.SIMULATION and self.epoch is not ClockEpoch.SIMULATION_ELAPSED:\n+            raise ValueError(\"simulation clock authorities must use epoch='simulation_elapsed'\")\n+        if self.origin is ClockOrigin.HOST_WALL and self.epoch is not ClockEpoch.UNIX:\n+            raise ValueError(\"host_wall clock authorities must use epoch='unix'\")\n+        if self.origin is ClockOrigin.HARDWARE_SYNCED and self.publishes_ros_clock:\n+            raise ValueError(\n+                \"hardware_synced authorities must be mapped into the ROS graph clock, \"\n+ "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/adr/0048-deploy-sim-clock-publisher.md\n@@\n ## Decision\n \n-Add an **optional sim `/clock` publisher** to the deploy-sim graph, gated by the existing\n-`enable_sim_clock` flag, and flip the whole graph to sim-time when it is on. Concretely:\n+Define a single OpenRAL clock authority per deployment, then add an **optional sim `/clock`\n+publisher** to project the simulator authority into the deploy-sim ROS graph. `/clock` is never\n+the origin; it is only the ROS transport for an authority selected before launch.\n+\n+Concretely:\n+\n+### 0. One authority, many projections\n+\n+Every OpenRAL timestamp (`stamp_ns` on joint states, sensor frames, world state, actions, traces)\n+is measured in the active deployment's `ClockAuthority` domain:\n+\n+- **Real deployment default**: `ClockAuthority.host_wall()` — host wall/ROS system time. No\n+  OpenRAL node publishes `/clock`; ROS node clocks use system time. Hardware drivers may stamp\n+  from device time only after the driver maps that device clock into this graph domain (for\n+  example PTP/chrony or a driver-provided conversion).\n+- **Simulation deployment with sim-time enabled**: `ClockAuthority.simulation(<backend>)` — the\n+  simulator's authoritative elapsed simulation time. The HAL publishes this onto ROS `/clock`,\n+  and every graph node sets `use_sim_time=true`.\n+- **Future hardware-synced deployment**: `ClockAuthority.hardware_synced(<clock_id>)` — a\n+  controller/PTP clock already synchronized into the graph. It is an authority, not a second ROS\n+  `/clock` publisher competing with the graph.\n+\n+rSkills never mint their own time origin. They consume `SensorFrame`, `WorldState`, and `Action`\n+timestamps from the active authority and may emit latency/trace wall-time separately for\n+observability.\n \n-### 1. Expose simulation time through the `SimRollout` seam\n+### 1. Expose simulation time through the `SimRollout` seam\n@@\n ### Timestep definitions\n \n OpenRAL uses three separate clocks; they must not be conflated:\n@@\n - **Wall-clock cadence**: host scheduling/pacing only (idle-step timer, publisher thread, viewer\n   pacing, runner sleeps). It decides *when* work happens on the CPU; it does not define the\n   timestamp carried by simulated data when sim-time is enabled.\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/00-core-schemas.md\n@@\n - `class JointType(str, Enum)` — URDF joint type. (L46)\n   `REVOLUTE, PRISMATIC, CONTINUOUS, FIXED, FLOATING, PLANAR`\n+- `class ClockOrigin(str, Enum)` — Authoritative source for OpenRAL `stamp_ns` values. `/clock` is a ROS projection, not an origin. (L57)\n+  `HOST_WALL, SIMULATION, HARDWARE_SYNCED`\n+- `class ClockEpoch(str, Enum)` — Epoch that a `stamp_ns` value is measured from. (L70)\n+  `UNIX, SIMULATION_ELAPSED, HARDWARE`\n - `JointRole: TypeAlias = Literal[…]` — Structural classification of a `JointSpec` (ADR-0028a). (L57)\n   `\"arm\", \"base\", \"gripper\", \"torso\", \"leg\", \"head\", \"neck\", \"wheel\", \"unknown\"`. Used by runner/safety/dataset-bridge to identify a channel without name-substring heuristics. Default `\"unknown\"` keeps legacy manifests loadable.\n@@\n - `scale_intrinsics_to(base, width, height) -> IntrinsicsPinhole` — Linearly rescale pinhole intrinsics to a new render resolution (fx/fy/cx/cy scale by width/height ratios; FOV and distortion preserved). ADR-0035: deploy-sim renders the same MuJoCo camera at `scene.observation_width/height`, so the HAL scales the manifest's nominal intrinsics to the render resolution before the depth back-projection — keeping the published camera model matched to what was rendered. Returns `base` unchanged when the target equals its resolution; raises `ValueError` on non-positive dims. (L205)\n+- `class ClockAuthority(BaseModel)` — Named origin for OpenRAL timestamps across sim, rSkills, ROS, and hardware. Fields: `origin`, `epoch`, `clock_id`, `publishes_ros_clock`, `timestep_s`, `notes`. Constructors: `host_wall()` for real-deployment wall/ROS system time, `simulation(clock_id, timestep_s=None, publishes_ros_clock=True)` for simulator elapsed time projected to `/clock`, and `hardware_synced(clock_id, epoch=UNIX)` for future synchronized controller/PTP clocks. Validator rejects impossible origin/epoch pairs and competing hardware `/clock` publishers. (L83)\n - `class CameraSimPlacement(BaseModel)` — ADR-0065. Where an RGB sensor's camera sits in the sim MJCF, so the generic HAL camera rig can splice it into a bare-arm MJCF (which ships no `<camera>`) for `deploy sim`. Fields: `parent_body: str | None` (MJCF body the camera mounts to / tracks; `None` = world-fixed in `<worldbody>`), `pos: (x,y,z)` + `target: (x,y,z)` (look-at point, parent or world frame), `fovy_deg: float | None` (else derived from the sensor's `intrinsics` as `2·atan(h/2·fy)`). `extra=\"forbid\"`. Replaces per-robot `scene_defaults.composition` for camera-only deploy twins.\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/01-hal.md\n@@\n   - `mujoco_handles() -> tuple[Any, Any] | None` — Forward the env's `(model, data)` MJCF handles. (L1267)\n   - `sim_time_ns() -> int | None` — Cross-reset-monotonic elapsed sim time in ns (ADR-0048 Phase 1) — the seam a sim `/clock` publisher reads. Returns the wrapped `SimRollout.sim_time_ns()` (per-episode) plus an accumulated offset: `connect` and the ADR-0036 auto-resets fold each finished episode's elapsed sim-time into the offset (`_accumulate_sim_time_before_reset`) BEFORE the backend rewinds its clock, so the value is monotonic non-decreasing across `env.reset` (robocasa rewinds `MjData.time` to 0). `None` when the wrapped rollout has no sim clock (clock-less backend / sidecar) — the consumer then falls back to wall time.\n+  - `clock_authority() -> ClockAuthority` — Return the timestamp authority this HAL contributes to the graph: `ClockAuthority.simulation(<backend>, timestep_s=body_twist_dt_s)` when `sim_time_ns()` is live, otherwise `ClockAuthority.host_wall()` so launch must keep `use_sim_time=false`.\n   - `estop() -> None` — Latch e-stop; subsequent `send_action` calls are dropped. (L1261)\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/methods/07-eval-sim.md\n@@\n-- `SimRollout` (Protocol) — Minimal gym-style env contract used by `SimRunner`: attrs `scene`, `task`; methods `reset(seed) -> Observation`, `step(action) -> StepResult`, `render() -> ndarray | None`, `close() -> None`. Optional duck-typed extensions: `mujoco_handles() -> (MjModel, MjData) | None`, `viewer_render()`, `enable_intrinsic_viewer()`. (L85)\n-  - duck-typed extension: `sim_time_ns() -> int | None` — Optional, NOT part of the Protocol (ADR-0048 Phase 1); the backend's authoritative elapsed sim time in ns, the seam a sim `/clock` publisher reads. MuJoCo-backed adapters return `round(MjData.time * 1e9)` via `sim_time_ns_from_mujoco_handles`. Monotonic non-decreasing *within an episode*; backends that rewind `MjData.time` on `reset` (robocasa) restart it, so a cross-reset-monotonic consumer maintains its own offset (`SimAttachedHAL.sim_time_ns`). `None` = no sim clock (PushT, Isaac Sim sidecar). Callers MUST `getattr(env, \"sim_time_ns\", None)` and treat both missing + `None` as \"no clock\" (fall back to wall time).\n+- `SimRollout` (Protocol) — Minimal gym-style env contract used by `SimRunner`: attrs `scene`, `task`; methods `reset(seed) -> Observation`, `step(action) -> StepResult`, `render() -> ndarray | None`, `close() -> None`. Optional duck-typed extensions: `mujoco_handles() -> (MjModel, MjData) | None`, `viewer_render()`, `enable_intrinsic_viewer()`. (L85)\n+  - duck-typed extension: `sim_time_ns() -> int | None` — Optional, NOT part of the Protocol (ADR-0048 Phase 1); the backend's authoritative elapsed sim time in ns, the seam a sim `/clock` publisher reads. MuJoCo-backed adapters return `round(MjData.time * 1e9)` via `sim_time_ns_from_mujoco_handles`; sidecars that carry `sim_time_ns` expose that wire value. Monotonic non-decreasing *within an episode*; backends that rewind `MjData.time` on `reset` (robocasa) restart it, so a cross-reset-monotonic consumer maintains its own offset (`SimAttachedHAL.sim_time_ns`). `None` = no sim clock (PushT or a sidecar lacking time). Callers MUST `getattr(env, \"sim_time_ns\", None)` and treat both missing + `None` as \"no clock\" (fall back to wall time).\n*** End Patch\n"

Callers MUST `getattr(env, "sim_time_ns", None)` and treat both missing + `None` as "no clock" (fall back to wall time).
27.   - duck-typed extension: `enable_intrinsic_viewer() -> None` — Optional, NOT part of the Protocol; adapters whose engine draws its own window (e.g. gym_pusht) implement it so `SimRunner` can switch them into live-view mode at activate() time. When present, `SimRunner` skips the MuJoCo viewer path entirely.

<details>
<summary>215 lines</summary>

```diff
diff --git a/python/core/src/openral_core/schemas.py b/python/core/src/openral_core/schemas.py
index 33b4e96..400889c 100644
--- a/python/core/src/openral_core/schemas.py
+++ b/python/core/src/openral_core/schemas.py
@@ -54,6 +54,26 @@ class JointType(str, Enum):
     PLANAR = "planar"
 
 
+class ClockOrigin(str, Enum):
+    """Authoritative source for OpenRAL ``stamp_ns`` values.
+
+    ``/clock`` is not an origin; it is the ROS 2 projection of one of these
+    authorities into nodes that run with ``use_sim_time=true``.
+    """
+
+    HOST_WALL = "host_wall"
+    SIMULATION = "simulation"
+    HARDWARE_SYNCED = "hardware_synced"
+
+
+class ClockEpoch(str, Enum):
+    """Epoch that a ``stamp_ns`` value is measured from."""
+
+    UNIX = "unix"
+    SIMULATION_ELAPSED = "simulation_elapsed"
+    HARDWARE = "hardware"
+
+
 JointRole: TypeAlias = Literal[
     "arm",
     "base",
@@ -78,6 +98,86 @@ fleet annotates incrementally as ADR-0028 sub-PRs land.
 """
 
 
+class ClockAuthority(BaseModel):
+"""Named origin for OpenRAL timestamps across sim, rSkills, ROS, and hardware.
+
+The invariant is: every serialized ``stamp_ns`` is measured in the active
+deployment's OpenRAL timestamp domain. Simulation deployments may publish
+that domain onto ROS ``/clock``; real deployments normally use host wall
+time or a PTP/hardware clock already synchronized into the graph. rSkills
+consume stamps from this domain; they do not create a separate clock.
+
+Attributes:
+    origin: Physical/logical source of the timestamp domain.
+    epoch: Epoch used by ``stamp_ns``.
+    clock_id: Stable human-readable authority identifier.
+    publishes_ros_clock: Whether this authority is projected onto ROS
+        ``/clock`` for nodes using ``use_sim_time``.
+    timestep_s: Optional logical simulation/control timestep in seconds.
+        ``None`` means event-driven or externally determined.
+    notes: Optional operator-facing context.
+
+Example:
+    >>> ClockAuthority.simulation("robocasa", timestep_s=0.05).epoch
+    <ClockEpoch.SIMULATION_ELAPSED: 'simulation_elapsed'>
+"""
+
+origin: ClockOrigin
+epoch: ClockEpoch
+clock_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
+publishes_ros_clock: bool = False
+timestep_s: float | None = Field(default=None, gt=0)
+notes: str | None = None
+
+@classmethod
+def host_wall(cls, clock_id: str = "host_wall") -> "ClockAuthority":
+    """Return the default real-deployment authority: host wall/ROS system time."""
+
+    return cls(origin=ClockOrigin.HOST_WALL, epoch=ClockEpoch.UNIX, clock_id=clock_id)
+
+@classmethod
+def simulation(
+    cls,
+    clock_id: str,
+    *,
+    timestep_s: float | None = None,
+    publishes_ros_clock: bool = True,
+) -> "ClockAuthority":
+    """Return a simulator-owned elapsed-time authority."""
+
+    return cls(
+        origin=ClockOrigin.SIMULATION,
+        epoch=ClockEpoch.SIMULATION_ELAPSED,
+        clock_id=clock_id,
+        publishes_ros_clock=publishes_ros_clock,
+        timestep_s=timestep_s,
+    )
+
+@classmethod
+def hardware_synced(
+    cls,
+    clock_id: str,
+    *,
+    epoch: ClockEpoch = ClockEpoch.UNIX,
+) -> "ClockAuthority":
+    """Return a hardware/PTP authority already synchronized to the graph."""
+
+    return cls(origin=ClockOrigin.HARDWARE_SYNCED, epoch=epoch, clock_id=clock_id)
+
+@model_validator(mode="after")
+def _validate_origin_epoch(self) -> "ClockAuthority":
+    if self.origin is ClockOrigin.SIMULATION and self.epoch is not ClockEpoch.SIMULATION_ELAPSED:
+        raise ValueError("simulation clock authorities must use epoch='simulation_elapsed'")
+    if self.origin is ClockOrigin.HOST_WALL and self.epoch is not ClockEpoch.UNIX:
+        raise ValueError("host_wall clock authorities must use epoch='unix'")
+    if self.origin is ClockOrigin.HARDWARE_SYNCED and self.publishes_ros_clock:
+        raise ValueError(
+            "hardware_synced authorities must be mapped into the ROS graph clock, "
+            "not published as a competing /clock source"
+        )
+    return self
+
+
 class ControlMode(str, Enum):
     """Action space / control interface exposed by a robot or skill."""

+    st.builds(
+        ClockAuthority.simulation,
+        clock_id=_name,

+        publishes_ros_clock=st.booleans(),

+)
+

+@given(_clock_authority_st)

     assert float(data.qpos[side_addr]) == pytest.approx(0.0, abs=1e-9)
     assert float(data.qpos[yaw_addr]) == pytest.approx(0.0, abs=1e-9)
+    # Direct-qpos is still one simulation timestep. This is the deploy-sim
+    # /clock contract: Nav2 BODY_TWIST streams must not freeze sim time just
+    # because the base bypasses env.step.
+    assert float(data.time) == pytest.approx(0.05, abs=1e-12)
+    assert hal.sim_time_ns() == 50_000_000

     # robocasa composite controller (which doesn't honour planar

101 |   class ClockAuthority(BaseModel):
102 | / """Named origin for OpenRAL timestamps across sim, rSkills, ROS, and hardware.
103 | |
104 | | The invariant is: every serialized ``stamp_ns`` is measured in the active
105 | | deployment's OpenRAL timestamp domain. Simulation deployments may publish
106 | | that domain onto ROS ``/clock``; real deployments normally use host wall
107 | | time or a PTP/hardware clock already synchronized into the graph. rSkills
108 | | consume stamps from this domain; they do not create a separate clock.
109 | |
110 | | Attributes:
111 | |     origin: Physical/logical source of the timestamp domain.
112 | |     epoch: Epoch used by ``stamp_ns``.
113 | |     clock_id: Stable human-readable authority identifier.
114 | |     publishes_ros_clock: Whether this authority is projected onto ROS
115 | |         ``/clock`` for nodes using ``use_sim_time``.
116 | |     timestep_s: Optional logical simulation/control timestep in seconds.
117 | |         ``None`` means event-driven or externally determined.
118 | |     notes: Optional operator-facing context.
119 | |
120 | | Example:

<sub>⏱️ 15m 1s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The unified origin is now **`ClockAuthority` in `openral_core`**. `/clock` is explicitly **not** the origin; it is only the ROS projection of the active authority.

Current rule:
- **Real deployment default:** `ClockAuthority.host_wall()` → host wall / ROS system time, no OpenRAL `/clock` publisher.
- **Sim with sim-time enabled:** `ClockAuthority.simulation(\<backend\>)` → backend elapsed sim time, HAL publishes it on `/clock`, all nodes use `use_sim_time=true`.
- **Future hardware/PTP:** `ClockAuthority.hardware_synced(\<clock_id\>)` → a controller/device clock already synchronized into the graph, not a competing `/clock`.
- **rSkills:** never mint time; they consume `stamp_ns` from the active authority.

I added this as a public core schema, wired `SimAttachedHAL.clock_authority()`, documented the origin model in ADR-0048, and kept the previous BODY_TWIST fix so MuJoCo direct-qpos ticks advance sim time consistently. Targeted tests/lint pass.

---

<sub>⏱️ 19m 43s</sub>

### 👤 User

SO there shouldnt bw a use_sim_time=false anymore right? If so maybe drop it completely. Or am I worng?=

<sub>⏱️ 19m 57s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

You’re half right: `use_sim_time=false` should disappear as an operator choice, but ROS still needs `use_sim_time=true` internally when the active `ClockAuthority` is simulation. I’m checking the launch/CLI surface so we can remove the manual flag rather than the ROS parameter itself.
