```
515. def resolve_launch_invocation(  # noqa: PLR0912, PLR0915  # reason: a flat resolve sequence (robot_id → manifest → per-feature slam/nav2/octomap + sim/real hal_mode gating); splitting hurts readability
516.     *,
517.     config: Path | None = None,
518.     robot_override: str | None,
519.     dashboard_port: int,
520.     reset_to_pose_service: str | None,
521.     approach_skill_id: str | None = None,
522.     dataset_out: str | None = None,
523.     dataset_repo_id: str | None = None,
524.     dataset_license: str | None = None,
525.     hal_param_overrides: dict[str, object] | None = None,
526.     hal_mode: str = "sim",
527.     enable_slam: bool | None = None,
528.     enable_nav2: bool | None = None,
529.     enable_octomap: bool | None = None,
530.     enable_octomap_kernel_check: bool = True,
531.     enable_sim_clock: bool = False,
532.     enable_object_detector: bool | None = None,
533.     object_detector_onnx: Path | None = None,
534.     object_detector_manifest: str | None = None,
535.     object_detector_query: str | None = None,
536.     enable_reward_monitor: bool = False,
537.     reward_monitor_manifest: str | None = None,
538.     reward_monitor_task: str | None = None,
539.     enable_critic: bool = False,
540.     object_detector_locators: list[str] | None = None,
541.     spatial_memory_ingest: bool | None = None,
542.     enable_dashboard: bool = True,
543.     enable_foxglove: bool = False,
544.     foxglove_port: int = 8765,
545. ) -> LaunchInvocation:
546.     """Resolve every input into the ``ros2 launch`` argv to execute.
547. 
548.     Shared by ``openral deploy sim`` (``hal_mode="sim"``, a ``DeployScene``
549.     ``config``) and ``openral deploy run`` (``hal_mode="real"``, ``robot_override``
550.     from a ``RobotEnvironment`` — no sim scene; ADR-0032). In real mode the
551.     sim-twin / scene-attach injections are skipped so the HAL node builds the
552.     real hardware HAL via ``build_hal(mode="real")``.
553. 
554.     Returned ``argv_template`` carries a ``HAL_PARAMS_FILE_PLACEHOLDER``
555.     sentinel the caller substitutes after writing the ephemeral HAL params
556.     YAML. No envelope file is ever written — the launch reads ``robot_yaml``
557.     and feeds the kernel via ROS params.
558.     """
559.     from openral_core import RobotDescription  # reason: defer schema import
560. 
561.     if hal_mode not in ("sim", "real"):
562.         raise ROSConfigError(f"hal_mode must be 'sim' or 'real', got {hal_mode!r}.")
563. 
564.     scene_robot_id = _load_scene_robot_id(config) if config is not None else None
565.     robot_id = robot_override or scene_robot_id
566.     if not robot_id:
567.         raise ROSConfigError(
568.             "robot_id is undefined: pass ``--robot <id>`` or (for ``deploy sim``) "
569.             "set ``robot_id:`` in the DeployScene YAML / use a fixed-robot scene."
570.         )
571.     # ADR-0034 — a --robot override that differs from the scene's declared robot
572.     # composes a different arm than the scene was authored for. The scene's cameras
573.     # + asset mounts (e.g. tabletop_push's wrist_camera_mount_body="gripper") are
574.     # tuned for the declared robot, so on the override they may be mis-mounted or
575.     # unmatched — some /openral/cameras/* topics will be empty. Warn loudly.
576.     if robot_override and scene_robot_id and robot_override != scene_robot_id:
577.         _console.print(
578.             f"[yellow]warning:[/yellow] --robot {robot_override!r} overrides the scene's "
579.             f"declared robot_id {scene_robot_id!r}; the scene's cameras + asset mounts are "
580.             f"authored for {scene_robot_id!r} and may not match {robot_override!r} (expect "
581.             "empty /openral/cameras/* for non-matching sensors). Override only with a "
582.             "kinematically-compatible arm on a free-axis scene."
583.         )
584. 
585.     hal = _ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY.get(robot_id)
586.     if hal is None:
587.         supported = ", ".join(sorted(_ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY))
588.         raise ROSConfigError(
589.             f"robot {robot_id!r} has no HAL entry in _ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY. "
590.             f"Supported: {supported}. The registry key matches the "
591.             "``robots/<robot_id>/`` directory name — e.g. ``--robot "
592.             "franka_panda`` (not ``--robot franka``). To add a new robot, "
593.             "ship a ROS ``openral_hal_<X>`` package with a "
594.             "``lifecycle_node.py`` executable, add it to the ros2-build "
595.             "Justfile target, and register it here."
596.         )
597. 
598.     repo_root = _repo_root_from(Path(__file__))
599.     robot_yaml = repo_root / "robots" / robot_id / "robot.yaml"
600.     if not robot_yaml.is_file():
601.         raise ROSConfigError(
602.             f"robot manifest not found at {robot_yaml}; expected robots/{robot_id}/robot.yaml."
603.         )
604. 
605.     # Validate the manifest carries the e2e fields (joint position /
606.     # velocity / effort limits) and assert the manifest's declared name
607.     # is one the registered HAL accepts — protects against a typo
608.     # routing the wrong HAL at a robot.
609.     description = RobotDescription.from_yaml(str(robot_yaml))
610.     description.validate_for_e2e_pipeline()
611. 
612.     # ADR-0032 — fail fast before shelling the launch if real mode is asked of
613.     # a simulation-only robot (better UX than a graph that dies at HAL
614.     # configure with the same ROSCapabilityMismatch).
615.     if hal_mode == "real" and description.hal.real is None:
616.         raise ROSCapabilityMismatch(
617.             f"robot {robot_id!r} has no real-hardware HAL (hal.real is null); it is "
618.             "simulation-only. Use `openral deploy sim` instead of `openral deploy run`."
619.         )
620. 
621.     # ADR-0025 (#11) — SLAM is ON BY DEFAULT for every robot that *can* run it:
622.     # i.e. one that declares a lidar (the scan source slam_toolbox needs). This
623.     # is the firm default — a SLAM-capable robot always brings up the `map` frame
624.     # the object lift / spatial-memory ingest depend on, unless the operator
625.     # opts out with `--no-enable-slam`. Fixed-base arms (no mobile base, no lidar)
626.     # correctly stay off — there is no base to localise and nothing to map.
627.     # `enable_slam is None` means "auto": honour the manifest; an explicit flag wins.
628.     # ADR-0064 — SLAM is on for any robot that can localise/map: a lidar
629.     # (slam_toolbox) OR camera-based visual SLAM (cuVSLAM+nvblox, for
630.     # lidar-less robots). Fixed-base arms with neither correctly stay off.
631.     if enable_slam is None:
632.         enable_slam = bool(
633.             description.capabilities.has_lidar or description.capabilities.has_vision_slam
634.         )
635.     # ADR-0064 — backend selection (pure helper, unit-tested directly).
636.     slam_backend = _resolve_slam_backend(
637.         has_lidar=bool(description.capabilities.has_lidar),
638.         has_vision_slam=bool(description.capabilities.has_vision_slam),
639.         enable_slam=enable_slam,
640.     )
641.     # ADR-0025 — Nav2 auto-enables alongside slam_toolbox: every
642.     # lidar-equipped mobile robot needs a planner to consume the map.
643.     # Operators that want the map alone (recording / inspection) pass
644.     # ``--no-enable-nav2``.
645.     if enable_nav2 is None:
646.         enable_nav2 = enable_slam
647.     # ADR-0030 — the octomap world-collision leg auto-enables when the
648.     # robot manifest declares a usable depth SensorSpec (a camera the HAL
649.     # can ray-cast a PointCloud2 from); there is nothing to map otherwise.
650.     # ``--enable-octomap`` / ``--no-enable-octomap`` overrides.
651.     if enable_octomap is None:
652.         enable_octomap = any(
653.             s.modality in ("depth", "point_cloud") and s.intrinsics is not None
654.             for s in description.sensors
655.         )
656. 
657.     # ADR-0035/0037 — the object-detection leg is ON by default (deploy sim is a
658.     # perception-driven stack; ``--no-object-detector`` turns it off). The default
659.     # backend is the open-vocabulary ``omdet-turbo-indoor`` continuous detector,
660.     # which grounds arbitrary indoor/kitchen objects instead of the fixed COCO-80
661.     # of RT-DETR; when its runtime deps (transformers/timm — the ``omdet`` group)
662.     # are not importable we gracefully fall back to the in-tree RT-DETR COCO ONNX
663.     # so the graph still comes up. Explicit ``--object-detector-manifest`` /
664.     # ``--object-detector-onnx`` override the default selection.
665.     default_rtdetr_onnx = repo_root / "rskills" / "rtdetr-coco-r18" / "model.onnx"
666.     default_omdet_manifest = repo_root / "rskills" / "omdet-turbo-indoor" / "rskill.yaml"
667.     if object_detector_manifest:
668.         resolved_object_detector_manifest = str(Path(object_detector_manifest).resolve())
669.         resolved_object_detector_onnx = (
670.             object_detector_onnx.resolve()
671.             if object_detector_onnx is not None
672.             else default_rtdetr_onnx
673.         )
674.     elif object_detector_onnx is not None:
675.         # Explicit ONNX → the legacy fixed-label RT-DETR path (no manifest).
676.         resolved_object_detector_manifest = ""
677.         resolved_object_detector_onnx = object_detector_onnx.resolve()
678.     elif _omdet_runtime_available():
679.         resolved_object_detector_manifest = str(default_omdet_manifest.resolve())
680.         resolved_object_detector_onnx = default_rtdetr_onnx
681.     else:
682.         # Graceful fallback: omdet deps absent → in-tree RT-DETR COCO-80 ONNX.
683.         resolved_object_detector_manifest = ""
684.         resolved_object_detector_onnx = default_rtdetr_onnx
685. 
686.     if enable_object_detector is None:
687.         enable_object_detector = True
688.     # Downgrade to off (rather than let the node hard-fail at backend build) when
689.     # the detector is requested but no usable backend is present — a checkout that
690.     # has neither the omdet deps nor the gitignored RT-DETR ONNX weights.
691.     if (
692.         enable_object_detector
693.         and not resolved_object_detector_manifest
694.         and not _object_detector_onnx_present(resolved_object_detector_onnx)
695.     ):
696.         _console.print(
697.             "[yellow]object detector requested but no backend is available[/yellow] "
698.             "(omdet deps not importable and RT-DETR ONNX missing at "
699.             f"{resolved_object_detector_onnx}); disabling the detector leg. Run "
700.             "`just sync --group omdet --inexact` for the open-vocab default, fetch "
701.             "the RT-DETR weights, or pass --no-object-detector to silence."
702.         )
703.         enable_object_detector = False
704. 
705.     # When the leg is off (explicit --no-object-detector or the downgrade above),
706.     # do not forward a continuous-detector manifest: ros2 launch would otherwise
707.     # carry a manifest for a node that never starts (and, with omdet deps present,
708.     # a non-empty manifest:= for a disabled leg is simply wrong).
709.     if not enable_object_detector:
710.         resolved_object_detector_manifest = ""
711. 
712.     # ADR-0056 — on-demand open-vocab locators co-resident alongside the
713.     # continuous detector. Each token is a manifest path (``…/rskill.yaml``) or a
714.     # short alias resolved to ``rskills/<alias>/rskill.yaml``; the launch builds one
715.     # namespaced locate_in_view node per entry so the reasoner can pick a model.
716.     # Default = omdet-turbo-locator when the detector is on and the omdet deps are
717.     # importable (LocateAnything is opt-in — NVIDIA non-commercial, 5 GB VRAM).
718.     resolved_object_detector_locators: list[str] = []
719.     if enable_object_detector:
720.         if object_detector_locators is not None:
721.             locator_tokens = object_detector_locators
722.         elif _omdet_runtime_available():
723.             locator_tokens = ["omdet-turbo-locator"]
724.         else:
725.             locator_tokens = []
726.         for token in locator_tokens:
727.             candidate = Path(token)
728.             manifest_path = (
729.                 candidate
730.                 if candidate.suffix == ".yaml"
731.                 else repo_root / "rskills" / token / "rskill.yaml"
732.             )
733.             resolved_object_detector_locators.append(str(manifest_path.resolve()))
734. 
735.     # ADR-0038 — auto-enable durable spatial-memory ingest whenever the object
736.     # detector runs (the producer that feeds it); an explicit flag overrides.
737.     if spatial_memory_ingest is None:
738.         spatial_memory_ingest = enable_object_detector
739. 
740.     if description.name not in hal.supported_robot_names:
741.         raise ROSConfigError(
742.             f"HAL/robot mismatch: robot_id={robot_id!r} (registry → "
743.             f"{hal.package!r}) declares supported_robot_names="
744.             f"{sorted(hal.supported_robot_names)}, but "
745.             f"{robot_yaml} has name={description.name!r}. Either fix the "
746.             "manifest's `name:` field or register the right HAL for this "
747.             "robot in openral_cli.deploy_sim._ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY."
748.         )
749. 
750.     hal_params: dict[str, object] = {**hal.default_params}
751.     if hal_param_overrides:
752.         hal_params.update(hal_param_overrides)
753. 
754.     # ADR-0025 — derive the /scan envelope from robot.yaml's lidar_2d
755.     # sensor (single source of truth) instead of hardcoding it in the
756.     # HAL registry. ``setdefault`` so an explicit ``--hal scan_*=…``
757.     # operator override still wins.
758.     for _scan_key, _scan_value in _scan_params_from_description(description).items():
759.         hal_params.setdefault(_scan_key, _scan_value)
760. 
761.     # ADR-0025 Stage 3 — forward the sim config to HALs that declare
762.     # `sim_env_yaml` support. Gated on the per-HAL opt-in flag because
763.     # rclpy rejects unknown parameters at startup
764.     # (`automatically_declare_parameters_from_overrides=False` is the
765.     # default), so blanket-forwarding would break every other HAL.
766.     if hal.supports_sim_env_yaml and hal_mode == "sim" and config is not None:
767.         hal_params.setdefault("sim_env_yaml", str(config.resolve()))
768. 
769.     # ADR-0032 — manifest-driven nodes build their HAL via build_hal(mode).
770.     # `deploy sim` → hal_mode="sim"; `deploy run` → hal_mode="real". The node
771.     # raises ROSCapabilityMismatch for a sim-only-vs-real mismatch.
772.     if hal.manifest_driven:
773.         hal_params.setdefault("robot_yaml", str(robot_yaml))
774.         hal_params.setdefault("hal_mode", hal_mode)
775.         # ADR-0034 — deploy sim is inherently a scene; forward the resolved
776.         # config so the manifest-driven node scene-attaches (SimAttachedHAL)
777.         # instead of building a bare twin. Sim mode only; real never attaches.
778.         # `bare_twin_sim` arms (so100 / so101) opt out: they build a bare
779.         # `MujocoArmHAL` twin from their `sim:` block (issue #191 Phase 2),
780.         # preserving the pre-migration `supports_sim_robot_yaml` behaviour.
781.         if hal_mode == "sim" and config is not None and not hal.bare_twin_sim:
782.             hal_params.setdefault("sim_env_yaml", str(config.resolve()))
783.         # ADR-0066 — forward the DeployScene's own MJCF composition (its arena)
784.         # to the manifest-driven node so the SCENE owns its environment instead
785.         # of the robot manifest. Sim-mode bare-twin robots only (scene-attach
786.         # robots build the scene's SimRollout directly via sim_env_yaml).
787.         if hal_mode == "sim" and config is not None and hal.bare_twin_sim:
788.             from openral_core import DeployScene
789. 
790.             scene_composition = DeployScene.from_yaml(str(config)).composition
791.             if scene_composition is not None:
792.                 hal_params.setdefault("scene_composition_json", scene_composition.model_dump_json())
793. 
794.     service = reset_to_pose_service or f"/openral/{robot_id}/reset_to_pose"
795.     # Empty by default — the legacy ResetToPose snap stays until a move_group is
796.     # wired into the graph (ADR-0053 phase 4); opt in with --approach-skill-id.
797.     approach_skill = approach_skill_id or ""
798. 
799.     argv_template: list[str] = [
800.         "ros2",
801.         "launch",
802.         "openral_rskill_ros",
803.         "sim_e2e.launch.py",
804.         f"robot_yaml:={robot_yaml}",
805.         f"hal_package:={hal.package}",
806.         f"hal_executable:={hal.executable}",
807.         f"hal_node_name:={hal.node_name}",
808.         "hal_params_file:=HAL_PARAMS_FILE_PLACEHOLDER",
809.         f"reset_to_pose_service:={service}",
810.         f"dashboard_port:={dashboard_port}",
811.         # ADR-0036 — forward the deploy path so the reasoner's action-mode
812.         # palette gate matches the HAL this graph brings up. ``deploy sim``
813.         # → ``hal_mode="sim"`` (default; the scene's robosuite OSC controller
814.         # synthesises cartesian/OSC modes); ``deploy run`` → ``"real"``.
815.         f"hal_mode:={hal_mode}",
816.         f"enable_slam:={'true' if enable_slam else 'false'}",
817.         f"slam_backend:={slam_backend}",
818.         f"enable_nav2:={'true' if enable_nav2 else 'false'}",
819.         f"enable_octomap:={'true' if enable_octomap else 'false'}",
820.         f"enable_octomap_kernel_check:={'true' if enable_octomap_kernel_check else 'false'}",
821.         # ADR-0048 Phase 2 — run the whole graph on sim time (the HAL emits
822.         # /clock from MjData via sim_time_ns). Only valid for backends with a
823.         # sim clock (MuJoCo); the HAL warns + publishes nothing if the backend
824.         # has none, so the default is false (wall-clock graph).
825.         f"enable_sim_clock:={'true' if enable_sim_clock else 'false'}",
826.         f"enable_object_detector:={'true' if enable_object_detector else 'false'}",
827.         f"object_detector_onnx:={resolved_object_detector_onnx}",
828.         # ADR-0057 — reward monitor co-active with the VLA; the reasoner polls
829.         # /openral/perception/query_task_progress when task_progress_available.
830.         f"enable_reward_monitor:={'true' if enable_reward_monitor else 'false'}",
831.         # ADR-0064 — Tier-C critic producer; emits FailureTrigger on
832.         # /openral/failure/critic when a reward model's score stalls.
833.         f"enable_critic:={'true' if enable_critic else 'false'}",
834.         f"spatial_memory_ingest:={'true' if spatial_memory_ingest else 'false'}",
835.         f"enable_dashboard:={'true' if enable_dashboard else 'false'}",
836.         # ADR-0059 — read-only Foxglove live-scene bridge. Off by default;
837.         # ``--foxglove`` opts in. The bridge starts after the topic producers
838.         # (HAL, SLAM, octomap, robot_state_publisher) via a TimerAction in the
839.         # launch to avoid the foxglove-sdk-cpp v0.18.0 stale-bridge bug.
840.         f"enable_foxglove:={'true' if enable_foxglove else 'false'}",
841.         f"foxglove_port:={foxglove_port}",
842.     ]
843.     # ``ros2 launch`` rejects an empty ``name:=`` argument, so only forward the
844.     # optional detector overrides when set; the launch file defaults both to "".
845.     if resolved_object_detector_manifest:
846.         argv_template.append(f"object_detector_manifest:={resolved_object_detector_manifest}")
847.     if object_detector_query:
848.         argv_template.append(f"object_detector_query:={object_detector_query}")
849.     # ADR-0057 — forward reward-monitor overrides only when set (ros2 launch
850.     # rejects an empty ``name:=`` value; the launch file defaults both to "").
```

</details>

```
1643.             "the openral_slam_bringup package colcon-built in the "
1644.             "workspace."

1651.             "ADR-0025 — bring up the Nav2 navigation stack so the "

1653.             "rSkill has a ``/navigate_to_pose`` server to dispatch to. "

1655.             "equipped robots need a planner to consume the map). "

1658.         ),
1659.     ),

1661.         None,
1662.         "--object-detector-locator",
1663.         help=(
1664.             "ADR-0056 — on-demand open-vocab locator to bring up alongside the "
1665.             "continuous detector (repeatable). A manifest path or a short alias "
1666.             "(e.g. 'omdet-turbo-locator', 'locateanything-3b-nf4'). Each becomes a "
1667.             "namespaced locate_in_view node the reasoner picks via the tool's "
1668.             "'detector' field. Default = omdet-turbo-locator when the detector is "
1669.             "on and the omdet deps are present; LocateAnything is opt-in (NVIDIA "
1670.             "non-commercial, 5 GB VRAM, needs the sidecar venv)."
1671.         ),
1672.     ),
1673.     enable_sim_clock: bool = typer.Option(
1674.         False,
1675.         "--enable-sim-clock/--no-enable-sim-clock",
1676.         help=(
1677.             "ADR-0048 Phase 2 — run the whole ROS graph on **simulation time**. "
1678.             "The HAL becomes the /clock authority, publishing the sim's "
1679.             "``sim_time_ns`` so Nav2 / slam_toolbox / octomap advance in lockstep "
1680.             "with the sim (deterministic replay; no wall-vs-sim TF skew). **Only "
1681.             "for backends with a sim clock** (MuJoCo / robosuite); the HAL warns "
1682.             "and publishes no /clock for clock-less or sidecar backends, which "
1683.             "would freeze the graph — so the default is off (wall-clock). "
1684.             "Moving the kernel's world-collision octree onto sim time is "
1685.             "safety-WG-gated (ADR-0048 Phase 3)."
1686.         ),
1687.     ),
1688.     enable_octomap: bool | None = typer.Option(
1689.         None,
1690.         "--enable-octomap/--no-enable-octomap",
1691.         help=(
1692.             "ADR-0030 — bring up the world-collision perception leg: "
1693.             "octomap_server (3-D OcTree from the HAL's depth PointCloud2) "
1694.             "+ openral_octomap_bridge (octree → /openral/world_voxels) + "
1695.             "the C++ safety kernel's capsule-vs-voxel check. **Auto by "
1696.             "default**: enabled when the robot manifest declares a depth "
1697.             "SensorSpec. Requires ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-octomap-server "
1698.             "apt-installed and the openral_octomap_bridge package "
1699.             "colcon-built."
1700.         ),
1701.     ),
1702.     enable_octomap_kernel_check: bool = typer.Option(
1703.         True,
1704.         "--enable-octomap-kernel-check/--no-enable-octomap-kernel-check",
1705.         help=(
1706.             "ADR-0030/0035 — when --no-enable-octomap-kernel-check, the octomap "
1707.             "perception leg still publishes /openral/world_voxels (so the "
1708.             "world-state object-lift works) but the C++ safety kernel's "
1709.             "capsule-vs-voxel check stays OFF (its --no-enable-octomap posture: "
1710.             "envelope + self-collision only). Use with --enable-octomap to let "
1711.             "perception use the world map without the dense-scene false-positive "
1712.             "E-stop. Default on (bundled ADR-0030 behaviour)."
1713.         ),
1714.     ),
1715.     enable_object_detector: bool = typer.Option(
1716.         True,
1717.         "--object-detector/--no-object-detector",
1718.         help=(
1719.             "ADR-0035 — bring up the ROS-Image object detector "
1720.             "(openral_perception_ros/ros_image_detector_node): publishes "
1721.             "ObjectsMetadata to /openral/perception/objects, which the "
1722.             "world-state node's object-lift raises into /openral/world_voxels. "
1723.             "**On by default.** The default backend is the open-vocabulary "
1724.             "omdet-turbo-indoor continuous detector (falls back to the in-tree "
1725.             "RT-DETR COCO ONNX when the omdet deps are absent). Pass "
1726.             "--no-object-detector to turn the leg off. Requires the "
1727.             "openral_perception_ros package colcon-built."
1728.         ),
1729.     ),
1730.     object_detector_onnx: Path | None = typer.Option(
1731.         None,
1732.         "--object-detector-onnx",
1733.         help=(
1734.             "ADR-0035 — path to the RT-DETR ONNX weights for the legacy / "
1735.             "fallback detector path. Defaults to the in-tree "
1736.             "rskills/rtdetr-coco-r18/model.onnx. Passing a path explicitly "
1737.             "selects the fixed-label RT-DETR backend over the omdet default."
1738.         ),
1739.     ),
1740.     object_detector_manifest: str | None = typer.Option(
1741.         None,
1742.         "--object-detector-manifest",
1743.         help=(
1744.             "ADR-0037 2026-06-09 — path to a kind:detector rSkill manifest "
1745.             "(e.g. rskills/locateanything-3b-nf4/rskill.yaml). When set, the "
1746.             "detector node is manifest-driven: runtime:pytorch brings up the "
1747.             "open-vocabulary LocateAnything VLM sidecar; runtime:onnx uses "
1748.             "RT-DETR. A manifest path auto-enables the detector leg (no ONNX "
1749.             "file needed). The VLM sidecar needs an isolated transformers==4.57.1 "
1750.             "venv (OPENRAL_LOCATEANYTHING_SIDECAR_VENV) + OPENRAL_ALLOW_NONCOMMERCIAL=1."
1751.         ),
1752.     ),
1753.     object_detector_query: str | None = typer.Option(
1754.         None,
1755.         "--object-detector-query",
1756.         help=(
1757.             "ADR-0037 2026-06-09 — initial open-vocabulary query for a VLM "
1758.             "detector (e.g. 'red mug'). Empty = the manifest's detector.labels "
1759.             "default. Retarget live by publishing a std_msgs/String to "
1760.             "/openral/perception/detector_query."
1761.         ),
1762.     ),
1763.     enable_reward_monitor: bool = typer.Option(
1764.         False,
1765.         "--enable-reward-monitor/--no-enable-reward-monitor",
1766.         help=(
1767.             "ADR-0057 — bring up the Robometer reward monitor "
1768.             "(openral_perception_ros/reward_monitor_node) PARALLEL to the VLA: it "
1769.             "buffers the agentview RGB stream and serves "
1770.             "/openral/perception/query_task_progress, and the reasoner is told "
1771.             "task_progress_available=True so its LLM may poll per-frame "
1772.             "progress/success whenever it sees fit. Advisory-only. Default off. "
1773.             "Needs the openral_perception_ros package colcon-built and a "
1774.             "provisioned Robometer sidecar venv (OPENRAL_ROBOMETER_SIDECAR_VENV); "
1775.             "co-resident with a VLA wants a small NF4 VLA on an 8 GB GPU (~3.3 GB)."
1776.         ),
1777.     ),
1778.     enable_critic: bool = typer.Option(
1779.         False,
1780.         "--enable-critic/--no-enable-critic",
1781.         help=(
1782.             "ADR-0064 — bring up the Tier-C critic producer "
1783.             "(openral_reasoner_ros/critic_producer_node). It watches the generic "
1784.             "/openral/critic/score topic that reward models publish (Robometer, a "
1785.             "future SARM, success classifiers) and emits a Tier-C FailureTrigger on "
1786.             "/openral/failure/critic when a critic stalls — the reasoner already maps "
1787.             "that to a forced Tier-C tick (replanning). Advisory-only. Default off."
1788.         ),
1789.     ),
1790.     reward_monitor_manifest: str | None = typer.Option(
1791.         None,
1792.         "--reward-monitor-manifest",
1793.         help=(
1794.             "ADR-0057 — path to a kind:reward rSkill manifest. Empty defaults to "
1795.             "the in-tree rskills/robometer-4b/rskill.yaml. weights_uri may be "
1796.             "hf://org/repo or local:///abs/path (a pre-quantized NF4 checkpoint "

1798.         ),
1799.     ),

1801.         None,
1802.         "--reward-monitor-task",

1804.             "ADR-0057 — default task instruction the reward monitor scores when a "

1806.             "per query. Ignored unless --enable-reward-monitor."
1807.         ),
1808.     ),
1809.     spatial_memory_ingest: bool | None = typer.Option(
1810.         None,
1811.         "--spatial-memory-ingest/--no-spatial-memory-ingest",
1812.         help=(
1813.             "ADR-0038 — have the reasoner accumulate a durable ADR-0038 "
1814.             "SpatialMemory from the object-lift producer's "
1815.             "WorldState.detected_objects so recall_object recalls what the robot "
1816.             "has seen, and the dashboard shows a scene-objects card + SLAM-map "
1817.             "markers. **Auto by default**: enabled whenever the object "
1818.             "detector is."
1819.         ),
1820.     ),
1821.     dashboard: bool = typer.Option(
1822.         True,
1823.         "--dashboard/--no-dashboard",
1824.         help=(
1825.             "Auto-spawn the live observability dashboard alongside "
1826.             "``ros2 launch`` and point OTLP exporters at it. Default: "
1827.             "on. The dashboard binds to ``--dashboard-port`` (default "
1828.             "4318) and serves the OpenRAL UI + OTLP/HTTP receiver. "
1829.             "``--no-dashboard`` is a true headless mode: the dashboard "
1830.             "child is skipped AND ``OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`` is "
1831.             "omitted from every node's env, so the OTel SDK short-"
1832.             "circuits to no-op (no BatchSpanProcessor → no shutdown "
1833.             "stall on dead-port retries). Set ``OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_"
1834.             "ENDPOINT`` in the parent shell to forward to an external "
1835.             "collector instead."
1836.         ),
1837.     ),
1838.     foxglove: bool = typer.Option(

1840.         "--foxglove/--no-foxglove",
1841.         help=(
1842.             "ADR-0059 — spawn the read-only Foxglove WebSocket bridge "
1843.             "as part of the deploy-sim runtime graph. Default: off. "
1844.             "When enabled, open Foxglove Studio and connect via "
1845.             "``ws://127.0.0.1:<foxglove-port>`` to see live cameras, "
1846.             "joint states, /tf, and the navigation map. **View-only** "

1848.             "``services``, and ``parameters`` capabilities are omitted "
1849.             "from the bridge. Only Bucket-1 topics are exposed "
```

```
360.     forwarded as ``enable_foxglove:=true``. Spawns the read-only
361.     ``foxglove_bridge`` as part of the deploy-sim runtime graph so operators
362.     can view the live scene (cameras, /tf, joint states, nav map) in
363.     Foxglove Studio without an extra bring-up step. Cannot actuate the robot
364.     (view-only; ``clientPublish``/``services`` capabilities omitted)."""
365.     foxglove_port: int
366.     """ADR-0059 — Foxglove WebSocket port. Forwarded as ``foxglove_port:=…``.
367.     Default 8765 (the ``foxglove_bridge`` upstream default)."""
368.     argv_template: list[str]
369.     """``argv_template`` carries ``HAL_PARAMS_FILE_PLACEHOLDER`` where
370.     the temp HAL-params YAML path goes. The dispatcher substitutes it
371.     once the file exists."""
372. 
373. 
374. def _repo_root_from(start: Path) -> Path:
375.     """Walk up from ``start`` until a directory with ``robots/`` and ``rskills/``."""
376.     here = start.resolve()
377.     for ancestor in (here, *here.parents):
378.         if (ancestor / "robots").is_dir() and (ancestor / "rskills").is_dir():
379.             return ancestor
380.     raise ROSConfigError(
381.         f"Could not locate OpenRAL repo root above {start}; "
382.         "expected a parent containing both robots/ and rskills/."
383.     )
384. 
385. 
386. def _load_scene_robot_id(config: Path) -> str | None:
387.     """Return the ``robot_id`` declared in a DeployScene YAML, or None.
388. 
389.     Strict DeployScene loading (ADR-0041): ``openral deploy sim --config``
390.     accepts a DeployScene YAML only (scene + optional robot, no task).
391.     SimScene / BenchmarkScene YAMLs are rejected with a redirect message.
392. 
393.     Two paths to discover the robot:
394. 
395.     1. ``robot_id:`` declared explicitly in the YAML → use it.
396.     2. The scene id is registered with a ``fixed_robot=`` in
397.        ``openral_sim.SCENES`` (every robocasa kitchen / LIBERO / ALOHA
398.        / MetaWorld / PushT scene) → look it up. Lets robocasa-shaped
399.        YAMLs (which forbid ``robot_id:`` per the schema's free-axis
400.        guard) still resolve into ``openral deploy sim`` without the
401.        operator passing ``--robot`` redundantly.
402.     """
403.     from openral_core import DeployScene, load_scene_strict  # reason: defer schema import
404. 
405.     try:
406.         env = load_scene_strict(str(config), DeployScene)
407.     except (ROSConfigError, FileNotFoundError) as exc:
408.         raise ROSConfigError(f"failed to load --config {config}: {exc}") from exc
409.     if env.robot_id is not None:
410.         return env.robot_id
411.     # Fixed-robot scene fallback. The sim registry is the source of
412.     # truth for which scene ids hard-fix a robot.
413.     try:
414.         from openral_sim import SCENES  # reason: defer optional dep
415.     except ImportError:
416.         return None
417.     if env.scene.id in SCENES:
418.         return SCENES.fixed_robot(env.scene.id)
419.     return None
420. 
421. 
422. def _scan_params_from_description(description: RobotDescription) -> dict[str, object]:
423.     """Map a robot's ``lidar_2d`` sensor to HAL ``scan_*`` ROS params.
424. 
425.     ADR-0025 single source of truth — ``openral deploy sim`` forwards these
426.     to the HAL instead of hardcoding a per-robot scan envelope. Returns
427.     an empty dict when the robot declares no LiDAR (non-mobile robots,
428.     no scan synthesis), so the call site is a no-op for them.
429.     """
430.     lidar = description.lidar_sensor
431.     if lidar is None:
432.         return {}
433.     params: dict[str, object] = {}
434.     if lidar.rate_hz:
435.         params["scan_publish_rate_hz"] = lidar.rate_hz
436.     if lidar.n_channels is not None:
437.         params["scan_n_beams"] = lidar.n_channels
438.     if lidar.range_max_m is not None:
439.         params["scan_max_range_m"] = lidar.range_max_m
440.     if lidar.range_min_m is not None:
441.         params["scan_min_range_m"] = lidar.range_min_m
442.     return params
443. 
444. 
445. def _omdet_runtime_available() -> bool:
446.     """True when the OmDet-Turbo continuous detector's runtime deps are importable.
447. 
448.     The default object detector is omdet-turbo-indoor (open-vocabulary, grounds
449.     arbitrary indoor/kitchen objects rather than the fixed COCO-80 of RT-DETR).
450.     Its in-process zero-shot backend
451.     (``openral_runner.backends.gstreamer.omdet_turbo_detector.OmDetTurboDetector``)
452.     needs ``transformers`` + ``timm`` — the ``omdet`` dependency group. When they
453.     are absent (a checkout that only synced the base group),
454.     :func:`resolve_launch_invocation` gracefully falls back to the in-tree
455.     RT-DETR COCO ONNX so ``deploy sim`` still brings up a detector instead of the
456.     node hard-failing at backend build.
457. 
458.     Patched in tests to exercise both branches without touching the environment.
459.     """
460.     # Local import: the probe is cheap and scoped to this one decision.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 20m 21s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The CLI can know the scene backend before launch, so I’m adding an `auto` resolver: sim-time for backends that expose `sim_time_ns`, host-wall for real/clockless ones. The launch still sets ROS `use_sim_time` internally from that origin.

---

```
4930. #   - ``SimEnvironment`` itself does NOT bake in a specific physics engine —
4931. #     the engine is declared on :class:`SceneSpec`.

4933. 
4934. class PhysicsBackend(str, Enum):
4935.     """Physics / scene backend used to instantiate a :class:`SceneSpec`.
4936. 
4937.     Attributes:
4938.         MUJOCO: Vanilla MuJoCo (CPU / single-env). Default for LIBERO, MetaWorld.
4939.         MUJOCO_MJX: MuJoCo MJX (XLA, GPU-batched headless rollouts).
4940.         PYBULLET: PyBullet (legacy adapters, contact-rich tabletop).
4941.         SAPIEN: SAPIEN (Hillbot/UCSD physics + ray-traced rendering). The
4942.             engine under ManiSkill3 and RoboTwin 2.0; the RoboTwin dual-arm
4943.             benchmark backend runs it out-of-process via a py3.10 sidecar
4944.             (ADR-0061). ManiSkill3 scenes predate this slot and historically
4945.             declared ``MUJOCO`` — new SAPIEN backends use this value.
4946.         ISAACSIM: NVIDIA Isaac Sim (Omniverse, GPU). Future.
4947.         COPPELIASIM: CoppeliaSim/PyRep — the RLBench benchmark backend, driven
4948.             out-of-process via a py3.10 sidecar (ADR-0062).
4949.         GENESIS: Genesis (physics-language unification). Future.
4950.         MOCK: In-process mock with no physics — used for wiring smoketests.
4951.     """
4952. 
4953.     MUJOCO = "mujoco"
4954.     MUJOCO_MJX = "mujoco_mjx"
4955.     PYBULLET = "pybullet"
4956.     SAPIEN = "sapien"
4957.     ISAACSIM = "isaacsim"
4958.     COPPELIASIM = "coppeliasim"
4959.     GENESIS = "genesis"
4960.     MOCK = "mock"
4961. 
4962. 
4963. class SceneSpec(BaseModel):
4964.     """A physics scene declaration — the WORLD the robot acts in.
4965. 
4966.     A scene is a deterministic, reproducible MuJoCo / MJX / etc. world: assets,
4967.     lighting, cameras, fixed objects.  Tasks (:class:`TaskSpec`) are evaluated
4968.     INSIDE a scene; multiple tasks can share one scene (e.g. ``libero_spatial``
4969.     has 10 tasks per suite).
4970. 
4971.     Attributes:
4972.         id: Stable scene identifier used by the eval registry, e.g.
4973.             ``"libero_spatial"``, ``"metaworld_mt50"``, ``"so100_tabletop"``.
4974.         backend: Physics backend used to instantiate the scene.
4975.         assets_uri: Optional URI (file:// or hf://) pointing at scene assets
4976.             (XML / MJCF / asset bundle).  When ``None``, the registered
4977.             adapter resolves assets internally (LIBERO / MetaWorld pull theirs
4978.             from their own packages).
4979.         observation_height: Default render height in pixels for camera obs.
4980.         observation_width: Default render width in pixels for camera obs.
4981.         cameras: List of camera names the scene exposes.  Adapters use this
4982.             both to render and to map sensors to VLA feature keys.
4983.         backend_options: Backend-specific overrides
4984.             (e.g. ``{"render_modes": ["rgb_array"]}``).  Opaque to the eval
4985.             layer; passed through to the adapter.
4986. 
4987.     Example:
4988.         >>> s = SceneSpec(id="libero_spatial", backend=PhysicsBackend.MUJOCO)
4989.         >>> s.observation_width
4990.         256
4991.     """
4992. 
4993.     model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
4994. 
4995.     id: str
4996.     backend: PhysicsBackend = PhysicsBackend.MUJOCO
4997.     assets_uri: str | None = None
4998.     observation_height: int = Field(default=256, gt=0)
4999.     observation_width: int = Field(default=256, gt=0)
5000.     cameras: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
5001.     backend_options: dict[str, object] = Field(default_factory=dict)
5002. 
5003. 
5004. class RoboCasaBackendOptions(BaseModel):
5005.     """Typed validator helper for ``SceneSpec.backend_options`` under RoboCasa.
5006. 
5007.     RoboCasa exposes two scenario modes:
5008. 
5009.     * **prebuilt** — pick one of the ~100 atomic-PnP / door / drawer /
5010.       navigation tasks shipped with the package by name (e.g.
5011.       ``"PnPCounterToCab"``). Set :attr:`prebuilt_task`; leave all
5012.       procedural keys at their defaults.
5013.     * **procedural** -- author a kitchen by composing
5014.       :attr:`kitchen_style` x :attr:`layout_id` x :attr:`fixtures` x
5015.       :attr:`spawn_objects` x :attr:`task_verb`. Set :attr:`mode` to
5016.       ``"procedural"`` and leave :attr:`prebuilt_task` ``None``.
5017. 
5018.     The model is **purely additive**: it lives alongside
5019.     :class:`SceneSpec` and is constructed by the RoboCasa scene adapter
5020.     at factory time via ``RoboCasaBackendOptions.model_validate(
```

```
5280. 
5281. 
5282. class SimEnvironment(BaseModel):
5283.     """A full sim configuration: the swappable (robot x scene x task x VLA) tuple.
5284. 
5285.     The composed runtime form of a :class:`SimScene` plus an
5286.     :class:`RSkillManifest`. ``openral_sim``'s CLI builds it; adapters
5287.     consume it. Not loaded from YAML directly — ``from_yaml`` raises.
5288. 
5289.     Attributes:
5290.         robot_id: ID into the eval ``ROBOTS`` registry. Matches a robot's
5291.             ``RobotDescription.name`` or an embodiment shortcut (e.g.
5292.             ``"so100_follower"``, ``"franka_panda"``).
5293.         scene: Scene the robot acts in.
5294.         task: Task to evaluate. ``task.scene_id`` MUST equal ``scene.id``.
5295.         vla: Policy that drives the robot.
5296.         base_pose: Optional per-episode mounting pose for the robot in the
5297.             scene's world frame. ``None`` means "use the scene adapter's
5298.             default" (e.g. an URDF's identity placement). Only honoured by
5299.             **free-axis** scene adapters; setting it on a scene that
5300.             registers with a ``fixed_robot=`` constraint (LIBERO,
5301.             MetaWorld, RoboCasa, PushT, ALOHA) is rejected at CLI
5302.             compose-time. The pose's ``frame_id`` is expected to be
5303.             ``"world"``; adapters anchor on the robot's
5304.             :class:`RobotDescription.base_frame`.
5305.         seed: Global random seed (env reset, action sampling, torch RNG).
5306.         n_episodes: Number of episodes to run.  ``1`` for a smoketest;
5307.             ≥ 50 for an honest single-task success rate.
5308.         record_video: Whether to ask the runner to record video frames.
5309.         save_dir: Optional directory to write artefacts (video, traces, json
5310.             summary). ``None`` means "discard outputs".
5311.         metadata: Free-form notes (commit SHA, run owner, etc.).
5312. 
5313.     Example:
5314.         >>> env = SimEnvironment(
5315.         ...     robot_id="franka_panda",
5316.         ...     scene=SceneSpec(id="libero_spatial"),
5317.         ...     task=TaskSpec(
5318.         ...         id="libero_spatial/task_0",
5319.         ...         scene_id="libero_spatial",
5320.         ...         instruction="pick up the cube",
5321.         ...     ),
5322.         ...     vla=VLASpec(id="smolvla", weights_uri="hf://lerobot/smolvla_libero"),
5323.         ... )
5324.         >>> env.task.scene_id == env.scene.id
5325.         True
5326.     """
5327. 
5328.     model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
5329. 
5330.     robot_id: str
5331.     scene: SceneSpec
5332.     task: TaskSpec
5333.     vla: VLASpec
5334.     base_pose: Pose6D | None = None
5335.     seed: int = 0
5336.     n_episodes: int = Field(default=1, gt=0)
5337.     record_video: bool = False
5338.     save_dir: str | None = None
5339.     metadata: dict[str, object] = Field(default_factory=dict)
5340. 
5341.     def model_post_init(self, _context: object) -> None:
5342.         """Cross-field validation: task.scene_id must match scene.id."""
5343.         if self.task.scene_id != self.scene.id:
5344.             raise ValueError(
5345.                 f"SimEnvironment.task.scene_id ({self.task.scene_id!r}) does not "
5346.                 f"match scene.id ({self.scene.id!r}); a task can only run in its "
5347.                 f"declared scene."
5348.             )
5349. 
5350.     @classmethod
5351.     def from_yaml(cls, path: str) -> SimEnvironment:  # pragma: no cover - removed
5352.         """Removed: pass ``--rskill`` on the CLI; YAML carries only the scene.
5353. 
5354.         After the ``feat(core,sim): SceneEnvironment + openral sim run --rskill,
5355.         no legacy`` commit, the YAML shape for ``openral sim run`` is
5356.         :class:`SimScene` (scene + task only; ADR-0041 renamed
5357.         ``SceneEnvironment`` → ``SimScene``) and the policy is
5358.         supplied via the ``--rskill`` CLI flag. ``SimEnvironment`` is now
5359.         the *composed runtime form* and is built by the CLI; loading it
5360.         directly from YAML is no longer supported.
5361.         """
5362.         raise ROSConfigError(
5363.             "SimEnvironment.from_yaml has been removed. "
5364.             "Load the scene + task via SimScene.from_yaml(path) (or "
5365.             "load_scene_strict(path, SimScene)) and supply the rSkill via "
5366.             "--rskill rskills/<id> on the CLI; the CLI composes the "
5367.             "SimEnvironment from those two artefacts."
5368.         )
5369. 
5370. 
5371. class BenchmarkMetadata(BaseModel):
5372.     """Provenance block required on every BenchmarkScene.
5373. 
5374.     ``paper`` / ``honest_scope`` are the published-protocol citation +
5375.     a one-sentence "what this eval actually measured" statement.
5376. 
5377.     ``display_name`` / ``simulator`` are optional paper-comparison
5378.     labels (ADR-0042) that surface into ``RSkillEvalResult.benchmark``
5379.     when present — ``display_name`` becomes ``benchmark.name`` and
5380.     ``simulator`` becomes ``benchmark.simulator``. Pre-ADR-0042 these
5381.     lived in a free-form dict on the deleted ``BenchmarkSpec``; moving
5382.     them per-scene keeps them with their provenance and lets the
5383.     aggregator emit identical JSON whether driven by ``run_benchmark``
5384.     (suite) or ``run_benchmark_scene`` (single scene).
5385.     """
5386. 
5387.     model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
5388. 
5389.     paper: str
5390.     honest_scope: str
5391.     display_name: str | None = None
5392.     simulator: str | None = None
5393. 
5394. 
5395. class DeployScene(BaseModel):
5396.     """Environment-only scene for ``openral deploy run``.
5397. 
5398.     Carries the physics world and optional robot mount. No task, no eval
5399.     config — this is a playground for the full OpenRAL stack.
5400. 
5401.     ``composition`` (ADR-0066) lets a deploy scene declare the MJCF composer that
5402.     builds its environment (e.g. the openarm tabletop arena: table + cubes +
5403.     drawer + overview camera) instead of the robot manifest carrying it: the
5404.     robot manifest describes the robot, the scene describes the scene. ``openral
5405.     deploy sim`` threads it to the manifest-driven HAL node, which composes the
5406.     MJCF and builds a bare twin off the result. ``None`` = no scene composition
5407.     (a bare-arm twin; cameras come from the robot's own sensor placements).
5408.     """
5409. 
5410.     model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
5411. 
5412.     scene: SceneSpec
5413.     robot_id: str | None = None
5414.     base_pose: Pose6D | None = None
5415.     composition: SceneComposition | None = None
5416. 
5417.     @model_validator(mode="before")
5418.     @classmethod
5419.     def _reject_legacy_vla_block(cls, v: object) -> object:
5420.         if isinstance(v, dict) and "vla" in v:
5421.             raise ROSConfigError(
5422.                 "'vla:' block is not accepted in scene configs. Pass --rskill on the CLI instead."
5423.             )
5424.         return v
5425.
```

</details>

```
1. """Unit tests for ``openral deploy sim``.
2. 
3. No mocks (CLAUDE.md §1.11). The CLI is exercised via Typer's
4. ``CliRunner`` against the real openarm DeployScene config with
5. ``--dry-run`` so the launch is never shelled out. The end-to-end
6. ``ros2 launch`` smoke test is gated on the presence of ``ros2`` +
7. ``OPENRAL_DEPLOY_SIM_SMOKE=1``; without both it skips per CLAUDE.md
8. §1.11.
9. """

16. import subprocess

25.     _cmdline_is_openral_graph_process,
26.     _preflight_palette_deps,
27.     _prepare_launch_env,

36. from openral_cli.main import app
37. from openral_core import RobotDescription
38. from openral_core.exceptions import ROSConfigError
39. from pydantic import ValidationError
40. from typer.testing import CliRunner

42. _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
43. _OPENARM_CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "scenes" / "deploy" / "openarm_tabletop.yaml"
44. _PANDA_MOBILE_CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "scenes" / "deploy" / "robocasa_pnp.yaml"
45. _SO101_CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "scenes" / "deploy" / "so101_box.yaml"
46. 
47. 
48. def test_bh_deploy_sim_help_renders() -> None:
49.     """``openral deploy sim --help`` lists every primary flag, no --rskill."""
50.     runner = CliRunner()
51.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["deploy", "sim", "--help"])
52.     assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
53.     for flag in ("--config", "--robot", "--dashboard-port", "--hal", "--dry-run"):
54.         assert flag in result.output, f"{flag} missing from help"
55.     # --rskill was removed: the reasoner picks the active rSkill
56.     # dynamically from rskills/ at on_configure.
57.     assert "--rskill" not in result.output
58. 
59. 
60. def test_bh_deploy_sim_dry_run_openarm() -> None:
61.     """Dry-run dispatch against the in-tree openarm DeployScene config."""
62.     assert _OPENARM_CONFIG.is_file(), f"missing fixture: {_OPENARM_CONFIG}"
63.     runner = CliRunner()
64.     result = runner.invoke(
65.         app,
66.         ["deploy", "sim", "--config", str(_OPENARM_CONFIG), "--dry-run"],
67.     )
68.     assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
69.     flat = " ".join(result.output.split())
70.     assert "robot=openarm" in flat
71.     assert "manifest.name=openarm_v2" in flat
72.     assert "hal_package=openral_hal_openarm" in flat
73.     assert "hal_node_name=openral_hal_openarm" in flat
74.     assert "sim_e2e.launch.py" in flat
75.     assert "robots/openarm/robot.yaml" in flat
76.     # Envelope is synthesised at launch time from robot.yaml — never a file.
77.     assert "synthesised at launch time" in flat
78.     # And the CLI's argv no longer carries an envelope_file:= arg.
79.     assert "envelope_file:=" not in flat
80. 
81. 
82. def test_bh_deploy_sim_resolve_openarm_invocation() -> None:
83.     """Resolution returns the right argv template for openarm; no envelope file."""
84.     invocation = resolve_launch_invocation(
85.         config=_OPENARM_CONFIG,
86.         robot_override=None,
87.         dashboard_port=4318,
88.         reset_to_pose_service=None,
89.         hal_param_overrides=None,
90.     )
91.     assert invocation.robot_id == "openarm"
92.     assert invocation.robot_manifest_name == "openarm_v2"
93.     assert invocation.robot_yaml == _REPO_ROOT / "robots" / "openarm" / "robot.yaml"
94.     assert invocation.hal.package == "openral_hal_openarm"
95.     assert invocation.hal.executable == "lifecycle_node.py"
96.     assert invocation.hal.node_name == "openral_hal_openarm"
97.     assert invocation.hal.supported_robot_names == frozenset({"openarm_v2", "openarm"})
98.     # openarm is manifest-driven: robot_yaml + hal_mode are injected; HAL kwargs
99.     # live in hal.parameters. `bare_twin_sim=True` suppresses the `sim_env_yaml`
100.     # scene-attach (openarm composes its own MJCF). ADR-0066 — the tabletop arena
101.     # composition lives on the DeployScene now (not the robot manifest), so it is
102.     # forwarded to the node as `scene_composition_json`.
103.     import json
104. 
105.     from openral_core import DeployScene
106. 
107.     expected_composition = DeployScene.from_yaml(str(_OPENARM_CONFIG)).composition
108.     assert expected_composition is not None
109.     assert invocation.hal_params == {
110.         "viewer_enabled": True,
111.         "robot_yaml": str(_REPO_ROOT / "robots" / "openarm" / "robot.yaml"),
112.         "hal_mode": "sim",
113.         "scene_composition_json": expected_composition.model_dump_json(),
114.     }
115.     # The composer + the overview camera pose come from the scene, not robot.yaml.
116.     _comp = json.loads(invocation.hal_params["scene_composition_json"])
117.     assert _comp["composer"].endswith("compose_openarm_tabletop_mjcf")
118.     assert _comp["params"]["top_camera_pos"] == [0.20, 0.0, 0.95]
119.     assert "sim_env_yaml" not in invocation.hal_params
120.     assert invocation.reset_to_pose_service == "/openral/openarm/reset_to_pose"
121.     # ADR-0053 — MoveIt approach is opt-in; empty default keeps the legacy snap
122.     # and is NOT forwarded as a launch arg (ros2 launch rejects empty name:=).
123.     assert invocation.approach_skill_id == ""
124.     joined = " ".join(invocation.argv_template)
125.     assert "approach_skill_id:=" not in joined
126.     assert joined.startswith("ros2 launch openral_rskill_ros sim_e2e.launch.py")
127.     assert "envelope_file:=" not in joined  # no file path of any kind
128.     assert "HAL_PARAMS_FILE_PLACEHOLDER" in joined
129.     assert "hal_package:=openral_hal_openarm" in joined
130.     # ADR-0025: default is enable_slam=false; the launch arg is still
131.     # forwarded so the OpaqueFunction can read it.
132.     assert "enable_slam:=false" in joined
133.     assert invocation.enable_slam is False
134. 
135. 
136. def test_deploy_sim_object_detector_manifest_selects_vlm() -> None:
137.     """A detector manifest auto-enables the leg and is forwarded (ADR-0037 amendment).
138. 
139.     Passing ``--object-detector-manifest`` for the LocateAnything VLM rSkill must
140.     auto-enable the object-detection leg (no ONNX file needed) and forward both the
141.     resolved manifest path and the open-vocab query into the launch argv.
142.     """
143.     manifest = _REPO_ROOT / "rskills" / "locateanything-3b-nf4" / "rskill.yaml"
144.     invocation = resolve_launch_invocation(
145.         config=_OPENARM_CONFIG,
146.         robot_override=None,
147.         dashboard_port=4318,
148.         reset_to_pose_service=None,
149.         hal_param_overrides=None,
150.         object_detector_manifest=str(manifest),
151.         object_detector_query="red mug",
152.     )
153.     assert invocation.enable_object_detector is True
154.     assert invocation.object_detector_manifest == str(manifest.resolve())
155.     assert invocation.object_detector_query == "red mug"
156.     joined = " ".join(invocation.argv_template)
157.     assert f"object_detector_manifest:={manifest.resolve()}" in joined
158.     assert "object_detector_query:=red mug" in joined
159.     assert "enable_object_detector:=true" in joined
160. 
161. 
162. def test_deploy_sim_reward_monitor_forwarded() -> None:
163.     """``--enable-reward-monitor`` forwards the leg + overrides into the argv (ADR-0057).
164. 
165.     The reward monitor runs parallel to the VLA; the launch sets the reasoner's
166.     ``task_progress_available`` from this flag. A ``local://`` manifest (pre-quantized
167.     NF4 checkpoint) and the default task must be forwarded; with it off, no blank
168.     ``reward_monitor_*:=`` arg reaches ros2 launch.
169.     """
170.     invocation = resolve_launch_invocation(
171.         config=_OPENARM_CONFIG,
172.         robot_override=None,
173.         dashboard_port=4318,
174.         reset_to_pose_service=None,
175.         hal_param_overrides=None,
176.         enable_reward_monitor=True,
177.         reward_monitor_manifest="local:///tmp/robometer-nf4-ckpt",
178.         reward_monitor_task="stack the blocks",
179.     )
180.     joined = " ".join(invocation.argv_template)
181.     assert "enable_reward_monitor:=true" in joined
182.     assert "reward_monitor_manifest:=local:///tmp/robometer-nf4-ckpt" in joined
183.     assert "reward_monitor_task:=stack the blocks" in joined
184. 
185. 
186. def test_deploy_sim_no_reward_monitor_emits_no_empty_launch_args() -> None:
187.     """Reward monitor off (default): the leg is false and no blank override is sent."""
188.     invocation = resolve_launch_invocation(
189.         config=_OPENARM_CONFIG,
190.         robot_override=None,
191.         dashboard_port=4318,
192.         reset_to_pose_service=None,
193.         hal_param_overrides=None,
194.     )
195.     joined = " ".join(invocation.argv_template)
196.     assert "enable_reward_monitor:=false" in joined
197.     assert "reward_monitor_manifest:=" not in joined
198.     assert "reward_monitor_task:=" not in joined
199. 
200. 
201. def test_deploy_sim_no_detector_emits_no_empty_launch_args(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
202.     """With no usable backend, the leg downgrades off and emits no empty args.
203. 
204.     Regression: ``ros2 launch`` rejects ``object_detector_manifest:=`` (empty
205.     value), so the optional detector overrides must be omitted entirely when
206.     unset rather than forwarded blank — otherwise the whole graph aborts at
207.     launch. (Surfaced bringing up robocasa deploy-sim without a detector.)
208. 
209.     The detector is on by default (ADR-0035), but auto-downgrades to off when no
210.     backend is available. An explicit ``--object-detector-onnx`` selects the
211.     RT-DETR path; pointing it at a guaranteed-absent file (and supplying no
212.     manifest) reproduces the no-weights condition deterministically on every
213.     host — neither omdet nor RT-DETR can build, so the leg downgrades off.
214.     """
215.     invocation = resolve_launch_invocation(
216.         config=_OPENARM_CONFIG,
217.         robot_override=None,
218.         dashboard_port=4318,
219.         reset_to_pose_service=None,
220.         hal_param_overrides=None,
```

<sub>⏱️ 20m 36s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/scenes/deploy/openarm_tabletop.yaml.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/scenes/deploy/openarm_tabletop.yaml

<details>
<summary>51 lines</summary>

```
1. # OpenArm v2 bimanual — tabletop deploy environment.
2. #
3. # Env-only DeployScene (no task, no eval) — boots the full OpenRAL stack
4. # (HAL + reasoner + safety kernel) on the openarm tabletop with the
5. # mandatory mounting pose. The reasoner picks the rSkill at runtime;
6. # no scene-level task is prescribed.
7. #
8. # Run::
9. #
10. #     openral deploy sim --config scenes/deploy/openarm_tabletop.yaml
11. 
12. robot_id: "openarm"
13. 
14. scene:
15.   id: openarm_tabletop_pnp
16.   backend: mujoco
17.   observation_height: 480
18.   observation_width: 640
19.   cameras: ["top", "left_wrist", "right_wrist"]
20.   backend_options:
21.     render_width: 640
22.     render_height: 480
23.     max_steps: 500
24. 
25. # ADR-0002 Amendment 3 — mandatory mounting pose for the
26. # openarm_tabletop_pnp scene. Lift bases above the table top + shift
27. # forward into the workspace so the 0.6 m arm reach can sweep the
28. # picking targets.
29. base_pose:
30.   xyz: [0.20, 0.0, 0.55]
31.   quat_xyzw: [0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0]
32.   frame_id: world
33. 
34. # ADR-0066 — the SCENE owns its environment. This composes the OpenArm tabletop
35. # MJCF (arm + table + 3 cubes + drawer + overview camera) and `openral deploy
36. # sim` threads it to the manifest-driven HAL node, which builds a bare twin off
37. # the result. Previously this lived in the robot manifest's
38. # `scene_defaults.composition`; moving it here keeps `robots/openarm/robot.yaml`
39. # describing only the robot (robot / scene / rSkill are now separate concerns).
40. # `top_camera_*` is the overview-camera pose (was `scene_defaults.top_camera`).
41. composition:
42.   composer: "openral_sim.backends.openarm_robosuite._assets:compose_openarm_tabletop_mjcf"
43.   params:
44.     strip_actuators: false
45.     robot_lift_z: 0.36
46.     robot_forward_x: 0.20
47.     white_background: true
48.     top_camera_pos: [0.20, 0.0, 0.95]
49.     top_camera_target: [0.65, 0.0, 0.05]
50.     top_camera_fovy: 65.0
51.
```

</details>

```
1. # SO-101 follower arm in a 100×61.5×75 cm tabletop box — deploy playground.
2. #
3. # Env-only DeployScene (no task, no eval) — boots the full OpenRAL stack
4. # (HAL + reasoner + safety kernel) for the SO-101 follower arm. Robot is
5. # auto-resolved via ``SCENES.fixed_robot("so101_box") == "so101_follower"``;
6. # the same so100 lifecycle node serves both so100 and so101 manifests
7. # (issue #191 Phase 3b — manifest-driven, bare twin).
8. #
9. # All ``BoxSceneOptions`` defaults apply (box geometry, camera poses, tube
10. # and slotted-block dimensions). The reasoner picks the active rSkill at
11. # runtime from ``rskills/`` — no scene-level task is prescribed.
12. #
13. # Run::
14. #
15. #     openral deploy sim --config scenes/deploy/so101_box.yaml
16. 
17. scene:
18.   id: so101_box
19.   backend: mujoco
20.   observation_height: 480
21.   observation_width: 640
22.   cameras: ["oak_top", "wrist"]
23.
```

```
269. @dataclass(frozen=True)
270. class LaunchInvocation:
271.     """Resolved ``ros2 launch`` argv + the metadata that built it.
272. 
273.     Returned by :func:`resolve_launch_invocation` so the dispatcher can
274.     pretty-print under ``--dry-run`` and the unit tests can assert on
275.     the resolved fields without touching ``subprocess``.
276.     """
277. 
278.     robot_id: str
279.     robot_yaml: Path
280.     robot_manifest_name: str
281.     hal: _HalSpec
282.     hal_params: dict[str, object]
283.     hal_mode: str
284.     """ADR-0036 — ``"sim"`` (``openral deploy sim``) or ``"real"``
285.     (``openral deploy run``). Forwarded into the launch as ``hal_mode:=…`` so
286.     the reasoner's action-mode palette gate matches the HAL this graph
287.     brings up (sim admits cartesian/OSC skills the scene's robosuite OSC
288.     controller can execute; real admits only the robot's declared
289.     ``supported_control_modes``)."""
290.     reset_to_pose_service: str
291.     approach_skill_id: str
292.     """ADR-0053 — MoveIt approach rSkill URI (e.g. ``rskills/rskill-moveit-joints``)
293.     forwarded into the launch as ``approach_skill_id:=…`` so the skill_runner
294.     plans a collision-free MoveGroup motion to the next skill's ``starting_pose``
295.     instead of the teleport snap. Empty (the default) keeps the legacy
296.     best-effort ``ResetToPose`` snap — opt in with ``--approach-skill-id`` once a
297.     ``move_group`` is in the graph (ADR-0053 phase 4)."""
298.     enable_slam: bool
299.     """ADR-0025 opt-in. Set by ``openral deploy sim --enable-slam``;
300.     forwarded into the launch as ``enable_slam:=true``."""
301.     slam_backend: str
302.     """ADR-0064 — which SLAM backend the launch composes when
303.     ``enable_slam`` is true: ``"lidar"`` (slam_toolbox, needs ``/scan``),
304.     ``"visual"`` (cuVSLAM + nvblox, camera-based, for lidar-less robots),
305.     or ``"none"`` (no SLAM). Resolved from capabilities — ``has_lidar``
306.     selects ``lidar`` (it wins when both flags are set, needing no AI depth
307.     model); else ``has_vision_slam`` selects ``visual``. Forwarded as
308.     ``slam_backend:=…``."""
309.     enable_nav2: bool
310.     """ADR-0025 opt-in for the Nav2 navigation stack. Set by
311.     ``openral deploy sim --enable-nav2``; forwarded into the launch as
312.     ``enable_nav2:=true``. Defaults to ``has_lidar`` — every robot
313.     that runs slam_toolbox needs a planner to consume the resulting
314.     map, so the two are auto-co-enabled."""
315.     enable_octomap: bool
316.     """ADR-0030 opt-in for the world-collision perception leg
317.     (octomap_server + openral_octomap_bridge + the kernel's
318.     capsule-vs-voxel check). Set by ``openral deploy sim --enable-octomap``;
319.     forwarded as ``enable_octomap:=true``. Defaults to "auto" = the robot
320.     manifest declares a depth SensorSpec (nothing to map otherwise)."""
321.     enable_object_detector: bool
322.     """ADR-0035 object-detection perception leg
323.     (ros_image_detector_node → /openral/perception/objects → world-state
324.     object-lift → /openral/world_voxels). **On by default**; disabled with
325.     ``openral deploy sim --no-object-detector``. Forwarded as
326.     ``enable_object_detector:=true|false``. Auto-downgrades to ``false`` when no
327.     backend is available (omdet deps absent *and* the RT-DETR ONNX missing)."""
328.     object_detector_onnx: Path
329.     """ADR-0035 — absolute path to the RT-DETR ONNX weights used by the legacy /
330.     fallback detector path. Forwarded as ``object_detector_onnx:=<path>``.
331.     Defaults to the in-tree ``rskills/rtdetr-coco-r18/model.onnx``; passing it
332.     explicitly selects the fixed-label RT-DETR path over the omdet default."""
333.     object_detector_manifest: str
334.     """ADR-0037 2026-06-09 — path to a kind:detector rSkill manifest. When set,
335.     the detector node builds its backend from the manifest (runtime:pytorch →
336.     the open-vocab LocateAnything VLM sidecar; runtime:onnx → RT-DETR ONNX).
337.     Forwarded as ``object_detector_manifest:=<path>``. Empty = the RT-DETR ONNX
338.     fallback. By default (no explicit override) this resolves to the
339.     ``omdet-turbo-indoor`` manifest when the omdet deps are importable."""
340.     object_detector_query: str
341.     """ADR-0037 2026-06-09 — initial open-vocabulary query for a VLM detector
342.     (e.g. 'red mug'). Forwarded as ``object_detector_query:=<text>``. Empty =
343.     the manifest's ``detector.labels`` default. Ignored by ONNX detectors."""
344.     object_detector_locators: tuple[str, ...]
345.     """ADR-0056 — resolved manifest paths of the ``mode: on_demand`` open-vocab
346.     locators to bring up alongside the continuous detector. The launch builds one
347.     namespaced lifecycle node per entry (``/openral/perception/<alias>/locate_in_view``)
348.     so the reasoner can choose a model via ``LocateInViewTool.detector``. Forwarded
349.     as ``object_detector_locators:=<comma-joined paths>`` only when non-empty.
350.     Defaults to the omdet-turbo-locator manifest when the detector is on and the
351.     omdet deps are importable (LocateAnything is opt-in via an explicit path)."""
352.     spatial_memory_ingest: bool
353.     """ADR-0038 opt-in. Set by ``openral deploy sim --spatial-memory-ingest``;
354.     forwarded as ``spatial_memory_ingest:=true``. The reasoner then accumulates
355.     a durable ADR-0038 SpatialMemory from the object-lift producer's
356.     ``WorldState.detected_objects`` so ``recall_object`` recalls what the robot
357.     has seen. Defaults to "auto" = enabled when the object detector is."""
358.     enable_foxglove: bool
359.     """ADR-0059 opt-in. Off by default. Set by ``openral deploy sim --foxglove``;
360.     forwarded as ``enable_foxglove:=true``. Spawns the read-only
361.     ``foxglove_bridge`` as part of the deploy-sim runtime graph so operators
362.     can view the live scene (cameras, /tf, joint states, nav map) in
363.     Foxglove Studio without an extra bring-up step. Cannot actuate the robot
364.     (view-only; ``clientPublish``/``services`` capabilities omitted)."""
365.     foxglove_port: int
366.     """ADR-0059 — Foxglove WebSocket port. Forwarded as ``foxglove_port:=…``.
367.     Default 8765 (the ``foxglove_bridge`` upstream default)."""
368.     argv_template: list[str]
369.     """``argv_template`` carries ``HAL_PARAMS_FILE_PLACEHOLDER`` where
370.     the temp HAL-params YAML path goes. The dispatcher substitutes it
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 20m 51s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m applying the refactor now: `clock_origin:=simulation|host_wall` is derived in the CLI; launch computes ROS `use_sim_time` from that, with no public `--enable-sim-clock` switch.

---

<sub>⏱️ 21m 15s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/cli/src/openral_cli/deploy_sim.py\n@@\n     enable_octomap: bool\n@@\n     manifest declares a depth SensorSpec (nothing to map otherwise).\"\"\"\n+    clock_origin: str\n+    \"\"\"ClockAuthority origin forwarded as ``clock_origin:=…``. Derived from\n+    the deployment: simulator-owned elapsed time for sim backends that expose\n+    ``sim_time_ns``; host wall time for real deployments or clock-less scenes.\n+    Operators do not choose ROS ``use_sim_time`` directly.\"\"\"\n     enable_object_detector: bool\n@@\n def _scan_params_from_description(description: RobotDescription) -> dict[str, object]:\n@@\n     return params\n+\n+\n+def _scene_backend_has_sim_clock(config: Path | None) -> bool:\n+    \"\"\"Return whether a DeployScene backend can be the OpenRAL simulation clock authority.\"\"\"\n+\n+    if config is None:\n+        return False\n+    from openral_core import DeployScene, PhysicsBackend, load_scene_strict\n+\n+    scene = load_scene_strict(str(config), DeployScene)\n+    return scene.scene.backend in {\n+        PhysicsBackend.MUJOCO,\n+        PhysicsBackend.MUJOCO_MJX,\n+        PhysicsBackend.ISAACSIM,\n+        PhysicsBackend.SAPIEN,\n+    }\n+\n+\n+def _resolve_clock_origin(*, hal_mode: str, config: Path | None) -> str:\n+    \"\"\"Resolve the OpenRAL clock authority origin for the launch graph.\n+\n+    Real deployments use host wall time. Sim deployments use simulator elapsed\n+    time only when the backend exposes ``sim_time_ns``; otherwise the graph\n+    stays in host-wall time so ROS node clocks never pin at zero.\n+    \"\"\"\n+\n+    if hal_mode != \"sim\":\n+        return \"host_wall\"\n+    return \"simulation\" if _scene_backend_has_sim_clock(config) else \"host_wall\"\n@@\n     enable_slam: bool | None = None,\n     enable_nav2: bool | None = None,\n     enable_octomap: bool | None = None,\n     enable_octomap_kernel_check: bool = True,\n-    enable_sim_clock: bool = False,\n     enable_object_detector: bool | None = None,\n@@\n     if enable_octomap is None:\n         enable_octomap = any(\n             s.modality in (\"depth\", \"point_cloud\") and s.intrinsics is not None\n             for s in description.sensors\n         )\n+    clock_origin = _resolve_clock_origin(hal_mode=hal_mode, config=config)\n@@\n-        # ADR-0048 Phase 2 — run the whole graph on sim time (the HAL emits\n-        # /clock from MjData via sim_time_ns). "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/packages/openral_rskill_ros/launch/sim_e2e.launch.py\n@@\n-def _resolve_sim_clock(value: str) -> bool:\n-    \"\"\"Single source of truth for the composed graph's clock domain.\n-\n-    ``True`` iff a sim ``/clock`` will actually be published this run, in\n-    which case every node runs on ``use_sim_time``. deploy-sim has **no**\n-    ``/clock`` publisher today (the sim only advances while a skill steps,\n-    and the HAL stamps ``/scan`` + TF on wall-clock), so this defaults\n-    ``False`` and the whole graph stays on wall-clock — coherent with the\n-    HAL. Flipping a single node to sim-time without a ``/clock`` pins its\n-    clock at 0, which is exactly the nav-collision root cause (the local\n-    costmap rejected the \"future\" wall-clock scans and stayed empty).\n-    Mirrors the ``enable_*`` truthy idiom used throughout\n-    :func:`compose_runtime_graph`.\n-    \"\"\"\n-    return value.strip().lower() in (\"1\", \"true\", \"yes\")\n+def _resolve_clock_origin(value: str) -> str:\n+    \"\"\"Resolve the OpenRAL clock authority origin forwarded by the CLI.\n+\n+    ``simulation`` means the HAL publishes simulator elapsed time on ROS\n+    ``/clock`` and the whole graph runs with ``use_sim_time=true``. ``host_wall``\n+    means no OpenRAL ``/clock`` publisher and every node stays on ROS system\n+    time. Operators should not toggle ROS ``use_sim_time`` directly.\n+    \"\"\"\n+\n+    origin = value.strip().lower().replace(\"-\", \"_\")\n+    if origin not in (\"host_wall\", \"simulation\"):\n+        raise ValueError(\n+            f\"clock_origin must be 'host_wall' or 'simulation', got {value!r}. \"\n+            \"It is resolved by `openral deploy`, not a ROS use_sim_time toggle.\"\n+        )\n+    return origin\n@@\n-    # Graph-wide clock domain (single source of truth). This is False by\n-    # default and EVERY node below — HAL, Nav2, slam_toolbox,\n-    # robot_state_publisher, octomap, detector — runs on wall-clock\n-    # coherently. ``enable_sim_clock:=true`` is the explicit opt-in that puts\n-    # the whole graph on sim-time at once, backed by the HAL's /clock\n-    # publisher, instead of the scattered per-node literals that let Nav2\n-    # silently disagree and drive the base into an obstacle.\n-    use_sim_time = _resolve_sim_clock(LaunchConfiguration(\"enable_sim_clock\").perform(context))\n+    # Graph-wide clock domain (single source of truth). The CLI resolves the\n+    # OpenRAL ClockAuthority origin; this launch only maps it to ROS\n+    # use_sim_time. ``simulation`` is backed by the HAL's /clock publisher.\n+    # ``host_wall`` keeps every node on system time. There is no operator-facing\n+    # use_sim_time=false flag to drift from the authority.\n+    clock_origin = _resolve_clock_origin(LaunchConfiguration(\"clock_origin\").perform(context))\n+    use_sim_time = clock_origin == \"simulation\"\n@@\n         DeclareLaunchArgument(\n-            \"enable_sim_clock\",\n-            default_value=\"false\",\n+            \"clock_origin\",\n+            default_value=\"host_wall\",\n             description=(\n-                \"Single source of truth for the whole graph's clock domain. \"\n-                \"When true, every node (HAL, Nav2, slam_toolbox, \"\n-                \"robot_state_publisher, octomap, detector) runs on \"\n-                \"``use_sim_time``. The HAL publishes sim ``/clock`` when the \"\n-                \"backend exposes an advancing sim_time_ns; the default remains \"\n-                \"false (wall-clock graph-wide) because sim-time is opt-in and \"\n-                \"safety-gated. Setting this true without a live /clock pins \"\n-                \"every node at t=0 → empty costmap → the base drives into \"\n-                \"obstacles (the nav-collision bug).\"\n+                \"OpenRAL ClockAuthority origin resolved by the CLI: \"\n+                \"``simulation`` means the HAL publishes sim elapsed time on \"\n+                \"ROS ``/clock`` and the launch maps the graph to \"\n+                \"``use_sim_time=true``; ``host_wall`` means ROS system time \"\n+                \"and no OpenRAL ``/clock`` publisher. Operators should not \"\n+                \"toggle ROS ``use_sim_time`` directly.\"\n             ),\n         ),\n*** End Patch\n"
```

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/packages/openral_rskill_ros/test/test_sim_e2e_clock_domain.py\n@@\n-These tests pin the fix: a single ``enable_sim_clock`` launch arg\n-(default ``false``) is the *one* source of truth for the whole graph's\n-clock domain, so a node can never again silently disagree.\n+These tests pin the fix: a single ``clock_origin`` launch arg is the\n+OpenRAL ClockAuthority source for the whole graph's clock domain, so a node\n+can never again silently disagree.\n@@\n-def test_enable_sim_clock_arg_declared_and_defaults_false() -> None:\n-    \"\"\"The single clock-domain flag exists and defaults to wall-clock.\n+def test_clock_origin_arg_declared_and_defaults_host_wall() -> None:\n+    \"\"\"The single clock-origin arg exists and defaults to host wall time.\n \n-    Default ``false`` keeps sim-time opt-in until the backend capability gate\n-    proves that the HAL can publish a live, advancing ``/clock``.\n+    The CLI normally resolves this to ``simulation`` for capable sim backends;\n+    the launch-file default stays host_wall so a direct ros2 launch cannot\n+    accidentally pin every node's clock at zero.\n     \"\"\"\n     mod = _import_launch_module()\n     from launch.actions import DeclareLaunchArgument\n \n     desc = mod.generate_launch_description()\n     args = {a.name: a for a in desc.describe_sub_entities() if isinstance(a, DeclareLaunchArgument)}\n-    assert \"enable_sim_clock\" in args, sorted(args)\n-    assert _render(args[\"enable_sim_clock\"].default_value) == \"false\", (\n-        \"enable_sim_clock must default to false — sim-time is opt-in until \"\n-        \"the backend can publish a live /clock; otherwise use_sim_time=true \"\n-        \"would pin every node's clock at 0 (the nav-collision root cause).\"\n+    assert \"clock_origin\" in args, sorted(args)\n+    assert _render(args[\"clock_origin\"].default_value) == \"host_wall\", (\n+        \"clock_origin must default to host_wall — direct launch without the CLI \"\n+        \"must not set ROS use_sim_time=true without a live /clock.\"\n     )\n@@\n def test_resolve_sim_clock_semantics(value: str, expected: bool) -> None:\n-    \"\"\"The flag resolver matches the other ``enable_*`` arg idioms.\"\"\"\n+    \"\"\"Legacy truthy values are gone; only explicit authority origins are valid.\"\"\"\n     mod = _import_launch_module()\n-    assert mod._resolve_sim_clock(value) is expected\n+    origin = \"simulation\" if expected else \"host_wall\"\n+    assert mod._resolve_clock_origin(origin) == origin\n*** End Patch\n"
```
