/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/smolvla-metaworld/rskill.yaml (lines 1-160)

<details>
<summary>116 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — OpenRAL packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. # Wraps: lerobot/smolvla_metaworld (Apache-2.0)
3. # Base:  lerobot/smolvla_base  (arxiv:2506.01844)
4. 
5. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6. schema_version: "0.1"
7. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-smolvla-metaworld"
8. evaluated_tasks: ["metaworld"]  # ADR-0060 task-data gate. A *family* entry: the
9. # matcher (openral_sim.benchmark._task_matches) covers every "metaworld/<task>-v3"
10. # task id and the bare "metaworld" scene id, so this multi-task MT50 checkpoint is
11. # gated to the whole MetaWorld family (MT10 + MT50 suites, all 5 demo scenes) and
12. # blocked elsewhere — mirroring how the LIBERO rSkills declare "libero_spatial".
13. version: "0.1.0"
14. license: "apache-2.0"
15. role: "s1"
16. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
17. 
18. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19. model_family: "smolvla"
20. 
21. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
22. # MetaWorld MT50 targets 50 diverse manipulation tasks on a Rethink Sawyer
23. # arm (NOT a Franka — the underlying lerobot MetaworldEnv wraps a Sawyer URDF).
24. embodiment_tags:
25.   - "sawyer"
26. 
27. # MetaWorld renders one corner-view RGB camera (480x480 native, mapped to
28. # observation.images.camera1 by the metaworld scene adapter).
29. sensors_required:
30.   - modality: "rgb"
31.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera1"
32.     min_width: 224
33.     min_height: 224
34. 
35. # Output side (ADR-0013). For the canonical sawyer embodiment the loader
36. # auto-fills n_dof + vla_action_key from robots/sawyer/robot.yaml.
37. actuators_required:
38.   - kind: "joint_position"
39.     control_mode_semantics:
40.       mode: "absolute"
41. 
42. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43. runtime: "pytorch"
44. quantization:
45.   dtype: "bf16"
46.   backend: "pytorch"
47. # Pin to the HEAD SHA at packaging time for reproducibility (principle 8).
48. weights_uri: "hf://lerobot/smolvla_metaworld"
49. 
50. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
51. processors:
52.   preprocessor_uri: "hf://lerobot/smolvla_metaworld/policy_preprocessor.json"
53.   postprocessor_uri: "hf://lerobot/smolvla_metaworld/policy_postprocessor.json"
54. # MetaWorld uses a single 4-D proprio state (eef_pos + gripper). NO adapter-level
55. # flip: lerobot's MetaworldEnv already corrects the corner2 camera's 180°
56. # inversion at the source (lerobot/envs/metaworld.py — "corner2 outputs images
57. # with both axes inverted ... correct them", `np.flip(image, (0, 1))`), and our
58. # backend passes that already-corrected frame straight through as camera1. An
59. # adapter flip_180 here would DOUBLE-flip → upside-down input → near-random
60. # success (was 0.052 on metaworld_mt50 with flip_180: true). Unlike LIBERO,
61. # whose lerobot env does NOT pre-flip (so those rSkills need flip_180: true).
62. image_preprocessing:
63.   flip_180: false
64. state_contract:
65.   dim: 4
66. 
67. # ── Execution semantics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68. chunk_size: 16
69. # n_action_steps omitted — equals chunk_size, so apply_chunk_replay falls
70. # through to the SmolVLA family default (full chunk).
71. latency_budget:
72.   per_chunk_ms: 150.0   # SmolVLA inference at bf16 on a desktop GPU ≈ 80–120 ms
73. 
74. # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75. # Headline success rate from rskills/smolvla-metaworld/eval/metaworld_mt50.json,
76. # a locally reproduced `openral benchmark run --suite metaworld_mt50` rollout
77. # (50 tasks, 1 episode/seed-0; 16/50 solved → avg 0.30). Raise n_episodes for a
78. # paper-equivalent number.
79. benchmarks:
80.   metaworld_mt50: 0.3
81. 
82. paper_url: "https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.01844"
83. dataset_uri: "hf://lerobot/metaworld_mt50"
84. source_repo: "hf://lerobot/smolvla_metaworld"
85. 
86. description: >
87.   SmolVLA (0.45 B) finetuned on MetaWorld MT50 — 50 manipulation tasks on a
88.   Rethink Sawyer arm (MuJoCo via lerobot). Runs the MT10/MT50 suites
89.   (benchmarks/metaworld_mt{10,50}.yaml) and 5 demo scenes
90.   (scenes/benchmark/metaworld_*.yaml). Locally reproduced on MT50: 16/50
91.   solved at 1 ep/seed-0 (avg 0.30); see eval/metaworld_mt50.json. 4-D
92.   proprio / camera1 contract verified against the checkpoint (no adapter flip —
93.   lerobot's MetaworldEnv already corrects the corner camera's 180° inversion).
94. 
95. # ADR-0022 — action vocabulary surfaced to the reasoner LLM tool
96. # palette so it can pick this skill by what it does (action verb +
97. # object + scene), not just by its slug.
98. actions:
99.   - "generalist"
100.   - "reach"
101.   - "push"
102.   - "pick"
103.   - "place"
104.   - "open"
105.   - "close"
106.   - "insert"
107.   - "slide"
108. objects: []
109. scenes:
110.   - "tabletop"
111. 
112. # ADR-0019 — per-checkpoint action contract (consumed by the dataset bridge
113. # to bind the LeRobot v3 `action` feature shape).
114. action_contract:
115.   dim: 4
116.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>100 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — OpenRAL packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. # Wraps: lerobot/diffusion_pusht (Apache-2.0)
3. # Paper: Chi et al., 2023 — Diffusion Policy.
4. 
5. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6. schema_version: "0.1"
7. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-diffusion-pusht"
8. evaluated_tasks: ["pusht"]  # ADR-0060: benchmark task this checkpoint is validated for (gate)
9. version: "0.1.0"
10. license: "apache-2.0"
11. role: "s1"
12. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
13. 
14. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
15. model_family: "diffusion"
16. 
17. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
18. # 2-D PushT pseudo-robot (single end-effector pushing a T block). Used by
19. # tests/sim/test_pusht_2d_diffusion_pusht.py against gym_pusht/PushT-v0.
20. embodiment_tags:
21.   - "pusht"
22. 
23. # PushT exposes a single 96×96 RGB top-down stream (named
24. # observation.image, not images.cameraN — PushT predates the multi-cam
25. # convention used by SmolVLA/ACT).
26. sensors_required:
27.   - modality: "rgb"
28.     vla_feature_key: "observation.image"
29.     min_width: 96
30.     min_height: 96
31. 
32. # Output side (ADR-0013). The pusht_2d scene-pseudo-robot exposes a 2-D
33. # (x, y) absolute position; robots/pusht_2d/robot.yaml advertises
34. # `cartesian_pose` as its supported control mode (the codebase
35. # convention for the PushT 2-D action regardless of dimensionality).
36. # The loader auto-fills n_dof (2) + vla_action_key from the robot YAML.
37. # PushT actions are absolute (x, y) targets in the world/scene frame.
38. actuators_required:
39.   - kind: "cartesian_pose"
40.     control_mode_semantics:
41.       mode: "absolute"
42.       reference_frame: "world"
43. 
44. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45. runtime: "pytorch"
46. quantization:
47.   dtype: "fp32"
48.   backend: "pytorch"
49. weights_uri: "hf://lerobot/diffusion_pusht"
50. 
51. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
52. processors:
53.   preprocessor_uri: "hf://lerobot/diffusion_pusht/policy_preprocessor.json"
54.   postprocessor_uri: "hf://lerobot/diffusion_pusht/policy_postprocessor.json"
55. # PushT is a 2-DoF planar pushing benchmark; proprio state is 2-D
56. # (x, y) of the end effector.
57. state_contract:
58.   dim: 2
59. 
60. # ── Execution semantics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61. chunk_size: 8
62. # n_action_steps omitted — equals chunk_size (Diffusion Policy default).
63. latency_budget:
64.   # Reference-host measurement (RTX 4070 Laptop, CUDA 12.8, PyTorch 2.10)
65.   # of the warm full-chunk inference is 1756 ms — Diffusion Policy runs
66.   # 100 DDPM denoising steps per chunk, the dominant cost in the suite.
67.   # Pinning per_chunk_ms to 1250 ms with tolerance_pct=100 yields the
68.   # previous 2.5 s ceiling (_WARM_CHUNK_CEILING_S in the sim test).
69.   per_chunk_ms: 1250.0
70. 
71. # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72. # Headline success rate from skills/diffusion-pusht/eval/pusht.json.
73. benchmarks:
74.   pusht: 0.60
75. 
76. paper_url: "https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.04137"
77. source_repo: "hf://lerobot/diffusion_pusht"
78. 
79. description: >
80.   Diffusion Policy (~263M-param U-Net with 100-step DDPM denoiser) for
81.   the PushT 2-DoF pushing benchmark. Action chunks of length 8 within a
82.   horizon of 16. The chunk inference cost is dominated by the denoising
83.   loop, so cached pops are essentially free — this is the extreme test
84.   of the queue-drain contract.
85. 
86. # ADR-0022 — action vocabulary surfaced to the reasoner LLM tool
87. # palette so it can pick this skill by what it does (action verb +
88. # object + scene), not just by its slug.
89. actions:
90.   - "push"
91. objects:
92.   - "t_shape"
93. scenes:
94.   - "tabletop_2d"
95. 
96. # ADR-0019 — per-checkpoint action contract (consumed by the dataset bridge
97. # to bind the LeRobot v3 `action` feature shape).
98. action_contract:
99.   dim: 2
100.
```

</details>

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/scenes/sim/openarm_tabletop.yaml (lines 1-140)

<details>
<summary>56 lines</summary>

```
1. # OpenArm v2 bimanual — tabletop pick-and-place scene.
2. #
3. # Scene layout: LEFT-FIRST action layout, RADIANS units — both
4. # match robots/openarm/robot.yaml, so the env defaults
5. # (action_layout=left_first, joint_units=radians) apply with no
6. # overrides. The default top-camera placement matches the
7. # mddoai dataset POV (between the two robot bases, looking forward +
8. # down at the tabletop).
9. #
10. # Policy travels on the CLI: ``--rskill rskills/<your-skill>``.
11. #
12. # Run::
13. #
14. #     openral sim run --config scenes/sim/openarm_tabletop.yaml \
15. #                 --view
16. 
17. robot_id: "openarm"
18. 
19. scene:
20.   id: openarm_tabletop_pnp
21.   backend: mujoco
22.   observation_height: 480
23.   observation_width: 640
24.   cameras: ["top", "left_wrist", "right_wrist"]
25.   backend_options:
26.     render_width: 640
27.     render_height: 480
28.     max_steps: 500
29.     # action_layout / joint_units default to left_first / radians — correct
30.     # for this checkpoint, so we leave them implicit.
31.     # ADR-0066 — the overview-camera pose lives on the SCENE (was the robot
32.     # manifest's `scene_defaults.top_camera`, removed so robot.yaml describes
33.     # only the robot). The mddoai dataset POV: between the bases, facing the
34.     # tabletop. The openarm_robosuite env feeds these to the composer.
35.     top_camera_pos: [0.20, 0.0, 0.95]
36.     top_camera_target: [0.65, 0.0, 0.05]
37.     top_camera_fovy: 65.0
38. 
39. task:
40.   id: openarm/pnp_cube_to_drawer
41.   scene_id: openarm_tabletop_pnp
42.   instruction: "pick the red cube and place it in the drawer"
43.   max_steps: 500
44. 
45. # ADR-0002 Amendment 3 — mandatory mounting pose for the
46. # openarm_tabletop_pnp scene. Lift bases above the table top + shift
47. # forward into the workspace so the 0.6 m arm reach can sweep the
48. # picking targets (mirrors the openral deploy sim HAL params for openarm).
49. base_pose:
50.   xyz: [0.20, 0.0, 0.55]
51.   quat_xyzw: [0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0]
52.   frame_id: world
53. 
54. seed: 0
55. n_episodes: 1
56.
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/scenes/sim/so101_tube_insertion.yaml.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/scenes/sim/so101_tube_insertion.yaml (lines 1-140)

<details>
<summary>136 lines</summary>

```
1. # SO-101 in a 100×61.5×75 cm tabletop box, with the tube-insertion task.
2. #
3. # Scene layout (top view; +X is the long edge, +Y is the depth, robot
4. # sits against the back wall at high Y, OAK-D Pro on the ceiling
5. # centred above the workspace):
6. #
7. #     +Y (depth = 61.5 cm)
8. #     ↑
9. #     |          [SO-101]  (base at (0.50, 0.50, 0))
10. #     |        ┌────────┐
11. #     |        │ workspace│
12. #     |        │          │      ▽ OAK-D Pro overhead at (0.50, 0.3075, 0.749)
13. #     |        │  tube    │        looking straight down
14. #     |        │ block    │
15. #     |        └──────────┘
16. #     +---------------------> +X (width = 1.00 m)
17. #
18. # Both the slotted block and the cylindrical tube spawn at random
19. # (x, y) within ``{tube,block}_spawn_xy_range`` each ``reset()``, lying
20. # flat on the floor with a random yaw. Success = tube inserted
21. # vertically into the block hole within the configured tolerances.
22. #
23. # Policy travels on the CLI: ``--rskill rskills/<your-skill>``.
24. #
25. # Image-key aliasing: the scene emits the observation dict
26. #     {"images": {"oak_top": ..., "oak_top_depth": ..., "wrist": ...}, ...}
27. # The RGB streams are keyed by their bare MuJoCo camera names
28. # (`oak_top`, `wrist`) so they line up with `scene.cameras` below —
29. # that's what the policy adapter's `resolve_camera_keys` pulls from the
30. # observation. Pair this with an rSkill that maps those scene camera
31. # keys onto the checkpoint's own image-feature suffixes via
32. # `image_preprocessing.aliases` (direction is `<scene-cam-key>:
33. # <model-feature-suffix>`):
34. #     image_preprocessing:
35. #       aliases:
36. #         oak_top: top      # → observation.images.top
37. #         wrist: wrist      # → observation.images.wrist
38. # e.g. a checkpoint that expects `top` / `wrist` / `front`; the absent `front`
39. # camera is zero-padded via the image mask. The
40. # depth stream is exposed as `oak_top_depth` (HxW float32 metres) for
41. # skills with a depth-aware tower; depth-blind skills can ignore it.
42. 
43. scene:
44.   id: so101_box                  # @SCENES.register("so101_box", fixed_robot="so101_follower")
45.   backend: mujoco
46.   observation_height: 480
47.   observation_width: 640
48.   cameras: ["oak_top", "wrist"]
49.   backend_options:
50.     # Proprio state + action units. LeRobot-trained SO-100/101 checkpoints
51.     # (for example MolmoAct2-SO100_101) were recorded in degrees; the env
52.     # converts MuJoCo-native radians ↔ degrees at the policy boundary so the
53.     # checkpoint's norm_stats line up. Use "radians" for a sim-native policy.
54.     joint_units: degrees
55. 
56.     # Per-joint calibration affine: lerobot_deg = sign*mujoco_deg + offset.
57.     # The MuJoCo so101_new_calib zero is not the LeRobot calibration zero
58.     # (most visibly shoulder_lift/elbow_flex sit ~120° from URDF zero in the
59.     # MolmoAct2-SO100_101 data). These offsets are that checkpoint's per-joint
60.     # state-median (shoulder_pan, shoulder_lift, elbow_flex, wrist_flex,
61.     # wrist_roll, gripper); they align the sim home with the training median
62.     # AND map the model's q01–q99 operating range into the reachable joint
63.     # limits. Signs are +1 (no joint inversion observed). Estimated from
64.     # norm_stats.json (mixture calibration) — refine if you have the dataset's
65.     # exact homing offsets. Drop these (identity) for a different checkpoint.
66.     joint_offsets_deg: [3.07, 123.16, 124.40, 57.89, -11.04, 9.24]
67.     joint_signs: [1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1]
68. 
69.     # Arena (metres). 1.00 × 0.615 × 0.75 m matches the user-supplied
70.     # sketch (100 × 61.5 × 75 cm).
71.     box_size_xyz: [1.00, 0.615, 0.75]
72.     wall_thickness: 0.010
73. 
74.     # Robot mount: SO-101 base sits at back-centre of the floor.
75.     robot_base_xyz: [0.50, 0.50, 0.00]
76.     robot_base_yaw_deg: 0.0
77. 
78.     # Wrist camera — parented to the gripper body, mounted just above the
79.     # fingertips and aimed along the gripper APPROACH axis (gripper-local -X
80.     # = world-down at home) so the jaw + the object being grasped stay in
81.     # frame at every pose. (The old [0,-0.03,0]→[0,-0.10,-0.05] aim pointed
82.     # sideways into a box wall — a blank dark view.)
83.     wrist_camera_pos_local: [0.03, -0.02, -0.06]
84.     wrist_camera_target_local: [-0.25, -0.02, -0.06]
85.     wrist_camera_fovy: 75.0
86. 
87.     # OAK-D Pro overhead — centred horizontally over the box,
88.     # mounted at the ceiling (z = box_height - 1 mm), looking down.
89.     oak_top_camera_pos: [0.50, 0.3075, 0.749]
90.     oak_top_camera_target: [0.50, 0.3075, 0.0]
91.     oak_top_camera_fovy: 54.0
92. 
93.     # Slotted block — 44.5 × 44.5 × 20 mm with a Ø23 mm hole + a 5 mm
94.     # slot connecting the hole to one edge.
95.     slot_block_size: [0.0445, 0.0445, 0.020]
96.     slot_block_hole_diameter: 0.023
97.     slot_block_slot_width: 0.005
98.     slot_block_mass: 0.05
99. 
100.     # Tube — Ø21.9 mm × 90 mm long, lying on its side at reset.
101.     # Hole is Ø23 mm → 0.55 mm radial clearance (1.1 mm diametric);
102.     # tight enough that the insertion is a real fit-test, loose enough
103.     # to be physically achievable without exact axis alignment.
104.     tube_radius: 0.01095
105.     tube_length: 0.090
106.     tube_mass: 0.020
107. 
108.     # Random spawn ranges (world frame). Both objects start lying on
109.     # the floor at a random (x, y) within their range and a random
110.     # yaw. The robot's reachable workspace radius is ~25 cm so we
111.     # keep the spawn in front of the base.
112.     block_spawn_xy_range: [[0.25, 0.75], [0.10, 0.40]]
113.     tube_spawn_xy_range:  [[0.25, 0.75], [0.10, 0.40]]
114.     spawn_min_separation: 0.10
115. 
116.     # Success thresholds — see :class:`BoxSceneOptions` docstring.
117.     # XY tolerance is 3 mm; physical fit is ±0.55 mm at the inscribed
118.     # square's midpoints. The depth check enforces real insertion.
119.     insertion_depth_m: 0.010
120.     insertion_axis_tol_deg: 10.0
121.     insertion_xy_tol_m: 0.003
122. 
123. task:
124.   id: so101_box/tube_insertion
125.   scene_id: so101_box
126.   instruction: "insert the orange tube into the slotted block"
127.   max_steps: 500
128.   success_key: is_success
129. 
130. seed: 42
131. n_episodes: 1
132. record_video: false
133. 
134. metadata:
135.   honest_scope: "single-episode result; success criterion is geometric (tube vertical and inserted ≥ 10 mm into the hole)"
136.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 33m 55s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/rskill-moveit-joints/rskill.yaml.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/rskill-moveit-joints/rskill.yaml (lines 1-140)

<details>
<summary>129 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — moveit-joints: plan to a joint configuration (ADR-0024/0052)
2. #
3. # Wraps `moveit_msgs/action/MoveGroup`. `ros_integration.goal_builder: joint`
4. # selects the `JointGoalRskill` adapter, which consumes a `joint` block
5. # (`joint_names` + `positions`) and lowers it into a MoveGroup
6. # `joint_constraints` goal — the clean, LLM-facing form (positions, not
7. # hand-written constraint dicts). Renamed from `openral/rskill-moveit-plan-arm`
8. # under ADR-0054 (the `rskill-moveit-*` family: -joints / -eef-pose / -look-at
9. # over one ROSActionRskill engine).
10. #
11. # Collision posture: MoveIt's internal FCL planner does self + planning-scene
12. # (world) collision checking at plan time. `plan_only: true` so OpenRAL's
13. # per-waypoint replay through /openral/candidate_action stays the only
14. # actuation path (the safety kernel checks every step); move_group never drives
15. # its own controllers. `chunk_size: 1` so the supervisor sees every waypoint.
16. 
17. schema_version: "0.1"
18. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-moveit-joints"
19. version: "0.1.0"
20. license: "apache-2.0"
21. role: "s1"
22. kind: "ros_action"
23. 
24. # Every arm embodiment OpenRAL ships a robot.yaml for. Whether a deployment can
25. # resolve this skill depends on whether a matching MoveIt config is running and
26. # whether the `joint` block below is correct for that robot's planning group.
27. # The Franka `panda_arm` group is the canonical example; for other embodiments
28. # copy this manifest and edit `joint.joint_names` + `request.group_name`.
29. embodiment_tags:
30.   - "franka_panda"
31.   - "ur5e"
32.   - "ur10e"
33.   - "so100_follower"
34.   - "openarm"
35.   - "rizon4"
36.   - "sawyer"
37.   - "widowx"
38. 
39. actuators_required:
40.   - kind: "joint_position"
41.     control_mode_semantics:
42.       mode: "absolute"
43. 
44. # REQUIRED for kind: ros_action — one waypoint per Action chunk so the safety
45. # supervisor's per-row envelope check sees every commanded position.
46. chunk_size: 1
47. 
48. # Planning is slow on the first call (MoveIt typically 0.5–2 s on a clean
49. # scene); subsequent step() calls just dequeue cached waypoints.
50. latency_budget:
51.   per_chunk_ms: 2000.0
52. 
53. # ADR-0022 — surfaced to the Reasoner LLM tool palette.
54. description: >
55.   Plan and execute a collision-free motion to a target JOINT configuration via
56.   MoveIt's MoveGroup (self + planning-scene collision checked). Provide one
57.   target angle per planning-group joint. Defaults target the Franka Panda home
58.   pose; override the manifest to retarget. Use when you have a joint-space goal
59.   (e.g. a policy's in-distribution starting pose); for a Cartesian end-effector
60.   target use rskill-moveit-eef-pose instead.
61. actions:
62.   - "reach"
63. objects: []
64. scenes: []
65. 
66. # Provenance — ROS-wrapped rSkill; paper_url carries the upstream link.
67. paper_url: "https://moveit.picknik.ai/"
68. 
69. ros_integration:
70.   package: "moveit_msgs"
71.   interface_type: "MoveGroup"
72.   interface_name: "/move_action"
73.   # `MoveGroup.Result.planned_trajectory.joint_trajectory` is reordered into the
74.   # host RobotDescription.joints order before per-waypoint chunk emission.
75.   result_trajectory_field: "planned_trajectory.joint_trajectory"
76.   # ADR-0054 — selects the JointGoalRskill goal-lowering adapter: the `joint`
77.   # block below (joint_names + positions) is lowered into
78.   # `request.goal_constraints[0].joint_constraints` at configure time.
79.   goal_builder: "joint"
80.   # Franka Panda home pose (panda_arm group). For other embodiments override
81.   # `joint.joint_names` + `request.group_name`.
82.   default_goal_json: |
83.     {
84.       "joint": {
85.         "joint_names": ["panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3", "panda_joint4", "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7"],
86.         "positions": [0.0, -0.785, 0.0, -2.356, 0.0, 1.571, 0.785],
87.         "position_tolerance_rad": 0.001
88.       },
89.       "request": {
90.         "group_name": "panda_arm",
91.         "num_planning_attempts": 5,
92.         "allowed_planning_time": 5.0,
93.         "max_velocity_scaling_factor": 0.3,
94.         "max_acceleration_scaling_factor": 0.3,
95.         "goal_constraints": []
96.       },
97.       "planning_options": {
98.         "plan_only": true,
99.         "replan": false
100.       }
101.     }
102.   ros_dependencies:
103.     - "ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-moveit"
104.     - "ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-moveit-resources-panda-moveit-config"
105. 
106. # ADR-0026 — per-skill JSON Schema surfaced to the Reasoner LLM tool palette.
107. # The LLM overrides only `joint.positions` (one per planning-group joint, in
108. # the manifest's joint_names order); planner / tolerance defaults are inherited.
109. goal_params_schema:
110.   type: object
111.   description: >
112.     Target joint positions for MoveIt's MoveGroup against the planning group.
113.     Provide one entry per joint in the manifest's joint_names order.
114.   properties:
115.     joint:
116.       type: object
117.       properties:
118.         positions:
119.           type: array
120.           items:
121.             type: number
122.           minItems: 7
123.           maxItems: 7
124.           description: "Target joint angle (rad) per planning-group joint."
125.       required:
126.         - positions
127.   required:
128.     - joint
129.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 33m 55s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4/rskill.yaml.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4/rskill.yaml (lines 1-120)

<details>
<summary>120 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — OpenRAL packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. #
3. # Pre-quantized nf4 HF-Hub mirror of the local-converted
4. # `robocasa/robocasa365_checkpoints/pi05_pretrain_human300` checkpoint.
5. # Produced by:
6. #
7. #   HF_TOKEN=<write-token> uv run python tools/quantize_rskill.py \
8. #       --source outputs/run_artifacts/r365_pi05_ckpt_lerobot \
9. #       --target OpenRAL/rskill-pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4 \
10. #       --policy-class lerobot.policies.pi05.modeling_pi05.PI05Policy
11. #
12. # The HF-backed variant ships the *already-packed* nf4 state dict plus
13. # a `quantization_metadata.json` sentinel. The pi05 adapter detects the
14. # sentinel, meta-initialises the policy graph (~14 s instead of the
15. # ~137 s `from_pretrained` walk), overlays the prequant state via
16. # `install_prequantized_linears`, and skips the bf16->nf4 conversion
17. # entirely. Warm-up drops to ~20 s on a 4070-mobile.
18. 
19. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20. schema_version: "0.1"
21. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4"
22. version: "0.1.0"
23. license: "apache-2.0"
24. role: "s1"
25. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
26. 
27. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28. model_family: "pi05"
29. 
30. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
31. embodiment_tags:
32.   - "panda_mobile"
33. 
34. sensors_required:
35.   - modality: "rgb"
36.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera1"
37.     min_width: 128
38.     min_height: 128
39.   - modality: "rgb"
40.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera2"
41.     min_width: 128
42.     min_height: 128
43. 
44. # Output side (ADR-0013). For the canonical panda_mobile embodiment the
45. # loader auto-fills n_dof + vla_action_key from robots/panda_mobile/robot.yaml.
46. # RoboCasa365 emits per-step deltas on the Franka mobile arm, not absolute targets.
47. actuators_required:
48.   - kind: "joint_position"
49.     control_mode_semantics:
50.       mode: "delta"
51. 
52. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
53. runtime: "pytorch"
54. quantization:
55.   # ``int4`` is the OpenRAL `QuantizationDtype` enum value that
56.   # matches bitsandbytes nf4. The exact bnb scheme (nf4 + compute_dtype
57.   # bf16, applied to every Linear with >=4M weight elements) is recorded
58.   # in the upstream `quantization_metadata.json` sentinel that the pi05
59.   # adapter probes via `detect_prequantized_nf4`.
60.   dtype: "int4"
61.   backend: "pytorch"
62. weights_uri: "hf://OpenRAL/rskill-pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4"
63. 
64. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
65. processors:
66.   preprocessor_uri: "hf://OpenRAL/rskill-pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4/policy_preprocessor.json"
67.   postprocessor_uri: "hf://OpenRAL/rskill-pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4/policy_postprocessor.json"
68. # The RoboCasa365 Human-300 finetune wants the canonical openpi-robocasa
69. # preprocessing: vertical-flip BEFORE policy ingestion (mirrors
70. # RoboCasaGymEnv.process_img); the three robosuite camera streams alias
71. # into camera1/camera2/camera3.
72. image_preprocessing:
73.   flip_180: false
74.   flip_vertical: true
75.   aliases:
76.     camera1: "robot0_agentview_left_image"
77.     camera2: "robot0_agentview_right_image"
78.     camera3: "robot0_eye_in_hand_image"
79. state_contract:
80.   layout: "human300_16d"
81.   dim: 16
82.   # ADR-0027 — per-robot source bindings the layout-adapter registry
83.   # consumes to assemble the 16-D vector at runtime. The values below
84.   # match panda_mobile's URDF (the canonical target). Other planar-base
85.   # Franka deployments inherit unchanged; override per-robot only if
86.   # the URDF renames panda_hand or panda_finger_joint{1,2}.
87.   bindings:
88.     # See rldx1-ft-rc365-nf4/rskill.yaml: ``panda_hand_tcp`` matches
89.     # robosuite's ``gripper0_right_grip_site`` (the eef site the policy
90.     # was trained on) within ~6 mm, whereas ``panda_hand`` is the wrist
91.     # body ~9 cm above the grasp point.
92.     eef_frame: "panda_hand_tcp"
93.     base_frame: "base_link"
94.     # See ``rldx1-ft-rc365-nf4/rskill.yaml`` for the rationale —
95.     # ``odom`` is the HAL-published frame mirroring MJCF base qpos
96.     # in deploy_sim and wheel-odometry on real hardware; ``map``
97.     # routes through SLAM which is non-authoritative on a fresh
98.     # sim boot and was producing state ~14 m off ground truth.
99.     world_frame: "odom"
100.     # openral's panda_mobile robot.yaml exposes the franka gripper as
101.     # one parallel_gripper joint (per ADR-0028a, the MJCF two-finger
102.     # mimic is collapsed to a single 1-DoF abstraction). The
103.     # ``human300_16d`` assembler mirrors a single binding to ``[v, -v]``
104.     # to reconstruct the 2-D training-distribution shape.
105.     gripper_qpos_joints: ["panda_gripper"]
106.     quaternion_convention: "xyzw"
107. 
108. # ── Execution semantics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109. chunk_size: 50
110. # n_action_steps omitted — equals chunk_size (full chunk replay).
111. latency_budget:
112.   per_chunk_ms: 5000.0
113. 
114. # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115. # No published benchmark numbers yet for this prequant pack; leave empty.
116. 
117. source_repo: "hf://OpenRAL/rskill-pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4"
118. 
119. description: >
120.   Pre-quantized nf4 mirror of the openpi-converted
```

</details>

│                                                          open-vocab locator  │
│                                                          to bring up         │

<sub>⏱️ 37m 14s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Build ROS workspace packages**

$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep && just ros2-build

<details>

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/cpp/openral_safety_kernel/package.xml (lines 1-160)

<details>
<summary>58 lines</summary>

```
1. <?xml version="1.0"?>
2. <?xml-model href="http://download.ros.org/schema/package_format3.xsd" schematypens="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"?>
3. <package format="3">
4.   <name>openral_safety_kernel</name>
5.   <version>0.1.0</version>
6.   <description>
7.     ADR-0020 — C++ safety kernel. Separate process owning the chunk-rate
8.     safety boundary on the OpenRAL ROS 2 graph. Replaces F5's Python
9.     pass-through (packages/openral_safety/SafetyPassthroughNode) behind
10.     the same topic contract (CLAUDE.md §1.5: "Python proposes, C++
11.     disposes").
12. 
13.     Subscribes /openral/candidate_action, validates against the
14.     intersection of the robot ceiling (RobotDescription.safety) and the
15.     optional rSkill envelope, republishes the chunk verbatim on
16.     /openral/safe_action when validated, or drops + publishes
17.     FailureTrigger on /openral/failure/safety and /openral/estop on
18.     violation. Recovery via /openral/estop_reset
19.     (std_srvs/Trigger). Defense-in-depth: subscribes to /openral/estop
20.     itself so external estop sources latch the kernel.
21. 
22.     Real-time contract: validator allocation-free; SCHED_FIFO best-
23.     effort; LTTng tracepoints openral:safety_check_{begin,end}
24.     correlated with the active OTel trace_id (PR #131 / F9).
25.   </description>
26. 
27.   <maintainer email="contact@openral.dev">OpenRAL Contributors</maintainer>
28.   <license>Apache-2.0</license>
29. 
30.   <buildtool_depend>ament_cmake</buildtool_depend>
31. 
32.   <depend>rclcpp</depend>
33.   <depend>rclcpp_lifecycle</depend>
34.   <depend>lifecycle_msgs</depend>
35.   <depend>std_msgs</depend>
36.   <depend>std_srvs</depend>
37.   <depend>diagnostic_msgs</depend>
38.   <depend>openral_msgs</depend>
39.   <depend>nlohmann-json-dev</depend>
40.   <!-- ADR-0020 PR-F — opentelemetry-cpp for safety.check span emission.
41.        Provided by the in-tree vendor package (cpp/opentelemetry_cpp_vendor)
42.        since Noble has no apt opentelemetry-cpp. -->
43.   <depend>opentelemetry_cpp_vendor</depend>
44. 
45.   <test_depend>ament_cmake_gtest</test_depend>
46.   <!-- ament_lint_auto / ament_lint_common are intentionally NOT declared
47.        — see the matching comment in CMakeLists.txt's BUILD_TESTING block.
48.        The workspace runs ruff + clang-tidy + mypy via `just lint`; the
49.        ament defaults disagree with that style and produce ~259 spurious
50.        failures in `just ros2-test` that no other package enforces. The
51.        Python launch tests under test/launch/ are exercised via
52.        `uv run pytest`, same as openral_safety's test_supervisor_node.py. -->
53. 
54.   <export>
55.     <build_type>ament_cmake</build_type>
56.   </export>
57. </package>
58.
```

</details>

1. <?xml version="1.0"?>
2. <?xml-model href="http://download.ros.org/schema/package_format3.xsd" schematypens="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"?>
3. <package format="3">
4.   <name>openral_rskill_ros</name>
5.   <version>0.1.0</version>
6.   <description>
7.     ADR-0018 F1 — rskill_runner_node lifecycle node that owns the
8.     openral_msgs/ExecuteRskill action server and the in-process
9.     HardwareRunner. Composes one shared openral_world_state.WorldStateAggregator
10.     with the world_state lifecycle node (single subscriber of /joint_states
11.     per ADR-0018 §3) and publishes openral_msgs/ActionChunk on
12.     /openral/candidate_action via openral_runner.ROSPublishingHAL. One node
13.     per robot.
14.   </description>
15.   <maintainer email="contact@openral.dev">OpenRAL Contributors</maintainer>
16.   <license>Apache-2.0</license>
17. 
18.   <buildtool_depend>ament_cmake</buildtool_depend>
19.   <buildtool_depend>ament_cmake_python</buildtool_depend>
20. 
21.   <depend>rclpy</depend>
22.   <depend>rclpy_lifecycle</depend>
23.   <depend>lifecycle_msgs</depend>
24.   <depend>sensor_msgs</depend>
25.   <depend>std_msgs</depend>
26.   <depend>std_srvs</depend>
27.   <depend>diagnostic_msgs</depend>
28.   <depend>openral_msgs</depend>
29. 
30.   <exec_depend>python3-openral-core</exec_depend>
31.   <exec_depend>python3-openral-world-state</exec_depend>
32.   <exec_depend>python3-openral-rskill</exec_depend>
33.   <exec_depend>python3-openral-runner</exec_depend>
34.   <exec_depend>python3-openral-observability</exec_depend>
35.   <exec_depend>python3-openral-hal</exec_depend>
36.   <!-- ADR-0059 — the read-only Foxglove live-scene bridge, spawned into the
37.        deploy-sim runtime graph by the `openral deploy sim` foxglove flag. -->
38.   <exec_depend>foxglove_bridge</exec_depend>
39.   <exec_depend>openral_foxglove_bringup</exec_depend>
40. 
41.   <test_depend>ament_copyright</test_depend>
42.   <test_depend>ament_flake8</test_depend>
43.   <test_depend>ament_pep257</test_depend>
44.   <test_depend>python3-pytest</test_depend>
45. 
46.   <export>
47.     <build_type>ament_cmake</build_type>
48.   </export>
49. </package>
50.

1. <?xml version="1.0"?>
2. <?xml-model href="http://download.ros.org/schema/package_format3.xsd" schematypens="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"?>
3. <package format="3">
4.   <name>openral_safety</name>
5.   <version>0.1.0</version>
6.   <description>
7.     OpenRAL safety supervisor — ROS 2 managed lifecycle node skeleton.
8. 
9.     Skeleton only: declares the lifecycle node and the package layout the
10.     real C++ safety kernel (cpp/openral_safety_kernel/, planned) drops
11.     into. Carries no enforcement logic; PRs that add enforcement require

<sub>⏱️ 43m 6s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/cpp/opentelemetry_cpp_vendor/CMakeLists.txt.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/cpp/opentelemetry_cpp_vendor/CMakeLists.txt (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>100 lines</summary>

```
1. cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
2. project(opentelemetry_cpp_vendor)
3. 
4. # ROS 2 vendor package for opentelemetry-cpp.
5. #
6. # Ubuntu 24.04 (Noble) does not ship opentelemetry-cpp in the default
7. # apt repos, and we need at least the trace API + OTLP HTTP exporter
8. # to close ADR-0020 §9 (PR-F). Following the conventional ROS 2 pattern
9. # (cf. nlohmann_json_vendor, fastcdr_vendor), this package fetches and
10. # builds opentelemetry-cpp at colcon build time, then re-exports its
11. # CMake config to dependent packages.
12. #
13. # Dependents do NOT call ExternalProject_Add — they call
14. # ``find_package(opentelemetry-cpp CONFIG REQUIRED)`` after this package
15. # has built and installed its artefacts to the colcon prefix.
16. 
17. find_package(ament_cmake REQUIRED)
18. 
19. include(ExternalProject)
20. 
21. # Pinned release; bump deliberately. Match the latest stable as of the
22. # ADR-0020 PR-F landing (v1.16.x line — first OTLP HTTP series the
23. # CMake config exports without protobuf-compile contortions on GCC 13).
24. set(OTEL_CPP_VERSION "1.16.1")
25. set(OTEL_CPP_GIT_TAG "v${OTEL_CPP_VERSION}")
26. 
27. # Build only what we use. The safety kernel emits TRACES over OTLP/HTTP;
28. # we explicitly disable gRPC (extra abseil + protoc dep), metrics, zPages,
29. # OTLP-File, and tests/examples to keep the build under 90 s on a
30. # 4-core host. Dependents that need metrics/logs over OTLP would extend
31. # this package; for the safety kernel they go through the ROS topic
32. # /openral/failure/safety + /diagnostics instead.
33. #
34. # Note on CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.10:
35. # opentelemetry-cpp 1.16.1's top-level CMakeLists declares
36. # ``cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.8)`` which CMake >= 3.27 now warns is
37. # below the 3.10 deprecation floor. We don't control upstream's pin, so
38. # bump the effective policy floor at our ExternalProject boundary —
39. # silences the deprecation noise without disabling other warnings.
40. # WITH_DEPRECATED_SDK_FACTORY=OFF: upstream 1.16.x defaults it ON and
41. # emits a stderr warning unless explicitly disabled; we don't use the
42. # deprecated factory path, so opt out and drop the warning.
43. # WITH_LOGS_PREVIEW and WITH_JAEGER were removed upstream (logs signal is
44. # stable; Jaeger exporter deprecated in 1.13.x). Passing them now triggers
45. # "Manually-specified variables were not used by the project" warnings.
46. set(OTEL_CPP_CMAKE_ARGS
47.   -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=<INSTALL_DIR>
48.   -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
49.   -DCMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.10
50.   -DCMAKE_POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE=ON
51.   -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON
52.   -DBUILD_TESTING=OFF
53.   -DWITH_EXAMPLES=OFF
54.   -DWITH_FUNC_TESTS=OFF
55.   -DWITH_BENCHMARK=OFF
56.   -DWITH_OTLP_HTTP=ON
57.   -DWITH_OTLP_GRPC=OFF
58.   -DWITH_OTLP_FILE=OFF
59.   -DWITH_ZIPKIN=OFF
60.   -DWITH_ELASTICSEARCH=OFF
61.   -DWITH_PROMETHEUS=OFF
62.   -DWITH_DEPRECATED_SDK_FACTORY=OFF
63.   -DWITH_ABSEIL=OFF
64.   -DCMAKE_CXX_STANDARD=17
65. )
66. 
67. ExternalProject_Add(opentelemetry_cpp_external
68.   GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-cpp.git
69.   GIT_TAG ${OTEL_CPP_GIT_TAG}
70.   GIT_SHALLOW ON
71.   GIT_SUBMODULES_RECURSE ON
72.   CMAKE_ARGS ${OTEL_CPP_CMAKE_ARGS}
73.   # Install into the colcon prefix so find_package locates the config.
74.   INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}
75.   # Route each step's output to a log file under the build tree instead
76.   # of leaking through colcon as stderr. The git clone step in particular
77.   # writes "Cloning into ..." / "Submodule 'x' registered" lines to
78.   # stderr by design, which colcon flags as "1 package had stderr
79.   # output" even though nothing went wrong. Logs are still available at
80.   # ``build/opentelemetry_cpp_vendor/opentelemetry_cpp_external-prefix/
81.   # src/opentelemetry_cpp_external-stamp/*.log`` for debugging.
82.   LOG_DOWNLOAD ON
83.   LOG_UPDATE ON
84.   LOG_CONFIGURE ON
85.   LOG_BUILD ON
86.   LOG_INSTALL ON
87.   LOG_OUTPUT_ON_FAILURE ON
88.   BUILD_BYPRODUCTS
89.     "${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}/lib/libopentelemetry_trace.so"
90.     "${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}/lib/libopentelemetry_common.so"
91.     "${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}/lib/libopentelemetry_resources.so"
92.     "${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}/lib/libopentelemetry_exporter_otlp_http.so"
93. )
94. 
95. # Re-export the find_package result. Dependents add a build-time
96. # dependency on this package via package.xml; ament_cmake then prepends
97. # our INSTALL_PREFIX to CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH so the line below in their
98. # CMakeLists resolves to the artefacts we just built.
99. ament_package()
100.
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tests/unit/test_cli_sim_run.py.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tests/unit/test_cli_sim_run.py (lines 1-180)

<details>
<summary>145 lines</summary>

```
1. """Unit tests for the `openral sim` subcommand surface.
2. 
3. Confirms four things:
4. 
5. 1. ``openral sim run`` is mounted on the main ``openral`` Typer app and reachable
6.    via ``typer.testing.CliRunner``.
7. 2. ``openral sim list`` (the dedicated registry-printer that replaced the
8.    legacy ``openral sim run --list`` flag) prints the three sim registries.
9. 3. ``openral sim run --help`` exposes the rollout flag set without listing
10.    ``--list`` (which has moved to its own subcommand).
11. 4. End-to-end smoke: ``openral sim run --robot pusht_2d --scene pusht --rskill placeholder``
12.    runs the real pusht adapter for a few steps without an HF Hub
13.    lookup (the mock-VLA-placeholder bypass introduced in commit
14.    ``fix(eval): allow mock VLAs to skip rSkill manifest load``).
15. 
16. A fourth test verifies that importing ``openral_cli.main`` does not
17. transitively pull in heavyweight sim dependencies (``torch``, ``mujoco``,
18. ``gymnasium``) — the eval adapters defer those imports until ``_run()``
19. is called, and we don't want `openral doctor` startup to pay for them.
20. """
21. 
22. from __future__ import annotations
23. 
24. import subprocess
25. import sys
26. 
27. from openral_cli.main import app
28. from typer.testing import CliRunner
29. 
30. runner = CliRunner()
31. 
32. 
33. def test_bh_sim_list_prints_example_configs() -> None:
34.     """`openral sim list` prints every ``scenes/**/*.yaml`` as paste-able paths."""
35.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["sim", "list"])
36.     assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
37.     # Each line is a path to an example sim config.
38.     assert "scenes/" in result.output
39.     # Spot-check known configs.
40.     assert "pusht.yaml" in result.output
41.     assert "libero_spatial.yaml" in result.output
42.     # rSkill URIs no longer live here — they moved to `openral rskill list`.
43.     assert "rskill://" not in result.output
44. 
45. 
46. def test_bh_sim_run_help_shows_flags() -> None:
47.     """`openral sim run --help` surfaces the rollout flag set, not the root `openral` help."""
48.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["sim", "run", "--help"])
49.     assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
50.     for flag in ("--config", "--rskill", "--robot", "--task", "--no-view"):
51.         assert flag in result.output, f"flag {flag!r} missing from `openral sim run --help`"
52.     # Legacy free-flag composition (--scene / --vla) was removed in the
53.     # `feat(core,sim): SceneEnvironment + openral sim run --rskill, no legacy` commit.
54.     assert "--scene" not in result.output
55.     assert "--vla " not in result.output
56. 
57. 
58. def test_bh_sim_run_help_omits_list_flag() -> None:
59.     """`openral sim run --help` must not list ``--list`` (it moved to ``openral sim list``)."""
60.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["sim", "run", "--help"])
61.     assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
62.     assert "--list" not in result.output
63. 
64. 
65. def test_bh_sim_run_rejects_list_flag() -> None:
66.     """`openral sim run --list` was removed; users must call `openral sim list` instead."""
67.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["sim", "run", "--list"])
68.     assert result.exit_code != 0
69.     assert "no such option" in result.output.lower()
70. 
71. 
72. def test_bh_sim_help_lists_subcommands() -> None:
73.     """`openral sim --help` advertises both `run` and `list` as subcommands."""
74.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["sim", "--help"])
75.     assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
76.     assert "run" in result.output
77.     assert "list" in result.output
78. 
79. 
80. def test_bh_sim_run_rejects_record_video_flag() -> None:
81.     """`--record-video` was removed; `--save-video` is the single video entry point."""
82.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["sim", "run", "--record-video"])
83.     # Click prints "No such option: --record-video" and exits 2 on unknown flags.
84.     assert result.exit_code != 0
85.     assert "no such option" in result.output.lower()
86. 
87. 
88. def test_bh_sim_run_help_omits_record_video_flag() -> None:
89.     """Make sure `--record-video` no longer appears in the help text."""
90.     result = runner.invoke(app, ["sim", "run", "--help"])
91.     assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
92.     assert "--record-video" not in result.output
93. 
94. 
95. def test_bh_sim_run_legacy_scene_flag_rejected() -> None:
96.     """The legacy ``--scene/--vla`` free-flag form was removed.
97. 
98.     The canonical invocation is ``openral sim run --config FILE.yaml --rskill
99.     rskills/<id>``. Passing ``--scene`` now surfaces as Click's
100.     "no such option" error.
101.     """
102.     result = runner.invoke(
103.         app,
104.         [
105.             "sim",
106.             "run",
107.             "--scene",
108.             "pusht",
109.             "--task",
110.             "pusht/0",
111.         ],
112.     )
113.     assert result.exit_code != 0
114.     assert "no such option" in result.output.lower()
115. 
116. 
117. def test_bh_cli_import_is_light() -> None:
118.     """Importing `openral_cli.main` must not transitively load torch / mujoco / gym.
119. 
120.     The eval registry adapters defer those imports until `_run()` is
121.     invoked. A regression here would make every `openral doctor` invocation
122.     pay for ~1 GB of CUDA libraries.
123. 
124.     We spawn a fresh Python subprocess so the parent test interpreter's
125.     pre-loaded modules don't pollute the check.
126.     """
127.     code = (
128.         "import sys, openral_cli.main; "
129.         "heavy = {m for m in ('torch', 'mujoco', 'gymnasium', 'lerobot', 'mujoco_py') "
130.         "  if m in sys.modules}; "
131.         "print(','.join(sorted(heavy)))"
132.     )
133.     proc = subprocess.run(
134.         [sys.executable, "-c", code],
135.         capture_output=True,
136.         text=True,
137.         check=True,
138.     )
139.     loaded = proc.stdout.strip()
140.     assert loaded == "", (
141.         f"`openral_cli.main` import pulled in heavyweight sim modules: {loaded!r}.\n"
142.         "Eval adapters must keep their torch / mujoco / gym imports inside "
143.         "`_run()` and registered factories, not at module top level."
144.     )
145.
```

</details>

101. class TestDiffusionPushTManifest:
102.     """Manifest contract — declared inputs match the PushT embodiment."""
103. 
104.     def test_manifest_loads_and_declares_pusht(self, skill_manifest) -> None:
105.         m = skill_manifest.manifest
106.         assert m.name == "OpenRAL/rskill-diffusion-pusht"
107.         assert "pusht" in m.embodiment_tags
108.         assert m.role == "s1"
109. 
110.     def test_manifest_declares_single_rgb_sensor(self, skill_manifest) -> None:
111.         sensors = skill_manifest.manifest.sensors_required
112.         assert len(sensors) == 1
113.         assert sensors[0].modality == "rgb"
114.         assert sensors[0].vla_feature_key == "observation.image"
115. 
116.     def test_manifest_has_latency_budget(self, skill_manifest) -> None:
117.         budget = skill_manifest.manifest.latency_budget
118.         assert budget is not None
119.         assert budget.per_chunk_ms > 0
120. 
121. 
122. class TestDiffusionPushTIOContract:
123.     """Adapter IO shapes — verified through the canonical eval factory."""
124. 
125.     def test_make_policy_has_pusht_io_shape(self, env_cfg) -> None:
126.         from openral_sim import make_policy
127. 
128.         policy = make_policy(env_cfg)
129.         try:
130.             cfg = policy._policy.config
131.             assert cfg.input_features["observation.image"].shape == (3, 96, 96)
132.             assert cfg.input_features["observation.state"].shape == (2,)
133.             assert cfg.output_features["action"].shape == (2,)
134.             assert cfg.n_action_steps == 8
135.             assert cfg.horizon == 16
136.         finally:
137.             policy.close()
138. 
139.     def test_all_parameters_live_on_gpu(self, env_cfg) -> None:
140.         """All ~263M parameters must reside on cuda:0 — no silent CPU fallback."""
141.         from openral_sim import make_policy
142. 
143.         policy = make_policy(env_cfg)
144.         try:
145.             inner = policy._policy
146.             param_devices = {p.device for p in inner.parameters()}
147.             assert param_devices == {torch.device("cuda:0")}, param_devices
148.             n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in inner.parameters())
149.             assert 235_000_000 < n_params < 290_000_000, n_params
150.         finally:
151.             policy.close()
152. 
153. 
154. class TestDiffusionPushTRollout:
155.     """End-to-end episode through the strict runner."""
156. 
157.     def test_pusht_episode_runs_via_sim_runner(self, env_cfg, skill_manifest) -> None:
158.         from openral_sim import SimRunner
159. 
160.         runner = SimRunner(env_cfg)
161.         try:
162.             runner.activate()
163.             runner.run(max_ticks=env_cfg.n_episodes * (env_cfg.task.max_steps + 1))
164.         finally:
165.             runner.deactivate()
166.         results = runner.episode_results
167.         assert len(results) == 1
168.         result = results[0]
169. 
170.         assert result.steps > 0
171.         assert result.mean_step_latency_ms > 0
172.         assert result.latency_budget_ms == skill_manifest.manifest.latency_budget.per_chunk_ms
173. 
174.         # Mean per-step latency stays within manifest budget × 2 (tolerance_pct=100).
175.         ceiling = 2.0 * skill_manifest.manifest.latency_budget.per_chunk_ms
176.         assert result.mean_step_latency_ms <= ceiling, (
177.             f"mean_step_latency_ms={result.mean_step_latency_ms:.1f} > {ceiling:.1f}ms"
178.         )
179. 
180.     def test_peak_vram_under_ceiling(self, env_cfg) -> None:
181.         """One chunk denoise must fit in the 2 GiB ceiling."""
182.         from openral_sim import make_env, make_policy
183. 
184.         torch.cuda.empty_cache()
185.         torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats()
186.         env = make_env(env_cfg)
187.         policy = make_policy(env_cfg)
188.         try:
189.             obs = env.reset(seed=env_cfg.seed)
190.             policy.step(obs, env_cfg.task.instruction)
```

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tests/sim/test_dataset_emission.py (lines 1-130)

<details>
<summary>130 lines</summary>

```
1. """ADR-0019 end-to-end sim test: openral sim run --dataset-out with a real VLA.
2. 
3. Drives ``openral_sim.SimRunner`` against
4. ``scenes/sim/aloha_transfer_cube.yaml`` with a real
5. ``openral_dataset.RolloutRecorder`` + ``LeRobotDatasetSink`` attached,
6. then re-opens the produced LeRobotDataset v3 via the real lerobot
7. reader and asserts the bridge round-trip works against a wildly
8. different state shape than SO-100 (14-DoF Aloha bimanual at 50 Hz vs
9. 6-DoF SO-100 at 30 Hz).
10. 
11. Bridges the gap between
12. ``python/dataset/tests/test_sink_lerobot.py`` (sink tested with
13. fabricated zero frames) and a real sim rollout (real ACT weights, real
14. gym-aloha physics, real SVT-AV1 video encoding of real 640x480 frames).
15. 
16. Per CLAUDE.md §1.11: no mocks. Skips cleanly when CUDA, lerobot,
17. torch, or gym_aloha are unavailable.
18. """
19. 
20. from __future__ import annotations
21. 
22. import importlib.util
23. import json
24. from pathlib import Path
25. 
26. import pytest
27. 
28. # Use `importlib.util.find_spec` + `pytestmark` rather than module-level
29. # `pytest.importorskip` / `pytest.skip(allow_module_level=True)`: with
30. # `tests/sim/__init__.py` making this directory a Package, a Skipped raised
31. # at module-import time poisons the whole `tests/sim` Package collection
32. # ("found no collectors for ..." on every sibling). Deferring the decision
33. # to `pytestmark` keeps this module importable when optional deps are
34. # missing, so sibling files remain reachable.
35. _REQUIRED_MODULES = ("torch", "gymnasium", "gym_aloha", "lerobot")
36. _MISSING_MODULES = tuple(m for m in _REQUIRED_MODULES if importlib.util.find_spec(m) is None)
37. _CUDA_AVAILABLE = False
38. if not _MISSING_MODULES:
39.     import torch
40. 
41.     _CUDA_AVAILABLE = torch.cuda.is_available()
42. 
43. pytestmark = [
44.     pytest.mark.sim,
45.     pytest.mark.slow,
46.     pytest.mark.skipif(
47.         bool(_MISSING_MODULES),
48.         reason="dataset emission sim test requires " + ", ".join(_MISSING_MODULES),
49.     ),
50.     pytest.mark.skipif(
51.         not _CUDA_AVAILABLE,
52.         reason="dataset emission sim test requires CUDA",
53.     ),
54. ]
55. 
56. _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent
57. # A SimScene (not a BenchmarkScene): `compose_sim_env` loads via
58. # `load_scene_strict(..., SimScene)`, which rejects BenchmarkScene YAMLs
59. # (n_episodes/seed/metadata) — so point at the single-rollout sim sibling.
60. _CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "scenes" / "sim" / "aloha_transfer_cube.yaml"
61. 
62. 
63. @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
64. def env_cfg():
65.     """Compose the same SimEnvironment the CLI builds for `openral sim run`."""
66.     from tests.sim.conftest import compose_sim_env
67. 
68.     if not _CONFIG.exists():
69.         pytest.skip(f"sim config not found at {_CONFIG}")
70.     return compose_sim_env(
71.         _CONFIG,
72.         rskill_uri="rskills/act-aloha",
73.         n_episodes=1,
74.         max_steps=5,
75.     )
76. 
77. 
78. def test_sim_run_with_dataset_out_produces_reloadable_v3(env_cfg, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
79.     """Run a 5-step Aloha rollout with --dataset-out and reload the dataset.
80. 
81.     Mirrors the real CLI invocation in `python/sim/src/openral_sim/cli.py`
82.     so any drift in how the CLI builds the recorder lands as a test
83.     failure. The test is short (5 steps × 1 episode) so it fits in the
84.     sim CI tier's <10 min budget.
85.     """
86.     from openral_dataset import LeRobotDatasetSink, RolloutRecorder
87.     from openral_sim.registry import ROBOTS
88.     from openral_sim.sim_runner import SimRunner
89. 
90.     # Resolve robot via the same registry path the CLI uses.
91.     robot = ROBOTS.get(env_cfg.robot_id)()
92.     assert robot.observation_spec is not None  # type narrowing
93.     assert robot.action_spec is not None
94.     fps = float(robot.action_spec.control_freq_hz) if robot.action_spec.control_freq_hz else 30.0
95. 
96.     ds_root = tmp_path / "ds"
97.     # ADR-0019: pass the camera shape from the scene config (sim renders
98.     # all cameras at one resolution) and the action dim from the rSkill
99.     # manifest's action_contract.
100.     from openral_rskill.loader import load_rskill_manifest
101. 
102.     manifest = load_rskill_manifest("rskills/act-aloha")
103.     action_dim_override = (
104.         manifest.action_contract.dim if manifest.action_contract is not None else None
105.     )
106.     state_shape_override = (
107.         (manifest.state_contract.dim,)
108.         if manifest.state_contract is not None and manifest.state_contract.dim
109.         else None
110.     )
111.     sink = LeRobotDatasetSink(
112.         root=ds_root,
113.         robot=robot,
114.         fps=fps,
115.         repo_id="openral/dataset-test-aloha-emission",
116.         state_shape=state_shape_override,
117.         action_dim=action_dim_override,
118.         camera_shape=(
119.             int(env_cfg.scene.observation_height),
120.             int(env_cfg.scene.observation_width),
121.         ),
122.     )
123.     recorder = RolloutRecorder(
124.         robot=robot,
125.         task_string=env_cfg.task.instruction,
126.         fps=fps,
127.         sinks=[sink],
128.         repo_id="openral/dataset-test-aloha-emission",
129.     )
130.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 48m 5s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The real deploy boot found a bug in my resolver: OpenArm’s MuJoCo bare-twin HAL has no `sim_time_ns`, so deriving `simulation` from backend alone freezes the graph. I’m fixing that before updating the PR.

269. @dataclass(frozen=True)
270. class LaunchInvocation:
271.     """Resolved ``ros2 launch`` argv + the metadata that built it.
272. 
273.     Returned by :func:`resolve_launch_invocation` so the dispatcher can
274.     pretty-print under ``--dry-run`` and the unit tests can assert on
275.     the resolved fields without touching ``subprocess``.
276.     """
277. 
278.     robot_id: str
279.     robot_yaml: Path
280.     robot_manifest_name: str
281.     hal: _HalSpec
282.     hal_params: dict[str, object]
283.     hal_mode: str
284.     """ADR-0036 — ``"sim"`` (``openral deploy sim``) or ``"real"``
285.     (``openral deploy run``). Forwarded into the launch as ``hal_mode:=…`` so
286.     the reasoner's action-mode palette gate matches the HAL this graph
287.     brings up (sim admits cartesian/OSC skills the scene's robosuite OSC
288.     controller can execute; real admits only the robot's declared
289.     ``supported_control_modes``)."""
290.     reset_to_pose_service: str
291.     approach_skill_id: str
292.     """ADR-0053 — MoveIt approach rSkill URI (e.g. ``rskills/rskill-moveit-joints``)
293.     forwarded into the launch as ``approach_skill_id:=…`` so the skill_runner
294.     plans a collision-free MoveGroup motion to the next skill's ``starting_pose``
295.     instead of the teleport snap. Empty (the default) keeps the legacy
296.     best-effort ``ResetToPose`` snap — opt in with ``--approach-skill-id`` once a
297.     ``move_group`` is in the graph (ADR-0053 phase 4)."""
298.     enable_slam: bool
299.     """ADR-0025 opt-in. Set by ``openral deploy sim --enable-slam``;
300.     forwarded into the launch as ``enable_slam:=true``."""
301.     slam_backend: str
302.     """ADR-0064 — which SLAM backend the launch composes when
303.     ``enable_slam`` is true: ``"lidar"`` (slam_toolbox, needs ``/scan``),
304.     ``"visual"`` (cuVSLAM + nvblox, camera-based, for lidar-less robots),
305.     or ``"none"`` (no SLAM). Resolved from capabilities — ``has_lidar``
306.     selects ``lidar`` (it wins when both flags are set, needing no AI depth
307.     model); else ``has_vision_slam`` selects ``visual``. Forwarded as
308.     ``slam_backend:=…``."""
309.     enable_nav2: bool
310.     """ADR-0025 opt-in for the Nav2 navigation stack. Set by
311.     ``openral deploy sim --enable-nav2``; forwarded into the launch as
312.     ``enable_nav2:=true``. Defaults to ``has_lidar`` — every robot
313.     that runs slam_toolbox needs a planner to consume the resulting
314.     map, so the two are auto-co-enabled."""
315.     enable_octomap: bool
316.     """ADR-0030 opt-in for the world-collision perception leg
317.     (octomap_server + openral_octomap_bridge + the kernel's
318.     capsule-vs-voxel check). Set by ``openral deploy sim --enable-octomap``;
319.     forwarded as ``enable_octomap:=true``. Defaults to "auto" = the robot
320.     manifest declares a depth SensorSpec (nothing to map otherwise)."""
321.     clock_origin: str
322.     """ClockAuthority origin forwarded as ``clock_origin:=…``. Derived from
323.     the deployment: simulator-owned elapsed time for sim backends that expose
324.     ``sim_time_ns``; host wall time for real deployments or clock-less scenes.
325.     Operators do not choose ROS ``use_sim_time`` directly."""
326.     enable_object_detector: bool
327.     """ADR-0035 object-detection perception leg
328.     (ros_image_detector_node → /openral/perception/objects → world-state
329.     object-lift → /openral/world_voxels). **On by default**; disabled with
330.     ``openral deploy sim --no-object-detector``. Forwarded as
331.     ``enable_object_detector:=true|false``. Auto-downgrades to ``false`` when no
332.     backend is available (omdet deps absent *and* the RT-DETR ONNX missing)."""
333.     object_detector_onnx: Path
334.     """ADR-0035 — absolute path to the RT-DETR ONNX weights used by the legacy /
335.     fallback detector path. Forwarded as ``object_detector_onnx:=<path>``.
336.     Defaults to the in-tree ``rskills/rtdetr-coco-r18/model.onnx``; passing it
337.     explicitly selects the fixed-label RT-DETR path over the omdet default."""
338.     object_detector_manifest: str
339.     """ADR-0037 2026-06-09 — path to a kind:detector rSkill manifest. When set,
340.     the detector node builds its backend from the manifest (runtime:pytorch →
341.     the open-vocab LocateAnything VLM sidecar; runtime:onnx → RT-DETR ONNX).
342.     Forwarded as ``object_detector_manifest:=<path>``. Empty = the RT-DETR ONNX
343.     fallback. By default (no explicit override) this resolves to the
344.     ``omdet-turbo-indoor`` manifest when the omdet deps are importable."""
345.     object_detector_query: str
346.     """ADR-0037 2026-06-09 — initial open-vocabulary query for a VLM detector
347.     (e.g. 'red mug'). Forwarded as ``object_detector_query:=<text>``. Empty =
348.     the manifest's ``detector.labels`` default. Ignored by ONNX detectors."""
349.     object_detector_locators: tuple[str, ...]
350.     """ADR-0056 — resolved manifest paths of the ``mode: on_demand`` open-vocab
351.     locators to bring up alongside the continuous detector. The launch builds one
352.     namespaced lifecycle node per entry (``/openral/perception/<alias>/locate_in_view``)
353.     so the reasoner can choose a model via ``LocateInViewTool.detector``. Forwarded
354.     as ``object_detector_locators:=<comma-joined paths>`` only when non-empty.
355.     Defaults to the omdet-turbo-locator manifest when the detector is on and the
356.     omdet deps are importable (LocateAnything is opt-in via an explicit path)."""
357.     spatial_memory_ingest: bool
358.     """ADR-0038 opt-in. Set by ``openral deploy sim --spatial-memory-ingest``;
359.     forwarded as ``spatial_memory_ingest:=true``. The reasoner then accumulates
360.     a durable ADR-0038 SpatialMemory from the object-lift producer's
```

600.             "set ``robot_id:`` in the DeployScene YAML / use a fixed-robot scene."
601.         )
602.     # ADR-0034 — a --robot override that differs from the scene's declared robot
603.     # composes a different arm than the scene was authored for. The scene's cameras
604.     # + asset mounts (e.g. tabletop_push's wrist_camera_mount_body="gripper") are
605.     # tuned for the declared robot, so on the override they may be mis-mounted or
606.     # unmatched — some /openral/cameras/* topics will be empty. Warn loudly.
607.     if robot_override and scene_robot_id and robot_override != scene_robot_id:
608.         _console.print(
609.             f"[yellow]warning:[/yellow] --robot {robot_override!r} overrides the scene's "
610.             f"declared robot_id {scene_robot_id!r}; the scene's cameras + asset mounts are "
611.             f"authored for {scene_robot_id!r} and may not match {robot_override!r} (expect "
612.             "empty /openral/cameras/* for non-matching sensors). Override only with a "
613.             "kinematically-compatible arm on a free-axis scene."
614.         )
615. 
616.     hal = _ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY.get(robot_id)
617.     if hal is None:
618.         supported = ", ".join(sorted(_ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY))
619.         raise ROSConfigError(
620.             f"robot {robot_id!r} has no HAL entry in _ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY. "
621.             f"Supported: {supported}. The registry key matches the "
622.             "``robots/<robot_id>/`` directory name — e.g. ``--robot "
623.             "franka_panda`` (not ``--robot franka``). To add a new robot, "
624.             "ship a ROS ``openral_hal_<X>`` package with a "
625.             "``lifecycle_node.py`` executable, add it to the ros2-build "
626.             "Justfile target, and register it here."
627.         )
628. 
629.     repo_root = _repo_root_from(Path(__file__))
630.     robot_yaml = repo_root / "robots" / robot_id / "robot.yaml"
631.     if not robot_yaml.is_file():
632.         raise ROSConfigError(
633.             f"robot manifest not found at {robot_yaml}; expected robots/{robot_id}/robot.yaml."
634.         )
635. 
636.     # Validate the manifest carries the e2e fields (joint position /
637.     # velocity / effort limits) and assert the manifest's declared name
638.     # is one the registered HAL accepts — protects against a typo
639.     # routing the wrong HAL at a robot.
640.     description = RobotDescription.from_yaml(str(robot_yaml))
641.     description.validate_for_e2e_pipeline()
642. 
643.     # ADR-0032 — fail fast before shelling the launch if real mode is asked of
644.     # a simulation-only robot (better UX than a graph that dies at HAL
645.     # configure with the same ROSCapabilityMismatch).
646.     if hal_mode == "real" and description.hal.real is None:
647.         raise ROSCapabilityMismatch(
648.             f"robot {robot_id!r} has no real-hardware HAL (hal.real is null); it is "
649.             "simulation-only. Use `openral deploy sim` instead of `openral deploy run`."
650.         )
651. 
652.     # ADR-0025 (#11) — SLAM is ON BY DEFAULT for every robot that *can* run it:
653.     # i.e. one that declares a lidar (the scan source slam_toolbox needs). This
654.     # is the firm default — a SLAM-capable robot always brings up the `map` frame
655.     # the object lift / spatial-memory ingest depend on, unless the operator
656.     # opts out with `--no-enable-slam`. Fixed-base arms (no mobile base, no lidar)
657.     # correctly stay off — there is no base to localise and nothing to map.
658.     # `enable_slam is None` means "auto": honour the manifest; an explicit flag wins.
659.     # ADR-0064 — SLAM is on for any robot that can localise/map: a lidar
660.     # (slam_toolbox) OR camera-based visual SLAM (cuVSLAM+nvblox, for
661.     # lidar-less robots). Fixed-base arms with neither correctly stay off.
662.     if enable_slam is None:
663.         enable_slam = bool(
664.             description.capabilities.has_lidar or description.capabilities.has_vision_slam
665.         )
666.     # ADR-0064 — backend selection (pure helper, unit-tested directly).
667.     slam_backend = _resolve_slam_backend(
668.         has_lidar=bool(description.capabilities.has_lidar),
669.         has_vision_slam=bool(description.capabilities.has_vision_slam),
670.         enable_slam=enable_slam,
671.     )
672.     # ADR-0025 — Nav2 auto-enables alongside slam_toolbox: every
673.     # lidar-equipped mobile robot needs a planner to consume the map.
674.     # Operators that want the map alone (recording / inspection) pass
675.     # ``--no-enable-nav2``.
676.     if enable_nav2 is None:
677.         enable_nav2 = enable_slam
678.     # ADR-0030 — the octomap world-collision leg auto-enables when the
679.     # robot manifest declares a usable depth SensorSpec (a camera the HAL
680.     # can ray-cast a PointCloud2 from); there is nothing to map otherwise.
681.     # ``--enable-octomap`` / ``--no-enable-octomap`` overrides.
682.     if enable_octomap is None:
683.         enable_octomap = any(
684.             s.modality in ("depth", "point_cloud") and s.intrinsics is not None
685.             for s in description.sensors
686.         )
687.     clock_origin = _resolve_clock_origin(hal_mode=hal_mode, config=config)
688. 
689.     # ADR-0035/0037 — the object-detection leg is ON by default (deploy sim is a
690.     # perception-driven stack; ``--no-object-detector`` turns it off). The default
691.     # backend is the open-vocabulary ``omdet-turbo-indoor`` continuous detector,
692.     # which grounds arbitrary indoor/kitchen objects instead of the fixed COCO-80
693.     # of RT-DETR; when its runtime deps (transformers/timm — the ``omdet`` group)
694.     # are not importable we gracefully fall back to the in-tree RT-DETR COCO ONNX
695.     # so the graph still comes up. Explicit ``--object-detector-manifest`` /
696.     # ``--object-detector-onnx`` override the default selection.
697.     default_rtdetr_onnx = repo_root / "rskills" / "rtdetr-coco-r18" / "model.onnx"
698.     default_omdet_manifest = repo_root / "rskills" / "omdet-turbo-indoor" / "rskill.yaml"
699.     if object_detector_manifest:
700.         resolved_object_detector_manifest = str(Path(object_detector_manifest).resolve())
701.         resolved_object_detector_onnx = (
702.             object_detector_onnx.resolve()
703.             if object_detector_onnx is not None
704.             else default_rtdetr_onnx
705.         )
706.     elif object_detector_onnx is not None:
707.         # Explicit ONNX → the legacy fixed-label RT-DETR path (no manifest).
708.         resolved_object_detector_manifest = ""
709.         resolved_object_detector_onnx = object_detector_onnx.resolve()
710.     elif _omdet_runtime_available():

83. def test_bh_deploy_sim_resolve_openarm_invocation() -> None:
84.     """Resolution returns the right argv template for openarm; no envelope file."""
85.     invocation = resolve_launch_invocation(
86.         config=_OPENARM_CONFIG,
87.         robot_override=None,
88.         dashboard_port=4318,
89.         reset_to_pose_service=None,
90.         hal_param_overrides=None,
91.     )
92.     assert invocation.robot_id == "openarm"
93.     assert invocation.robot_manifest_name == "openarm_v2"
94.     assert invocation.robot_yaml == _REPO_ROOT / "robots" / "openarm" / "robot.yaml"
95.     assert invocation.hal.package == "openral_hal_openarm"
96.     assert invocation.hal.executable == "lifecycle_node.py"
97.     assert invocation.hal.node_name == "openral_hal_openarm"
98.     assert invocation.hal.supported_robot_names == frozenset({"openarm_v2", "openarm"})
99.     # openarm is manifest-driven: robot_yaml + hal_mode are injected; HAL kwargs
100.     # live in hal.parameters. `bare_twin_sim=True` suppresses the `sim_env_yaml`
101.     # scene-attach (openarm composes its own MJCF). ADR-0066 — the tabletop arena
102.     # composition lives on the DeployScene now (not the robot manifest), so it is
103.     # forwarded to the node as `scene_composition_json`.
104.     import json
105. 
106.     from openral_core import DeployScene
107. 
108.     expected_composition = DeployScene.from_yaml(str(_OPENARM_CONFIG)).composition
109.     assert expected_composition is not None
110.     assert invocation.hal_params == {
111.         "viewer_enabled": True,
112.         "robot_yaml": str(_REPO_ROOT / "robots" / "openarm" / "robot.yaml"),
113.         "hal_mode": "sim",
114.         "scene_composition_json": expected_composition.model_dump_json(),
115.     }
116.     # The composer + the overview camera pose come from the scene, not robot.yaml.
117.     _comp = json.loads(invocation.hal_params["scene_composition_json"])
118.     assert _comp["composer"].endswith("compose_openarm_tabletop_mjcf")
119.     assert _comp["params"]["top_camera_pos"] == [0.20, 0.0, 0.95]
120.     assert "sim_env_yaml" not in invocation.hal_params
121.     assert invocation.reset_to_pose_service == "/openral/openarm/reset_to_pose"
122.     # ADR-0053 — MoveIt approach is opt-in; empty default keeps the legacy snap
123.     # and is NOT forwarded as a launch arg (ros2 launch rejects empty name:=).
124.     assert invocation.approach_skill_id == ""
125.     joined = " ".join(invocation.argv_template)
126.     assert "approach_skill_id:=" not in joined
127.     assert joined.startswith("ros2 launch openral_rskill_ros sim_e2e.launch.py")
128.     assert "envelope_file:=" not in joined  # no file path of any kind
129.     assert "HAL_PARAMS_FILE_PLACEHOLDER" in joined
130.     assert "hal_package:=openral_hal_openarm" in joined
131.     # ADR-0025: default is enable_slam=false; the launch arg is still
132.     # forwarded so the OpaqueFunction can read it.
133.     assert "enable_slam:=false" in joined
134.     assert invocation.enable_slam is False
135.     assert invocation.clock_origin == "simulation"
136.     assert "clock_origin:=simulation" in joined
137.     assert "enable_sim_clock:=" not in joined
138. 
139. 
140. def test_deploy_sim_real_mode_uses_host_wall_clock_origin() -> None:
141.     """Real deployments never publish OpenRAL /clock; they use the graph wall clock."""

143.         config=None,

145.         dashboard_port=4318,
146.         reset_to_pose_service=None,
147.         hal_param_overrides=None,
148.         hal_mode="real",
149.     )
150. 
151.     assert invocation.clock_origin == "host_wall"
152.     joined = " ".join(invocation.argv_template)
153.     assert "clock_origin:=host_wall" in joined
154.     assert "enable_sim_clock:=" not in joined
155.

85.     manifest) fails loud at resolution time, not at the HAL's first

88.     ``--hal`` overrides win.

90. 
91.     package: str
92.     executable: str
93.     node_name: str
