```
96.     # ADR-0025 Stage 3 — HAL nodes that declare a `sim_env_yaml` ROS
97.     # parameter (today: openral_hal_panda_mobile) opt in via this
98.     # flag. When True, `openral deploy sim --config <yaml>` injects the
99.     # resolved config path into hal_params so the HAL builds a live
100.     # `openral_sim.SimRollout` env in-process. Other HALs leave it
101.     # False so rclpy doesn't reject the unknown parameter at startup
102.     # (`automatically_declare_parameters_from_overrides=False` is the
103.     # default).
104.     supports_sim_env_yaml: bool = False
105.     # ADR-0032 — HAL nodes built via `make_lifecycle_main_from_manifest`
106.     # (franka / ur5e / ur10e / aloha / g1 / h1 / rizon4 / so100 / so101) declare
107.     # `robot_yaml` + `hal_mode` params and construct their HAL through
108.     # `build_hal(mode=...)`. When True, `openral deploy sim` injects the resolved
109.     # manifest path + `hal_mode="sim"`; `openral deploy run` injects
110.     # `hal_mode="real"`.
111.     manifest_driven: bool = False
112.     # issue #191 Phase 2 — a manifest-driven arm that builds a *bare* MuJoCo
113.     # twin (`MujocoArmHAL.from_description`) in sim rather than scene-attaching:
114.     # A manifest-driven arm that builds its OWN sim MJCF rather than
115.     # scene-attaching: so100 / so101 derive a bare `MujocoArmHAL` twin from the
116.     # manifest's `sim:` block; openarm composes a tabletop MJCF from
117.     # `scene_defaults.composition` (issue #191 Phase 3b). When True, the
118.     # manifest-driven injection below skips `sim_env_yaml` so the node builds the
119.     # explicit `hal.sim` HAL (with the composed mjcf threaded in) instead of a
120.     # scene-attached `SimAttachedHAL`. Other manifest arms leave it False and
121.     # scene-attach (ADR-0034).
122.     bare_twin_sim: bool = False
123. 
124. 
125. _ROBOT_HAL_REGISTRY: dict[str, _HalSpec] = {
126.     # Keys match the directory name under ``robots/<robot_id>/``; the
127.     # CLI looks up ``robots/<robot_id>/robot.yaml`` against this key. To
128.     # add a new robot to ``openral deploy sim``: ship a ``openral_hal_<X>``
129.     # ROS package with a ``lifecycle_node.py`` executable, register it
130.     # in the ros2-build Justfile target, and add an entry here. The
131.     # Python HAL adapters under ``python/hal/src/openral_hal/`` are a
132.     # different layer (used by ``openral deploy run`` / ``openral sim run``);
133.     # they do not provide ROS lifecycle nodes by themselves.
134.     "openarm": _HalSpec(
135.         package="openral_hal_openarm",
136.         executable="lifecycle_node.py",
137.         node_name="openral_hal_openarm",
138.         supported_robot_names=frozenset({"openarm_v2", "openarm"}),
139.         # issue #191 Phase 3b — migrated onto the manifest-driven node. Scene
140.         # composition (robot_lift_z / robot_forward_x / white_background) moved to
141.         # the manifest's `scene_defaults.composition`; HAL kwargs (settle_steps /
142.         # gravity_enabled / staleness_limit_s) to `hal.parameters`; cameras via
143.         # SimSensorBridge + OpenArmMujocoHAL.read_images. Only the viewer toggle
144.         # remains a node ROS param.
145.         default_params={
146.             "viewer_enabled": True,
147.         },
148.         manifest_driven=True,
149.         # openarm builds its own MJCF via scene COMPOSITION (the manifest's
150.         # `scene_defaults.composition`), not scene-attach — so suppress the
151.         # `sim_env_yaml` injection (see `bare_twin_sim`).
152.         bare_twin_sim=True,
153.     ),
154.     "so100_follower": _HalSpec(
155.         # issue #191 Phase 2 — migrated onto the manifest-driven node
156.         # (`make_lifecycle_main_from_manifest`). `hal_mode="sim"` derives a bare
157.         # `MujocoArmHAL` twin; `hal_mode="real"` builds `SO100FollowerHAL` with
158.         # `port` / `calibrate_on_connect` from the manifest's `hal.parameters`
159.         # (so no `port` ROS param — the node would reject the unknown override).
160.         package="openral_hal_so100",
161.         executable="lifecycle_node.py",
162.         node_name="openral_hal_so100",
163.         supported_robot_names=frozenset({"so100_follower"}),
164.         manifest_driven=True,
165.         bare_twin_sim=True,
166.     ),
167.     "so101_follower": _HalSpec(
168.         # The SO-101 is a hardware revision of the SO-100: identical 6-DoF
169.         # kinematic chain driven by the same lerobot Feetech STS3215 serial
170.         # backend, so it reuses the ``openral_hal_so100`` ROS lifecycle node
171.         # (now manifest-driven) verbatim. ``openral deploy sim`` injects this
172.         # robot's manifest + ``hal_mode="sim"`` and the node builds a bare
173.         # ``MujocoArmHAL.from_description`` from ``robots/so101_follower/
174.         # robot.yaml`` (``assets.mjcf`` → ``so101_new_calib``). The SAME node
175.         # serves so100 (``so_arm100``) and so101 (``so101_new_calib``) from
176.         # their own MJCF, so no dedicated ``openral_hal_so101`` package exists
177.         # or is needed (CLAUDE.md §1.13). The robot-name guard below keeps this
178.         # entry bound to the so101 manifest.
179.         package="openral_hal_so100",
180.         executable="lifecycle_node.py",
181.         node_name="openral_hal_so100",
182.         supported_robot_names=frozenset({"so101_follower"}),
183.         manifest_driven=True,
184.         bare_twin_sim=True,
185.     ),
186.     "panda_mobile": _HalSpec(
187.         # ADR-0024 / ADR-0025 — panda_mobile publishes /joint_states + /odom +
188.         # /scan and broadcasts the odom -> base_link TF that slam_toolbox + Nav2
189.         # both need. issue #191 Phase 3 migrated it onto the manifest-driven node:
190.         # MobileBaseBridge owns /odom + TF + /cmd_vel (gated on the manifest's
191.         # `base_joints`), SimSensorBridge owns /scan + cameras + depth + viewer.
192.         package="openral_hal_panda_mobile",
193.         executable="lifecycle_node.py",
194.         node_name="openral_hal_panda_mobile",
195.         supported_robot_names=frozenset({"panda_mobile"}),
196.         default_params={
197.             "odom_publish_rate_hz": 20.0,
198.             # The /scan envelope (rate / beam count / range) is NOT hardcoded
199.             # here: it's derived from the robot.yaml lidar_2d sensor at resolve
200.             # time (see _resolve_deploy_target) so robot.yaml stays the single
201.             # source of truth. viewer_enabled opens a non-blocking
202.             # mujoco.viewer.launch_passive window (no-op headless).
203.             "viewer_enabled": True,
204.         },
205.         # Scene-attach (SimAttachedHAL via sim_env_yaml) when a scene config is

207.         manifest_driven=True,
208.         supports_sim_env_yaml=True,
```

```
801.     # ADR-0032 — manifest-driven nodes build their HAL via build_hal(mode).

813.         if hal_mode == "sim" and config is not None and not hal.bare_twin_sim:
```

I’m updating the resolver to account for HAL topology: scene-attached sim HALs can expose simulator time, but bare-twin MuJoCo HALs stay host-wall until they implement `sim_time_ns`.

Sim deployments use simulator elapsed\n-    time only when the backend exposes ``sim_time_ns``; otherwise the graph\n-    stays in host-wall time so ROS node clocks never pin at zero.\n+    time only when the HAL is scene-attached and the backend exposes\n+    ``sim_time_ns``; otherwise the graph stays in host-wall time so ROS node\n+    clocks never pin at zero.\n     \"\"\"\n     if hal_mode != \"sim\":\n         return \"host_wall\"\n+    if hal.bare_twin_sim:\n+        return \"host_wall\"\n     return \"simulation\" if _scene_backend_has_sim_clock(config) else \"host_wall\"\n@@\n-    clock_origin = _resolve_clock_origin(hal_mode=hal_mode, config=config)\n+    clock_origin = _resolve_clock_origin(hal_mode=hal_mode, config=config, hal=hal)\n*** End Patch\n"

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/adr/0048-deploy-sim-clock-publisher.md.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/adr/0048-deploy-sim-clock-publisher.md (lines 90-150)

<details>
<summary>61 lines</summary>

```
90.   and every graph node sets `use_sim_time=true`.
91. - **Future hardware-synced deployment**: `ClockAuthority.hardware_synced(<clock_id>)` — a
92.   controller/PTP clock already synchronized into the graph. It is an authority, not a second ROS
93.   `/clock` publisher competing with the graph.
94. 
95. rSkills never mint their own time origin. They consume `SensorFrame`, `WorldState`, and `Action`
96. timestamps from the active authority and may emit latency/trace wall-time separately for
97. observability.
98. 
99. ### 1. Expose simulation time through the `SimRollout` seam
100. 
101. Add an **optional** `sim_time_ns() -> int | None` to the `SimRollout` protocol
102. (`python/sim/src/openral_sim/rollout.py`). It returns the backend's authoritative elapsed
103. simulation time in nanoseconds, or `None` when the backend has no sim clock.
104. 
105. - **MuJoCo / robocasa / robosuite backends**: return `round(data.time * 1e9)` (the MuJoCo
106.   `MjData.time`, advanced by `model.opt.timestep × n_substeps` per `env.step`). Deploy-sim's
107.   MuJoCo BODY_TWIST direct-qpos path is still a simulation timestep even though it bypasses
108.   `env.step`: it advances `MjData.time` by `body_twist_dt_s`, the same interval used for
109.   kinematic pose integration.
110. - **Sidecar sim backends (Isaac Sim ADR-0045)**: carry `sim_time_ns` in the ZMQ step/reset reply
111.   and surface it here. A sidecar that does not yet carry sim time returns `None`.
112. - **Pure-gym backends without a clock (pusht, …)**: return `None`.
113. 
114. `SimAttachedHAL` re-exposes this as `sim_time_ns()`, reading it after each
115. `_step_and_cache` (both the `send_action` and `idle_step` paths) so the value is monotonic and
116. fresh.
117. 
118. ### Timestep definitions
119. 
120. OpenRAL uses three separate clocks; they must not be conflated:
121. 
122. - **Simulation timestep**: one physics/control transition in the simulator. For normal
123.   `env.step`, MuJoCo advances by `model.opt.timestep × n_substeps` (or the backend's equivalent).
124.   For deploy-sim MuJoCo BODY_TWIST direct-qpos, the transition is `body_twist_dt_s` because that
125.   is the interval used to integrate the commanded base velocity.
126. - **ROS timestamp domain**: selected graph-wide from `ClockAuthority.origin`. `host_wall` means
127.   node clocks and message headers use ROS system time. `simulation` means the HAL publishes
128.   `/clock` from `SimAttachedHAL.sim_time_ns()`, every node sets `use_sim_time=true`, and message
129.   headers, TF, odom, scans, camera frames, and Nav2 deadlines are stamped in sim time.
130. - **Wall-clock cadence**: host scheduling/pacing only (idle-step timer, publisher thread, viewer
131.   pacing, runner sleeps). It decides *when* work happens on the CPU; it does not define the
132.   timestamp carried by simulated data when sim-time is enabled.
133. 
134. ### 2. Publish `/clock` from the HAL's sensor bridge
135. 
136. The HAL — already the clock authority for `/scan` + TF — publishes `rosgraph_msgs/msg/Clock`
137. from `hal.sim_time_ns()` on **every** env step (action **and** idle), at the idle-stepper /
138. sensor-bridge cadence. Requirements:
139. 
140. - **monotonic** — never republish a stale or decreasing stamp;
141. - **first** — `/clock` must be on the bus before the sim-time consumers start their first
142.   control tick (startup ordering, §3);
143. - **gated** — only created when `clock_origin=simulation` **and** `hal.sim_time_ns()` is non-`None`.
144. 
145. ### 3. Flip the graph to sim-time via the resolved authority
146. 
147. `clock_origin=simulation` sets `use_sim_time=true` for the whole `compose_runtime_graph`,
148. including the HAL itself, so `/scan`, TF, and joint_states carry **sim-time** stamps consistent
149. with `/clock`. `clock_origin=host_wall` leaves the graph on ROS system time and creates no
150. OpenRAL `/clock` publisher. Startup ordering: bring the HAL (clock publisher) to ACTIVE before
```

</details>

```
246. 2. **Phase 1 (done — `feat/sim-time-ns-rollout-seam`).** `sim_time_ns()` on `SimRollout` +
247.    MuJoCo backends + `SimAttachedHAL`, monotonic across `env.step` / `env.reset`.
248. 3. **Phase 2 (done — `feat/deploy-sim-clock-publisher`).** The HAL publishes `/clock` from the
249.    captured `sim_time_ns` (via the ADR-0049 publisher thread; `ProprioFrame.sim_time_ns`), gated
250.    on a live sim clock with a loud refuse-and-warn when the backend has none. The CLI now derives
251.    `clock_origin=simulation|host_wall` from backend capability instead of exposing a public clock
252.    toggle. **Key subtlety:** the sim-driving
253.    idle stepper must run on a `SYSTEM_TIME` clock, else its node-clock timer deadlocks (no step →
254.    no `/clock` → no fire). BODY_TWIST direct-qpos ticks advance `MjData.time` by the same
255.    `body_twist_dt_s` used for pose integration so active Nav2 streams do not freeze `/clock`.
256.    **Verified live** (robocasa, RTX 4070): `/clock` advances ~0.46× realtime while idle,
257.    Nav2/slam/octomap run on sim-time, costmap populated (1382 cells), no "inf Hz".
258. 4. **Phase 3 (safety-gated — NOT signed off).** `clock_origin=simulation` moves the
```

<sub>⏱️ 50m 0s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

ADR-0048 needs one wording fix: the resolver is backend + HAL-topology derived, not backend-only.

---

<sub>⏱️ 50m 1s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/adr/0048-deploy-sim-clock-publisher.md.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/adr/0048-deploy-sim-clock-publisher.md (lines 150-172)

<details>
<summary>23 lines</summary>

```
150. OpenRAL `/clock` publisher. Startup ordering: bring the HAL (clock publisher) to ACTIVE before
151. Nav2's controller and the costmaps begin ticking — the existing `OnStateTransition(hal → active)`
152. gate that already defers the Nav2 include is the natural hook.
153. 
154. ### 4. Backend-capability gate (the CLI must refuse silent breakage)
155. 
156. `openral deploy sim` resolves `clock_origin` against the scene's backend:
157. 
158. - backend exposes sim-time (`sim_time_ns() is not None`) → `clock_origin=simulation`;
159. - backend returns `None` (sidecars before the protocol bump; clock-less gym envs) →
160.   `clock_origin=host_wall` and the graph stays on system time. Never set `use_sim_time=true` for
161.   a backend that cannot feed `/clock`; that reintroduces the exact `t=0` bug.
162. 
163. ### Backend capability matrix
164. 
165. | Backend | Sim-time source | `/clock` feasible | Notes |
166. |---|---|---|---|
167. | robocasa / robosuite (MuJoCo) | `MjData.time` | ✅ | Primary target; idle stepper already advances it |
168. | LIBERO / MetaWorld / aloha (MuJoCo) | `MjData.time` | ✅ | Same accessor |
169. | Isaac Sim (ADR-0045 sidecar) | PhysX sim-time, **inside the sidecar** | ✅ | ZMQ reply carries `sim_time_ns` |
170. | GR00T (ADR-0046 sidecar) | n/a (policy, not sim) | n/a | Not a sim backend |
171. | pusht / clock-less gym | none | ❌ | Forced wall-clock |
172.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 50m 4s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m patching ADR-0048 to document the real-run finding: bare-twin HALs stay host-wall until they expose sim time.

<sub>⏱️ 50m 9s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/docs/adr/0048-deploy-sim-clock-publisher.md\n@@\n-`openral deploy sim` resolves `clock_origin` against the scene's backend:\n+`openral deploy sim` resolves `clock_origin` against both the scene backend and the HAL topology:\n \n-- backend exposes sim-time (`sim_time_ns() is not None`) → `clock_origin=simulation`;\n-- backend returns `None` (sidecars before the protocol bump; clock-less gym envs) →\n+- scene-attached HAL plus backend exposes sim-time (`sim_time_ns() is not None`) →\n+  `clock_origin=simulation`;\n+- bare-twin HAL, backend returns `None` (sidecars before the protocol bump; clock-less gym envs), or\n+  no deploy scene →\n   `clock_origin=host_wall` and the graph stays on system time. Never set `use_sim_time=true` for\n   a backend that cannot feed `/clock`; that reintroduces the exact `t=0` bug.\n@@\n | Backend | Sim-time source | `/clock` feasible | Notes |\n |---|---|---|---|\n | robocasa / robosuite (MuJoCo) | `MjData.time` | ✅ | Primary target; idle stepper already advances it |\n | LIBERO / MetaWorld / aloha (MuJoCo) | `MjData.time` | ✅ | Same accessor |\n+| bare-twin MuJoCo HALs (OpenArm / SO-100 / SO-101) | not yet exposed via `sim_time_ns()` | ❌ | Forced host-wall until the bare HALs publish an authority |\n | Isaac Sim (ADR-0045 sidecar) | PhysX sim-time, **inside the sidecar** | ✅ | ZMQ reply carries `sim_time_ns` |\n*** End Patch\n"
```

</details>

nodes to drift into inconsistent timestamp domains.\n- A real OpenArm deploy boot showed bare-twin MuJoCo HALs do not expose sim_time_ns yet, so they now derive host_wall instead of enabling sim time PushT + rskills/diffusion-pusht` completed a real 5-step rollout and wrote `summary.json`.\n- `just ros2-build` completed all 25 ROS packages.\n- First `openral deploy sim` boot found a real bug: OpenArm bare-twin MuJoCo was incorrectly deriving `clock_origin:=simulation` even though its HAL exposes no `sim_time_ns`, causing the HAL to warn that no `/clock` would be published.\n- Added follow-up commit `049f14a` so bare-twin HALs derive `clock_origin:=host_wall`; reran `openral deploy sim --config scenes/deploy/openarm_tabletop.yaml --hal viewer_enabled=false --no-enable-slam --no-enable-nav2 --no-enable-octomap` and it booted the dashboard, safety kernel, runtime, reasoner, prompt router, OpenArm HAL, robot_state_publisher, and sensor bridge with `clock_origin:=host_wall` and no missing-/clock freeze warning.'

the graph-wide timestamp authority contract.\n+- Treat ROS /clock as a projection of simulation authority, not the source of truth.\n+- Derive deploy-sim clock_origin from HAL topology plus backend capability and remove the operator-facing enable_sim_clock flag.\n+- Keep ROS use_sim_time as an internal launch mapping from clock_origin.\n+- Advance MuJoCo direct BODY_TWIST qpos timesteps so /clock cannot freeze during Nav2 base control.\n+\n+Why\n+- OpenRAL needed one explicit clock origin that works across simulators, robots, rSkills, and future hardware instead of scattered /clock/use_sim_time choices.\n+- The old flag allowed nodes to drift into inconsistent timestamp domains.\n+- A real OpenArm deploy boot showed bare-twin MuJoCo HALs do not expose sim_time_ns yet, so 1. # gym-aloha AlohaTransferCube-v0 — ALOHA bimanual fixed.
```
2. # SimScene sibling of scenes/benchmark/aloha_transfer_cube.yaml: the single-
3. # rollout `openral sim run` shape (no benchmark-only n_episodes/seed/metadata,
4. # so it loads as a SimScene rather than a BenchmarkScene). Same scene + task as
5. # the benchmark variant; used by tests/sim/test_dataset_emission.py.
6. # Run: openral sim run --config scenes/sim/aloha_transfer_cube.yaml \
7. #                      --rskill rskill://rskills/act-aloha
8. # Setup: just sync --all-packages --group sim  (set MUJOCO_GL=egl)
9. #
10. # No `robot_id:` — the aloha_transfer_cube scene hard-fixes the physics robot
11. # to `aloha_bimanual`, so `openral sim run` rejects an explicit robot_id (it is
12. # reserved for free-axis scenes). The benchmark sibling carries robot_id
13. # because BenchmarkScene requires it; sim scenes omit it.
14. 
15. scene:
16.   id: aloha_transfer_cube
17.   backend: mujoco
18.   observation_height: 480
19.   observation_width: 640
20.   cameras: ["top"]
21. 
22. task:
23.   id: aloha_transfer_cube/0
24.   scene_id: aloha_transfer_cube
25.   instruction: "transfer the cube"
26.   success_key: is_success
27.   max_steps: 500
28.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 556m 58s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/act-aloha/rskill.yaml (lines 1-120)

<details>
<summary>110 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — OpenRAL packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. # Wraps: lerobot/act_aloha_sim_transfer_cube_human (MIT)
3. # Paper: Zhao et al., 2023 — Action Chunking Transformer.
4. #
5. # LEGACY PROCESSOR PATH: this checkpoint pre-dates lerobot's
6. # PolicyProcessorPipeline migration and ships its norm stats inside
7. # model.safetensors. The schema's processors block is therefore omitted;
8. # the ACT adapter dispatches on manifest.processors is None and falls
9. # back to the snapshot_download + _try_load_act_norm_stats path. Migrating
10. # to per-file URIs would require re-publishing the upstream checkpoint
11. # and is tracked as a follow-up.
12. 
13. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14. schema_version: "0.1"
15. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-act-aloha"
16. version: "0.1.0"
17. license: "mit"
18. role: "s1"
19. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
20. 
21. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22. model_family: "act"
23. 
24. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
25. # Bimanual ALOHA (2 × 7-DoF arms = 14-DoF action space). Used by
26. # tests/sim/test_aloha_bimanual_act_aloha.py (gym-aloha MuJoCo).
27. embodiment_tags:
28.   - "aloha"
29. 
30. # ACT for ALOHA cube-transfer ships with a single top-down 480×640 RGB stream.
31. sensors_required:
32.   - modality: "rgb"
33.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.top"
34.     min_width: 640
35.     min_height: 480
36. 
37. # Output side (ADR-0013). For the canonical aloha bimanual embodiment the
38. # loader auto-fills n_dof (14) + vla_action_key from
39. # robots/aloha_bimanual/robot.yaml.
40. actuators_required:
41.   - kind: "joint_position"
42.     control_mode_semantics:
43.       mode: "absolute"
44. 
45. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46. runtime: "pytorch"
47. quantization:
48.   dtype: "fp32"
49.   backend: "pytorch"
50. weights_uri: "hf://lerobot/act_aloha_sim_transfer_cube_human"
51. 
52. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
53. # processors omitted — legacy path; norm stats live inside model.safetensors.
54. # ACT manages its own preprocessing / state contract inside the lerobot
55. # ACTPolicy so nothing else needs to move.
56. 
57. # ── Execution semantics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
58. chunk_size: 100
59. # n_action_steps omitted — ACT default is 1 (per-step re-inference +
60. # temporal ensembling, paper-faithful).
61. latency_budget:
62.   # Reference-host measurement (RTX 4070 Laptop, CUDA 12.8, PyTorch 2.10)
63.   # of the warm full-chunk inference is 16 ms; bf16 autocast is ~12 ms.
64.   # We pin per_chunk_ms to 25 ms to keep the canonical
65.   # "tolerance_pct=100 → 2× ceiling" pattern (giving a 50 ms test ceiling,
66.   # matching the previous _WARM_CHUNK_CEILING_S = 0.050).
67.   per_chunk_ms: 25.0
68. 
69. # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70. # Headline success rate from skills/act-aloha/eval/aloha_transfer_cube.json
71. # (50 episodes via `openral benchmark run`).
72. benchmarks:
73.   aloha_transfer_cube: 0.82
74. 
75. paper_url: "https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.13705"
76. source_repo: "hf://lerobot/act_aloha_sim_transfer_cube_human"
77. 
78. description: >
79.   Action Chunking Transformer (~52M-param encoder-decoder) finetuned on
80.   the ALOHA bimanual cube-transfer demonstration set. Action chunks of
81.   length 100. The mean/std norm buffers live inside model.safetensors;
82.   modern lerobot ACTPolicy drops them on load and the adapter re-applies
83.   them — see tests/sim/test_aloha_bimanual_act_aloha.py.
84. 
85. # Task-data gate (ADR-0060): trained + validated on the ALOHA sim
86. # cube-transfer task, so the benchmark runner accepts the
87. # aloha_transfer_cube scene and refuses task-mismatched scenes.
88. evaluated_tasks: ["aloha_transfer_cube"]
89. 
90. # ADR-0022 — action vocabulary surfaced to the reasoner LLM tool
91. # palette so it can pick this skill by what it does (action verb +
92. # object + scene), not just by its slug.
93. actions:
94.   - "transfer"
95.   - "pick"
96.   - "place"
97. objects:
98.   - "cube"
99. scenes:
100.   - "tabletop"
101. 
102. # ADR-0019 — per-checkpoint action contract (consumed by the dataset bridge
103. # to bind the LeRobot v3 `action` feature shape).
104. action_contract:
105.   dim: 14
106. 
107. # ADR-0019 — per-checkpoint state contract.
108. state_contract:
109.   dim: 14
110.
```

</details>

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/scenes/sim/openarm_tabletop.yaml (lines 1-70)

<details>
<summary>56 lines</summary>

```
1. # OpenArm v2 bimanual — tabletop pick-and-place scene.
2. #
3. # Scene layout: LEFT-FIRST action layout, RADIANS units — both
4. # match robots/openarm/robot.yaml, so the env defaults
5. # (action_layout=left_first, joint_units=radians) apply with no
6. # overrides. The default top-camera placement matches the
7. # mddoai dataset POV (between the two robot bases, looking forward +
8. # down at the tabletop).
9. #
10. # Policy travels on the CLI: ``--rskill rskills/<your-skill>``.
11. #
12. # Run::
13. #
14. #     openral sim run --config scenes/sim/openarm_tabletop.yaml \
15. #                 --view
16. 
17. robot_id: "openarm"
18. 
19. scene:
20.   id: openarm_tabletop_pnp
21.   backend: mujoco
22.   observation_height: 480
23.   observation_width: 640
24.   cameras: ["top", "left_wrist", "right_wrist"]
25.   backend_options:
26.     render_width: 640
27.     render_height: 480
28.     max_steps: 500
29.     # action_layout / joint_units default to left_first / radians — correct
30.     # for this checkpoint, so we leave them implicit.
31.     # ADR-0066 — the overview-camera pose lives on the SCENE (was the robot
32.     # manifest's `scene_defaults.top_camera`, removed so robot.yaml describes
33.     # only the robot). The mddoai dataset POV: between the bases, facing the
34.     # tabletop. The openarm_robosuite env feeds these to the composer.
35.     top_camera_pos: [0.20, 0.0, 0.95]
36.     top_camera_target: [0.65, 0.0, 0.05]
37.     top_camera_fovy: 65.0
38. 
39. task:
40.   id: openarm/pnp_cube_to_drawer
41.   scene_id: openarm_tabletop_pnp
42.   instruction: "pick the red cube and place it in the drawer"
43.   max_steps: 500
44. 
45. # ADR-0002 Amendment 3 — mandatory mounting pose for the
46. # openarm_tabletop_pnp scene. Lift bases above the table top + shift
47. # forward into the workspace so the 0.6 m arm reach can sweep the
48. # picking targets (mirrors the openral deploy sim HAL params for openarm).
49. base_pose:
50.   xyz: [0.20, 0.0, 0.55]
51.   quat_xyzw: [0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0]
52.   frame_id: world
53. 
54. seed: 0
55. n_episodes: 1
56.
```

</details>

```
70. _PEAK_VRAM_CEILING_B = 500 * 1024**2  # 500 MB
71. 
72. _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent
73. # A SimScene (not a BenchmarkScene): `compose_sim_env` loads via
74. # `load_scene_strict(..., SimScene)`, which rejects BenchmarkScene YAMLs.
75. _CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "scenes" / "sim" / "aloha_transfer_cube.yaml"
76. _LOCAL_MANIFEST = _REPO_ROOT / "rskills" / "act-aloha" / "rskill.yaml"
77. 
78. 
79. @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
80. def env_cfg():
81.     """Load the canonical ACT-ALOHA sim env, capped at one short episode."""
82.     from tests.sim.conftest import compose_sim_env
83. 
84.     if not _CONFIG.exists():
85.         pytest.skip(f"sim config not found at {_CONFIG}")
86.     if not _LOCAL_MANIFEST.exists():
87.         pytest.skip(f"rSkill manifest not found at {_LOCAL_MANIFEST}")
88. 
89.     return compose_sim_env(
90.         _CONFIG,
91.         rskill_uri="rskills/act-aloha",
92.         n_episodes=1,
93.         max_steps=50,
94.     )
95. 
96. 
97. @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
98. def skill_manifest():
99.     """Load the rSkill manifest from disk (no network)."""
100.     from openral_rskill.loader import rSkill
101. 
102.     return rSkill.from_yaml(_LOCAL_MANIFEST)
103. 
104. 
105. class TestACTAlohaManifest:
106.     """Manifest contract — declared inputs match the bimanual ALOHA embodiment."""
107. 
108.     def test_manifest_loads_and_declares_aloha(self, skill_manifest) -> None:
109.         m = skill_manifest.manifest
110.         assert m.name == "OpenRAL/rskill-act-aloha"
111.         assert "aloha" in m.embodiment_tags
112.         assert m.role == "s1"
113. 
114.     def test_manifest_declares_top_camera(self, skill_manifest) -> None:
115.         sensors = skill_manifest.manifest.sensors_required
116.         assert len(sensors) == 1
117.         assert sensors[0].modality == "rgb"
118.         assert sensors[0].vla_feature_key == "observation.images.top"
119. 
120.     def test_manifest_has_latency_budget(self, skill_manifest) -> None:
121.         budget = skill_manifest.manifest.latency_budget
122.         assert budget is not None
123.         assert budget.per_chunk_ms > 0
124. 
125. 
126. class TestACTAlohaIOContract:
127.     """Adapter IO shapes — verified through the canonical eval factory."""
128. 
129.     def test_make_policy_has_aloha_io_shape(self, env_cfg) -> None:
130.         from openral_sim import make_policy
131. 
132.         policy = make_policy(env_cfg)
133.         try:
134.             cfg = policy._policy.config
135.             assert cfg.input_features["observation.images.top"].shape == (3, 480, 640)
136.             assert cfg.input_features["observation.state"].shape == (14,)
137.             assert cfg.output_features["action"].shape == (14,)
138.         finally:
139.             policy.close()
140. 
141. 
142. class TestACTAlohaRollout:
143.     """End-to-end episode through the strict runner."""
144. 
145.     def test_aloha_episode_runs_via_sim_runner(self, env_cfg, skill_manifest) -> None:
146.         from openral_sim import SimRunner
147. 
148.         runner = SimRunner(env_cfg)
149.         try:
150.             runner.activate()
151.             runner.run(max_ticks=env_cfg.n_episodes * (env_cfg.task.max_steps + 1))
152.         finally:
153.             runner.deactivate()
154.         results = runner.episode_results
155.         assert len(results) == 1
156.         result = results[0]
157. 
158.         assert result.steps == env_cfg.task.max_steps, (
159.             f"rollout terminated early at step {result.steps}/{env_cfg.task.max_steps}"
160.         )
161.         assert result.mean_step_latency_ms > 0
162.         assert result.latency_budget_ms == skill_manifest.manifest.latency_budget.per_chunk_ms
163. 
164.         # Mean per-step latency stays within manifest budget × 2 (tolerance_pct=100).
165.         # Most of the 50 steps are queue pops (chunk_size=100); only the first
166.         # pays the full 16 ms GPU cost, so the mean is dominated by sub-ms pops.
167.         ceiling = 2.0 * skill_manifest.manifest.latency_budget.per_chunk_ms
168.         assert result.mean_step_latency_ms <= ceiling, (
169.             f"mean_step_latency_ms={result.mean_step_latency_ms:.1f} > {ceiling:.1f}ms"
170.         )
171. 
172.     def test_peak_vram_under_ceiling(self, env_cfg) -> None:
173.         """50-step rollout must fit in the 500 MB ceiling."""
174.         from openral_sim import make_env, make_policy
175. 
176.         torch.cuda.empty_cache()
177.         torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats()
178.         env = make_env(env_cfg)
179.         policy = make_policy(env_cfg)
180.         try:
```

```
1. # SO-101 follower arm in a 100×61.5×75 cm tabletop box — deploy playground.
2. #
3. # Env-only DeployScene (no task, no eval) — boots the full OpenRAL stack
4. # (HAL + reasoner + safety kernel) for the SO-101 follower arm. Robot is
5. # auto-resolved via ``SCENES.fixed_robot("so101_box") == "so101_follower"``;
6. # the same so100 lifecycle node serves both so100 and so101 manifests
7. # (issue #191 Phase 3b — manifest-driven, bare twin).
8. #
9. # All ``BoxSceneOptions`` defaults apply (box geometry, camera poses, tube
10. # and slotted-block dimensions). The reasoner picks the active rSkill at
11. # runtime from ``rskills/`` — no scene-level task is prescribed.
12. #
13. # Run::
14. #
15. #     openral deploy sim --config scenes/deploy/so101_box.yaml
16. 
17. scene:
18.   id: so101_box
19.   backend: mujoco
20.   observation_height: 480
21.   observation_width: 640
22.   cameras: ["oak_top", "wrist"]
23.
```

</details>

```
160. libero = [
161.     "lerobot[libero]",  # reason: pulls hf-libero (linux-only) + LIBERO bddl task files + robosuite
162.     "transformers==5.3.0",  # reason: lerobot 0.5.1 hard-pins transformers==5.3.0 via its `transformers-dep` extra (smolvla / pi / xvla / libero all alias it). Stated explicitly here — matching the sim / metaworld / maniskill3 / robocasa groups — so a multi-group sync resolves to one version and the pin survives even if a future lerobot loosens its own bound.
163.     "num2words>=0.5.14",  # reason: required by SmolVLAProcessor (transformers) for number tokenization
164.     "bitsandbytes>=0.45",  # reason: 4-bit (NF4) quantization for π0.5 — 3.4 B PaliGemma backbone needs ≤8 GiB VRAM in nf4
165. ]
166. metaworld = [
167.     # The MetaWorld benchmark package itself (metaworld==3.0.0) is NOT listed here
168.     # because its transitive deps conflict with the workspace lock; install it
169.     # manually with `uv run pip install metaworld==3.0.0 --no-deps` (the
170.     # MetaWorld scene adapter raises ROSConfigError with this exact hint).
171.     "lerobot",  # reason: provides lerobot.envs.metaworld used by the MetaWorld scene adapter
172.     "gymnasium[mujoco]>=1.3.0",  # reason: gym backend MetaWorldEnv is built on
173.     "mujoco>=3.8.0",  # reason: physics engine for MetaWorld scenes
174.     "transformers==5.3.0",  # reason: SmolVLA backbone (pinned to match the sim group)
175.     "accelerate>=1.13.0",  # reason: device_map + dtype handling for SmolVLA
176.     "num2words>=0.5.14",  # reason: required by SmolVLAProcessor (transformers) for number tokenization
177. ]
178. maniskill3 = [
179.     # ADR-0010 — opt-in ManiSkill3 sim backend.
180.     # mani-skill is the PyPI distribution name; it pulls SAPIEN (Vulkan-backed
181.     # GPU physics, ~200 MB wheel) transitively. Bound at >=3.0.0b9 so SemVer-compatible
182.     # pre-release patches flow in; tighten to a >=3.0.0 pin when MS3 ships its stable release.
183.     "mani-skill>=3.0.0b9",
184.     "gymnasium>=1.0.0",  # reason: gym backend ManiSkill envs are built on
185.     "lerobot",  # reason: every non-RLDX policy adapter (smolvla / pi05 / xvla / act / diffusion) imports lerobot.policies.* at load time. Pulled bare (no `[libero]`) to keep robosuite unconstrained — `mani-skill` carries SAPIEN, not robosuite, so there is no robosuite version pin to clash with. Once an MS3-specific rSkill ships in-tree this becomes load-bearing; today it future-proofs the group + matches the metaworld group's convention.
186.     "transformers==5.3.0",  # reason: SmolVLA / π0.5 / xVLA / ACT / diffusion adapters all import transformers at load time (lerobot's own dep is too loose for the Py3.12 + lerobot compat path — see python/rskill/src/openral_rskill/_lerobot_compat.py). Pinned to match the sim + metaworld groups so a `--group sim --group maniskill3` sync resolves to a single transformers version.
187.     "accelerate>=1.13.0",  # reason: device_map + dtype handling for SmolVLA / pi05 (matches metaworld group convention)
188.     "num2words>=0.5.14",  # reason: required by SmolVLAProcessor (transformers) for number tokenization (matches metaworld group)
189. ]
190. simpler-env = [
```

<sub>⏱️ 557m 48s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m launching four deploy-sim examples now: OpenArm, SO-101, and two RoboCasa/PandaMobile scene-attached graphs. Logs go to the session folder.

---

<sub>⏱️ 557m 54s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Run four deploy sim examples**

$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep && set -u
source /opt/ros/jazzy/setup.bash
source install/setup.bash
OUT=/home/allopart/.copilot/session-state/d1d8eee7-0fb8-4ea5-b69e-86c20f5df18a/files/deploy_matrix_20260623
mkdir -p "$OUT"
run_deploy() {
  name="$1"; shift
  log="$OUT/${name}.log"
  echo "=== deploy $name ==="
  set +e
  OPENRAL_AUTO_INSTALL_DEPS=1 timeout --signal=INT 75s .venv/bin/openral deploy sim "$@" --hal viewer_enabled=false --no-enable-slam --no-enable-nav2 --no-enable-octomap --no-object-detector >"$log" 2>&1
  code=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$code" -eq 0 ] || [ "$code" -eq 124 ] || [ "$code" -eq 130 ]; then
    if grep -q "process started" "$log" && grep -q "HAL activated" "$log"; then
      echo "PASS $name code=$code log=$log"
      grep -E "argv:|clock_origin:=|HAL activated|use_sim_time=true but|/clock would freeze|ResetToPose service ready" "$log" | tail -20 || true
      return 0
    fi
  fi
  echo "FAIL $name code=$code log=$log"
  tail -80 "$log"
  return 1
}
run_deploy openarm_tabletop --config scenes/deploy/openarm_tabletop.yaml
run_deploy so101_box --config scenes/deploy/so101_box.yaml
run_deploy robocasa_pnp --config scenes/deploy/robocasa_pnp.yaml
run_deploy robocasa_navigate --config scenes/deploy/robocasa_navigate.yaml

### ✅ `bash`

**Run remaining deploy sim examples**

$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep
source /opt/ros/jazzy/setup.bash
source install/setup.bash
OUT=/home/allopart/.copilot/session-state/d1d8eee7-0fb8-4ea5-b69e-86c20f5df18a/files/deploy_matrix_20260623
mkdir -p "$OUT"
run_deploy() {
  name="$1"; shift
  log="$OUT/${name}.log"
  echo "=== deploy $name ==="
  set +e
  OPENRAL_AUTO_INSTALL_DEPS=1 timeout --signal=INT 120s .venv/bin/openral deploy sim "$@" --hal viewer_enabled=false --no-enable-slam --no-enable-nav2 --no-enable-octomap --no-object-detector >"$log" 2>&1
  code=$?
  set -e
  if grep -q "process started" "$log" && grep -q "HAL activated" "$log" && ! grep -q "use_sim_time=true but this backend exposes no sim clock" "$log"; then
    echo "BOOTED $name code=$code log=$log"
    grep -E "argv:|clock_origin:=|HAL activated|ResetToPose service ready|use_sim_time=true but" "$log" | tail -20 || true
  else
    echo "NOT_BOOTED $name code=$code log=$log"
    tail -100 "$log"
  fi
}

<sub>⏱️ 568m 10s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Run four sim run examples**

$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep
OUT=/home/allopart/.copilot/session-state/d1d8eee7-0fb8-4ea5-b69e-86c20f5df18a/files/sim_matrix_20260623
mkdir -p "$OUT"
PUSHT=/home/allopart/.copilot/session-state/d1d8eee7-0fb8-4ea5-b69e-86c20f5df18a/files/pusht_sim_run.yaml
run_sim() {
  name="$1"; shift
  log="$OUT/${name}.log"
  save="$OUT/${name}_out"
  rm -rf "$save"
  echo "=== sim $name ==="
  set +e
  MUJOCO_GL=egl timeout --signal=INT 240s .venv/bin/openral sim run "$@" --no-view --save-dir "$save" >"$log" 2>&1
  code=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$code" -eq 0 ] && grep -q "success_rate:" "$log" && [ -f "$save/summary.json" ]; then
    echo "PASS $name log=$log summary=$save/summary.json"
    grep -E "openral sim run|robot :|scene :|task  :|vla   :|episode_done|success_rate:|wrote " "$log" | tail -20 || true
  else
    echo "FAIL $name code=$code log=$log"
    tail -120 "$log"
  fi
}
run_sim pusht_seed1 --config "$PUSHT" --rskill rskills/diffusion-pusht --n-episodes 1 --max-steps 5 --seed 1
run_sim pusht_seed2 --config "$PUSHT" --rskill rskills/diffusion-pusht --n-episodes 1 --max-steps 5 --seed 2
run_sim aloha_seed0 --config scenes/sim/aloha_transfer_cube.yaml --rskill rskills/act-aloha --n-episodes 1 --max-steps 3 --seed 0
run_sim aloha_seed1 --config scenes/sim/aloha_transfer_cube.yaml --rskill rskills/act-aloha --n-episodes 1 --max-steps 3 --seed 1

### ✅ `bash`

**Summarize rSkill compatibility**

$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep && .venv/bin/python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
from openral_core import RSkillManifest
for p in sorted(Path('rskills').glob('*/rskill.yaml')):
    try:
        m=RSkillManifest.from_yaml(str(p))
    except Exception as e:
        print(p, 'ERR', e)
        continue
    print(f'{p.parent.name:36} family={m.model_family:14} tags={list(m.embodiment_tags)} eval={list(m.evaluated_tasks or [])}')
PY

### ✅ `bash`

**Summarize all rSkills**

$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep && .venv/bin/python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
from openral_core import RSkillManifest
for p in sorted(Path('rskills').glob('*/rskill.yaml')):
    try:
        m=RSkillManifest.from_yaml(str(p))
    except Exception as e:
        print(p, 'ERR', e)
        continue
    family = m.model_family or '-'
    print(f'{p.parent.name:36} family={family:16} tags={list(m.embodiment_tags)} eval={list(m.evaluated_tasks or [])}')
PY

```
1. # RoboCasa PickPlaceCounterToCabinet — PandaMobile robot.
2. # Not an officially benchmarked scene (no canonical published numbers).
3. # Run: openral sim run --config scenes/sim/robocasa_pnp.yaml \

12.   cameras: ["camera1", "camera2", "camera3"]
13.   backend_options:

16.     robots: ["PandaMobile"]
17.     controller: OSC_POSE
18.     horizon: 600
19. 
20. task:
```

```
1. # RoboCasa NavigateKitchen — PandaMobile robot.
2. # Not an officially benchmarked scene.
3. # Run: openral sim run --config scenes/sim/robocasa_panda_mobile_kitchen.yaml \
4. #                      --rskill rskill://rskills/<navigation-rskill>
5. # Setup: just sync --all-packages --group robocasa
6. 
7. scene:
8.   id: robocasa/NavigateKitchen
9.   backend: mujoco
10.   observation_height: 256
11.   observation_width: 256
12.   cameras: ["camera1", "camera2", "camera3"]
13.   backend_options:
14.     # NavigateKitchen is one of RoboCasa's prebuilt atomic tasks. The scene
15.     # id resolves the env name, but RoboCasaBackendOptions still requires
16.     # the matching prebuilt_task to be named explicitly (it validates the
17.     # options dict in isolation, before the scene-id resolution runs) —
18.     # mirrors every other shipped robocasa scene (e.g. robocasa_pnp.yaml).
19.     mode: prebuilt
20.     prebuilt_task: NavigateKitchen
21.     robots: ["PandaMobile"]
22.     controller: OSC_POSE
23.     horizon: 600
24. 
25. task:
26.   id: robocasa/NavigateKitchen/0
27.   scene_id: robocasa/NavigateKitchen
28.   instruction: >
29.     navigate through the kitchen, opening and closing drawers
30.     and cabinets as needed to reach the target location
31.   success_key: is_success
32.   # max_steps: not enforced
33. 
34. seed: 42
35.
```

</details>

```
1. # RoboCasa GR1 tabletop — PnP cup to drawer and close.
2. # Community fork (robocasa-gr1-tabletop-tasks); not an official benchmark.
3. # The kitchen + GR1 forks share the python package name `robocasa`; the
4. # `robocasa` group syncs the shared deps and the auto-installer picks the
5. # GR1 variant for `robocasa/gr1/*` scene ids.
6. # Setup: just sync --all-packages --group robocasa
7. # Run: openral sim run --config scenes/sim/robocasa_gr1_pnp_cup_to_drawer.yaml \
8. #                      --rskill rskill://rskills/rldx1-ft-gr1-nf4
9. 
10. scene:
11.   id: robocasa/gr1/PnPCupToDrawerClose
12.   backend: mujoco
13.   observation_height: 256
14.   observation_width: 256
15.   cameras: ["camera1"]
16.   # GR1 tabletop tasks route through the `gr1_unified/<task>_<robot>_Env`
17.   # gymnasium wrapper (BASIC composite controller + Fourier-native 29-D
18.   # state). `robots[0]` must be a GR1 composition so `_is_gr1_robot` flips
19.   # the env construction to that path; `state_layout: gr1` selects the
20.   # RLDX-1-FT-GR1 general_embodiment proprioception layout.
21.   backend_options:
22.     mode: prebuilt
23.     prebuilt_task: PnPCupToDrawerClose
24.     robots: ["GR1ArmsAndWaistFourierHands"]
25.     controller: BASIC
26.     state_layout: gr1
27.     horizon: 600
28. 
29. task:
30.   id: robocasa/gr1/PnPCupToDrawerClose/0
31.   scene_id: robocasa/gr1/PnPCupToDrawerClose
32.   instruction: "pick the cup from the table and place it into the drawer, then close the drawer"
33.   success_key: is_success
34.   # max_steps: not enforced
35. 
36. seed: 0
37.
```

</details>

```
80.         "(adds pyzmq + msgpack for the RLDX adapter sidecar)."
81.     ),
82.     "gr00t": (

84.         "(adds pyzmq + msgpack for the GR00T sidecar client). The GR00T 3B "

87.     ),
88.     "diffuser_actor": (

90.         "(adds pyzmq + msgpack for the 3D Diffuser Actor sidecar client). The "
91.         "policy + the CoppeliaSim/PyRep RLBench env run in tools/rlbench_*"
92.         "_sidecar.py's own externally-provisioned Python 3.10 venv (ADR-0062)."

94.     # `mock` has no external deps — included so a smoke that mentions a

97. }
98. 
99. 
100. # Model-family → ``uv sync --group …`` group names. Parallel to
101. # :data:`_FAMILY_INSTALL_HINTS` but machine-readable so callers can
102. # compose a single ``uv sync`` for the union of missing families
103. # (deploy_sim's pre-flight prompt joins these across all blocked
104. # rSkills into one command).
105. _FAMILY_INSTALL_GROUPS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
106.     "smolvla": ("sim",),
107.     "pi05": ("sim", "libero"),
108.     "act": ("sim",),
109.     "diffusion": ("sim",),
110.     "xvla": ("sim",),
111.     "rldx": ("rldx",),
112.     "gr00t": ("gr00t",),
113.     "diffuser_actor": ("rlbench",),
114.     "mock": (),
115. }
116. 
117. 
118. # Model-family → leaf module(s) whose presence proves the factory will
119. # clear its import gates. Picked to mirror the FIRST import inside each
120. # policy's factory (e.g.
121. # ``from lerobot.policies.smolvla.modeling_smolvla import SmolVLAPolicy``
122. # is what ``_build_smolvla`` does on line 300 of smolvla.py).
123. _FAMILY_REQUIRED_IMPORTS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
124.     "smolvla": ("transformers", "lerobot.policies.smolvla.modeling_smolvla"),
125.     "pi05": ("transformers", "bitsandbytes", "lerobot.policies.pi05.modeling_pi05"),
126.     "act": ("lerobot.policies.act.modeling_act",),
127.     "diffusion": ("lerobot.policies.diffusion.modeling_diffusion",),
128.     "xvla": ("lerobot.policies.xvla.modeling_xvla",),
129.     "rldx": ("zmq", "msgpack"),
130.     # GR00T shares the rldx out-of-process contract; the openral-side client
131.     # only needs the ZMQ + msgpack wire (the 3B policy lives in the sidecar's
132.     # own Py3.10 venv). See openral_sim.policies.gr00t / ADR-0046.
133.     "gr00t": ("zmq", "msgpack"),
134.     # 3D Diffuser Actor shares the out-of-process sidecar contract; the
135.     # openral-side client only needs the ZMQ + msgpack wire (the policy + the
136.     # CoppeliaSim/PyRep RLBench env live in the sidecar's own py3.10 venv).
137.     # See openral_sim.policies.rlbench_3dda / ADR-0062.
138.     "diffuser_actor": ("zmq", "msgpack"),
139.     "mock": (),
140. }
141.

149.     """
```

### ✅ `view`

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/rldx1-ft-rc365-nf4/rskill.yaml (lines 1-130)

<details>
<summary>130 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — openral packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. # Wraps: RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-RC365  — RoboCasa-365 cross-task finetune
3. # Base:  RLWRLD/RLDX-1-PT
4. #
5. # LICENSE: RLWRLD Model License v1.0 (non-commercial). See
6. # rskills/rldx1-ft-libero-nf4/README.md for the full posture.
7. #
8. # RUNTIME: auto-managed out-of-process sidecar (ZMQ + msgpack). Shared
9. # with the rest of the RLDX-1 family. The openral adapter forks
10. # tools/rldx_sidecar.py transparently on first observation; user
11. # workflow is one command:
12. #
13. #   openral sim run --config scenes/sim/robocasa_pnp.yaml \
14. #               --rskill rskills/rldx1-ft-rc365-nf4 \
15. #               --rskill rskills/rldx1-ft-rc365-nf4
16. #
17. # The adapter passes `--embodiment-tag GENERAL_EMBODIMENT` (matches the
18. # upstream model card's inference example). The general_embodiment slot
19. # in this checkpoint is the PandaMobile / RoboCasa-365 contract (NOT
20. # the cross-embodiment pretrain slot).
21. #
22. # Manual boot (debug / shared host) — set OPENRAL_RLDX_AUTO_SPAWN=0
23. # and start the sidecar yourself:
24. #   python tools/rldx_sidecar.py --model RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-RC365 \
25. #       --port 5555 --quantization nf4 \
26. #       --embodiment-tag GENERAL_EMBODIMENT
27. 
28. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29. schema_version: "0.1"
30. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-rldx1-ft-rc365-nf4"
31. evaluated_tasks: ["robocasa"]  # ADR-0060: benchmark task this checkpoint is validated for (gate)
32. version: "0.1.0"
33. license: "rlwrld_non_commercial"
34. role: "s1"
35. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
36. 
37. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38. model_family: "rldx"
39. 
40. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
41. # RoboCasa-365 runs on PandaMobile — same embodiment we already wire
42. # for pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4.
43. embodiment_tags:
44.   - "panda_mobile"
45. 
46. # Three RGB streams: agentview_left / right (256x256) + eye_in_hand.
47. # RoboCasa's RoboCasaGymEnv emits these as
48. # `robot0_agentview_left_image` / `..right_image` / `eye_in_hand_image`,
49. # aliased to camera1/2/3 inside our scene adapter. RC365's general_
50. # embodiment video keys are `video.robot0_agentview_left` /
51. # `..agentview_right` / `..eye_in_hand`; the rldx adapter handles the
52. # rename via _RC365_VIDEO_KEYS in openral_sim.policies.rldx.
53. sensors_required:
54.   - modality: "rgb"
55.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera1"
56.     min_width: 128
57.     min_height: 128
58.   - modality: "rgb"
59.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera2"
60.     min_width: 128
61.     min_height: 128
62.   - modality: "rgb"
63.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera3"
64.     min_width: 128
65.     min_height: 128
66. 
67. # RoboCasa-365 emits per-step deltas on the mobile-Franka arm — not absolute targets.
68. actuators_required:
69.   - kind: "joint_position"
70.     control_mode_semantics:
71.       mode: "delta"
72. 
73. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74. runtime: "pytorch"
75. quantization:
76.   dtype: "int4"
77.   backend: "pytorch"
78. min_vram_gb:
79.   bf16: 18.0
80.   int4: 7.0
81. weights_uri: "hf://RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-RC365"
82. 
83. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
84. processors:
85.   preprocessor_uri: "hf://RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-RC365/policy_preprocessor.json"
86.   postprocessor_uri: "hf://RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-RC365/policy_postprocessor.json"
87. # RC365 was trained against the standard robocasa pipeline
88. # (``gymnasium_basic.RoboCasaEnv.get_basic_observation``), whose
89. # ``process_img`` does ``np.copy(img[::-1, :, :])`` — a vertical-only
90. # flip — before passing every ``*_image`` field to the policy. The
91. # openral RoboCasa scene adapter (``openral_sim.backends.robocasa.
92. # _RoboCasaSim._wrap_obs``) now mirrors that flip at the source so
93. # EVERY consumer (dashboard, /openral/cameras/<name>/image publisher,
94. # this rldx adapter) sees the standard top-down orientation. No
95. # additional flip needed in the rldx adapter — schema defaults apply.
96. # image_preprocessing omitted intentionally.
97. # RC365's general_embodiment state is 16-D laid out as
98. # eef_pos_rel(3) + eef_rot_rel_quat(4) + gripper_qpos(2) + base_pos(3)
99. # + base_rot_quat(4). The openral RoboCasa scene emits a 16-D
100. # human300_16d state — same total dim but with gripper at the END;
101. # the rldx adapter re-slices it into the RC365 5-key obs dict.
102. state_contract:
103.   layout: "rc365"
104.   dim: 16
105.   # ADR-0027 — same panda_mobile bindings as the pi05 / robocasa-nf4
106.   # manifests. The rldx adapter re-slices the assembled vector into
107.   # the RC365 5-key obs dict downstream of this binding.
108.   bindings:
109.     # ``panda_hand_tcp`` (URDF fixed-frame, 0.1034 m along panda_hand's
110.     # local z) matches MJCF's ``gripper0_right_grip_site`` -- the
111.     # eef site robosuite uses to compute ``robot0_base_to_eef_pos``,
112.     # which is what the policy was trained on. ``panda_hand`` alone
113.     # is the wrist body, ~9 cm above the actual grasp point in world,
114.     # which left the assembled base_to_eef.z 9 cm higher than sim_run.
115.     eef_frame: "panda_hand_tcp"
116.     base_frame: "base_link"
117.     # ``odom`` (HAL-published, mirrors MJCF base qpos in deploy_sim
118.     # and wheel-odometry on real hardware) rather than ``map``
119.     # (SLAM-mediated). On a fresh ``openral deploy sim`` launch the
120.     # ``map`` frame is initialised by SLAM at an arbitrary pose so
121.     # ``tf(map, base_link)`` returned coordinates ~14 m off MJCF
122.     # ground truth, and the human300_16d assembler fed the policy
123.     # a state vector that far from the training distribution.
124.     # The training data lives in robocasa's world frame (no SLAM);
125.     # ``odom`` is the matching authoritative source post-ADR-0027.
126.     world_frame: "odom"
127.     # openral's panda_mobile robot.yaml exposes the franka gripper as
128.     # one parallel_gripper joint (per ADR-0028a, the MJCF two-finger
129.     # mimic is collapsed to a single 1-DoF abstraction). The
130.     # ``human300_16d`` assembler mirrors a single binding to ``[v, -v]``
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/rldx1-ft-gr1-nf4/rskill.yaml.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/rldx1-ft-gr1-nf4/rskill.yaml (lines 1-110)

<details>
<summary>110 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — openral packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. # Wraps: RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-GR1  — RLDX-1 finetuned on GR-1 humanoid tabletop tasks
3. # Base:  RLWRLD/RLDX-1-PT
4. #
5. # LICENSE: RLWRLD Model License v1.0 (non-commercial). See
6. # rskills/rldx1-ft-libero-nf4/README.md for the full posture.
7. #
8. # RUNTIME: auto-managed out-of-process sidecar (ZMQ + msgpack). Shared
9. # with the rest of the rldx1-ft-* family. The openral adapter forks
10. # tools/rldx_sidecar.py transparently on first observation; user
11. # workflow is one command:
12. #
13. #   openral sim run --config scenes/sim/robocasa_gr1_pnp_cup_to_drawer.yaml \
14. #               --rskill rskills/rldx1-ft-gr1-nf4 \
15. #               --rskill rskills/rldx1-ft-gr1-nf4
16. #
17. # The adapter passes `--embodiment-tag GENERAL_EMBODIMENT` (the model
18. # card's own inference example) which exposes the Fourier-native 5-key
19. # state contract (right_arm 7 + left_arm 7 + waist 3 + right_hand 6 +
20. # left_hand 6 = 29-D) — override via vla.extra.embodiment_tag or
21. # OPENRAL_RLDX_EMBODIMENT_TAG if needed.
22. #
23. # Manual boot (debug / shared host) — set OPENRAL_RLDX_AUTO_SPAWN=0
24. # and start the sidecar yourself:
25. #   python tools/rldx_sidecar.py --model RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-GR1 \
26. #       --port 5555 --quantization nf4 \
27. #       --embodiment-tag GENERAL_EMBODIMENT
28. 
29. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30. schema_version: "0.1"
31. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-rldx1-ft-gr1-nf4"
32. version: "0.1.0"
33. license: "rlwrld_non_commercial"
34. role: "s1"
35. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
36. 
37. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38. model_family: "rldx"
39. 
40. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
41. # Fourier GR-1 humanoid (ArmsAndWaistFourierHands) — matches the
42. # robots/gr1/robot.yaml shipped with the GR1 tabletop tasks branch.
43. embodiment_tags:
44.   - "gr1"
45. 
46. # RoboCasa GR1 tabletop tasks emit a single head-mounted `camera1`
47. # egoview stream at 256x256 (see scenes/sim/robocasa_gr1_pnp_cup_to_drawer.yaml).
48. sensors_required:
49.   - modality: "rgb"
50.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera1"
51.     min_width: 256
52.     min_height: 256
53. 
54. # GR-1 ArmsAndWaistFourierHands BASIC composite action: 29-D
55. # (right arm 7 + left arm 7 + waist 3 + right hand 6 + left hand 6).
56. # The loader auto-fills n_dof + vla_action_key from robots/gr1/robot.yaml
57. # for joint_position actuators.
58. actuators_required:
59.   - kind: "joint_position"
60.     control_mode_semantics:
61.       mode: "absolute"
62. 
63. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64. runtime: "pytorch"
65. quantization:
66.   dtype: "int4"
67.   backend: "pytorch"
68. min_vram_gb:
69.   bf16: 18.0
70.   int4: 7.0
71. weights_uri: "hf://RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-GR1"
72. 
73. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
74. processors:
75.   preprocessor_uri: "hf://RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-GR1/policy_preprocessor.json"
76.   postprocessor_uri: "hf://RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-GR1/policy_postprocessor.json"
77. # image_preprocessing omitted — GR-1 RoboCasa scenes emit upright frames
78. # (the LIBERO rotate-180 hack is LIBERO-specific). Schema default
79. # (flip_180=False, empty aliases) applies.
80. state_contract:
81.   # 29-D Fourier GR-1 BASIC composite state, emitted by
82.   # openral_sim.backends.robocasa._wrap_obs_gr1 in the order
83.   # [waist(3) | right_arm(7) | left_arm(7) | right_hand(6) | left_hand(6)].
84.   layout: "gr1"
85.   dim: 29
86. 
87. # ── Execution semantics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
88. chunk_size: 16
89. # n_action_steps omitted — equals chunk_size (full chunk replay). The
90. # upstream MSAT head emits 16 actions per inference; replaying the
91. # whole chunk halves the visible viewer stalls (vs the half-chunk
92. # default of 8). Trade-off: 16 env steps of open-loop control between
93. # observations, fine for slow-evolution PnP tasks. Override via
94. # vla.extra.replan_steps in the YAML or --n-action-steps on the CLI.
95. latency_budget:
96.   per_chunk_ms: 1500.0
97. 
98. # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99. 
100. paper_url: "https://huggingface.co/RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-GR1"
101. source_repo: "hf://RLWRLD/RLDX-1-FT-GR1"
102. 
103. description: >
104.   RLDX-1 fine-tuned on the Fourier GR-1 humanoid tabletop tasks (RoboCasa
105.   GR1 fork, 24-task suite). 29-D action space (per-arm joint deltas +
106.   waist + Fourier dexhand grasps); 39-D proprio (joint_pos + per-hand
107.   gripper_qpos). Shares the out-of-process rldx sidecar runtime with the
108.   LIBERO finetune. Non-commercial license.
109. 
110. # ADR-0022 — action vocabulary surfaced to the reasoner LLM tool
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 577m 43s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Running two RoboCasa `sim run` examples with the in-tree PandaMobile rSkill, capped to one step to validate scene/policy wiring without claiming task success.

---

<sub>⏱️ 578m 29s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Run two RoboCasa sim scenes**

$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep
OUT=/home/allopart/.copilot/session-state/d1d8eee7-0fb8-4ea5-b69e-86c20f5df18a/files/extra_sim_scenes_20260623
mkdir -p "$OUT"
run_sim() {
  name="$1"; shift
  log="$OUT/${name}.log"
  save="$OUT/${name}_out"
  rm -rf "$save"
  echo "=== sim $name ==="
  set +e
  OPENRAL_ALLOW_NONCOMMERCIAL=1 OPENRAL_AUTO_INSTALL_DEPS=1 MUJOCO_GL=egl timeout --signal=INT 420s .venv/bin/openral sim run "$@" --no-view --save-dir "$save" >"$log" 2>&1
  code=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$code" -eq 0 ] && grep -q "success_rate:" "$log" && [ -f "$save/summary.json" ]; then
    echo "PASS $name log=$log summary=$save/summary.json"
    grep -E "robot :|scene :|task  :|vla   :|episode_done|success_rate:|wrote " "$log" | tail -20 || true
  else
    echo "FAIL $name code=$code log=$log"
    tail -140 "$log"
  fi
}
run_sim robocasa_pnp_pi05 --config scenes/sim/robocasa_pnp.yaml --rskill rskills/pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4 --n-episodes 1 --max-steps 1 --seed 3
run_sim robocasa_nav_pi05 --config scenes/sim/robocasa_panda_mobile_kitchen.yaml --rskill rskills/pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4 --n-episodes 1 --max-steps 1 --seed 4

```
1. # gym-aloha AlohaInsertion-v0 — ALOHA bimanual fixed.
2. # Run: openral benchmark --config scenes/benchmark/aloha_insertion.yaml \
3. #                        --rskill rskill://rskills/act-aloha-insertion
4. # Note: set MUJOCO_GL=egl.
5. 
6. robot_id: aloha_bimanual
7. 
8. scene:
9.   id: aloha_insertion
10.   backend: mujoco
11.   observation_height: 480
12.   observation_width: 640
13.   cameras: ["top"]
14. 
15. task:
16.   id: aloha_insertion/0
17.   scene_id: aloha_insertion
18.   instruction: "insert the peg"
19.   success_key: is_success
20.   max_steps: 400         # ACT paper sim episode_len (Zhao et al. SIM_TASK_CONFIGS)
21. 
22. n_episodes: 50           # ACT paper eval count
23. seed: 0
24. 
25. metadata:
26.   paper: "https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.13705"
27.   honest_scope: >
28.     AlohaInsertion-v0 — the harder ALOHA task in the original paper.
29.     Official ACT sim protocol: episode_len=400 over 50 eval episodes
30.     (gym-aloha's registration TimeLimit default is 300; we honour the
31.     paper's 400). ACT paper reports ~50% success on insertion.
32.
```

<details>
<summary>106 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — OpenRAL packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. # Wraps: lerobot/act_aloha_sim_insertion_human (MIT)
3. # Paper: Zhao et al., 2023 — Action Chunking Transformer.
4. #
5. # Sibling of skills/act-aloha (which wraps the transfer-cube checkpoint).
6. # This package targets the harder bimanual peg-in-socket *insertion* task
7. # from the same paper. Both skills share the ALOHA 14-DoF action space and
8. # the single 480x640 top camera contract.
9. #
10. # LEGACY PROCESSOR PATH: same shape as rskills/act-aloha — the mean/std
11. # norm stats live inside model.safetensors and the schema processors block
12. # is omitted. Modern lerobot ACTPolicy no longer hosts normalize_inputs/
13. # unnormalize_outputs modules, so it drops those keys on load ("Unexpected
14. # key(s)") — the ACT adapter re-reads them from the safetensors and applies
15. # them manually. Without that the policy runs unnormalised and flails.
16. 
17. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18. schema_version: "0.1"
19. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-act-aloha-insertion"
20. version: "0.1.0"
21. license: "mit"
22. role: "s1"
23. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
24. 
25. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26. model_family: "act"
27. 
28. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
29. embodiment_tags:
30.   - "aloha"
31. 
32. sensors_required:
33.   - modality: "rgb"
34.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.top"
35.     min_width: 640
36.     min_height: 480
37. 
38. # Output side (ADR-0013). For the canonical aloha bimanual embodiment the
39. # loader auto-fills n_dof (14) + vla_action_key from
40. # robots/aloha_bimanual/robot.yaml.
41. actuators_required:
42.   - kind: "joint_position"
43.     control_mode_semantics:
44.       mode: "absolute"
45. 
46. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
47. runtime: "pytorch"
48. quantization:
49.   dtype: "fp32"
50.   backend: "pytorch"
51. weights_uri: "hf://lerobot/act_aloha_sim_insertion_human"
52. 
53. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
54. # processors omitted — legacy path; norm stats live inside model.safetensors.
55. 
56. # ── Execution semantics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
57. chunk_size: 100
58. # n_action_steps omitted — ACT default is 1 (per-step re-inference +
59. # temporal ensembling, paper-faithful).
60. latency_budget:
61.   # Same ACT architecture as the cube-transfer sibling; reference-host
62.   # measurements match within noise. The 25 ms ceiling keeps the
63.   # canonical tolerance_pct=100 → 50 ms test ceiling.
64.   per_chunk_ms: 25.0
65. 
66. # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67. # Headline success rate from skills/act-aloha-insertion/eval/aloha_insertion.json.
68. benchmarks:
69.   aloha_insertion: 0.20
70. 
71. paper_url: "https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.13705"
72. source_repo: "hf://lerobot/act_aloha_sim_insertion_human"
73. 
74. description: >
75.   ACT (~52M params, chunk=100) finetuned on the ALOHA bimanual
76.   sim-insertion demonstration set. Insertion is the harder ALOHA task
77.   in the original paper; the 0.20 success rate reflects that. See the
78.   norm-stats note above re: the legacy safetensors-resident buffers.
79. 
80. # Task-data gate (ADR-0060): trained + validated on the ALOHA sim
81. # insertion task, so the benchmark runner accepts the aloha_insertion
82. # scene and refuses task-mismatched scenes with ROSCapabilityMismatch.
83. evaluated_tasks: ["aloha_insertion"]
84. 
85. # ADR-0022 — action vocabulary surfaced to the reasoner LLM tool
86. # palette so it can pick this skill by what it does (action verb +
87. # object + scene), not just by its slug.
88. actions:
89.   - "insert"
90.   - "pick"
91.   - "place"
92. objects:
93.   - "peg"
94.   - "socket"
95. scenes:
96.   - "tabletop"
97. 
98. # ADR-0019 — per-checkpoint action contract (consumed by the dataset bridge
99. # to bind the LeRobot v3 `action` feature shape).
100. action_contract:
101.   dim: 14
102. 
103. # ADR-0019 — per-checkpoint state contract.
104. state_contract:
105.   dim: 14
106.
```

</details>

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/rskills/smolvla-maniskill-franka/rskill.yaml (lines 1-130)

<details>
<summary>130 lines</summary>

```
1. # rSkill manifest — OpenRAL packaging format V1 (CLAUDE.md §6.4)
2. # Wraps: Calvert0921/smolvla_franka_liftcube_1000
3. # Base:  lerobot/smolvla_base   (arxiv:2506.01844)
4. #
5. # Community SmolVLA finetune on a ManiSkill-style Franka LiftCube task,
6. # trained on the paired dataset Calvert0921/SmolVLA_LiftCube_Franka_1000
7. # (1000 demos). The upstream model card is empty so this rSkill is
8. # wired strictly from the checkpoint's `config.json`:
9. #
10. #   type: smolvla           → routed via the in-tree `smolvla` adapter.
11. #   input_features:
12. #     observation.state              shape (9,)        — Franka qpos
13. #                                                       (7 arm + 2 fingers)
14. #     observation.images.up          shape (3,256,256) — top / overhead view
15. #     observation.images.wrist       shape (3,256,256) — eye-in-hand view
16. #   output_features:
17. #     action                          shape (8,)        — 7 joint pos + 1 gripper
18. #   chunk_size: 50  n_action_steps: 50  flow-matching steps: 10
19. #
20. # Camera-name mapping
21. # -------------------
22. # The ManiSkill3 backend surfaces cameras under `camera1` / `camera2`
23. # (matching the franka_panda RobotDescription's `sensors` block); the
24. # `image_preprocessing.aliases` map below renames those to the model's
25. # `up` / `wrist` keys so the SmolVLA preprocessor finds them.
26. #
27. # End-to-end wiring
28. # -----------------
29. # The companion ManiSkill3 backend changes (this PR) surface every
30. # entry in `sensor_data` as `camera1` / `camera2` / ... in declaration
31. # order, plumb `backend_options.robot_uids` through to `gym.make` so
32. # the YAML's `panda_wristcam` brings in the wrist camera, and forward
33. # `task.max_steps` to MS3's `max_episode_steps` so the rollout isn't
34. # silently truncated at MS3's default 50 steps.
35. #
36. # The upstream model repo (Calvert0921/smolvla_franka_liftcube_1000)
37. # ships only config.json + model.safetensors + train_config.json — no
38. # `policy_*processor.json`. Rather than rely on the SmolVLA adapter's
39. # dataset-stats fallback at every load (a 404 + recompute against
40. # `dataset_uri`'s `meta/episodes_stats.jsonl`), the processor pair was
41. # rebuilt once via `make_pre_post_processors(policy.config,
42. # dataset_stats=...)` and uploaded to THIS rSkill's own HF repo
43. # (OpenRAL/rskill-smolvla-maniskill-franka). The rebuilt normalizer
44. # stats match the checkpoint's baked `normalize_inputs` /
45. # `normalize_targets` buffers exactly, so the processors are faithful to
46. # how the model was trained. The fallback path still exists for any
47. # other community finetune uploaded without processors.
48. 
49. # ── Identity ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50. schema_version: "0.1"
51. name: "OpenRAL/rskill-smolvla-maniskill-franka"
52. version: "0.1.0"
53. license: "apache-2.0"
54. role: "s1"
55. kind: "vla"  # ADR-00XX: rSkill kind discriminator. "vla" = learnable Vision-Language-Action policy.
56. 
57. # ── Policy identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
58. model_family: "smolvla"
59. 
60. # ── Compatibility contract ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
61. # ManiSkill3 PickCube / LiftCube run on the Franka Panda agent — same
62. # embodiment as the LIBERO / RoboCasa Panda rSkills.
63. embodiment_tags:
64.   - "franka_panda"
65. 
66. # Two RGB camera streams matching the checkpoint's input_features. The
67. # franka_panda RobotDescription declares matching `camera1` (agentview /
68. # base_camera) and `camera2` (eye_in_hand / hand_camera) sensors so the
69. # compatibility check resolves cleanly.
70. sensors_required:
71.   - modality: "rgb"
72.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera1"
73.     min_width: 256
74.     min_height: 256
75.   - modality: "rgb"
76.     vla_feature_key: "observation.images.camera2"
77.     min_width: 256
78.     min_height: 256
79. 
80. # Output side (ADR-0013). 7 arm joints + 1 gripper command. For
81. # franka_panda the loader auto-fills n_dof + vla_action_key from
82. # robots/franka_panda/robot.yaml.
83. actuators_required:
84.   - kind: "joint_position"
85.     control_mode_semantics:
86.       mode: "absolute"
87. 
88. # ── Runtime / weights ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
89. runtime: "pytorch"
90. quantization:
91.   dtype: "bf16"
92.   backend: "pytorch"
93. weights_uri: "hf://Calvert0921/smolvla_franka_liftcube_1000"
94. 
95. # ── Preprocessing (all knobs needed to interpret IO) ───────────────────────
96. # Per-file URIs for the lerobot PolicyProcessorPipeline. The upstream
97. # weights repo ships no `policy_*processor.json`, so the pair (plus the
98. # normalizer/unnormalizer `*.safetensors` state files referenced by the
99. # JSON `steps`) was rebuilt from the checkpoint config + training-dataset
100. # stats and uploaded to this rSkill's own HF repo. `materialize_processor_dir`
101. # downloads exactly these files (and their `state_file` siblings) — no 404,
102. # no dataset-stats recompute at load time.
103. processors:
104.   preprocessor_uri: "hf://OpenRAL/rskill-smolvla-maniskill-franka/policy_preprocessor.json"
105.   postprocessor_uri: "hf://OpenRAL/rskill-smolvla-maniskill-franka/policy_postprocessor.json"
106. # Per-checkpoint knobs the processor JSONs don't encode. The model was
107. # trained on raw SAPIEN renders (no 180° flip); cameras need an alias
108. # remap from the in-tree scene-side keys (camera1 / camera2) to the
109. # checkpoint's `up` / `wrist`.
110. image_preprocessing:
111.   flip_180: false
112.   aliases:
113.     camera1: "up"
114.     camera2: "wrist"
115. state_contract:
116.   dim: 9
117. 
118. # ── Execution semantics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
119. # Matches the checkpoint's config.json (chunk_size=50, n_action_steps=50).
120. chunk_size: 50
121. n_action_steps: 50
122. latency_budget:
123.   per_chunk_ms: 200.0   # SmolVLA bf16 on a desktop GPU ≈ 80–150 ms; SAPIEN
124.                         # step adds another ~10–20 ms — generous headroom
125. 
126. # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127. # No benchmarks shipped yet — see eval/.gitkeep. The headline LiftCube
128. # success rate will be populated by `openral benchmark run` once the MS3
129. # backend supports the wrist-camera surface (see header comment).
130. paper_url: "https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.01844"
```

</details>

OUT=/home/allopart/.copilot/session-state/d1d8eee7-0fb8-4ea5-b69e-86c20f5df18a/files/extra_sim_scenes_20260623
mkdir -p "$OUT"

save="$OUT/maniskill_pickcube_smolvla_out"
rm -rf "$save"
MUJOCO_GL=egl timeout --signal=INT 480s .venv/bin/openral sim run --config /home/allopart/.copilot/session-state/d1d8eee7-0fb8-4ea5-b69e-86c20f5df18a/files/maniskill_pick_cube_sim_run.yaml --rskill rskills/smolvla-maniskill-franka --n-episodes 1 --max-steps 1 --seed 0 --no-view --save-dir "$save" >"$log" 2>&1
code=$?
if [ "$code" -eq 0 ] && grep -q "success_rate:" "$log" && [ -f "$save/summary.json" ]; then
  echo "PASS maniskill_pickcube_smolvla log=$log summary=$save/summary.json"
  grep -E "robot :|scene :|task  :|vla   :|episode_done|success_rate:|wrote " "$log" | tail -20
else
  echo "FAIL maniskill_pickcube_smolvla code=$code log=$log"
  tail -180 "$log"
  exit 1
fi

<sub>⏱️ 580m 22s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Difference:** `clock_origin:=host_wall` means ROS node clocks, TF, headers, deadlines, etc. use system wall time and OpenRAL does **not** publish `/clock`. `clock_origin:=simulation` means the HAL publishes `/clock` from simulator elapsed time and the whole ROS graph maps internal `use_sim_time=true` to that authority.

It is **not the same for every `deploy sim`** because not every deploy-sim HAL currently exposes simulator time. Scene-attached HALs like PandaMobile/RoboCasa expose `sim_time_ns()`, so they can safely use `simulation`. Bare-twin HALs like OpenArm/SO-101 currently build their own MuJoCo HAL but do not expose `sim_time_ns()` to the ROS graph, so forcing `simulation` would set `use_sim_time=true` without a live `/clock` and freeze node time at zero. Those stay `host_wall` until their HALs publish a real clock authority.

Ran 4 more `openral sim run` scenes beyond PushT and ALOHA transfer cube:
- RoboCasa PickPlaceCounterToCabinet + `pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4`
- RoboCasa NavigateKitchen + `pi05-robocasa365-human300-nf4`
- ALOHA insertion + `act-aloha-insertion`
- ManiSkill PickCube + `smolvla-maniskill-franka`

All completed short real rollouts and wrote `summary.json`; no repo changes. Posted results to PR #98.

---

<sub>⏱️ 590m 13s</sub>

### 👤 User

Does it make sense to ensure all bare-twin HALs also expose sim_time_ns() through their sim scenes or is that unfeaseable?

---

<sub>⏱️ 590m 17s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes, it makes sense — and it’s feasible — but it should be done as a follow-up, not assumed for this PR.

Bare-twin HALs are still real MuJoCo simulations, so conceptually they should expose `sim_time_ns()` from their `MjData.time`, just like scene-attached `SimAttachedHAL` does. Then `deploy sim` could use `clock_origin:=simulation` consistently for OpenArm/SO-100/SO-101/etc. The reason I didn’t force that here is safety/correctness: today those HALs don’t provide the clock seam, and setting ROS `use_sim_time=true` without a live `/clock` freezes the graph.

The right direction is:
1. Add a common `sim_time_ns()`/`clock_authority()` interface to the bare MuJoCo HAL base.
2. Have OpenArm/SO-100/SO-101 return monotonic `MjData.time` in ns, including reset handling.
3. Enable `/clock` publication for those HALs.
4. Flip their derived `clock_origin` from `host_wall` to `simulation`.
5. Cover with real `deploy sim` boots for each bare-twin robot.

So: **not unfeasible; it’s probably the ideal end-state.** The current PR keeps behavior safe until that seam exists.

---

### 👤 User
