@@ -16,25 +16,27 @@ _HAL Protocol — the normative interface every HAL adapter must satisfy._
 ### `python/hal/src/openral_hal/_mujoco_arm.py`
 _Internal MuJoCo-backed HAL implementation shared by UR / Franka / SO-100 / G1 / H1 / Rizon-4 / OpenArm / ALOHA adapters. Reads its wiring from `RobotDescription.sim` (ADR-0023)._
 
-- `class _MujocoArmInitKwargs(TypedDict)` — Typed shape of the kwargs accepted by `MujocoArmHAL.__init__`. (L60) Lets `MujocoArmHAL._sim_kwargs_for` return a value that unpacks cleanly into the constructor under `mypy --strict` without the `# type: ignore[arg-type]` hatch every thin subclass used to need. Fields: `mjcf_path, joint_qpos_addr, joint_qvel_addr, actuator_index, grippers, keyframe_index, seed_ctrl_from_qpos, settle_steps, gravity_enabled, staleness_limit_s`.
-- `_resolve_mjcf_path(desc: RobotDescription) -> str` [private] — Resolve `desc.assets.mjcf` (ADR-0058) to an absolute MJCF path via `openral_core.assets.resolve_asset`; raises `ROSConfigError` when the ref is unset or unresolvable. Replaced the former public `resolve_mjcf_uri` / `SimDescription.mjcf_uri` (ADR-0058). (L80)
+- `class _MujocoArmInitKwargs(TypedDict)` — Typed shape of the kwargs accepted by `MujocoArmHAL.__init__`. (L61) Lets `MujocoArmHAL._sim_kwargs_for` return a value that unpacks cleanly into the constructor under `mypy --strict` without the `# type: ignore[arg-type]` hatch every thin subclass used to need. Fields: `mjcf_path, joint_qpos_addr, joint_qvel_addr, actuator_index, grippers, keyframe_index, seed_ctrl_from_qpos, settle_steps, gravity_enabled, staleness_limit_s`.
+- `_resolve_mjcf_path(desc: RobotDescription) -> str` [private] — Resolve `desc.assets.mjcf` (ADR-0058) to an absolute MJCF path via `openral_core.assets.resolve_asset`; raises `ROSConfigError` when the ref is unset or unresolvable. Replaced the former public `resolve_mjcf_uri` / `SimDescription.mjcf_uri` (ADR-0058). (L81)
 - `build_hal(description, *, mode: Literal["sim","real"], transport=None, sim_env_yaml=None) -> HAL` — Single seam for constructing a robot's simulation or real-hardware HAL from its manifest (`resolver.py`, L50). `mode="sim"` + `sim_env_yaml` set → calls `build_sim_env_from_yaml` and returns a `SimAttachedHAL` wrapping the scene's `SimRollout` (ADR-0034); bypasses the bare-twin / `hal.sim` class entirely. `mode="sim"` without `sim_env_yaml` builds `description.hal.sim` or derives `MujocoArmHAL.from_description` when it is null + a `sim:` block exists. `mode="real"` imports `description.hal.real` and threads `transport` kwargs (real HALs take `port` / `robot_ip` / `fci_ip` and embed their own description). Both modes merge `description.hal.parameters.defaults` (ADR-0029) **underneath** the explicit `transport` so the manifest carries a robot's construction kwargs; unaccepted keys are dropped. `sim_env_yaml` + `mode="real"` → `ROSConfigError`. Missing HAL for the mode → `ROSCapabilityMismatch`; malformed/unresolvable entry → `ROSConfigError`. Routed by `deploy sim` (sim) and `deploy run` (real). (ADR-0031, ADR-0034)
   - `_import_object(path: str) -> object` [private] — Resolve a `"module.path:Attribute"` import string; raises `ROSConfigError` on malformed/unimportable/missing. **Reuse watch:** the canonical entrypoint-string importer for HAL classes — do not hand-roll `importlib` in HAL callers.
-- `class MujocoArmHAL` — Generic MuJoCo-backed HAL adapter for position-controlled arms (and, via the `_per_step_update` hook, torque-controlled humanoids like the H1). (L113)
+- `class MujocoArmHAL` — Generic MuJoCo-backed HAL adapter for position-controlled arms (and, via the `_per_step_update` hook, torque-controlled humanoids like the H1). (L114)
   - `read_images() -> dict[str, NDArray]` — Render the manifest's RGB `SensorSpec`s off the live MJCF, keyed by sensor `name` (issue #191 Phase 3b). Same contract `SimAttachedHAL.read_images` exposes, so `SimSensorBridge` publishes a composed-scene arm's cameras (openarm) through the shared path. Renders the MJCF camera `sim_camera_name or name` **at that sensor's own `intrinsics` resolution** — one `mujoco.Renderer` is cached per distinct `(height, width)`, so e.g. a 256×256 wrist camera alongside a 640×480 overhead publishes 256×256 (not a shared max); the published frame size always matches the sensor's camera model. A missing camera / render error is skipped with a one-shot warning (never raises). Renderers are created lazily per resolution so each EGL context binds on the caller (executor) thread. Returns `{}` when disconnected / no RGB sensors / after a renderer failure.
-  - `__init__(description, *, mjcf_path, joint_qpos_addr, actuator_index, joint_qvel_addr=None, grippers=(), keyframe_index=None, seed_ctrl_from_qpos=False, settle_steps=1, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5)` — Init only; MJCF is not loaded until `connect()`. `joint_qvel_addr` defaults to `joint_qpos_addr` (correct for arms without a floating base) and is passed explicitly by humanoid HALs like `G1MujocoHAL` / `H1MujocoHAL` where the free joint shifts the qvel indices by 1. `grippers` is a sequence of `SimGripperDescription` entries; single-arm robots ship one (or none), bimanual robots (Aloha, OpenArm) ship two. (L163)
+  - `__init__(description, *, mjcf_path, joint_qpos_addr, actuator_index, joint_qvel_addr=None, grippers=(), keyframe_index=None, seed_ctrl_from_qpos=False, settle_steps=1, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5)` — Init only; MJCF is not loaded until `connect()`. `joint_qvel_addr` defaults to `joint_qpos_addr` (correct for arms without a floating base) and is passed explicitly by humanoid HALs like `G1MujocoHAL` / `H1MujocoHAL` where the free joint shifts the qvel indices by 1. `grippers` is a sequence of `SimGripperDescription` entries; single-arm robots ship one (or none), bimanual robots (Aloha, OpenArm) ship two. (L164)
   - `_per_step_update(targets) -> None` — Hook invoked before every `mj_step` inside the settle loop. Default no-op; subclasses driving torque-mode actuators (`H1MujocoHAL`) override to recompute the actuator torque each step from the current `qpos` / `qvel`.
   - `connect() -> None` — Load MJCF, prepare `MjData` buffer. Before compiling, runs the generic camera rig (`_camera_rig.rig_cameras_into_mjcf`, ADR-0065): if the MJCF lacks a manifest RGB camera that declares a `sim_placement`, it splices the camera (+ visual-only floor + fill light) into a sibling `<name>_camrig.xml` and loads that — so a bare-arm deploy twin (so100/so101) renders its declared cameras without a scene composer. Idempotent: a scene-attached / composed MJCF that already has the cameras loads unchanged.
   - `disconnect() -> None` — Release the MuJoCo model (idempotent).
   - `read_state() -> JointState` — Joint state in description-joint order. Reads live in-process `MjData` (always current), so it **never latches `ROSPerceptionStale`**: a gap > `staleness_limit_s` since the last service means the single-threaded executor was starved (e.g. a slow camera render), not bad data — it emits a one-shot `hal.read_state.starved` WARNING and returns the live state (re-armed on the next healthy read). The prior behaviour raised *before* refreshing the clock, so one transient stall bricked the HAL permanently (the deploy-sim "Joint state is X s old" loop). Async live-feedback staleness is policed by the subscription HALs (`ros_control`/`aloha`), not here.
   - `send_action(action: Action) -> None` — Forward last waypoint to MuJoCo and step. Stamps `_last_action_ns` so the idle stepper yields to a recent command.
+  - `sim_time_ns() -> int | None` — Bare-twin MuJoCo elapsed time in ns, read from live `MjData.time`; `None` before connect / after disconnect or e-stop. This is the `/clock` seam for OpenArm / SO-100 / SO-101 deploy-sim graphs, matching `SimAttachedHAL.sim_time_ns()` for scene-attached rollouts.
+  - `clock_authority() -> ClockAuthority` — Return `ClockAuthority.simulation("mujoco", timestep_s=model.opt.timestep)` while connected, otherwise `ClockAuthority.host_wall()`.
   - `idle_step() -> bool` — **Sim-only** HOLD stepper that gives a bare `MujocoArmHAL` the ADR-0034 (idle cameras stay live) + ADR-0049 (joint_state published off the executor via `ProprioSnapshot` + dedicated thread) treatment the lifecycle node gates on a *callable* `idle_step`. Leaves `ctrl` untouched (it already holds the last commanded / seeded pose) and advances one `mj_step` so physics + the staleness clock refresh without moving the arm. Returns `False` (no-op) when not connected — i.e. after `estop()` disconnects — so it can never autonomously drive an e-stopped robot; `True` otherwise. **Defined on the sim-only `MujocoArmHAL` hierarchy only** (no real HAL inherits it), mirroring the same method-only real-hardware guard as `SimAttachedHAL.idle_step`.
   - **(property)** `last_action_ns -> int` — `time.monotonic_ns()` of the last `send_action` (`0` if never actuated → idle-stepping starts immediately). The `SimSensorBridge` reads it (`should_idle_step`) to yield the idle stepper to a recently-commanded skill. Mirrors `SimAttachedHAL.last_action_ns`.
-  - `reset_to_pose(pose: list[float]) -> None` — Snap `qpos` to a manifest `starting_pose` and re-seed `ctrl` (instantaneous teleport; best-effort). The ADR-0053 collision-aware alternative is **not** a HAL method — the runner dispatches the `rskill-moveit-joints` rSkill to plan a collision-free MoveGroup motion to `starting_pose` (see `05-inference-runner` / `08-cli`). (L555)
+  - `reset_to_pose(pose: list[float]) -> None` — Snap `qpos` to a manifest `starting_pose` and re-seed `ctrl` (instantaneous teleport; best-effort). The ADR-0053 collision-aware alternative is **not** a HAL method — the runner dispatches the `rskill-moveit-joints` rSkill to plan a collision-free MoveGroup motion to `starting_pose` (see `05-inference-runner` / `08-cli`). (L587)
   - `estop() -> None` — Zero `ctrl` and raise `ROSEStopRequested`.
-  - **(classmethod)** `from_description(description, *, settle_steps=None, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5, mjcf_path_override=None) -> MujocoArmHAL` — Manifest-driven constructor. Reads `description.sim` and builds the HAL with the right MJCF path, qpos/qvel/actuator maps and gripper config. Removes the need for per-robot Python subclasses (ADR-0023). (L858)
-  - **(staticmethod)** `_sim_kwargs_for(description, *, settle_steps=None, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5, mjcf_path_override=None) -> _MujocoArmInitKwargs` — Translate `description.sim` into the `__init__` kwarg dict.  Default 1:1 joint→qpos/actuator mapping is derived from `description.joints`, offset by 7 (qpos) / 6 (qvel) when `sim.floating_base=True`.  Used by both `from_description`, `_init_from_description`, and any caller that wants to post-process the kwargs. (L782)
-  - **(instance method)** `_init_from_description(description, *, mjcf_path=None, settle_steps=None, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5) -> None` — Seam every thin per-robot subclass (UR5e/UR10e, Franka, ALOHA, OpenArm, Rizon4, G1, H1, SO-100) uses to drop the boilerplate `super().__init__(DESC, **MujocoArmHAL._sim_kwargs_for(DESC, …))` dance. Subclasses keep their typed `__init__(*, mjcf_path, settle_steps, gravity_enabled, staleness_limit_s)` signature (so IDEs still surface the four user-tunable knobs) and forward straight to here. (L914)
+  - **(classmethod)** `from_description(description, *, settle_steps=None, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5, mjcf_path_override=None) -> MujocoArmHAL` — Manifest-driven constructor. Reads `description.sim` and builds the HAL with the right MJCF path, qpos/qvel/actuator maps and gripper config. Removes the need for per-robot Python subclasses (ADR-0023). (L890)
+  - **(staticmethod)** `_sim_kwargs_for(description, *, settle_steps=None, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5, mjcf_path_override=None) -> _MujocoArmInitKwargs` — Translate `description.sim` into the `__init__` kwarg dict.  Default 1:1 joint→qpos/actuator mapping is derived from `description.joints`, offset by 7 (qpos) / 6 (qvel) when `sim.floating_base=True`.  Used by both `from_description`, `_init_from_description`, and any caller that wants to post-process the kwargs. (L814)
+  - **(instance method)** `_init_from_description(description, *, mjcf_path=None, settle_steps=None, gravity_enabled=True, staleness_limit_s=0.5) -> None` — Seam every thin per-robot subclass (UR5e/UR10e, Franka, ALOHA, OpenArm, Rizon4, G1, H1, SO-100) uses to drop the boilerplate `super().__init__(DESC, **MujocoArmHAL._sim_kwargs_for(DESC, …))` dance. <sub>⏱️ 602m 37s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Pushed the follow-up to PR #98: `b288754 fix(clock): publish sim time from bare mujoco HALs`.

Bare MuJoCo twins now expose `sim_time_ns()` / `clock_authority()`, and OpenArm, SO-100, and SO-101 deploy-sim paths resolve to `clock_origin:=simulation`. I also posted the validation matrix to the PR, including real deploy boots for all three showing `/clock` publishing from sim time and HAL activation without the previous missing sim-clock warning.

<sub>⏱️ 604m 30s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes for the intended matrix, with one caveat: **simulation time is used when the backend/HAL can actually expose a simulator clock**.

- **`deploy sim`**: MuJoCo scene-attached HALs and bare MuJoCo twins now use `clock_origin:=simulation` and publish ROS `/clock`. That covers OpenArm, SO-100, SO-101, RoboCasa/robosuite, LIBERO/ALOHA/MetaWorld-style MuJoCo paths, and other registered sim-clock-capable backends.
- **`sim run`**: uses the backend rollout clock where available (`sim_time_ns()` / sim elapsed time). It doesn’t need ROS `/clock` unless it is booting a ROS deploy graph.
- **Real deploy / hardware**: stays `host_wall` today, with `hardware_synced` reserved for future PTP/device-clock authority.

So not literally “all scenes” if a scene/backend is clockless or a sidecar has not implemented `sim_time_ns()` yet; those safely remain `host_wall` instead of pretending to run in sim time.

450. def _scene_backend_has_sim_clock(config: Path | None) -> bool:
451.     """Return whether a DeployScene backend can be the OpenRAL simulation clock authority."""
452.     if config is None:
453.         return False
454.     from openral_core import DeployScene, PhysicsBackend, load_scene_strict
455. 
456.     scene = load_scene_strict(str(config), DeployScene)
457.     return scene.scene.backend in {
458.         PhysicsBackend.MUJOCO,
459.         PhysicsBackend.MUJOCO_MJX,
460.         PhysicsBackend.ISAACSIM,
461.         PhysicsBackend.SAPIEN,
462.     }
463. 
464. 
465. def _resolve_clock_origin(*, hal_mode: str, config: Path | None) -> str:
466.     """Resolve the OpenRAL clock authority origin for the launch graph.
467. 
468.     Real deployments use host wall time. Sim deployments use simulator elapsed
469.     time when the deploy scene backend exposes a sim clock. Scene-attached HALs
470.     and bare MuJoCo twins both expose ``sim_time_ns``; clock-less scenes stay in
471.     host-wall time so ROS node clocks never pin at zero.
472.     """
473.     if hal_mode != "sim":
474.         return "host_wall"
475.     return "simulation" if _scene_backend_has_sim_clock(config) else "host_wall"
476. 
477. 
478. def _omdet_runtime_available() -> bool:

1. """Sim rollout protocol — the typed contract every scene adapter must satisfy.
2. 
3. A :class:`SimRollout` is what comes out of :func:`openral_sim.make_env`.
4. It is *gym-flavoured* (``reset``, ``step``, ``close``) but intentionally
5. narrower so adapters can wrap any underlying engine (gymnasium, dm_env,
6. custom) without leaking framework specifics into the eval layer.
7. """
8. 
9. from __future__ import annotations
10. 
11. from dataclasses import dataclass, field
12. from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Protocol, runtime_checkable
13. 
14. import numpy as np
15. from numpy.typing import NDArray
16. 
17. if TYPE_CHECKING:
18.     from openral_core import SceneSpec, TaskSpec
19. 
20. 
21. def sim_time_ns_from_mujoco_handles(handles: tuple[Any, Any] | None) -> int | None:
22.     """Derive sim-time nanoseconds from a MuJoCo ``(model, data)`` handle pair.
23. 
24.     Shared helper for the MuJoCo-backed :class:`SimRollout` adapters
25.     (``robocasa`` / ``libero`` / ``metaworld`` / ``aloha`` / the native
26.     backends) so the ``data.time``-to-nanoseconds conversion lives in exactly
27.     one place (ADR-0048 Phase 1). ``mujoco.MjData.time`` is the authoritative
28.     elapsed simulation time in seconds, advanced by ``model.opt.timestep`` on
29.     every physics step; this rounds ``data.time * 1e9`` to the nearest integer
30.     nanosecond.
31. 
32.     Args:
33.         handles: The adapter's ``mujoco_handles()`` return — a
34.             ``(MjModel, MjData)`` tuple, or ``None`` when the backend exposes
35.             no live MuJoCo handle. ``model`` is unused (kept so callers pass the
36.             tuple verbatim).
37. 
38.     Returns:
39.         ``round(data.time * 1e9)`` when ``handles`` is a tuple, else ``None``
40.         (no MuJoCo clock available).
41. 
42.     Example:
43.         >>> sim_time_ns_from_mujoco_handles(None) is None
44.         True
45.     """
46.     if handles is None:
47.         return None
48.     _model, data = handles
49.     return round(float(data.time) * 1e9)
50. 
51. 
52. Observation = dict[str, Any]
53. """Free-form observation dict — keys are adapter-specific.
54. 
55. Adapters SHOULD include at least:
56.     * ``"images"`` → ``dict[str, np.ndarray]`` of HWC uint8 RGB frames.
57.     * ``"state"``  → 1-D float32 NumPy array of proprioception.
58.     * ``"task"``   → str natural-language instruction.
59. 
60. The :class:`~openral_sim.policy.PolicyAdapter` is responsible for mapping
61. this dict to its own input format. This keeps the eval layer agnostic to VLA
62. input conventions.
63. """
64. 
65. 
66. @dataclass
67. class StepResult:
68.     """One environment transition.
69. 
70.     Attributes:
71.         observation: New observation dict after applying the action.
72.         reward: Scalar reward (gym semantics).
73.         terminated: Whether the episode ended naturally (success / failure).
74.         truncated: Whether the episode hit its step budget.
75.         info: Adapter-specific dict; runners read ``info[task.success_key]``.
76.     """
77. 
78.     observation: Observation
79.     reward: float
80.     terminated: bool
81.     truncated: bool
82.     info: dict[str, Any]
83. 
84. 
85. @runtime_checkable
86. class SimRollout(Protocol):
87.     """Minimal gym-style env contract used by the eval runner.
88. 
89.     Every scene factory in :data:`openral_sim.SCENES` must return an
90.     object satisfying this protocol.
91. 
92.     Attributes:
93.         scene: Scene spec the rollout was built from.
94.         task:  Task spec it is currently solving.
95. 
96.     Optional duck-typed extensions (any of, mutually exclusive per adapter):
97.         ``mujoco_handles(self) -> tuple[mujoco.MjModel, mujoco.MjData] | None``
98.             — MuJoCo-backed adapters expose this so ``openral sim run --view``
99.             can open a passive ``mujoco.viewer`` window against the
100.             adapter's own ``MjModel`` / ``MjData``.
101. 
102.         ``sim_time_ns(self) -> int | None``
103.             — ADR-0048 Phase 1. The backend's authoritative elapsed
104.             simulation time in nanoseconds — the seam a sim ``/clock``
105.             publisher reads so the deploy-sim ROS graph runs on simulation
106.             time rather than wall time. Contract:
107. 
108.             * MuJoCo-backed adapters return ``round(MjData.time * 1e9)`` (the
109.               physics clock advanced by ``model.opt.timestep`` per step) via
110.               :func:`sim_time_ns_from_mujoco_handles`.
111.             * The value is **monotonic non-decreasing within a single
112.               episode**. It is NOT guaranteed monotonic across ``reset`` —
113.               backends that rewind ``MjData.time`` to ``0`` on reset (e.g.
114.               robocasa) restart their clock, so a consumer that needs
115.               cross-reset monotonicity maintains its own offset (see
116.               :meth:`openral_hal.sim_attached.SimAttachedHAL.sim_time_ns`).
117.             * Returns ``None`` when the backend has **no sim clock** —
118.               clock-less backends (PushT) and out-of-process sidecars
119.               (Isaac Sim) whose time the adapter cannot read.
120.
```

</details>

1. """gym-pusht scene adapter — wraps ``gym_pusht/PushT-v0``.
2. 
3. Driven by a :class:`openral_core.SimEnvironment` config, this adapter is
4. the canonical entry point for PushT (pymunk 2-D rigid-body) rollouts (used
5. by ``tests/sim/test_pusht_2d_diffusion_pusht.py``).
6. 
7. gym-pusht is opt-in: the underlying ``gym_pusht`` package is heavy and only
8. linux-supported. The factory imports lazily so this module is safe to
9. import on any platform; the failure surfaces at ``make_env()`` time.
10. """
11. 
12. from __future__ import annotations
13. 
14. from dataclasses import dataclass
15. from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
16. 
17. import numpy as np
18. from numpy.typing import NDArray
19. from openral_core.exceptions import ROSConfigError
20. 
21. from openral_sim.registry import SCENES
22. from openral_sim.rollout import StepResult
23. 
24. if TYPE_CHECKING:
25.     from openral_core import SceneSpec, SimEnvironment, TaskSpec
26. 
27.     from openral_sim.rollout import Observation
28. 
29. 
30. _PUSHT_SCENE_ID = "pusht"
31. _PUSHT_ENV_ID = "gym_pusht/PushT-v0"
32. 
33. 
34. @dataclass
35. class _PushTSim:
36.     """Thin :class:`SimRollout` wrapper around ``gym_pusht/PushT-v0``."""
37. 
38.     scene: SceneSpec
39.     task: TaskSpec
40.     _env: Any  # gymnasium env
41.     _last_pixels: NDArray[np.uint8] | None = None
42.     # Live-view pygame window populated by enable_intrinsic_viewer().
43.     # ``render_mode="human"`` is *not* an option here: gym_pusht zeroes
44.     # the pixel obs when human-mode owns the window, which breaks the
45.     # Diffusion Policy adapter (it consumes ``observation.image``). We
46.     # therefore keep the env in rgb_array mode and paint each frame to
47.     # our own window from inside reset / step.
48.     _view_screen: Any = None
49.     _view_pygame: Any = None
50. 
51.     def reset(self, seed: int | None = None) -> Observation:
52.         obs, _info = self._env.reset(seed=seed)
53.         wrapped = self._wrap_obs(obs)
54.         self._paint_view()
55.         return wrapped
56. 
57.     def step(self, action: NDArray[np.float32]) -> StepResult:
58.         a = np.asarray(action, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1)
59.         obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info = self._env.step(a)
60.         wrapped = self._wrap_obs(obs)
61.         self._paint_view()
62.         return StepResult(
63.             observation=wrapped,
64.             reward=float(reward),
65.             terminated=bool(terminated),
66.             truncated=bool(truncated),
67.             info=dict(info),
68.         )
69. 
70.     def render(self) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
71.         if self._last_pixels is None:
72.             return None
73.         return self._last_pixels.copy()
74. 
75.     def close(self) -> None:
76.         if self._view_pygame is not None:
77.             try:
78.                 self._view_pygame.display.quit()
79.             finally:
80.                 self._view_screen = None
81.                 self._view_pygame = None
82.         self._env.close()
83. 
84.     def enable_intrinsic_viewer(self) -> None:
85.         # Open a pygame window we drive from _paint_view(). SimRunner
86.         # calls this once at activate() time, before the first reset, so
87.         # the window exists for every paint that follows.
88.         import pygame
89. 
90.         pygame.init()
91.         pygame.display.init()
92.         # gym_pusht renders at 96x96 by default; upscale 5x so the
93.         # rollout is legible in a desktop window without forcing a
94.         # second resampling step on the policy's 96x96 input.
95.         size = (480, 480)
96.         self._view_screen = pygame.display.set_mode(size)
97.         pygame.display.set_caption("openral sim — gym_pusht (Diffusion Policy)")
98.         self._view_pygame = pygame
99. 
100.     def _paint_view(self) -> None:
101.         # No-op when --view is off. When on, blit the last pixel frame
102.         # (HWC uint8 RGB) scaled to the window. The pixel frame is the
103.         # same array the policy consumes, so what you see is exactly
104.         # what the network sees.
105.         if self._view_screen is None or self._last_pixels is None:
106.             return
107.         pygame = self._view_pygame
108.         # pygame.surfarray expects (W, H, 3); env frames are (H, W, 3).
109.         surf = pygame.surfarray.make_surface(self._last_pixels.swapaxes(0, 1))
110.         surf = pygame.transform.scale(surf, self._view_screen.get_size())
111.         self._view_screen.blit(surf, (0, 0))
112.         pygame.display.flip()
113.         # Pump the OS event queue so the window stays responsive (and
114.         # the WM doesn't mark it "not responding"). We drain quit / X
115.         # events with the same effect as a deadman switch.
116.         for event in pygame.event.get():
117.             if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
118.                 # User closed the window — best-effort: close the window,
119.                 # let the rollout continue offscreen. We deliberately
120.                 # don't raise; killing the policy mid-rollout would
121.                 # invalidate the trace.
122.                 pygame.display.quit()
123.                 self._view_screen = None
124.                 self._view_pygame = None
125.                 return
126. 
127.     def _wrap_obs(self, obs: dict[str, Any]) -> Observation:
128.         """Translate gym-pusht obs into the eval-layer Observation schema."""
129.         pixels = obs.get("pixels")
130.         agent_pos = obs.get("agent_pos")
131. 
132.         pixel_arr: NDArray[np.uint8] | None = None
133.         if pixels is not None:
134.             pixel_arr = np.asarray(pixels).astype(np.uint8)
135.             self._last_pixels = pixel_arr
136. 
137.         # Diffusion Policy adapter reads `images["camera1"]` by default;
138.         # expose the PushT topdown stream under that key.
139.         images: dict[str, NDArray[np.uint8]] = {}
140.         if pixel_arr is not None:
141.             images["camera1"] = pixel_arr
142. 
143.         state = (
144.             np.asarray(agent_pos, dtype=np.float32)
145.             if agent_pos is not None
146.             else np.zeros(2, dtype=np.float32)
147.         )
148. 
149.         return {
150.             "images": images,
151.             "state": state,
152.             "task": self.task.instruction,
153.             "raw": obs,
154.         }
155. 
156. 
157. def _build_pusht_scene(env_cfg: SimEnvironment) -> _PushTSim:
158.     """Lazily import ``gym_pusht`` and build a :class:`_PushTSim`."""
159.     if env_cfg.scene.id != _PUSHT_SCENE_ID:
160.         raise ROSConfigError(
161.             f"pusht scene id must be {_PUSHT_SCENE_ID!r}, got {env_cfg.scene.id!r}"
162.         )
163. 
164.     try:
165.         import gym_pusht  # noqa: F401  (registers gym envs as a side effect)
166.         import gymnasium as gym
167.     except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover
168.         raise ROSConfigError(
169.             "gym-pusht backend not installed; install with: just sync --all-packages --group sim"
170.         ) from exc
171. 
172.     env = gym.make(
173.         _PUSHT_ENV_ID,
174.         obs_type="pixels_agent_pos",
175.     )
176.     return _PushTSim(scene=env_cfg.scene, task=env_cfg.task, _env=env)
177. 
178. 
179. # gym-pusht hard-wires the 2-D pusher; the scene rejects mismatched robot_id values.
180. SCENES.register(_PUSHT_SCENE_ID, fixed_robot="pusht_2d")(_build_pusht_scene)
181.
```

280.     Kept for backwards-compatibility / tests that probe a single stream;
281.     the multi-camera surface used at runtime is :func:`_extract_rgb_streams`.

283.     streams = _extract_rgb_streams(flat)
284.     if not streams:
285.         return None
286.     return next(iter(streams.values()))
287. 
288. 
289. def _extract_rgb_streams(flat: Any) -> dict[str, NDArray[np.uint8]]:
290.     """Pull every RGB camera stream from a ManiSkill3 obs dict.
291. 
292.     Returns an ordered ``{"camera1": rgb1, "camera2": rgb2, ...}`` map
293.     that mirrors the declaration order in ``sensor_data``. The keys are
294.     the scene-side names the franka_panda RobotDescription declares;
295.     an rSkill manifest's ``image_preprocessing.aliases`` block renames
296.     them to model-side keys before preprocessing.
297.     """
298.     if not isinstance(flat, dict):
299.         return {}
300.     sensor_data = flat.get("sensor_data")
301.     if not isinstance(sensor_data, dict):
302.         return {}
303.     out: dict[str, NDArray[np.uint8]] = {}
304.     for idx, cam_obs in enumerate(sensor_data.values(), start=1):
305.         if isinstance(cam_obs, dict):
306.             rgb = cam_obs.get("rgb")
307.             if rgb is not None:
308.                 out[f"camera{idx}"] = np.asarray(rgb, dtype=np.uint8)
309.     return out
310. 
311. 
312. def _extract_state(flat: Any) -> NDArray[np.float32]:
313.     """Concatenate ``agent.qpos`` + ``agent.qvel`` into a 1-D state vector."""
314.     if not isinstance(flat, dict):
315.         return np.zeros(0, dtype=np.float32)
316.     agent = flat.get("agent")
317.     if not isinstance(agent, dict):
318.         return np.zeros(0, dtype=np.float32)
319.     parts: list[NDArray[np.float32]] = []
320.     for key in ("qpos", "qvel"):
321.         value = agent.get(key)
322.         if value is not None:
323.             parts.append(np.asarray(value, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1))
324.     return np.concatenate(parts) if parts else np.zeros(0, dtype=np.float32)
325. 
326. 
327. @SCENES.register(_MANISKILL3_SCENE_ID)
328. def _build_maniskill3_scene(env_cfg: SimEnvironment) -> _ManiSkill3Sim:
329.     """Lazily import ``mani_skill`` and build a :class:`_ManiSkill3Sim`."""
330.     from openral_sim._deps import ensure_backend_deps
331. 
332.     ensure_backend_deps("maniskill3")
333. 
334.     try:
335.         import gymnasium as gym
336.         import mani_skill.envs  # type: ignore[import-not-found,import-untyped,unused-ignore]  # noqa: F401  reason: opt-in via --group maniskill3, registers gym envs
337.     except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover — tested via runtime error path
338.         # ensure_backend_deps re-probes after running its plan; this
339.         # branch is only reached when the install ran but mani_skill
340.         # still refuses to import (e.g. SAPIEN wheel mismatch).
341.         raise ROSConfigError(
342.             "ManiSkill3 backend installed but `mani_skill` still refuses to "
343.             "import. Inspect the auto-install output above and re-run: "
344.             "uv sync --all-packages --group maniskill3 --inexact"
345.         ) from exc
346. 
347.     env_id = _parse_task_id(env_cfg.task.id)
348.     # Single-env eval — the harness expects one Observation per step, and
349.     # the per-(task, seed) outer loop in run_benchmark is the right place
350.     # to parallelise (clear semantics, OTel spans per episode).
```

</details>

310.     def _wrap_obs(self, obs: Any) -> Observation:
311.         """Translate the underlying ManiSkill3 obs into the eval Observation."""
312.         flat = _unbatch_obs(obs)
313.         image = _extract_rgb(flat)
314.         state = _extract_state(flat)
315.         # Rebuild the legacy SimplerEnv ``agent.eef_pos`` vector so the
316.         # rldx ``simpler_widowx`` / ``simpler_google`` wire-shapers can
317.         # consume the same 8-D ``[x, y, z, qw, qx, qy, qz, gripper]``
318.         # contract the upstream reference implementation reads.
319.         eef_pos = _compute_eef_pos(self._env, _resolve_gripper_qpos(flat))
320.         if eef_pos is not None and isinstance(flat, dict):
321.             agent = flat.setdefault("agent", {})
322.             if isinstance(agent, dict):
323.                 agent["eef_pos"] = eef_pos
324.         if image is not None:
325.             self._last_image = image
326.         h = self.scene.observation_height
327.         w = self.scene.observation_width
328.         if image is None:
329.             image = np.zeros((h, w, 3), dtype=np.uint8)
330.         return {
331.             "images": {"camera1": image},
332.             "state": state,
333.             "task": self.task.instruction,
334.             "raw": flat,
335.         }

339. def _build_simpler_env_scene(env_cfg: SimEnvironment) -> _SimplerEnvSim:

344.     RoboCasa: one Rich banner + ``typer.confirm`` (bypassed by

210. def _locate_sidecar_script() -> Path:
211.     override = os.environ.get(_SIDECAR_SCRIPT_ENV)
212.     if override:
213.         p = Path(override).expanduser().resolve()
214.         if not p.is_file():
215.             raise ROSConfigError(f"{_SIDECAR_SCRIPT_ENV}={override!r} is not a file.")
216.         return p
217.     here = Path(__file__).resolve()
218.     for parent in here.parents:
219.         candidate = parent / "tools" / "rlbench_sidecar.py"
220.         if candidate.is_file():
221.             return candidate
222.     raise ROSConfigError(
223.         f"Could not locate tools/rlbench_sidecar.py upwards from {here}. Set "
224.         f"{_SIDECAR_SCRIPT_ENV} to its absolute path."
225.     )

88. def _coerce_int(value: object, default: int) -> int:
89.     """Best-effort int coercion for values pulled from a dict[str, object]."""
90.     if value is None:
91.         return default
92.     if isinstance(value, bool):
93.         return int(value)
94.     if isinstance(value, (int, float, str)):
95.         return int(value)
96.     raise TypeError(f"cannot coerce {value!r} to int")

99. @SCENES.register("mock")
100. def _build_mock_scene(env_cfg: SimEnvironment) -> _MockSim:
101.     """Build the mock scene from the SimEnvironment.
102. 
103.     The optional ``backend_options`` dict supports:
104.         ``success_step`` (int): step at which the mock task succeeds.
105.         ``action_dim``   (int): action vector dimensionality.
106.     """

108.     return _MockSim(
109.         scene=env_cfg.scene,
110.         task=env_cfg.task,
111.         success_step=_coerce_int(opts.get("success_step"), 5),
112.         action_dim=_coerce_int(opts.get("action_dim"), _MOCK_ACTION_DIM),
113.     )

78.     from mani_skill.agents.registration import (  # type: ignore[import-not-found,import-untyped,unused-ignore]  # reason: opt-in dep (--group maniskill3), no py.typed
79.         REGISTERED_AGENTS,

85.     env_spec = REGISTERED_ENVS.get(env_id)
86.     if env_spec is None:
87.         # Unknown env id — let gym.make raise its own (clearer) error.
88.         return
89.     supported = getattr(env_spec.cls, "SUPPORTED_ROBOTS", None)
90.     if not supported:
91.         # Task imposes no allowlist — any robot is acceptable.
92.         return
93.     # Entries may be tuples (multi-agent tasks); flatten to a uid set.
94.     supported_uids: set[str] = set()
95.     for entry in supported:
96.         if isinstance(entry, (list, tuple)):
97.             supported_uids.update(entry)
98.         else:
99.             supported_uids.add(entry)
100.     if robot_uids in supported_uids:
101.         return
102.     agent_spec = REGISTERED_AGENTS.get(robot_uids)
103.     if agent_spec is not None:
104.         for base in agent_spec.agent_cls.__mro__:
105.             if getattr(base, "uid", None) in supported_uids:
106.                 # Registered camera-variant of a supported base — usable.
107.                 return
108.     raise ROSCapabilityMismatch(
109.         f"ManiSkill3 task {env_id!r} does not support robot_uids={robot_uids!r}. "
110.         f"Supported base robots: {sorted(supported_uids)}. Set "
111.         "scene.backend_options.robot_uids to one of these, or to a registered "
112.         "camera-variant subclass of one (e.g. 'panda_wristcam' for 'panda')."
113.     )

116. _UNSUPPORTED_ROBOT_WARNING = "not in the task's list of supported robots"

118. 
119. class _DropUnsupportedRobotWarning(logging.Filter):
120.     """Drops MS3's false "unsupported robot" warning for validated variants."""
121. 
122.     def filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool:
123.         return _UNSUPPORTED_ROBOT_WARNING not in record.getMessage()
124. 
125. 
126. @contextlib.contextmanager
127. def _suppress_unsupported_robot_warning() -> Iterator[None]:
128.     """Silence MS3's false "not in the task's list of supported robots" warning.
129. 
130.     Used around ``gym.make`` *after* :func:`_reconcile_robot_uids` has already
131.     validated the requested robot (so the warning is provably a false positive
132.     about a registered camera-variant). Only that one message is dropped; every
133.     other ``mani_skill`` log record passes through untouched.
134.     """
135.     logger = logging.getLogger("mani_skill")
136.     filt = _DropUnsupportedRobotWarning()
137.     logger.addFilter(filt)
138.     try:
139.         yield
140.     finally:
141.         logger.removeFilter(filt)
142. 
143. 
144. @dataclass
145. class _ManiSkill3Sim:
146.     """Thin :class:`SimRollout` wrapper around a ManiSkill3 gym env.
147. 
148.     ManiSkill3 envs default to vectorised GPU rollouts; the eval-layer
149.     contract is single-env so we pass ``num_envs=1`` at construction and
150.     unwrap the leading batch dim on every observation / step.
151.     """
152. 
153.     scene: SceneSpec
154.     task: TaskSpec
155.     _env: Any  # mani_skill env (lazy-imported)
156.     _last_image: NDArray[np.uint8] | None = None
157.     # Deferred-window mode for ``openral sim run --view`` (mirrors the
158.     # simpler_env backend in PR #160). When True the env was constructed
159.     # with ``render_mode=None`` and the SAPIEN viewer hasn't been opened
160.     # yet; the first :meth:`viewer_render` call promotes
161.     # ``env.unwrapped.render_mode`` to ``"human"`` so the window opens
162.     # *after* the slow policy build, not during it.
163.     _view_pending: bool = False
164. 
165.     def reset(self, seed: int | None = None) -> Observation:
166.         obs, _info = self._env.reset(seed=seed)
167.         return self._wrap_obs(obs)
168. 
169.     def step(self, action: NDArray[np.float32]) -> StepResult:
170.         action_np = np.asarray(action, dtype=np.float32).reshape(1, -1)
171.         obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info = self._env.step(action_np)
172.         reward_f = float(_unbatch(reward))
173.         return StepResult(
174.             observation=self._wrap_obs(obs),
175.             reward=reward_f,
176.             terminated=bool(_unbatch(terminated)),
177.             truncated=bool(_unbatch(truncated)),
178.             info=_unbatch_info(info),
179.         )
180.
```

</details>

180. 
181. def _resolve_pose_at_base(controller: Any) -> tuple[Any, Any] | None:
182.     """Return ``(pos, quat)`` from the controller's ``ee_pose_at_base``, if any.
183. 
184.     Most MS3 PD-EE controllers expose ``ee_pose_at_base`` as a
185.     property; for compound controllers the ``arm`` sub-controller is
186.     typically the one that owns it.
187.     """
188.     candidates = [controller]
189.     arm = getattr(controller, "controllers", None)
190.     if isinstance(arm, dict):
191.         candidates.append(arm.get("arm"))
192.     for cand in candidates:
193.         if cand is None:
194.             continue
195.         pose = getattr(cand, "ee_pose_at_base", None)
196.         if pose is None:
197.             continue
198.         return pose.p, pose.q
199.     return None
200. 
201. 
202. def _resolve_pose_at_base_from_links(robot: Any) -> tuple[Any, Any] | None:
203.     """Compute (ee_link.pose in root_link frame) by hand.
204. 
205.     Used when the agent's controller does not expose ``ee_pose_at_base``
206.     directly. We look up the first matching TCP link and the robot's
207.     root link, then return ``root.pose.inv() * ee.pose`` decomposed
208.     into (p, q).
209.     """
210.     links_map = getattr(robot, "links_map", None)
211.     if not isinstance(links_map, dict):
212.         return None
213.     root_link = getattr(robot, "root", None)
214.     if root_link is None:
215.         return None
216.     for name in _TCP_LINK_CANDIDATES:
217.         ee_link = links_map.get(name)
218.         if ee_link is None:
219.             continue
220.         relative = root_link.pose.inv() * ee_link.pose
221.         return relative.p, relative.q
222.     return None
223. 
224. 
225. def _to_numpy(value: Any) -> NDArray[np.float32]:
226.     if hasattr(value, "cpu"):
227.         value = value.cpu().numpy()
228.     return np.asarray(value, dtype=np.float32)
229. 
230. 
231. def _resolve_gripper_qpos(flat: Any) -> float:
232.     """Return the last qpos channel — the gripper opening MS2 stored at index 7."""
233.     if not isinstance(flat, dict):
234.         return 0.0
235.     agent = flat.get("agent")
236.     if not isinstance(agent, dict):
237.         return 0.0
238.     qpos = agent.get("qpos")
239.     if qpos is None:
240.         return 0.0
241.     arr = np.asarray(qpos, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1)
242.     return float(arr[-1]) if arr.size else 0.0
243. 
244. 
245. @dataclass
246. class _SimplerEnvSim:
247.     """Thin :class:`SimRollout` wrapper around a SimplerEnv-via-MS3 gym env."""
248. 
249.     scene: SceneSpec
250.     task: TaskSpec
251.     _env: Any  # ManiSkill3-registered gym env (lazy-imported)
252.     # When True, the env was constructed with ``render_mode=None`` and
253.     # we need to promote it to ``"human"`` (and lazily open the SAPIEN
254.     # viewer) on the first :meth:`viewer_render` call. This is the
255.     # deferred-window mode used by ``openral sim run --view`` so the live
256.     # SAPIEN window doesn't open during the slow rldx sidecar boot
257.     # (during which the runner's main thread is blocked and the WM
258.     # would mark the empty window "Not Responding"). Set by
259.     # :func:`_build_simpler_env_scene` when ``OPENRAL_SIM_VIEW=1`` and
260.     # cleared on first promotion.
261.     _view_pending: bool = False
262.     _last_image: NDArray[np.uint8] | None = None
263. 
264.     def reset(self, seed: int | None = None) -> Observation:
265.         obs, _info = self._env.reset(seed=seed)
266.         return self._wrap_obs(obs)
267. 
268.     def step(self, action: NDArray[np.float32]) -> StepResult:
269.         # SimplerEnv envs run with num_envs=1 under the hood; reshape so
270.         # the upstream batched API accepts the single-env vector.
271.         action_np = np.asarray(action, dtype=np.float32).reshape(1, -1)
272.         obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info = self._env.step(action_np)
273.         return StepResult(
274.             observation=self._wrap_obs(obs),
275.             reward=float(_unbatch(reward)),
276.             terminated=bool(_unbatch(terminated)),
277.             truncated=bool(_unbatch(truncated)),
278.             info=_unbatch_info(info),
279.         )
280. 
281.     def render(self) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
282.         return None if self._last_image is None else self._last_image.copy()
283. 
284.     def viewer_render(self) -> None:
285.         """Pump the SAPIEN live viewer; promotes to ``human`` mode on first call.
286. 
287.         Picked up by :func:`openral_sim.sim_runner._open_viewer_and_pacing`
288.         as the engine-owns-the-viewer hook (returns a
289.         ``_SapienViewerProxy`` that the runner ``.sync()``-s after each
290.         applied step). The first call lazily promotes
291.         ``env.unwrapped.render_mode`` from ``None`` to ``"human"`` —
292.         MS3's ``render_human`` (``sapien_env.py:1355``) then creates
293.         the SAPIEN viewer + runs ``_setup_viewer`` against the
294.         already-populated scene (env was reset during ``gym.make``'s
295.         ``__init__``, so the carrot/plate/robot are present from
296.         construction). Doing this lazily here, rather than at
297.         ``gym.make`` time, lets the SAPIEN window open *after* the
298.         slow rldx sidecar boot — the runner's first viewer_render()
299.         call fires from inside ``_step_tick``, post-policy-build, so
300.         the WM never sees an empty unresponsive window.
301.         """
302.         if self._view_pending:
303.             self._env.unwrapped.render_mode = "human"
304.             self._view_pending = False
305.         self._env.render()
306. 
307.     def close(self) -> None:
308.         self._env.close()
309. 
310.     def _wrap_obs(self, obs: Any) -> Observation:
```

</details>

170.     _client: SidecarClient
171.     _last_image: NDArray[np.uint8] | None = None
172.     _action_dim: int | None = None
173.     _last_sim_time_ns: int | None = None
174. 
175.     @property
176.     def action_dim(self) -> int:
177.         """Flat action width ``step`` accepts — queried from the sidecar ping (14)."""
178.         if self._action_dim is None:
179.             reply = self._client.call("ping")
180.             self._action_dim = int(self._client.require(reply, "action_dim"))
181.         return self._action_dim
182. 
183.     def reset(self, seed: int | None = None) -> Observation:
184.         reply = self._client.call("reset", {"seed": seed})
185.         self._last_sim_time_ns = _coerce_sim_time_ns(reply.get("sim_time_ns"))
186.         return self._wrap_obs(self._client.require(reply, "observation"))
187. 
188.     def step(self, action: NDArray[np.float32]) -> StepResult:
189.         action_np = np.asarray(action, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1)
190.         reply = self._client.call("step", {"action": action_np})
191.         self._last_sim_time_ns = _coerce_sim_time_ns(reply.get("sim_time_ns"))
192.         return StepResult(
193.             observation=self._wrap_obs(self._client.require(reply, "observation")),
194.             reward=float(self._client.require(reply, "reward")),
195.             terminated=bool(self._client.require(reply, "terminated")),
196.             truncated=bool(self._client.require(reply, "truncated")),
197.             info=dict(reply.get("info", {})),
198.         )
199. 
200.     def sim_time_ns(self) -> int | None:
201.         """Elapsed simulation time in ns from the last sidecar reply, or ``None``."""
202.         return self._last_sim_time_ns
203. 
204.     def render(self) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
205.         return None if self._last_image is None else self._last_image.copy()
206. 
207.     def close(self) -> None:
208.         with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
209.             self._client.call("close")
210.         self._client.close()
```

<sub>⏱️ 609m 49s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Current **known gaps**:

| Area | Does not currently expose reliable `sim_time_ns()` |
|---|---|
| **PushT** (`pusht`) | No sim-time seam; gym/pymunk wrapper is clockless today. |
| **ManiSkill3** (`maniskill3`) | Wrapper does not expose SAPIEN elapsed time yet. |
| **SimplerEnv** (`simpler_env`) | Wrapper does not expose SAPIEN/ManiSkill elapsed time yet. |
| **RLBench** (`rlbench`) | CoppeliaSim sidecar wrapper does not expose sim time. |
| **Mock scene** | No sim-time seam; test/demo-only. |
| **Older sidecars** | Isaac/Robotwin only work when the sidecar reply includes `sim_time_ns`; older protocol replies return `None`. |
| **Real hardware HALs** | Intentionally no `sim_time_ns()`; they use `host_wall` today, future `hardware_synced` later. |

Supported now: MuJoCo-backed rollouts like RoboCasa/robosuite, LIBERO, MetaWorld, ALOHA, tabletop_push, SO-101 box, OpenArm robosuite, plus bare MuJoCo HALs for OpenArm/SO-100/SO-101. Isaac and Robotwin are conditional on the sidecar reporting `sim_time_ns()`.

One thing I’d still tighten in a follow-up: `deploy sim` currently has a backend-level allowlist (`mujoco`, `mujoco_mjx`, `isaacsim`, `sapien`), but actual support is implementation-level. For SAPIEN-style wrappers like ManiSkill3/SimplerEnv, we should either add `sim_time_ns()` or make the gate probe the actual rollout/HAL capability instead of trusting the backend enum.

---

<sub>⏱️ 616m 32s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral/.agents/skills/scene-creator/references/scene-blueprint.md.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral/.agents/skills/scene-creator/references/scene-blueprint.md (lines 1-180)

<details>
<summary>111 lines</summary>

````
1. # Scene Creation Blueprint
2. 
3. Use this reference when creating or reviewing OpenRAL simulation scenes, BDDL tasks, benchmark configs, or scene adapters.
4. 
5. ## Scene Path Decision
6. 
7. | Need | Recommended path |
8. | --- | --- |
9. | New task in an existing registered backend | Add a `SceneEnvironment` YAML |
10. | Custom Panda + LIBERO object/task variant | Write BDDL and use `franka_libero_custom_bddl` |
11. | New arena, non-LIBERO robot, custom physics, or new simulator | Add a Python scene adapter |
12. | Reproducible evaluation suite | Add benchmark config plus docs/eval command |
13. 
14. Prefer YAML or BDDL before adding Python code.
15. 
16. ## Scene YAML Blueprint
17. 
18. Minimal shape:
19. 
20. ```yaml
21. scene:
22.   id: <registered_scene_id>
23.   backend: mujoco
24.   cameras: []
25. 
26. task:
27.   id: <task_id>
28.   scene_id: <same_as_scene.id>
29.   instruction: "<task instruction>"
30.   success_key: <info_key>
31. 
32. seed: 42
33. n_episodes: 1
34. record_video: false
35. 
36. metadata:
37.   honest_scope: "what this scene does and does not prove"
38. ```
39. 
40. Rules:
41. 
42. - Do not include `vla:` in scene YAML.
43. - Pass the rSkill separately with `--rskill`.
44. - Add `robot_id` only for free-axis scenes.
45. - Keep `task.scene_id` equal to `scene.id`.
46. - `success_key` must be produced by the adapter's `StepResult.info`.
47. - Use `metadata` for honest scope, paper links, and reproduction notes.
48. 
49. ## BDDL Checklist
50. 
51. For custom LIBERO scenes:
52. 
53. - Start from an upstream LIBERO BDDL file.
54. - Keep `:objects`, `:obj_of_interest`, `:init`, `:goal`, and `:language` coherent.
55. - Use object names that exist in LIBERO's object registry.
56. - Remember that `--instruction` changes the prompt only; success still follows `:goal`.
57. - Prefer this path for pi0.5-LIBERO when staying inside Panda + robosuite + HOPE object assumptions.
58. - Record the BDDL path and scene YAML path together.
59. 
60. ## Adapter Checklist
61. 
62. Add Python scene adapters only when needed:
63. 
64. - Register with `@SCENES.register("<id>")`.
65. - Ensure module import triggers registration.
66. - Return an object satisfying the `SimRollout` protocol.
67. - Provide conventional observation keys: `images`, `state`, and `task` where possible.
68. - Ensure `StepResult.info` includes the declared success key.
69. - Expose `mujoco_handles()` only with a real MuJoCo model/data pair.
70. - Add real adapter tests and sim tests.
71. 
72. ## rSkill Pairing Checklist
73. 
74. - rSkill `embodiment_tags` match the robot/scene.
75. - Camera keys and preprocessing match what the adapter emits.
76. - State/action dimensions match the rollout observation and action space.
77. - `policy_extras` or adapter knobs are in the rSkill manifest when policy-specific.
78. - Language instruction does not contradict success predicate.
79. - License gates allow intended local eval/deployment.
80. 
81. ## Validation Commands
82. 
83. ```bash
84. openral sim list
85. openral sim run --config scenes/<path>.yaml --rskill rskills/<skill> --n-episodes 1
86. openral sim run --config scenes/<path>.yaml --rskill rskills/<skill> --task <task-id>
87. openral sim run --config scenes/<path>.yaml --rskill rskills/<skill> --instruction "<prompt>"
88. openral benchmark run --suite <suite-id> --vla <vla-id>:rskills/<skill>
89. ```
90. 
91. If optional simulator dependencies are missing, tests may skip only with a concrete dependency reason. Do not replace a sim run with a mock success.
92. 
93. ## Output Checklist
94. 
95. - Scene config path.
96. - Scene ID and backend.
97. - Robot ID and fixed/free-axis behavior.
98. - Task ID, instruction, success key, and success predicate source.
99. - rSkill used for validation.
100. - Commands run and artifacts produced.
101. - Docs/tests updated.
102. - Known limitations, missing assets, or unreproduced metrics.
103. 
104. ## Hard Stops
105. 
106. - Adapter is not registered.
107. - Success key or predicate is unknown.
108. - Robot/rSkill action semantics differ.
109. - BDDL goal conflicts with the instruction.
110. - Required simulator assets or licenses are unavailable.
111. - Eval metrics would need to be invented.
````

</details>

<details>
<summary>70 lines</summary>

```
1. """ManiSkill3 scene adapter — wraps SAPIEN-backed GPU manipulation envs.
2. 
3. ADR-0014. ManiSkill3 is opt-in via the ``maniskill3`` dependency group
4. (``just sync --all-packages --group maniskill3``); without it this module still imports
5. fine but the scene factory raises a typed :class:`ROSConfigError` with
6. the install hint.
7. 
8. Task ID convention
9. ------------------
10. ``"maniskill3/<env_id>"`` e.g. ``"maniskill3/PickCube-v1"``,
11. ``"maniskill3/StackCube-v1"``. The ``<env_id>`` is passed through to
12. ``gymnasium.make`` so the full ManiSkill3 task catalogue is reachable
13. without a per-task adapter edit (any future MS3 release adds new tasks
14. transparently). The ``scene.id`` MUST be ``"maniskill3"`` for
15. cross-field validation to succeed.
16. """
17. 
18. from __future__ import annotations
19. 
20. import contextlib
21. import logging
22. import os
23. from collections.abc import Iterator
24. from dataclasses import dataclass
25. from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
26. 
27. import numpy as np
28. from numpy.typing import NDArray
29. from openral_core.exceptions import ROSCapabilityMismatch, ROSConfigError
30. 
31. from openral_sim.registry import SCENES
32. from openral_sim.rollout import StepResult
33. 
34. if TYPE_CHECKING:
35.     from openral_core import SceneSpec, SimEnvironment, TaskSpec
36. 
37.     from openral_sim.rollout import Observation
38. 
39. 
40. _MANISKILL3_SCENE_ID = "maniskill3"
41. # Mirrors the constant in :mod:`openral_sim.sim_runner` and
42. # :mod:`openral_sim.backends.simpler_env`. :meth:`SimRunner.activate`
43. # sets it to ``"1"`` for the duration of the scene-build window when
44. # ``openral sim run --view`` is on; we read it here to decide whether to
45. # build with deferred SAPIEN viewer plumbing.
46. _VIEW_ENV = "OPENRAL_SIM_VIEW"
47. 
48. 
49. def _parse_task_id(task_id: str) -> str:
50.     """Parse ``"maniskill3/<env_id>"`` and return ``<env_id>``."""
51.     parts = task_id.split("/", maxsplit=1)
52.     expected_parts = 2
53.     if len(parts) != expected_parts or parts[0] != _MANISKILL3_SCENE_ID:
54.         raise ROSConfigError(f"maniskill3 task id must be 'maniskill3/<env_id>', got {task_id!r}")
55.     return parts[1]
56. 
57. 
58. def _reconcile_robot_uids(env_id: str, robot_uids: str) -> None:
59.     """Validate ``robot_uids`` against a ManiSkill3 task's ``SUPPORTED_ROBOTS``.
60. 
61.     MS3's per-task ``SUPPORTED_ROBOTS`` lists *base* agent uids only, so a
62.     registered camera-variant subclass (e.g. ``panda_wristcam``, a ``Panda``
63.     that only adds a ``hand_camera``) trips a false "not in the task's list of
64.     supported robots" warning even though it is genuinely usable. We walk the
65.     requested agent's MRO: if any base uid is supported, the variant is
66.     accepted; otherwise raise a typed :class:`ROSCapabilityMismatch` at the
67.     scene boundary instead of MS3's vague warning + downstream crash.
68. 
69.     Args:
70.         env_id: The bare ManiSkill3 env id (e.g. ``"PickCube-v1"``).
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 616m 40s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/sim/src/openral_sim/backends/simpler_env.py.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/sim/src/openral_sim/backends/simpler_env.py (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

```
1. """SimplerEnv scene adapter — wraps the real-to-sim correlator envs.
2. 
3. ADR-0014. SimplerEnv is opt-in via the ``simpler-env`` dependency group
4. (``just sync --all-packages --group simpler-env``); without it this module still imports
5. fine but the scene factory raises a typed :class:`ROSConfigError` with
6. the install hint.
7. 
8. The metric SimplerEnv targets is the real-to-sim correlation (MMRV /
9. Pearson) — see Li et al. CoRL 2024. It exposes the same Google Robot +
10. WidowX setups used by RT-1, RT-2, Octo and OpenVLA. On the upstream
11. ``maniskill3`` branch the eval envs are now ManiSkill3-registered
12. gymnasium environments (``mani_skill.envs.tasks.digital_twins.*``), so
13. this adapter shares the obs / state extraction helpers with
14. :mod:`openral_sim.backends.maniskill3`.
15. 
16. Task ID convention
17. ------------------
18. ``"simpler_env/<friendly_name>"`` e.g.
19. ``"simpler_env/widowx_carrot_on_plate"``. The friendly name is
20. translated via ``simpler_env.ENVIRONMENT_MAP`` to the underlying MS3
21. env id + kwargs, so configs stay readable. Friendly names that are
22. not in the map fall through to ``gym.make(<friendly_name>)`` so users
23. can also pass a raw MS3 env id directly when needed.
24. 
25. Only the WidowX bridge tasks are registered end-to-end in MS3 v3.0.x. The
26. ``google_robot_*`` friendly names exist in ``ENVIRONMENT_MAP`` but their MS3
27. envs (``GraspSingleOpenedCokeCanInScene``, ``MoveNearGoogleBakedTexInScene``,
28. …) are not registered upstream yet, so they raise ``NameNotFound`` — the
29. ``simpler_env_google_robot`` benchmark suite was removed for that reason.
30. """
31. 
32. from __future__ import annotations
33. 
34. import os
35. from dataclasses import dataclass
36. from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
37. 
38. import numpy as np
39. from numpy.typing import NDArray
40. from openral_core.exceptions import ROSConfigError
41. 
42. from openral_sim.backends.maniskill3 import (
43.     _extract_rgb,
44.     _extract_state,
45.     _unbatch,
46.     _unbatch_info,
47.     _unbatch_obs,
48. )
49. from openral_sim.registry import SCENES
50. from openral_sim.rollout import StepResult
51. 
52. if TYPE_CHECKING:
53.     from openral_core import SceneSpec, SimEnvironment, TaskSpec
54. 
55.     from openral_sim.rollout import Observation
56. 
57. 
58. # Set to "1" by :meth:`openral_sim.SimRunner.activate` for the duration
59. # of the scene build when ``openral sim run --view`` is on. See the same
60. # constant in :mod:`openral_sim.sim_runner`. We read it here to decide
61. # whether to advertise ``render_mode='human'`` to ``gym.make`` — that
62. # is the toggle that tells SAPIEN to build a live window. Reading an
63. # env var (rather than threading a kwarg through every SCENES factory)
64. # keeps the registry contract untouched.
65. _VIEW_ENV = "OPENRAL_SIM_VIEW"
66. 
67. 
68. _SIMPLER_ENV_SCENE_ID = "simpler_env"
69. # SimplerEnv bridge envs ported into ManiSkill3 v3.0.x advertise a single
70. # ``rgb+segmentation`` obs mode (see SUPPORTED_OBS_MODES on
71. # BridgeDatasetEvalBase). Earlier branches accepted ``rgbd`` — the
72. # upstream ``simpler_env.make()`` still passes that, which is why
73. # calling it directly errors on a fresh MS3 install. Override via
74. # ``scene.backend_options.obs_mode`` if a future upstream relaxes it.
75. _DEFAULT_OBS_MODE = "rgb+segmentation"
76. 
77. 
78. def _parse_task_id(task_id: str) -> str:
79.     """Parse ``"simpler_env/<env_id>"`` and return ``<env_id>``."""
80.     parts = task_id.split("/", maxsplit=1)
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 616m 51s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tests/sim/test_franka_panda_maniskill3_adapter.py (lines 1-130)

<details>
<summary>102 lines</summary>

```
1. """Sim test: real ManiSkill3 PickCube-v1 via the openral scene adapter.
2. 
3. Exercises ``openral_sim.backends.maniskill3._build_maniskill3_scene``
4. end-to-end against the upstream ``mani_skill`` SAPIEN backend so the
5. adapter's obs / state / action plumbing is validated without dragging
6. in a (non-existent) MS3-specific rSkill.
7. 
8. What is asserted
9. ----------------
10. * ``SimScene.from_yaml`` accepts (and ``BenchmarkScene.from_yaml`` validates)
11.   ``scenes/benchmark/maniskill_pick_cube.yaml``
12.   with ``robot_id: franka_panda`` (free-axis scene, no fixed_robot).
13. * The adapter resets the env from a seed and emits a populated
14.   :class:`Observation` (non-empty RGB + non-empty state vector under the
15.   ``state_dict+rgb`` obs mode).
16. * A canonical zero-action step propagates through the env and returns
17.   finite reward / a typed info dict.
18. 
19. Skips automatically when ``mani_skill`` is missing.
20. """
21. 
22. from __future__ import annotations
23. 
24. import importlib.util
25. from pathlib import Path
26. 
27. import numpy as np
28. import pytest
29. 
30. _REQUIRED_MODULES = ("mani_skill", "gymnasium")
31. _MISSING_MODULES = tuple(m for m in _REQUIRED_MODULES if importlib.util.find_spec(m) is None)
32. 
33. pytestmark = [
34.     pytest.mark.sim,
35.     pytest.mark.skipif(
36.         bool(_MISSING_MODULES),
37.         reason="ManiSkill3 sim test requires " + ", ".join(_MISSING_MODULES),
38.     ),
39. ]
40. 
41. _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent
42. _CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "scenes" / "benchmark" / "maniskill_pick_cube.yaml"
43. 
44. 
45. @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
46. def scene_env():
47.     from openral_core import SimScene, load_scene_strict
48. 
49.     if not _CONFIG.exists():
50.         pytest.skip(f"sim config not found at {_CONFIG}")
51.     return load_scene_strict(str(_CONFIG), SimScene)
52. 
53. 
54. @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
55. def sim(scene_env):
56.     """Build the ManiSkill3 scene exactly the way ``openral sim run`` does."""
57.     from openral_core import SimEnvironment, VLASpec
58.     from openral_sim.registry import SCENES
59. 
60.     # The adapter cares about scene + task only; supply a placeholder VLASpec
61.     # so SimEnvironment validates (the scene factory never reads vla).
62.     env_cfg = SimEnvironment(
63.         robot_id=scene_env.robot_id or "franka_panda",
64.         scene=scene_env.scene,
65.         task=scene_env.task,
66.         vla=VLASpec(id="zero", weights_uri="stub"),
67.         seed=scene_env.seed,
68.         n_episodes=scene_env.n_episodes,
69.     )
70.     built = SCENES.get("maniskill3")(env_cfg)
71.     yield built
72.     built.close()
73. 
74. 
75. class TestManiSkill3Adapter:
76.     def test_reset_returns_populated_observation(self, sim) -> None:
77.         obs = sim.reset(seed=0)
78.         assert "images" in obs and "camera1" in obs["images"]
79.         rgb = obs["images"]["camera1"]
80.         # Resolution is driven by the scene config (sensor_configs), not hardcoded.
81.         expected_hw = (sim.scene.observation_height, sim.scene.observation_width, 3)
82.         assert rgb.shape == expected_hw
83.         assert rgb.dtype == np.uint8
84.         # state_dict+rgb yields agent.qpos + agent.qvel; Panda has 9 joints
85.         # (7 arm + 2 finger), so the concatenation is 18-D.
86.         assert obs["state"].shape == (18,)
87.         assert obs["state"].dtype == np.float32
88. 
89.     def test_step_propagates_action(self, sim) -> None:
90.         sim.reset(seed=0)
91.         # Action dim follows the env's controller, which is version-dependent
92.         # (ManiSkill 3.0.x panda_wristcam = 7 arm + 1 gripper = 8); derive it
93.         # from the action space rather than hardcoding.
94.         action_dim = int(sim._env.action_space.shape[-1])
95.         result = sim.step(np.zeros(action_dim, dtype=np.float32))
96.         # PickCube-v1 reward is shaped; a zero-action step yields a small
97.         # positive value at the initial pose. The contract we care about is
98.         # "finite and machine-comparable", not a specific magnitude.
99.         assert np.isfinite(result.reward)
100.         assert "success" in result.info
101.         assert isinstance(result.info["success"], (bool, np.bool_))
102.
```

</details>

1. """Sim test: real SimplerEnv WidowX carrot-on-plate via the openral scene adapter.
2. 
3. Exercises ``openral_sim.backends.simpler_env._build_simpler_env_scene``
4. end-to-end against the upstream ``mani_skill`` Bridge-data digital twin
5. so the adapter's friendly-name → MS3 env-id resolution, version bump,
6. and shared MS3 obs-extraction helpers all run on the real path.
7. 
8. What is asserted
9. ----------------
10. * ``SimScene.from_yaml`` accepts (and ``BenchmarkScene.from_yaml`` validates)
11.   ``scenes/benchmark/widowx_carrot_on_plate.yaml`` with
12.   ``robot_id: widowx`` (free-axis scene, no fixed_robot).
13. * The friendly task name ``widowx_carrot_on_plate`` resolves to
14.   ``PutCarrotOnPlateInScene-v1`` (auto-bumped from the upstream
15.   ENVIRONMENT_MAP's ``-v0`` suffix).
16. * The adapter resets the env and emits a populated :class:`Observation`
17.   (RGB from ``3rd_view_camera`` + agent.qpos/qvel state vector).
18. * A canonical zero-action step propagates with finite reward and the
19.   Bridge-task ``success`` info field.
20. 
21. Skips automatically when ``mani_skill`` or ``simpler_env`` are missing.
22. """
23. 
24. from __future__ import annotations
25. 
26. import importlib.util
27. from pathlib import Path
28. 
29. import numpy as np
30. import pytest
31. 
32. _REQUIRED_MODULES = ("mani_skill", "simpler_env", "gymnasium")
33. _MISSING_MODULES = tuple(m for m in _REQUIRED_MODULES if importlib.util.find_spec(m) is None)
34. 
35. pytestmark = [
36.     pytest.mark.sim,
37.     pytest.mark.skipif(
38.         bool(_MISSING_MODULES),
39.         reason="SimplerEnv sim test requires " + ", ".join(_MISSING_MODULES),
40.     ),
41. ]
42. 
43. _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent
44. _CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "scenes" / "benchmark" / "widowx_carrot_on_plate.yaml"
45. 
46. 
47. @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
48. def scene_env():
49.     from openral_core import SimScene, load_scene_strict
50. 
51.     if not _CONFIG.exists():
52.         pytest.skip(f"sim config not found at {_CONFIG}")
53.     return load_scene_strict(str(_CONFIG), SimScene)
54. 
55. 
56. @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
57. def sim(scene_env):
58.     from openral_core import SimEnvironment, VLASpec
59.     from openral_sim.registry import SCENES
60. 
61.     env_cfg = SimEnvironment(
62.         robot_id=scene_env.robot_id or "widowx",
63.         scene=scene_env.scene,
64.         task=scene_env.task,
65.         vla=VLASpec(id="zero", weights_uri="stub"),
66.         seed=scene_env.seed,
67.         n_episodes=scene_env.n_episodes,
68.     )
69.     built = SCENES.get("simpler_env")(env_cfg)
70.     yield built
71.     built.close()
72. 
73. 
74. class TestSimplerEnvAdapter:
75.     def test_friendly_name_resolves_with_version_bump(self) -> None:
76.         from openral_sim.backends.simpler_env import _resolve_friendly_name
77. 
78.         env_id, kwargs = _resolve_friendly_name("widowx_carrot_on_plate")
79.         # ENVIRONMENT_MAP carries -v0 in upstream simpler-env; MS3 v3.0.x
80.         # registers -v1, so the adapter must bump.
81.         assert env_id == "PutCarrotOnPlateInScene-v1"
82.         assert kwargs == {}
83. 
84.     def test_reset_returns_populated_observation(self, sim) -> None:
85.         obs = sim.reset(seed=0)
86.         assert "images" in obs and "camera1" in obs["images"]
87.         rgb = obs["images"]["camera1"]
88.         # The Bridge env renders at its native 480x640; the adapter
89.         # forwards what MS3 emits without resizing.
90.         assert rgb.shape == (480, 640, 3)
91.         assert rgb.dtype == np.uint8
92.         # WidowX-250s has 8 joints (6 arm + 2 finger); state = qpos + qvel.
93.         assert obs["state"].shape == (16,)
94.         assert obs["state"].dtype == np.float32
95. 
96.     def test_step_propagates_action(self, sim) -> None:
97.         sim.reset(seed=0)
98.         result = sim.step(np.zeros(7, dtype=np.float32))
99.         assert np.isfinite(result.reward)
100.         assert "success" in result.info
101.         assert isinstance(result.info["success"], (bool, np.bool_))
102.
```

</details>

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tests/sim/test_maniskill3_robot_uids.py (lines 1-140)

<details>
<summary>71 lines</summary>

```
1. """Reconcile a scene's requested ``robot_uids`` against a ManiSkill3 task's
2. ``SUPPORTED_ROBOTS`` allowlist.
3. 
4. MS3's per-task allowlist enumerates *base* agent uids only — it does not list
5. registered camera-variant subclasses (e.g. ``panda_wristcam``, a ``Panda``
6. subclass that only adds a ``hand_camera``). So MS3 logs a false
7. "not in the task's list of supported robots" warning for a genuinely-usable
8. robot. The adapter reconciles by walking the agent's MRO: a variant whose base
9. uid is supported is accepted; a genuinely-unsupported robot raises a typed
10. ``ROSCapabilityMismatch`` at the boundary instead of MS3's vague warning +
11. downstream crash.
12. 
13. Registry-only (no env construction / GPU); skipped when maniskill3 is absent.
14. """
15. 
16. from __future__ import annotations
17. 
18. import pytest
19. 
20. pytest.importorskip("mani_skill")
21. 
22. import logging
23. 
24. from openral_core.exceptions import ROSCapabilityMismatch
25. from openral_sim.backends.maniskill3 import (
26.     _reconcile_robot_uids,
27.     _suppress_unsupported_robot_warning,
28. )
29. 
30. 
31. def test_reconcile_accepts_directly_supported_robot() -> None:
32.     # 'panda' is in PickCube-v1's SUPPORTED_ROBOTS — accepted, no raise.
33.     _reconcile_robot_uids("PickCube-v1", "panda")
34. 
35. 
36. def test_reconcile_accepts_camera_variant_of_supported_base() -> None:
37.     # 'panda_wristcam' is a registered Panda subclass (MRO base uid 'panda',
38.     # which IS supported) — accepted despite not being enumerated directly.
39.     _reconcile_robot_uids("PickCube-v1", "panda_wristcam")
40. 
41. 
42. def test_reconcile_raises_typed_error_for_unsupported_robot() -> None:
43.     with pytest.raises(ROSCapabilityMismatch):
44.         _reconcile_robot_uids("PickCube-v1", "totally_not_a_registered_robot")
45. 
46. 
47. def test_suppress_drops_only_the_false_unsupported_warning(
48.     caplog: pytest.LogCaptureFixture,
49. ) -> None:
50.     log = logging.getLogger("mani_skill")
51.     with (
52.         _suppress_unsupported_robot_warning(),
53.         caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger="mani_skill"),
54.     ):
55.         log.warning(
56.             "panda_wristcam is not in the task's list of supported robots. "
57.             "Code may not run as intended"
58.         )
59.         log.warning("a genuinely important mani_skill warning")
60.     messages = [r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records]
61.     assert not any("not in the task's list of supported robots" in m for m in messages)
62.     assert any("genuinely important" in m for m in messages)
63. 
64. 
65. def test_suppress_filter_is_removed_on_exit() -> None:
66.     log = logging.getLogger("mani_skill")
67.     before = list(log.filters)
68.     with _suppress_unsupported_robot_warning():
69.         pass
70.     assert list(log.filters) == before  # no leaked filter
71.
```

</details>

if value is None:\n+        return None\n+    if callable(value):\n+        return None\n+    try:\n+        if hasattr(value, \"detach\"):\n+            value = value.detach()\n+        if dtype=np.float64).reshape(-1)\n+    except (TypeError, ValueError):\n+        return None\n+    if arr.size == 0:\n+        return None\n+    return float(arr[0])\n+\n+\n+def _first_scalar_attr(obj: object, names: tuple[str, ...]) -> float | None:\n+    for name in names:\n+        value = getattr(obj, name, None)\n+        scalar = _scalar_float(value)\n+        if scalar is not None:\n+            return scalar\n+    return None\n+\n+\n+def _sapien_sim_time_ns(env: object) -> int | None:\n+    \"\"\"Derive elapsed SAPIEN/ManiSkill control time from a live env.\n+\n+    ManiSkill3/SimplerEnv expose the elapsed control step counter as an array or\n+    tensor (`elapsed_steps` / `_elapsed_steps`) and the control period as a\n+    scalar (`control_timestep`, `control_dt`, or a `control_freq` inverse).\n+    \"\"\"\n+    candidates = (env, getattr(env, \"unwrapped\", env))\n+    elapsed_s_names = (\"elapsed_time\", \"time_elapsed\", \"sim_time\", \"elapsed_seconds\")\n+    step_names = (\"elapsed_steps\", \"_elapsed_steps\")\n+    dt_names = (\"control_timestep\", \"_control_timestep\", \"control_dt\", \"dt\")\n+    freq_names = (\"control_freq\", \"_control_freq\")\n+\n+    for candidate in candidates:\n+        elapsed_s = _first_scalar_attr(candidate, elapsed_s_names)\n+        if elapsed_s is not None:\n+            return round(elapsed_s * 1_000_000_000)\n+\n+        steps = _first_scalar_attr(candidate, step_names)\n+        if steps is None:\n+            continue\n+\n+        dt_s = _first_scalar_attr(candidate, dt_names)\n+        if dt_s is None:\n+            freq_hz = _first_scalar_attr(candidate, freq_names)\n+            if freq_hz is not None and freq_hz > 0:\n+                dt_s = 1.0 / freq_hz\n+        if dt_s is not None:\n+            return round(steps * dt_s * 1_000_000_000)\n+    return None\n+\n+\n @dataclass\n class _ManiSkill3Sim:\n@@\n     def step(self, action: NDArray[np.float32]) -> StepResult:\n@@\n             info=_unbatch_info(info),\n         )\n \n+    def sim_time_ns(self) -> int | None:\n+        \"\"\"Elapsed SAPIEN/ManiSkill simulation time in nanoseconds.\"\"\"\n+        return _sapien_sim_time_ns(self._env)\n+\n     def render(self) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:\n         return None if self._last_image is None else self._last_image.copy()\n*** End Patch\n"

183.                 }
184.             elif endpoint == "reset":
185.                 # ADR-0048 Phase 2 — carry sim time on reset too (≈0 after the
186.                 # world reset) so the HAL's cross-reset offset stays monotonic.
187.                 reply = {
188.                     "observation": scene.reset(seed=data.get("seed")),
189.                     "sim_time_ns": scene.sim_time_ns(),
190.                 }
191.             elif endpoint == "step":
192.                 reply = scene.step(np.asarray(data["action"], dtype=np.float32))
193.             elif endpoint == "render":
194.                 reply = {"frame": scene.render()}
195.             elif endpoint == "close":
196.                 reply = {"ok": True}
197.                 running = False
198.             else:
199.                 reply = {"error": f"unknown endpoint {endpoint!r}"}
200.         except Exception as exc:
```

</details>

Replaces the removed `_OBS_STATE_DIM` module-level constant so the scene is self-consistent when the rSkill ships a different state contract. (L152)
253.   - `_resolve_initial_pose_from_rskill(rskill_manifest)` — Read the per-rSkill initial joint pose if the manifest pins one. (L219)
