$ cd /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep && .venv/bin/openral sim run --help | sed -n '1,220p'

<details>
<summary>135 lines</summary>

```
Usage: openral sim run [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...                             
                                                                                
 Run a sim eval composed from a SimScene YAML (--config, scenes/sim/) and an    
 rSkill manifest (--rskill).                                                    
                                                                                
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --config                            PATH     Path to a SimScene YAML (scene  │
│                                              + task + optional robot_id).    │
│                                              Strict: DeployScene /           │
│                                              BenchmarkScene YAMLs are        │
│                                              rejected with a redirect to     │
│                                              `openral deploy sim` / `openral │
│                                              benchmark scene`.               │
│ --rskill                            TEXT     rSkill reference — a bare name  │
│                                              ('smolvla-libero'), a path      │
│                                              ('rskills/smolvla-libero'), or  │
│                                              an HF Hub repo id               │
│                                              ('OpenRAL/rskill-smolvla-liber… │
│                                              Required. Resolves to the       │
│                                              policy manifest; the policy     │
│                                              adapter is selected from the    │
│                                              manifest's model_family field.  │
│                                              Run `openral sim list` for      │
│                                              paste-able strings.             │
│ --robot                             TEXT     robot_id for free-axis scenes.  │
│                                              Rejected on scenes with a       │
│                                              fixed_robot (LIBERO, MetaWorld, │
│                                              PushT, ALOHA, RoboCasa).        │
│ --task                              TEXT     Override the YAML's task.id     │
│                                              (preserves instruction /        │
│                                              max_steps / success_key).       │
│                                              Useful for sweeping a task      │
│                                              suite without editing the YAML. │
│ --instruction                       TEXT     Override the natural-language   │
│                                              task instruction.               │
│ --max-steps                         INTEGER  Override task.max_steps.        │
│ --n-episodes                        INTEGER  Override n_episodes.            │
│ --n-action-steps                    INTEGER  Override the chunk-replay       │
│                                              cadence. spec_extra > manifest  │
│                                              > checkpoint chunk_size.        │
│ --seed                              INTEGER  Override the global seed.       │
│ --device                            TEXT     Torch device override for the   │
│                                              policy (cpu, cuda:0, mps,       │
│                                              auto).                          │
│ --save-dir                          PATH     Directory to write the JSON     │
│                                              summary (and any adapter        │
│                                              artefacts).                     │
│ --save-video                        TEXT     Write a 3-panel debug MP4 after │
│                                              each episode (rSkill input |    │
│                                              rollout view | joint positions  │
│                                              plot). Pass a directory, a      │
│                                              filename ending in .mp4, or the │
│                                              empty string to default to      │
│                                              'example_videos/<config-stem>[… │
│                                              Setting this flag also enables  │
│                                              per-step frame capture for the  │
│                                              run.                            │
│ --video-style                       TEXT     Style of the --save-video       │
│                                              output. 'debug' (default)       │
│                                              writes the 3-panel montage      │
│                                              (rSkill input | rollout | joint │
│                                              plot). 'world' writes a clean   │
│                                              single-view MP4 of just the     │
│                                              simulated viewer (world         │
│                                              render), named                  │
│                                              <scene>_<rskill>_<success|fail… │
│                                              and logged to videos.json — for │
│                                              website hero clips where        │
│                                              overlays are rendered by the    │
│                                              page, not burned into pixels.   │
│                                              [default: debug]                │
│ --video-size                        INTEGER  Square edge (px) for            │
│                                              --video-style world output.     │
│                                              Frames are center-cropped to a  │
│                                              square and resized to this      │
│                                              size. Source sharpness is       │
│                                              bounded by the scene's native   │
│                                              render resolution; this does    │
│                                              not change the policy's         │
│                                              observation resolution.         │
│                                              [default: 1024]                 │
│ --dataset-out                       PATH     ADR-0019: write a               │
│                                              LeRobotDataset v3.0 to PATH as  │
│                                              the sim runs. Every episode     │
│                                              (success or failure) becomes    │
│                                              rows in the dataset;            │
│                                              meta/info.json carries the      │
│                                              per-dataset success rate. Path  │
│                                              MUST NOT pre-exist (lerobot v3  │
│                                              refuses to write into a         │
│                                              populated root).                │
│ --dataset-repo-id                   TEXT     Repo id for the produced        │
│                                              dataset (e.g.                   │
│                                              openral/dataset-pick-cube).     │
│                                              Lands in meta/info.json; not    │
│                                              pushed to HF Hub by `openral    │
│                                              sim run` (PR5's `openral        │
│                                              dataset push` owns publishing). │
│                                              Defaults to                     │
│                                              openral/dataset-<robot_id>.     │
│ --dataset-license                   TEXT     SPDX license string for the     │
│                                              produced dataset. Default       │
│                                              matches the official LeRobot    │
│                                              convention. PII-bearing         │
│                                              datasets MUST set a more        │
│                                              restrictive license; the        │
│                                              consent prompt at `openral      │
│                                              dataset push` (PR5) enforces    │
│                                              this.                           │
│                                              [default: CC-BY-4.0]            │
│ --view                 --no-view             Open a passive mujoco.viewer    │
│                                              window and stream the rollout   │
│                                              in real time. Default: on when  │
│                                              a display is available and the  │
│                                              scene is MuJoCo-backed;         │
│                                              otherwise auto-disabled with a  │
│                                              WARNING. Pass --view to require │
│                                              a window (errors loud if        │
│                                              unsupported), or --no-view to   │
│                                              force offscreen. Incompatible   │
│                                              with MUJOCO_GL=egl.             │
│ --verbose          -v                        Verbose logging.                │
│ --dashboard                                  Boot `openral dashboard` as a   │
│                                              child process, point OTel at    │
│                                              it, and shut it down on exit.   │
│                                              Convenience for live-viewing a  │
│                                              single sim run; equivalent to   │
│                                              running `openral dashboard` in  │
│                                              another terminal and exporting  │
│                                              OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT by  │
│                                              hand.                           │
│ --dashboard-port                    INTEGER  Port for the spawned dashboard  │
│                                              when --dashboard is set.        │
│                                              [default: 4318]                 │
│ --help                                       Show this message and exit.     │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Usage: openral benchmark scene [OPTIONS]                                       
                                                                                
 Run a single-scene benchmark and write a validated `RSkillEvalResult` JSON.    
                                                                                
 Single-scene sibling of ``openral benchmark run --suite`` — accepts            
 exactly one :class:`BenchmarkScene` YAML and emits the same eval JSON          
 shape so ``openral benchmark report`` does not need to distinguish             
 the two entrypoints.                                                           
                                                                                
 Example:                                                                       
     >>> # openral benchmark scene \\                                           
     >>> #   --config scenes/benchmark/pusht.yaml \\                            
     >>> #   --rskill diffusion-pusht                                           
                                                                                
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *  --config                                    PATH     Path to a            │
│                                                         BenchmarkScene YAML  │
│                                                         (`scenes/benchmark/… │
│                                                         DeployScene and      │
│                                                         SimScene YAMLs are   │
│                                                         rejected with a      │
│                                                         redirect — `openral  │
│                                                         benchmark scene`     │
│                                                         accepts              │
│                                                         BenchmarkScene only  │
│                                                         (scene + task +      │
│                                                         `n_episodes` +       │
│                                                         `seed` +             │
│                                                         `metadata.paper` +   │
│                                                         `metadata.honest_sc… │
│                                                         [required]           │
│ *  --rskill                                    TEXT     rSkill reference — a │
│                                                         bare name            │
│                                                         ('smolvla-libero'),  │
│                                                         a path               │
│                                                         ('rskills/smolvla-l… │
│                                                         or an HF Hub repo id │
│                                                         ('OpenRAL/rskill-sm… │
│                                                         Raw hf:// is         │
│                                                         rejected (weights    │
│                                                         must come from a     │
│                                                         manifest). The       │
│                                                         policy adapter id is │
│                                                         read from the        │
│                                                         manifest's           │
│                                                         `model_family`       │
│                                                         field.               │
│                                                         [required]           │
│    --out                                       PATH     Output path for the  │
│                                                         RSkillEvalResult     │
│                                                         JSON. Defaults to    │
│                                                         rskills/<dir>/eval/… │
│                                                         derived from the     │
│                                                         rSkill ref.          │
│    --device                                    TEXT     Torch device         │
│                                                         override for the     │
│                                                         policy (cpu, cuda:0, │
│                                                         mps, auto).          │
│    --save-dir                                  PATH     Optional             │
│                                                         adapter-side         │
│                                                         artefact directory   │
│                                                         (videos, traces).    │
│    --save-video                                PATH     Write a clean        │
│                                                         single-view world    │
│                                                         MP4 per episode to   │
│                                                         this directory,      │
│                                                         named                │
│                                                         <task>_<rskill>_<su… │
│                                                         plus a videos.json   │
│                                                         manifest — for       │
│                                                         website hero clips   │
│                                                         (overlays are        │
│                                                         rendered by the      │
│                                                         page, not burned     │
│                                                         into pixels). The    │
│                                                         task slug keeps      │
│                                                         benchmark scenes     │
│                                                         sharing one backend  │
│                                                         from overwriting     │
│                                                         each other. Enables  │
│                                                         per-step frame       │
│                                                         capture. Pair with   │
│                                                         --n-episodes 1 for a │
│                                                         single demo clip.    │
│    --video-size                                INTEGER  Square edge (px) for │
│                                                         --save-video output. │
│                                                         Frames are           │
│                                                         center-cropped to a  │
│                                                         square and resized   │
│                                                         to this size. Source │
│                                                         sharpness is bounded │
│                                                         by the scene's       │
│                                                         native render        │
│                                                         resolution.          │
│                                                         [default: 1024]      │
│    --n-episodes                                INTEGER  Override             │
│                                                         `BenchmarkScene.n_e… │
│                                                         (lower for quick     │
│                                                         smoke runs). The     │
│                                                         published-protocol   │
│                                                         value lives in the   │
│                                                         YAML; this flag is   │
│                                                         for fast iteration,  │
│                                                         not for              │
│                                                         paper-comparison     │
│                                                         numbers.             │
│    --view               --no-view                       Open a passive       │
│                                                         mujoco.viewer window │
│                                                         and stream the       │
│                                                         rollout in real time │
│                                                         (parity with         │
│                                                         `openral sim run     │
│                                                         --view`). Default    │
│                                                         (unset): headless —  │
│                                                         benchmark eval       │
│                                                         artefacts and        │
│                                                         CI/deploy runs are   │
│                                                         unaffected. Pass     │
│                                                         --view to require a  │
│                                                         window (errors loud  │
│                                                         if unsupported), or  │
│                                                         --no-view to force   │
│                                                         offscreen.           │
│                                                         Incompatible with    │
│                                                         MUJOCO_GL=egl.       │
│    --dry-run                                            Resolve the scene +  │
│                                                         rSkill and print the │
│                                                         planned (task x      │
│                                                         seed) matrix without │
│                                                         running any          │
│                                                         rollouts. Useful in  │
│                                                         CI to validate       │
│                                                         config wiring.       │
│    --update-manifest    --no-update-manife…             On success, write    │
│                                                         the avg_success_rate │
│                                                         back into the rSkill │
│                                                         manifest at          │
│                                                         `benchmarks.<scene_… │
│                                                         Surgical edit —      │
│                                                         preserves comments.  │
│                                                         Only fires for       │
│                                                         locally-resolvable   │
│                                                         rSkills.             │
│                                                         [default:            │
│                                                         update-manifest]     │
│    --write-eval         --no-write-eval                 Persist the          │
│                                                         RSkillEvalResult     │
│                                                         JSON to --out        │
│                                                         (default             │
│                                                         rskills/<dir>/eval/… │

<sub>⏱️ 628m 35s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

ManiSkill deploy exposed a real deploy-scene bug: taskless DeployScene injects `_hal_deploy_noop`, which ManiSkill rejects. I’m patching the backend to resolve a deploy default task.

---

<sub>⏱️ 628m 38s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/hal/src/openral_hal/sim_bringup.py.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/hal/src/openral_hal/sim_bringup.py (lines 220-285)

<details>
<summary>47 lines</summary>

```
220.             f"(resolved to {yaml_path})."
221.         )
222.     scene_env = _load_scene_for_hal(str(yaml_path))
223. 
224.     scene_env = _maybe_force_ignore_done(scene_env)
225. 
226.     from openral_sim.registry import SCENES  # noqa: PLC0415  # reason: optional dep
227. 
228.     scene_id = scene_env.scene.id
229.     if scene_id not in SCENES:
230.         raise ROSConfigError(
231.             f"build_sim_env_from_yaml: scene id {scene_id!r} is not registered in "
232.             f"openral_sim.SCENES. Available: {sorted(SCENES)}."  # type: ignore[call-overload]  # reason: _Registry is iterable and yields comparable str keys at runtime
233.         )
234. 
235.     # Resolve robot_id from the YAML, the SCENES fixed_robot registry,
236.     # or the caller-supplied fallback. Schema requires the field but
237.     # robocasa-shaped YAMLs forbid it — same fallback chain
238.     # `openral_cli.deploy_sim._load_scene_robot_id` uses.
239.     robot_id = scene_env.robot_id or SCENES.fixed_robot(scene_id) or robot_id_fallback
240.     if robot_id is None:
241.         raise ROSConfigError(
242.             f"build_sim_env_from_yaml: cannot resolve robot_id for scene "
243.             f"{scene_id!r}; pass robot_id_fallback or set scene.robot_id."
244.         )
245. 
246.     # Dummy VLA — required by the SimEnvironment schema but never
247.     # invoked from the HAL lifecycle path (the HAL drives env.step
248.     # directly via SimAttachedHAL.send_action).
249.     dummy_vla = VLASpec(id="smolvla", weights_uri="hf://lerobot/smolvla_base")
250.     sim_env = SimEnvironment(
251.         robot_id=robot_id,
252.         scene=scene_env.scene,
253.         task=scene_env.task,
254.         vla=dummy_vla,
255.         # ADR-0002 Amendment 3: free-axis scenes (openarm_tabletop_pnp) read
256.         # the mandatory mounting pose off ``SimEnvironment.base_pose``. The
257.         # YAML carries it on the ``SimScene``; propagate it through so
258.         # the loader composes the same env the direct factory path does.
259.         # ``None`` (most scenes) leaves backend behaviour unchanged.
260.         base_pose=scene_env.base_pose,
261.         seed=scene_env.seed,
262.         n_episodes=1,
263.         record_video=False,
264.     )
265.     return SCENES.get(scene_id)(sim_env), scene_env.seed
266.
```

</details>

```
430.     # flag on the adapter. The first :meth:`viewer_render` call promotes
431.     # ``env.unwrapped.render_mode`` to ``"human"`` and lazily opens the
432.     # SAPIEN window — after the policy has loaded, so the WM never sees
433.     # an empty unresponsive window. Mirrors PR #160's simpler_env path.
434.     view_pending = os.environ.get(_VIEW_ENV) == "1"
435.     make_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
436.         "num_envs": 1,
437.         "obs_mode": env_cfg.scene.backend_options.get("obs_mode", "state_dict+rgb"),
438.         "control_mode": env_cfg.scene.backend_options.get("control_mode", "pd_ee_delta_pose"),
439.         "render_mode": None,
440.         # MS3's gym.register pins PickCube-v1 (and most tabletop tasks)
441.         # to max_episode_steps=50, which truncates rollouts at step 50
442.         # regardless of the YAML's task.max_steps. Forward the YAML's
443.         # value so long-horizon configs aren't silently clipped. Same
444.         # pattern as PR #160 for simpler_env.
445.         "max_episode_steps": env_cfg.task.max_steps,
446.         "sensor_configs": {
447.             "width": env_cfg.scene.observation_width,
448.             "height": env_cfg.scene.observation_height,
449.         },
450.     }
451.     robot_uids = env_cfg.scene.backend_options.get("robot_uids")
452.     if robot_uids is not None:
453.         # Reconcile against the task's SUPPORTED_ROBOTS: accept registered
454.         # camera-variants of a supported base (silencing MS3's false warning),
455.         # raise ROSCapabilityMismatch for genuinely-unsupported robots.
456.         _reconcile_robot_uids(env_id, str(robot_uids))
457.         make_kwargs["robot_uids"] = robot_uids
458.     with _suppress_unsupported_robot_warning():
459.         env = gym.make(env_id, **make_kwargs)
460.     return _ManiSkill3Sim(
461.         scene=env_cfg.scene,
462.         task=env_cfg.task,
463.         _env=env,
464.         _view_pending=view_pending,
465.     )
466.
```

<sub>⏱️ 628m 42s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m adding deploy-noop handling to ManiSkill/Simpler so taskless DeployScene boots use a backend default task.

---

<sub>⏱️ 628m 43s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/sim/src/openral_sim/backends/simpler_env.py.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/python/sim/src/openral_sim/backends/simpler_env.py (lines 330-420)

<details>
<summary>91 lines</summary>

```
330.             self._last_image = image
331.         h = self.scene.observation_height
332.         w = self.scene.observation_width
333.         if image is None:
334.             image = np.zeros((h, w, 3), dtype=np.uint8)
335.         return {
336.             "images": {"camera1": image},
337.             "state": state,
338.             "task": self.task.instruction,
339.             "raw": flat,
340.         }
341. 
342. 
343. @SCENES.register(_SIMPLER_ENV_SCENE_ID)
344. def _build_simpler_env_scene(env_cfg: SimEnvironment) -> _SimplerEnvSim:
345.     """Lazily import ``simpler_env`` and build a :class:`_SimplerEnvSim`.
346. 
347.     Delegates the install chain to :func:`openral_sim._deps.ensure_backend_deps`
348.     (`backend_id="simpler_env"`) so the first-use UX mirrors LIBERO /
349.     RoboCasa: one Rich banner + ``typer.confirm`` (bypassed by
350.     ``OPENRAL_AUTO_INSTALL_DEPS=1``) and the upstream git package gets
351.     pinned via ``uv pip install`` -- not ``uv run pip install``, which
352.     silently no-ops on this venv layout.
353.     """
354.     from openral_sim._deps import ensure_backend_deps
355. 
356.     ensure_backend_deps("simpler_env")
357. 
358.     try:
359.         import gymnasium as gym
360.         import simpler_env  # type: ignore[import-not-found,import-untyped,unused-ignore]  # noqa: F401  reason: registers gym envs as a side effect of import
361.     except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover — tested via runtime error path
362.         # ensure_backend_deps re-probes after running its plan; this
363.         # branch only fires when the install ran but `import simpler_env`
364.         # still refuses to resolve. Surface the manual hint as a typed
365.         # error so users have somewhere to look.
366.         raise ROSConfigError(
367.             "SimplerEnv backend installed but `import simpler_env` still fails. "
368.             "Inspect the auto-install output above and re-run: "
369.             "uv sync --all-packages --group simpler-env --inexact && "
370.             "uv pip install "
371.             '"simpler-env @ git+https://github.com/simpler-env/SimplerEnv.git@maniskill3"'
372.         ) from exc
373. 
374.     task_name = _parse_task_id(env_cfg.task.id)
375.     env_id, env_kwargs = _resolve_friendly_name(task_name)
376. 
377.     # Upstream ``simpler_env.make`` injects ``prepackaged_config=True`` and
378.     # ``obs_mode='rgbd'`` — both reject on MS3 v3.0.x (the bridge envs
379.     # only accept ``rgb+segmentation``). Calling ``gym.make`` directly
380.     # with the resolved kwargs keeps the adapter forward-compatible with
381.     # whatever the registered env actually supports.
382.     obs_mode = env_cfg.scene.backend_options.get("obs_mode", _DEFAULT_OBS_MODE)
383.     # SAPIEN/ManiSkill3 opens its viewer eagerly inside the env's
384.     # ``__init__`` (line ~327 of ``sapien_env.py``: ``self.reset(...)``
385.     # is called during construction, which calls ``_reconfigure``,
386.     # which then ``create_viewer`` whenever ``render_mode == "human"``).
387.     # That means passing ``render_mode='human'`` to ``gym.make`` here
388.     # would surface the SAPIEN window during the (slow, blocking)
389.     # rldx sidecar boot — the WM marks the unresponsive window "Not
390.     # Responding" and may force-close it.
391.     #
392.     # Deferred-window mode: when ``OPENRAL_SIM_VIEW=1`` we construct
393.     # with ``render_mode=None`` (no viewer) and stash a
394.     # ``_view_pending`` flag on the adapter. The first
395.     # ``viewer_render()`` call — which fires only after the runner has
396.     # built the policy and started ticking — promotes
397.     # ``env.unwrapped.render_mode`` to ``"human"`` and triggers
398.     # ``render_human()``, which lazily opens the SAPIEN viewer against
399.     # the already-populated scene.
400.     view_pending = os.environ.get(_VIEW_ENV) == "1"
401.     # MS3 registers each bridge / fractal env with a canonical
402.     # ``max_episode_steps`` (60 for the carrot/spoon/cube tasks, 120 for
403.     # the eggplant-in-basket task). Pass the YAML's ``task.max_steps``
404.     # through so the env's TimeLimitWrapper honours the user's budget —
405.     # otherwise long-horizon interactive runs get silently truncated
406.     # back to 60 steps. MS3's ``TimeLimitWrapper`` introspects the
407.     # ``gym.make`` frame and prefers the caller-supplied value when
408.     # non-None (see ``mani_skill.utils.registration.TimeLimitWrapper``).
409.     env = gym.make(
410.         env_id,
411.         obs_mode=obs_mode,
412.         render_mode=None,
413.         max_episode_steps=env_cfg.task.max_steps,
414.         **env_kwargs,
415.     )
416.     return _SimplerEnvSim(
417.         scene=env_cfg.scene,
418.         task=env_cfg.task,
419.         _env=env,
420.         _view_pending=view_pending,
```

</details>

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/pyproject.toml (lines 380-415)

<details>
<summary>36 lines</summary>

```
380. robometer = [
381.     # ADR-0057 — opt-in Robometer-4B reward monitor for the reasoner's
382.     # query_task_progress tool.
383.     #
384.     # The model itself runs in its OWN sidecar venv
385.     # (`tools/robometer_sidecar.py`, provisioned separately — it pins the
386.     # robometer package + transformers==4.57.1); what we install here is the
387.     # node-side ZMQ + msgpack client that
388.     # `openral_runner.backends.reward.robometer_reward` uses to talk to that
389.     # sidecar — the same wire transport as qwen-vlm / locateanything / rldx.
390.     # Required by the reward_monitor_node's query_task_progress service.
391.     "pyzmq>=25",
392.     "msgpack>=1",
393. ]
394. omdet = [
395.     # ADR-0037 (2026-06-12 amendment) — opt-in OmDet-Turbo in-process
396.     # open-vocabulary detector (rskills/omdet-turbo-indoor, engine: zeroshot_hf).
397.     #
398.     # Unlike locateanything, OmDet-Turbo is a first-class transformers
399.     # architecture (AutoModelForZeroShotObjectDetection), so it loads under the
400.     # workspace's own transformers and runs IN PROCESS — no sidecar venv. The
401.     # backend (openral_runner.backends.gstreamer.omdet_turbo_detector) lazily
402.     # imports torch + transformers; this group provides them. Required by the
403.     # `deploy sim --object-detector-manifest <…omdet-turbo-indoor…>` leg.
404.     "transformers==5.3.0",  # reason: AutoModelForZeroShotObjectDetection (OmDet-Turbo). Pinned to match the sim / vla groups so a multi-group sync resolves to one transformers version.
405.     "torch>=2.2",           # reason: OmDet-Turbo forward pass + .to(device); CUDA when available, CPU fallback for GPU-less hosts / deploy sim.
406.     "pillow>=10.0",         # reason: BGR frame -> RGB PIL.Image for the processor.
407.     "timm>=1.0",            # reason: OmDet-Turbo's Swin backbone loads via TimmBackbone under transformers 5.3; also used to rebuild the backbone at the processor's 640² input (the pretrained checkpoint is pinned to 224² — see _fit_timm_backbone_to_processor).
408. ]
409. tensorrt = [
410.     "tensorrt>=10.0,<11",      # reason: ONNX->TRT build-on-load for rSkill runtime_tensorrt; engines are arch/version-specific so rSkills ship ONNX and build here. Match the CUDA-suffixed wheel (tensorrt-cu13 / -cu12) to the host toolkit if the meta-wheel mis-resolves.
411.     "cuda-python>=12.0",       # reason: device malloc/memcpy/stream for TensorRTRuntime.infer via the cuda.bindings API (new-style import: `from cuda.bindings import runtime as cudart`).
412.     "torch>=2.1",              # reason: ONNX fixture export in test_runtime_tensorrt.py — keeps `uv sync --group tensorrt` a self-contained test env (torch.onnx.export authors the tiny model the ONNX->TRT build/infer tests parse).
413. ]
414. onnx-export = [
415.     "transformers>=4.45",  # reason: RTDetrForObjectDetection for ONNX export (one-time, ADR-0037)
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>260 lines</summary>

```
1. r"""RLBench scene adapter — drives a CoppeliaSim/PyRep task through a sidecar.
2. 
3. ADR-0062. RLBench (James et al., 2020, arXiv:1909.12271) is the standard
4. benchmark for 3D/keyframe manipulation policies: 100 Franka tasks on CoppeliaSim
5. via PyRep. CoppeliaSim is the heaviest sim dependency in the tree — a proprietary
6. (free-EDU) ~py3.10 stack whose PyRep Cython extension links a specific
7. CoppeliaSim 4.1.0 install, and the released 3D policies pin the
8. ``MohitShridhar/RLBench@peract`` fork. None of that can live in the openral
9. py3.12 workspace, so — exactly like the Isaac Sim backend
10. (:mod:`openral_sim.backends.isaac_sim`) — we run RLBench in its own venv and talk
11. to it over ZMQ REQ/REP framed by msgpack.
12. 
13. This module is the **openral side**: a thin :class:`SimRollout` that marshals
14. ``reset`` / ``step`` / ``render`` / ``close`` to the sidecar
15. (``tools/rlbench_sidecar.py``) and unwraps the responses. The sidecar owns
16. CoppeliaSim, the RLBench task, and the keyframe motion executor.
17. 
18. Scene category: **single-robot (fixed)** — RLBench tasks are baked onto the
19. Franka Panda, so ``fixed_robot="franka_panda"`` and the CLI rejects ``--robot``.
20. 
21. The ``step`` action is an 8-D keyframe ``[x y z qx qy qz qw gripper_open]`` (the
22. contract the released 3D keyframe policies emit). The sidecar executes it via a
23. plan-and-retry mover and returns multi-view RGB-D point clouds + gripper pose +
24. instruction — the observation those policies consume.
25. 
26. Licensing (CLAUDE.md §1.9): CoppeliaSim is proprietary and NEVER vendored — it is
27. an externally-provisioned dependency the user installs (free EDU license). RLBench
28. and PyRep are open source. The sidecar venv is provisioned out of band (there is
29. no auto-install plan for a multi-GB proprietary simulator); ``_sidecar_python``
30. raises a typed :class:`ROSConfigError` carrying the exact provisioning commands
31. when it is absent.
32. """
33. 
34. from __future__ import annotations
35. 
36. import contextlib
37. import hashlib
38. import os
39. from dataclasses import dataclass
40. from pathlib import Path
41. from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
42. 
43. import numpy as np
44. from numpy.typing import NDArray
45. from openral_core.exceptions import ROSConfigError
46. 
47. from openral_sim.registry import SCENES
48. from openral_sim.rollout import StepResult
49. from openral_sim.sidecar import SidecarClient
50. 
51. if TYPE_CHECKING:
52.     from openral_core import SceneSpec, SimEnvironment, TaskSpec
53. 
54.     from openral_sim.rollout import Observation
55. 
56. _RLBENCH_SCENE_ID = "rlbench"
57. _SIDECAR_PYTHON_ENV = "OPENRAL_RLBENCH_SIDECAR_PYTHON"
58. _SIDECAR_SCRIPT_ENV = "OPENRAL_RLBENCH_SIDECAR_SCRIPT"
59. _COPPELIASIM_ROOT_ENV = "COPPELIASIM_ROOT"
60. _AUTO_SPAWN_ENV = "OPENRAL_RLBENCH_AUTO_SPAWN"
61. 
62. # Externally-provisioned defaults (see the ROSConfigError hint for the recipe).
63. _RLBENCH_VENV = Path.home() / ".cache" / "openral" / "rlbench-policy" / ".venv"
64. _COPPELIASIM_DEFAULT = (
65.     Path.home() / ".cache" / "openral" / "coppeliasim" / "CoppeliaSim_Edu_V4_1_0_Ubuntu20_04"
66. )
67. # Per-scene ZMQ port band (clear of well-known + ephemeral ranges), so two
68. # DIFFERENT rlbench tasks never collide on a shared default port.
69. _PORT_MIN = 21_000
70. _PORT_MAX = 21_999
71. # RLBench launch + first reset is fast (~7 s); a keyframe step runs a motion plan
72. # (seconds). Generous timeouts so a slow plan is not read as a dead sidecar.
73. _DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000
74. _DEFAULT_BOOT_TIMEOUT_S = 300.0
75. _VIDEO_FRAMES_INFO_KEY = "_openral_video_frames"
76. _RENDER_CAMERA = "front"
77. 
78. 
79. def _scene_default_port(rlbench_task: str, variation: int) -> int:
80.     """Deterministic per-task ZMQ port, stable across processes (SHA-256, not ``hash``)."""
81.     key = f"rlbench|{rlbench_task}|{variation}".encode()
82.     digest = int.from_bytes(hashlib.sha256(key).digest()[:4], "big")
83.     return _PORT_MIN + (digest % (_PORT_MAX - _PORT_MIN))
84. 
85. 
86. def _opt_int(value: object, default: int) -> int:
87.     """Coerce a ``backend_options`` value (typed ``object``) to int, else default.
88. 
89.     Rejects ``bool`` (an ``int`` subclass we do not want silently accepted) and
90.     non-scalar / unparsable values — never raises.
91.     """
92.     if isinstance(value, bool) or not isinstance(value, (int, float, str)):
93.         return default
94.     try:
95.         return int(value)
96.     except (ValueError, TypeError):
97.         return default
98. 
99. 
100. @dataclass
101. class _RLBenchSidecar:
102.     """:class:`SimRollout` proxying an RLBench task over the sidecar."""
103. 
104.     scene: SceneSpec
105.     task: TaskSpec
106.     _client: SidecarClient
107.     _record_video: bool = False
108.     _last_image: NDArray[np.uint8] | None = None
109. 
110.     @property
111.     def action_dim(self) -> int:
112.         reply = self._client.call("ping")
113.         return int(self._client.require(reply, "action_dim"))
114. 
115.     def reset(self, seed: int | None = None) -> Observation:
116.         reply = self._client.call("reset", {"seed": seed})
117.         return self._wrap_obs(self._client.require(reply, "observation"))
118. 
119.     def step(self, action: NDArray[np.float32]) -> StepResult:
120.         action_np = np.asarray(action, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1)
121.         reply = self._client.call(
122.             "step",
123.             {"action": action_np, "record_video": self._record_video},
124.         )
125.         info = dict(reply.get("info", {}))
126.         if self._record_video and "video_frames" in reply:
127.             info[_VIDEO_FRAMES_INFO_KEY] = [
128.                 np.asarray(frame, dtype=np.uint8) for frame in reply["video_frames"]
129.             ]
130.         return StepResult(
131.             observation=self._wrap_obs(self._client.require(reply, "observation")),
132.             reward=float(self._client.require(reply, "reward")),
133.             terminated=bool(self._client.require(reply, "terminated")),
134.             truncated=bool(self._client.require(reply, "truncated")),
135.             info=info,
136.         )
137. 
138.     def render(self) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
139.         return None if self._last_image is None else self._last_image.copy()
140. 
141.     def close(self) -> None:
142.         with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
143.             self._client.call("close")
144.         self._client.close()
145. 
146.     def _wrap_obs(self, raw: dict[str, Any]) -> Observation:
147.         images = {k: np.asarray(v, dtype=np.uint8) for k, v in raw.get("images", {}).items()}
148.         frame = self._render_frame(images)
149.         if frame is not None:
150.             self._last_image = frame
151.         clouds = {
152.             k: np.asarray(v, dtype=np.float32) for k, v in raw.get("point_clouds", {}).items()
153.         }
154.         obs: Observation = {
155.             "images": images,
156.             "point_clouds": clouds,
157.             "state": np.asarray(raw.get("state", []), dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1),
158.             "task": raw.get("task", self.task.instruction),
159.         }
160.         gp = raw.get("gripper_pose")
161.         if gp is not None:
162.             obs["gripper_pose"] = np.asarray(gp, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1)
163.         if "gripper_open" in raw:
164.             obs["gripper_open"] = float(raw["gripper_open"])
165.         return obs
166. 
167.     def _render_frame(self, images: dict[str, NDArray[np.uint8]]) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
168.         if _RENDER_CAMERA in images:
169.             return images[_RENDER_CAMERA]
170.         for frame in images.values():
171.             return frame
172.         return None
173. 
174. 
175. def _sidecar_python() -> Path:
176.     """Resolve the rlbench sidecar venv interpreter, or raise with the install hint."""
177.     override = os.environ.get(_SIDECAR_PYTHON_ENV)
178.     if override:
179.         p = Path(override).expanduser()
180.         if not p.is_file():
181.             raise ROSConfigError(f"{_SIDECAR_PYTHON_ENV}={override!r} is not a file.")
182.         return p
183.     default = _RLBENCH_VENV / "bin" / "python"
184.     if default.is_file():
185.         return default
186.     raise ROSConfigError(
187.         "RLBench sidecar venv not found. RLBench needs CoppeliaSim (proprietary, "
188.         "free EDU license; NEVER vendored — ADR-0062) + PyRep + the peract RLBench "
189.         "fork in a py3.10 venv. Provision it (one-time) and point "
190.         f"{_SIDECAR_PYTHON_ENV} at its python:\n"
191.         "  # 1) CoppeliaSim 4.1.0 (Ubuntu20_04 build) -> set COPPELIASIM_ROOT\n"
192.         "  # 2) uv venv --python 3.10 ~/.cache/openral/rlbench-policy/.venv\n"
193.         "  # 3) uv pip install (with COPPELIASIM_ROOT set): PyRep (stepjam) +\n"
194.         "  #    RLBench (MohitShridhar@peract, editable) + gymnasium==1.0.0a2\n"
195.         "See docs/adr/0062-rlbench-benchmark-backend.md for the full recipe."
196.     )
197. 
198. 
199. def _coppeliasim_root() -> Path:
200.     override = os.environ.get(_COPPELIASIM_ROOT_ENV)
201.     root = Path(override).expanduser() if override else _COPPELIASIM_DEFAULT
202.     if not root.is_dir():
203.         raise ROSConfigError(
204.             f"CoppeliaSim root not found at {root}. Set {_COPPELIASIM_ROOT_ENV} to the "
205.             "CoppeliaSim_Edu_V4_1_0_Ubuntu20_04 directory (ADR-0062)."
206.         )
207.     return root
208. 
209. 
210. def _locate_sidecar_script() -> Path:
211.     override = os.environ.get(_SIDECAR_SCRIPT_ENV)
212.     if override:
213.         p = Path(override).expanduser().resolve()
214.         if not p.is_file():
215.             raise ROSConfigError(f"{_SIDECAR_SCRIPT_ENV}={override!r} is not a file.")
216.         return p
217.     here = Path(__file__).resolve()
218.     for parent in here.parents:
219.         candidate = parent / "tools" / "rlbench_sidecar.py"
220.         if candidate.is_file():
221.             return candidate
222.     raise ROSConfigError(
223.         f"Could not locate tools/rlbench_sidecar.py upwards from {here}. Set "
224.         f"{_SIDECAR_SCRIPT_ENV} to its absolute path."
225.     )
226. 
227. 
228. @SCENES.register(_RLBENCH_SCENE_ID, fixed_robot="franka_panda")
229. def _build_rlbench_scene(env_cfg: SimEnvironment) -> _RLBenchSidecar:
230.     """Build an RLBench task behind the out-of-process CoppeliaSim sidecar.
231. 
232.     ``scene.backend_options`` keys: ``rlbench_task`` (required task file stem,
233.     e.g. ``open_drawer``), ``variation`` (default 0), ``port`` (default a
234.     per-task hash), ``max_tries`` (mover retries, default 10).
235.     """
236.     from openral_sim._deps import ensure_backend_deps
237. 
238.     ensure_backend_deps("rlbench_client")
239.     try:
240.         import msgpack  # type: ignore[import-not-found,import-untyped,unused-ignore]  # noqa: F401  reason: opt-in rlbench group
241.         import zmq  # type: ignore[import-not-found,import-untyped,unused-ignore]  # noqa: F401  reason: opt-in rlbench group
242.     except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover — runtime-error path
243.         raise ROSConfigError(
244.             "rlbench backend needs pyzmq + msgpack on the openral venv: "
245.             "uv sync --all-packages --group rlbench --inexact"
246.         ) from exc
247. 
248.     opts = env_cfg.scene.backend_options
249.     rlbench_task = str(opts.get("rlbench_task") or "")
250.     if not rlbench_task:
251.         raise ROSConfigError(
252.             "rlbench scene requires backend_options.rlbench_task (e.g. 'open_drawer')."
253.         )
254.     variation = _opt_int(opts.get("variation"), 0)
255.     host = str(opts.get("host", "127.0.0.1"))
256.     port = _opt_int(opts.get("port"), _scene_default_port(rlbench_task, variation))
257.     max_tries = _opt_int(opts.get("max_tries"), 10)
258.     auto_spawn = os.environ.get(_AUTO_SPAWN_ENV, "1") != "0"
259. 
260.     root = _coppeliasim_root()
```

</details>

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tools/rlbench_sidecar.py (lines 1-280)

<details>
<summary>280 lines</summary>

```
1. r"""RLBench scene sidecar — drives a CoppeliaSim/PyRep RLBench task over ZMQ.
2. 
3. ADR-0062. RLBench (James et al., 2020, arXiv:1909.12271) runs on CoppeliaSim +
4. PyRep, a heavy, externally-provisioned, ~py3.10 stack that cannot be loaded into
5. the openral py3.12 workspace (PyRep builds a Cython extension against a specific
6. CoppeliaSim 4.1.0 install; the released 3D keyframe policies pin the
7. ``MohitShridhar/RLBench@peract`` fork). So — exactly like the Isaac Sim scene
8. backend (:mod:`openral_sim.backends.isaac_sim`) — we run RLBench in its own venv
9. and talk to it over ZMQ REQ/REP framed by msgpack.
10. 
11. This file is the **sidecar side** (no openral import — it runs under the
12. externally-provisioned ``rlbench`` venv). It owns CoppeliaSim, the RLBench task,
13. and the keyframe motion executor. The openral side is
14. :mod:`openral_sim.backends.rlbench`.
15. 
16. Wire protocol (mirrors ``tools/isaac_sidecar.py``)::
17. 
18.     ping  -> {"ok": True, "action_dim": 8, "task": <task_str>, "layout": "rlbench"}
19.     reset -> {"observation": {...}}
20.     step  -> {"observation": {...}, "reward", "terminated", "truncated", "info"}
21. 
22. Observation dict (eval-layer shape + RLBench multi-view extras the 3D keyframe
23. policies consume)::
24. 
25.     images:        {<cam>: HWC uint8 RGB}            # left_shoulder/right_shoulder/wrist/front
26.     point_clouds:  {<cam>: HWC float32 world-frame}  # one per camera
27.     gripper_pose:  (7,) float32  [x y z qx qy qz qw]
28.     gripper_open:  float                              # 1.0 open, 0.0 closed
29.     state:         (8,) float32  [gripper_pose(7), gripper_open(1)]
30.     task:          str instruction
31. 
32. Action: an 8-D keyframe ``[x y z qx qy qz qw gripper_open]`` (world frame,
33. ``wxyz``→ executed as the RLBench convention). The sidecar appends the peract
34. fork's ``ignore_collisions`` channel and executes via a retry-mover that re-tries
35. the planned motion until the end-effector reaches the target pose (<5 mm) — the
36. same closed-loop execution the upstream 3D-policy evaluators use.
37. 
38. License (CLAUDE.md §1.9): CoppeliaSim is proprietary (free EDU license) and is
39. NEVER vendored — it is an externally-provisioned dependency the user installs
40. (see :mod:`openral_sim.backends.rlbench` for the provisioning hint). RLBench and
41. PyRep are open source.
42. """
43. 
44. from __future__ import annotations
45. 
46. import argparse
47. import io
48. import sys
49. from typing import Any
50. 
51. import numpy as np
52. from numpy.typing import NDArray
53. 
54. # Camera set the PerAct/3D-policy checkpoints were trained on (NOT overhead).
55. _CAMERAS = ("left_shoulder", "right_shoulder", "wrist", "front")
56. _RENDER_CAMERA = "front"
57. # peract-fork action layout: [pose(7), gripper(1), ignore_collisions(1)] = 9-D.
58. # The openral side sends 8-D (pose+gripper); we append ignore_collisions=1.
59. _POSE_REACH_TOL_M = 5e-3
60. 
61. # RLBench / PyRep motion-planner path-finding failures. EndEffectorPoseViaPlanning
62. # is sampling-based and raises one of these when a predicted keypose is
63. # unreachable (no path / IK could not solve / no collision-free config path). The
64. # reference 3D Diffuser Actor evaluator counts such a step as a *failed episode*
65. # and continues, so a single stochastic miss does not abort a whole multi-episode
66. # benchmark run. Matched by class name (incl. base classes) so the openral side
67. # never needs to import the externally-provisioned rlbench/pyrep venv.
68. _PLANNER_FAILURE_EXC_NAMES = frozenset(
69.     {
70.         "InvalidActionError",  # rlbench.backend.exceptions — planner found no path
71.         "IKError",  # pyrep.errors — IK could not solve for the target pose
72.         "ConfigurationPathError",  # pyrep.errors — no collision-free config path
73.     }
74. )
75. 
76. 
77. def _is_planner_path_failure(exc: BaseException) -> bool:
78.     """True when *exc* is a RLBench/PyRep motion-planner path-finding failure.
79. 
80.     See :data:`_PLANNER_FAILURE_EXC_NAMES`. Walks the exception's MRO so
81.     subclasses of the known planner errors are matched too.
82.     """
83.     return any(t.__name__ in _PLANNER_FAILURE_EXC_NAMES for t in type(exc).__mro__)
84. 
85. 
86. def _encode_ndarray(obj: Any) -> Any:
87.     if isinstance(obj, np.ndarray):
88.         buf = io.BytesIO()
89.         np.save(buf, obj, allow_pickle=False)
90.         return {"__ndarray__": True, "npy": buf.getvalue()}
91.     return obj
92. 
93. 
94. def _decode_ndarray(obj: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
95.     if "__ndarray__" in obj:
96.         return np.load(io.BytesIO(obj["npy"]), allow_pickle=False)
97.     return obj
98. 
99. 
100. def _parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> argparse.Namespace:
101.     p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="OpenRAL RLBench scene sidecar")
102.     p.add_argument("--task", required=True, help="task id, e.g. rlbench/open_drawer")
103.     p.add_argument(
104.         "--rlbench-task",
105.         required=True,
106.         help="RLBench task file stem, e.g. open_drawer / meat_off_grill / close_jar",
107.     )
108.     p.add_argument("--variation", type=int, default=0)
109.     p.add_argument("--instruction", default="")
110.     p.add_argument("--obs-height", type=int, default=256)
111.     p.add_argument("--obs-width", type=int, default=256)
112.     p.add_argument("--max-steps", type=int, default=25)
113.     p.add_argument("--success-key", default="is_success")
114.     p.add_argument("--max-tries", type=int, default=10, help="retry-mover motion attempts")
115.     p.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
116.     p.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=21100)
117.     p.add_argument("--headless", action="store_true")
118.     return p.parse_args(argv)
119. 
120. 
121. class _RLBenchScene:
122.     """Holds the RLBench env + keyframe executor; speaks the openral obs shape."""
123. 
124.     def __init__(self, args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
125.         self._args = args
126.         self._cameras = _CAMERAS
127.         self._env: Any = None
128.         self._task: Any = None
129.         self._success_key = args.success_key
130.         self._arm_action_mode: Any = None
131.         self._motion_frames: list[NDArray[np.uint8]] = []
132.         # Last wrapped observation (from reset / a successful step). Returned
133.         # verbatim when a planner path-failure ends an episode — there is no
134.         # fresh obs on a failed mover step.
135.         self._last_wrapped_obs: dict[str, Any] = {}
136. 
137.     @property
138.     def action_dim(self) -> int:
139.         # openral-facing keyframe action: pose(7) + gripper(1).
140.         return 8
141. 
142.     def build(self) -> None:
143.         from rlbench.action_modes.action_mode import MoveArmThenGripper
144.         from rlbench.action_modes.arm_action_modes import EndEffectorPoseViaPlanning
145.         from rlbench.action_modes.gripper_action_modes import Discrete
146.         from rlbench.environment import Environment
147.         from rlbench.observation_config import ObservationConfig
148. 
149.         obs_config = ObservationConfig()
150.         obs_config.set_all(False)
151.         # Enable exactly the four PerAct cameras' RGB + point clouds.
152.         for cam in self._cameras:
153.             cam_cfg = getattr(obs_config, f"{cam}_camera")
154.             cam_cfg.rgb = True
155.             cam_cfg.point_cloud = True
156.             cam_cfg.depth = False
157.             cam_cfg.mask = False
158.             cam_cfg.image_size = (self._args.obs_height, self._args.obs_width)
159.         obs_config.gripper_pose = True
160.         obs_config.gripper_open = True
161.         obs_config.joint_positions = True
162. 
163.         self._arm_action_mode = EndEffectorPoseViaPlanning(collision_checking=False)
164.         action_mode = MoveArmThenGripper(
165.             arm_action_mode=self._arm_action_mode,
166.             gripper_action_mode=Discrete(),
167.         )
168.         # An empty dataset root is fine: we only run live tasks (no recorded demos).
169.         self._env = Environment(
170.             action_mode, "/tmp/openral_rlbench_empty", obs_config, headless=True
171.         )
172.         self._env.launch()
173.         self._task = self._env.get_task(_task_class(self._args.rlbench_task))
174.         self._task.set_variation(self._args.variation)
175.         print(
176.             f"[rlbench_sidecar] launched {self._args.rlbench_task} v{self._args.variation}",
177.             flush=True,
178.         )
179. 
180.     def reset(self, seed: int | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
181.         if seed is not None:
182.             np.random.seed(int(seed))
183.         self._task.set_variation(self._args.variation)
184.         descriptions, obs = self._task.reset()
185.         self._instruction = descriptions[0] if descriptions else self._args.instruction
186.         self._last_wrapped_obs = self._wrap_obs(obs)
187.         return self._last_wrapped_obs
188. 
189.     def step(self, action: NDArray[np.float32], *, record_video: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]:
190.         target = np.asarray(action, dtype=np.float64).reshape(-1)[:8]
191.         act9 = np.ones(9, dtype=np.float64)  # peract fork: pose7 + gripper1 + ignore_collisions1
192.         act9[:8] = target
193.         act9[8] = 1.0  # ignore collisions during planning (eval default)
194.         obs = None
195.         reward = 0.0
196.         terminate = False
197.         self._motion_frames = []
198.         if self._arm_action_mode is not None:
199.             self._arm_action_mode.set_callable_each_step(
200.                 self._record_motion_observation if record_video else None
201.             )
202.         planner_failed = False
203.         try:
204.             for _ in range(int(self._args.max_tries)):
205.                 obs, reward, terminate = self._task.step(act9)
206.                 reached = float(np.linalg.norm(target[:3] - obs.gripper_pose[:3]))
207.                 if reached < _POSE_REACH_TOL_M or reward == 1:
208.                     break
209.         except BaseException as exc:
210.             # A genuine fault must still surface; only the sampling-based
211.             # planner's path-finding misses are downgraded to a failed episode.
212.             if not _is_planner_path_failure(exc):
213.                 raise
214.             planner_failed = True
215.             print(
216.                 f"[rlbench_sidecar] planner path-failure ({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}) "
217.                 "-> ending episode as a failure (not crashing the run)",
218.                 flush=True,
219.             )
220.         finally:
221.             if self._arm_action_mode is not None:
222.                 self._arm_action_mode.set_callable_each_step(None)
223. 
224.         if planner_failed:
225.             # EndEffectorPoseViaPlanning could not reach the predicted keypose
226.             # (unreachable target / collision). End the episode as a failure —
227.             # matching the reference 3DDA evaluator — so a single stochastic
228.             # planner miss can't abort a whole multi-episode benchmark run
229.             # (ADR-0062). No fresh obs exists; return the last wrapped one.
230.             reply = {
231.                 "observation": self._last_wrapped_obs,
232.                 "reward": 0.0,
233.                 "terminated": True,
234.                 "truncated": False,
235.                 "info": {self._success_key: False},
236.             }
237.             if record_video:
238.                 reply["video_frames"] = self._motion_frames
239.             return reply
240. 
241.         success = bool(reward == 1)
242.         final_frame = self._frame_from_obs(obs)
243.         if record_video and final_frame is not None:
244.             self._motion_frames.append(final_frame)
245.         self._last_wrapped_obs = self._wrap_obs(obs)
246.         reply = {
247.             "observation": self._last_wrapped_obs,
248.             "reward": float(reward),
249.             "terminated": bool(terminate or success),
250.             "truncated": False,
251.             "info": {self._success_key: success},
252.         }
253.         if record_video:
254.             reply["video_frames"] = self._motion_frames
255.         return reply
256. 
257.     def _record_motion_observation(self, obs: Any) -> None:
258.         frame = self._frame_from_obs(obs)
259.         if frame is not None:
260.             self._motion_frames.append(frame)
261. 
262.     def _wrap_obs(self, obs: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
263.         images: dict[str, NDArray[np.uint8]] = {}
264.         clouds: dict[str, NDArray[np.float32]] = {}
265.         for cam in self._cameras:
266.             rgb = getattr(obs, f"{cam}_rgb", None)
267.             if rgb is not None:
268.                 images[cam] = np.asarray(rgb, dtype=np.uint8)
269.             pcd = getattr(obs, f"{cam}_point_cloud", None)
270.             if pcd is not None:
271.                 clouds[cam] = np.asarray(pcd, dtype=np.float32)
272.         gripper_pose = np.asarray(obs.gripper_pose, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1)
273.         gripper_open = float(obs.gripper_open)
274.         state = np.concatenate([gripper_pose, [gripper_open]]).astype(np.float32)
275.         frame = self._frame_from_images(images)
276.         if frame is not None:
277.             self._last_image = frame
278.         return {
279.             "images": images,
280.             "point_clouds": clouds,
```

</details>

260.     root = _coppeliasim_root()
261.     ld = f"{root}:{os.environ.get('LD_LIBRARY_PATH', '')}".rstrip(":")
262.     display = os.environ.get("DISPLAY", ":1")
263.     # Wrap with `env` so the sidecar's child process gets the CoppeliaSim vars
264.     # (SidecarClient._spawn inherits os.environ minus PYTHONPATH; an explicit
265.     # `env` prefix is clearer than mutating the parent's global environment).
266.     launch_argv = [
267.         "env",
268.         f"{_COPPELIASIM_ROOT_ENV}={root}",
269.         f"LD_LIBRARY_PATH={ld}",
270.         f"QT_QPA_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_PATH={root}",
271.         f"DISPLAY={display}",
272.         str(_sidecar_python()),
273.         str(_locate_sidecar_script()),
274.         "--task",
275.         env_cfg.task.id,
276.         "--rlbench-task",
277.         rlbench_task,
278.         "--variation",
279.         str(variation),

282.         "--obs-height",
283.         str(env_cfg.scene.observation_height),
284.         "--obs-width",
285.         str(env_cfg.scene.observation_width),
286.         "--max-steps",
287.         str(env_cfg.task.max_steps if env_cfg.task.max_steps is not None else 25),
288.         "--success-key",
289.         env_cfg.task.success_key or "is_success",
290.         "--max-tries",
291.         str(max_tries),
292.         "--host",
293.         host,
294.         "--port",
295.         str(port),
296.         "--headless",
297.     ]
298. 
299.     client = SidecarClient(
300.         name="rlbench",
301.         host=host,
302.         port=port,
303.         timeout_ms=_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS,
304.         boot_timeout_s=_DEFAULT_BOOT_TIMEOUT_S,
305.         launch_argv=launch_argv,
306.         auto_spawn=auto_spawn,
307.         expected_identity={"task": env_cfg.task.id, "layout": "rlbench"},
308.     )
309.     client.connect()
310.     return _RLBenchSidecar(
311.         scene=env_cfg.scene,
312.         task=env_cfg.task,
313.         _client=client,
314.         _record_video=env_cfg.record_video,
315.     )

280.             "point_clouds": clouds,

285.         }

287.     def _frame_from_obs(self, obs: Any) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
288.         if obs is None:
289.             return None
290.         preferred = getattr(obs, f"{_RENDER_CAMERA}_rgb", None)
291.         if preferred is not None:
292.             return np.asarray(preferred, dtype=np.uint8)
293.         for cam in self._cameras:
294.             rgb = getattr(obs, f"{cam}_rgb", None)
295.             if rgb is not None:
296.                 return np.asarray(rgb, dtype=np.uint8)
297.         return None
298. 
299.     def _frame_from_images(self, images: dict[str, NDArray[np.uint8]]) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
300.         if _RENDER_CAMERA in images:
301.             return np.asarray(images[_RENDER_CAMERA], dtype=np.uint8)
302.         for frame in images.values():
303.             return np.asarray(frame, dtype=np.uint8)
304.         return None
305. 
306.     def render(self) -> NDArray[np.uint8] | None:
307.         return None
308. 
309.     def close(self) -> None:
310.         if self._env is not None:
311.             self._env.shutdown()
312. 
313. 
314. def _task_class(task_file: str) -> Any:
315.     """Resolve an RLBench task file stem (``open_drawer``) to its task class."""
316.     import importlib
317. 
318.     mod = importlib.import_module(f"rlbench.tasks.{task_file}")
319.     cls_name = "".join(w.capitalize() for w in task_file.split("_"))
320.     return getattr(mod, cls_name)
321. 
322. 
323. def _serve(scene: _RLBenchScene, *, host: str, port: int, task: str) -> int:
324.     import msgpack
325.     import zmq
326. 
327.     ctx = zmq.Context()
328.     sock = ctx.socket(zmq.REP)
329.     sock.bind(f"tcp://{host}:{port}")
330.     print(f"[rlbench_sidecar] serving on tcp://{host}:{port}", flush=True)
331. 
332.     running = True
333.     while running:
334.         raw = sock.recv()
335.         req = msgpack.unpackb(raw, object_hook=_decode_ndarray, raw=False)
336.         endpoint = req.get("endpoint")
337.         data = req.get("data", {}) or {}
338.         try:
339.             if endpoint == "ping":
340.                 reply: dict[str, Any] = {
341.                     "ok": True,
342.                     "action_dim": scene.action_dim,
343.                     "task": task,
344.                     "layout": "rlbench",
345.                 }
346.             elif endpoint == "reset":
347.                 reply = {"observation": scene.reset(seed=data.get("seed"))}
348.             elif endpoint == "step":
349.                 reply = scene.step(
350.                     np.asarray(data["action"], dtype=np.float32),
351.                     record_video=bool(data.get("record_video")),
352.                 )
353.             elif endpoint == "render":
354.                 reply = {"frame": scene.render()}
355.             elif endpoint == "close":
356.                 reply = {"ok": True}
357.                 running = False
358.             else:
359.                 reply = {"error": f"unknown endpoint {endpoint!r}"}
360.         except Exception as exc:
361.             reply = {"error": f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"}
362.         sock.send(msgpack.packb(reply, default=_encode_ndarray, use_bin_type=True))
363. 
364.     sock.close(linger=0)
365.     ctx.term()
366.     scene.close()
367.     return 0
368. 
369. 
370. def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
371.     import os
372. 
373.     os.makedirs("/tmp/openral_rlbench_empty", exist_ok=True)
374.     args = _parse_args(argv)
375.     scene = _RLBenchScene(args)
376.     scene.build()
377.     return _serve(scene, host=args.host, port=args.port, task=args.task)
378. 
379. 
380. if __name__ == "__main__":
381.     raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))
382.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 640m 30s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tests/unit/test_rlbench_sidecar_planner_failure.py.**

/home/allopart/workspace/openral-clock-timestep/tests/unit/test_rlbench_sidecar_planner_failure.py (lines 1-180)

<details>
<summary>113 lines</summary>

```
1. """Regression test: the RLBench scene sidecar must NOT crash a benchmark run
2. when RLBench's sampling-based motion planner fails to reach a predicted keypose.
3. 
4. ADR-0062. ``EndEffectorPoseViaPlanning`` is stochastic — on some seeds it raises
5. ``InvalidActionError`` / ``IKError`` / ``ConfigurationPathError`` because the
6. keypose is unreachable (no path / IK failure / collision). The reference 3D
7. Diffuser Actor evaluator counts that as a *failed episode* and keeps going; if
8. the sidecar instead re-raises, the openral backend turns it into a fatal
9. ``ROSRuntimeError`` and the whole multi-episode run aborts (observed live
10. 2026-06-20 on ``open_drawer`` — one attempt crashed, the rerun passed 2/2).
11. 
12. The sidecar's top-level imports are py3.12-safe (rlbench/pyrep are imported
13. lazily inside ``_RLBenchScene.build``), so this runs as a plain unit test with
14. no CoppeliaSim provisioning. Planner exceptions are matched by class name so the
15. assertion needs no rlbench install — a stand-in exception named
16. ``InvalidActionError`` reproduces the real failure mode (CLAUDE.md §1.11: real
17. control flow, no mock of the code under test).
18. """
19. 
20. from __future__ import annotations
21. 
22. import argparse
23. import importlib.util
24. import sys
25. from pathlib import Path
26. from typing import Any
27. 
28. import numpy as np
29. import pytest
30. 
31. REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
32. 
33. _spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
34.     "rlbench_sidecar", REPO_ROOT / "tools" / "rlbench_sidecar.py"
35. )
36. assert _spec is not None and _spec.loader is not None
37. rlbench_sidecar = importlib.util.module_from_spec(_spec)
38. sys.modules[_spec.name] = rlbench_sidecar
39. _spec.loader.exec_module(rlbench_sidecar)
40. 
41. 
42. # A stand-in for rlbench.backend.exceptions.InvalidActionError — the sidecar
43. # matches planner failures by class name, so the name is what matters.
44. class InvalidActionError(Exception):
45.     """No path could be found (mirrors the RLBench peract-fork exception)."""
46. 
47. 
48. class _UnplannableTask:
49.     """A task whose ``step`` always raises a planner path-failure."""
50. 
51.     def __init__(self) -> None:
52.         self.calls = 0
53. 
54.     def step(self, action: Any) -> Any:
55.         self.calls += 1
56.         raise InvalidActionError("A path could not be found.")
57. 
58. 
59. class _RealFaultTask:
60.     """A task whose ``step`` raises a genuine (non-planner) bug."""
61. 
62.     def step(self, action: Any) -> Any:
63.         raise ValueError("genuine programming error")
64. 
65. 
66. def _scene() -> Any:
67.     args = argparse.Namespace(max_tries=10, success_key="is_success", variation=0)
68.     scene = rlbench_sidecar._RLBenchScene(args)
69.     # Stand in for the wrapped obs cached by reset()/a prior successful step.
70.     scene._last_wrapped_obs = {
71.         "state": np.zeros(8, dtype=np.float32),
72.         "task": "open the bottom drawer",
73.     }
74.     scene._arm_action_mode = None
75.     return scene
76. 
77. 
78. def test_planner_path_failure_ends_episode_as_failure_not_a_crash() -> None:
79.     scene = _scene()
80.     scene._task = _UnplannableTask()
81. 
82.     reply = scene.step(np.zeros(8, dtype=np.float32))
83. 
84.     assert reply["terminated"] is True
85.     assert reply["truncated"] is False
86.     assert reply["reward"] == 0.0
87.     assert reply["info"]["is_success"] is False
88.     # The cached observation is returned (no fresh obs exists on a planner miss).
89.     assert reply["observation"] is scene._last_wrapped_obs
90. 
91. 
92. def test_genuine_error_still_propagates() -> None:
93.     scene = _scene()
94.     scene._task = _RealFaultTask()
95. 
96.     with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="genuine programming error"):
97.         scene.step(np.zeros(8, dtype=np.float32))
98. 
99. 
100. @pytest.mark.parametrize(
101.     ("exc_name", "expected"),
102.     [
103.         ("InvalidActionError", True),
104.         ("IKError", True),
105.         ("ConfigurationPathError", True),
106.         ("ValueError", False),
107.         ("RuntimeError", False),
108.     ],
109. )
110. def test_is_planner_path_failure_matches_by_name(exc_name: str, expected: bool) -> None:
111.     exc = type(exc_name, (Exception,), {})("boom")
112.     assert rlbench_sidecar._is_planner_path_failure(exc) is expected
113.
```

</details>
